emails.subject: Re: Mastercard Partnership – Placed on Hold Pending Revised Commercial Terms
emails.cc:
Senthil Velan <Senthil@raynatours.com>, Hafsa <hafsa@raynatours.com>, 
	Sabah <sabah@raynatours.com>, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, 
	Prateek <prateek@raynatours.com>
emails.body:
Wow, great to know that they dint offer everything for free.

On Thu, 9 Jul 2026, 13:16 , <azhar@raynatours.com> wrote:

> Dear Mr. Manoj,
>
> I would like to update you on the status of the Mastercard partnership.
>
> With the support of Mr. Senthil, I had a chance to review the contract
> which was signed by our colleague Shyam with Mastercard. However, upon my
> detailed review of the executed terms and subsequent discussions with the
> Mastercard team, I have taken the decision to place this partnership on
> hold until the commercial terms are renegotiated to a mutually workable
> structure.
>
> Key concerns identified during review:
>
> 1. Cashback Structure
>
> *Component*
>
> *Rate *
>
> *Beneficiary*
>
> Customer cashback
>
> 7%
>
> End Customer
>
> Mastercard commission
>
> 1%
>
> Mastercard
>
> Total cashback cost
>
> 8%
>
> Borne by Rayna
>
>
>
> 2. Cancellation Risk Sits Entirely with Rayna
> Cashback is pre-funded and credited to the customer's account immediately
> upon purchase. In the event of a customer cancellation, Rayna absorbs the
> full loss with no recovery mechanism from Mastercard.
>
> 3. No Product Exclusions
> The cashback applies universally across all products, including
> already-discounted offer inventory, creating a risk of double discounting
> and margin erosion on promotional lines.
>
> 4. No Direct Marketing Commitment (Our GETs Are Unsecured)
> Mastercard has no direct control over partnership marketing in the region. All
> visibility and promotion depend on their bank partners and are subject to
> each bank's internal policies. This means our GETs cannot be confirmed or
> guaranteed which does not pass our pre-signature governance gate.
>
> Since these terms do not meet the partnership standards we have
> established, this partnership will remain on hold until Mastercard reverts
> with a revised structure that is commercially viable and mutually workable.
> I have communicated this position clearly to their team. I will keep you
> updated as discussions progress and will bring any revised proposal to you
> for review before reactivation.
>
>
>
> *Thanks & Best Regards,*
>
>
>
>

emails.subject: Re: Negative Review Protocol & Department Accountability Framework
emails.body:
Dear Abby,

Thank you for your email.

We are aligned and committed to delivering exceptional guest experiences
for our guests and partners.

Great reviews directly leads to more business and increases the
stickiness of the partners thus building a long association.

A proactive approach is reporting all cases the moment they are identified
to internal teams through a common channel so we can act swiftly before the
review becomes public, perform guest/agent recovery and reinstate trust and
confidence.

We must aim to address the multiple touch points across the tours we
operate.

Please share any documentation you have regarding the use cases or issues
identified over the last year, including how they were resolved at a
process level for both B2B and B2C interactions.

Looking forward

emails.subject: Negative Review Protocol & Department Accountability Framework
emails.body:
Dear Team,

Greetings From Guest Experience Team.

 

Please be inform of a critical shift in how we will manage negative guest reviews posted on public platforms. This new protocol emphasizes departmental accountability, systematic investigation, and management oversight to ensure we address service quality issues at their source.

 

CURRENT SITUATION:

 

We are currently receiving negative guest reviews on public platforms including:

 

   • Google Business Profile

   • TripAdvisor

   • Trustpilot

   • Social Media Platforms

   • Other review channels

 

Negative reviews cite recurring operational issues. Each of these issues reflects gaps in departmental operations and standards. We must address these at their source, not just respond to reviews.

  

 PREVENTION, NOT REACTION

o   Focus on preventing future issues

o   Improving quality at source

o   Reducing negative reviews through better operations

 

This new protocol reflects our commitment to operational excellence and guest satisfaction. By addressing issues at their source and focusing on systemic improvement, we will reduce negative reviews and strengthen Rayna Tours' reputation.

 

Your departmental excellence is critical to our success. We appreciate your attention to this important matter and look forward to your full cooperation and implementation.

 

Thank you for your leadership and dedication to service quality.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Description: Description: C:\Users\User\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\Content.Outlook\L9NVUKD7\Abby Mascarenhas copy.jpg

emails.subject: "Guest Testimonials & Photos - Website Feature"
emails.body:
Dear All,

 

Greetings from Guest Experience Team.

 

We are delighted to showcase the exceptional experiences our valued guests
have enjoyed with Rayna Tours. As part of our ongoing efforts to enhance our
website and share authentic guest stories, we are reaching out to request
your assistance.

 

We would like to feature guest testimonials and travel photos on our website
to showcase real experiences from our wonderful travelers. (attached above
for reference)

 

We kindly request your assistance in collecting feedback from guests who
traveled with us.

 

REQUEST:

 

Please collect from 15-20 guests:

o    Written testimonials (2-3 sentences about their experience)

o    Guest names and travel dates

o    Product

o    Star ratings

o    High-quality photos from their trips

o    Written permission to use their name, testimonial & photos on our
website 

 

DUE DATE: Monday, July 6th 2026

 

SEND TO: feedback@raynatours.com

 

Thank you for helping us share these wonderful travel stories!

 

 

Thank you & Regards,

emails.subject: Weekly Update Sea Region
emails.body:
Dear Mr.Senthil,


Please find below this week's update on holiday packages and activities
across our key destinations, along with the current count of live products.


*Live Package Count by Destination:*

*Key Updates:*

   1. *Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam:* On track — we're continuously adding
   new products and combo packages tailored to our target markets, keeping
   these destinations highly competitive on both range and pricing.
   2. *Bali & Philippines:* Progressing well — new products are being
   listed daily, and we're actively working with suppliers to secure better
   service quality and pricing, in line with our planned timeline.
   3. *New Product Development:* On schedule — driven by ongoing market
   research, our team is launching new packages weekly to stay aligned with
   evolving market demand.
   4. *Banner Image Issue:* Being resolved — a small number of pages are
   currently missing banner images due to an auto-banner system issue. This
   has been escalated to the tech team and remains on track for resolution.
   5. *Package Title Display Issue:* Being resolved — we've identified a
   display issue affecting package names/headlines on a few pages, which has
   also been escalated to the tech team and is progressing toward resolution.

We remain aligned to our timelines on all the above and will keep you
updated on progress.

Thanks
Ranjan

We are looking to enhance our marketing and website content by leveraging authentic guest photos from Rayna Reviews submissions.

✓ Organize and store these photos

✓ Get guest permission to use them on our website/social media

✓ Attribute photos to guests (with credit)

✓ Automate the collection process

 

 

Please let us know how we can go with it.

 

Thank you & Regards,

emails.subject: Fwd: 'China Gives Me a Big Surprise !' – 2026 FAM Recap
emails.body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: China Highlights <info@chinahighlights.org>
Date: Fri, 26 Jun 2026, 18:05
Subject: 'China Gives Me a Big Surprise !' – 2026 FAM Recap
To: <manoj@raynatours.com>


Beyond Itineraries: Ops Deep-Dives, Hotel Inspections & Unique Experiences
Win Partners' Trust
*More Than a FAM Trip*
From June 10 to 20, 15 travel professionals from 11 different countries
joined us on a journey through Beijing, Xi'an, Guilin (Yangshuo), and
Shanghai.

Of course, there were iconic landmarks, beautiful landscapes, and
unforgettable cultural experiences.
But what made this journey special wasn't only where we went—it was what we
discovered along the way.

As one participant later reflected, many arrived with preconceived ideas
about China, only to leave with a completely different perspective.
*What surprised them most was not only how modern and dynamic the country
is, but also how diverse it is—every region offering its own character,
culture, and story.*

Because sometimes the most meaningful discoveries aren't found in an
itinerary. They're found in the people you meet, the perspectives you gain,
and the experiences you share.
*2027 Is Already in the Making*
Preparations for the 2027 China FAM Trip are already underway.
If you are looking to better understand today's China, exchange ideas with
global industry peers, and discover new opportunities for your market, we
invite you to join us on the next journey.
Stay connected and be among the first to apply for the 2027 China FAM Trip.

We hope to see you in China soon.

*The China Highlights Team** ❤️*
Stay Tuned ↓

emails.subject: Action Required: Selecting the Right AI Model for API Calls
emails.to:
Anowar Hossain <ww_anowar@yahoo.com>, Alok Agrawal <alok@raynatours.com>,
	"vaibhav  " <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, Gaurav Verma
	<gaurav.verma@raynatours.com>, "manish@technoheaven.net"
	<manish@technoheaven.net>, Sowmya Chittedath <sowmya@raynatours.com>, Prateek
	<prateek@raynatours.com>, Adnan Zaheer <adnan@raynab2b.com>
emails.body:
Team,
As we scale up the number of AI-powered features and workflows at Rayna, I want to draw your attention to an important engineering and cost discipline: choosing the right AI model for each API call.
With multiple projects now consuming LLM APIs, we are beginning to see significant variation in model selection across teams. In several cases, frontier models are being used for tasks that could be handled effectively � and at a fraction of the cost � by lighter, purpose-fit models. This is not sustainable as we grow.
Why This Matters
Output tokens are typically 5�10x more expensive than input tokens. High-volume tasks such as itinerary drafting, email template generation, document summarisation, or data extraction do not require frontier-level intelligence. Routing these to an appropriate model can reduce API costs by 60�80% per task without any loss in output quality.
Model Pricing Reference
Please refer to the table below when making model selection decisions. Pricing is per million tokens as of June 2026:
Model
Input ($/1M tokens)
Output ($/1M tokens)
Tier
Best For
Claude Haiku 4.5
$1.00
$5.00
Fast / Light
Templates, classification, routing
GLM-5.2 (Zhipu AI)
$1.40
$4.40
Mid
Long docs, structured reasoning
GPT-4.1 (OpenAI)
$2.00
$8.00
Mid-High
General purpose, production APIs
GPT-4o (OpenAI)
$2.50
$10.00
Mid-High
Multimodal, legacy integrations
Claude Sonnet 4.6
$3.00
$15.00
High
Complex reasoning, agentic tasks
Claude Opus 4.8
$5.00
$25.00
Frontier
Highest complexity, creative, strategic

Recommended Routing Guidelines

Routine / high-volume tasks (classification, extraction, template generation): Use Claude Haiku 4.5 or GLM-5.2. GLM-5.2 is particularly cost-effective for output-heavy tasks given its lower output token rate. Mid-complexity tasks (multi-step reasoning, long document analysis, structured agent workflows): Use GPT-4.1 or Claude Sonnet 4.6. Frontier tasks only (customer-facing creative responses, strategic content, high-stakes decisions): Use Claude Opus 4.8 or GPT-4o. Additionally, both Anthropic and OpenAI offer 50% cost reductions for batch processing. If your workload is not latency-sensitive, please explore batch API mode. Next Steps Please review your current API integrations and identify any instances where a lighter model would suffice. If you are unsure about the right model for a specific use case, feel free to reach out � I am happy to review and advise. We will revisit model usage and costs in our next AI review meeting. Going forward, model selection should be a conscious, documented decision in every AI feature design.

Regards, Senthil

emails.subject: Re: SEO & PR Action Items – Summary from Our Discussion
emails.body:
Hi vaibhav,

 
 Immediate Next Steps


	Share the old .aspx URL list for redirect mapping - please check the attachment 
	Coordinate with Alok on FAQ automation - Yes , will discuss  this monday
	Build 2–3 strong backlinks before retrying Wikipedia - yes in process
	Share Quora/Reddit/forum strategies  (https://docs.google.com/document/d/1CN5SjhtH4R065O6qQphWRh9ZzzRnf4FewoyeDDVetRU/edit?tab=t.0)I have  included my 30-day AEO and AI Visibility strategy roadmap




 



	
		
		
		
	
	
		
			
			Title & Description Auto-Update
			
			
			Auto-change quarterly based on CTR and impressions data 
			
			
			Aparna / Tech
			
		
	


 

Develop an automation process that pulls the last three months of Search Console data, identifies high-impression low-CTR pages, generates AI-powered title and meta recommendations, and publishes approved updates automatically through the override system. 

I am planning to build the initial version in Excel(with help of claude). I will try to have it ready by Wednesday. If I face any challenges or limitations, I will take Alok's help to move the automation forward. 
 



	
		
		
		
	
	
		
			
			404 Fixes
			
			
			Remove all broken links – expired products, manual URLs, etc.
			
			
			Aparn

emails.subject: Action Required — Please Sign MSA & SOW | TestingXperts QA Engagement
emails.body:
Hi Senthil Sir,

Hope you are doing well.

As discussed, we have finalised the QA agency selection and agreed on
TestingXperts for the mobile app launch testing. After multiple rounds of
review and negotiation, both the Master Services Agreement (MSA) and
Statement of Work (SOW) have been finalised.

Here is a quick summary of what has been agreed:

- Scope: Functional Testing, Performance Testing, and Security Testing
across Android, iOS, and Web
- Service categories covered: Tours, Visa, Cruise, Yacht, Holiday Packages,
Multi-service Cart Checkout, and Payment Gateways (Checkout.com, Tabby,
Tamara, Telr)
- Device coverage: 6 physical devices + 18 BrowserStack = 24 devices total
- 4-hour P1 critical bug escalation SLA — committed in writing in the SOW
- Total cost: $11,500 (discounted from $14,000) + $500 tool costs
- Milestone-based exit right after each milestone with 7 days notice
- Governing law: Dubai courts
- IP ownership: Full ownership to Rayna Tours on payment

Both documents are attached for your review. Request you to please sign and
return at your earliest so we can kick off the engagement without delay.
Our target start date is 22nd June 2026 to meet the June 25th launch
deadline.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Gaurav Verma
Rayna Tours & Travels
+971566995257

From: Manoj Tulsani manoj@raynatours.com <mailto:manoj@raynatours.com>  
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 9:53 AM
To: Pari pari@raynatours.com <mailto:pari@raynatours.com> 
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com <mailto:senthil@raynatours.com> ; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com <mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com> >
Subject: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari

 

Hi Pari,

We need to recruit for a new and important role — Category Head, Events & Experiences. Please find the complete job description below and begin the search immediately.

  _____  

JOB DESCRIPTION — CATEGORY HEAD, EVENTS & EXPERIENCES

Reports to: Founder and CEO
Department: Events & Experiences (new category)
Location: Dubai, UAE

  _____  

ABOUT THE ROLE

Rayna Tours is launching a dedicated Events & Experiences category. We are looking for an entrepreneurial, well-connected individual to build this category from the ground up. This person will start by personally driving three areas and, as the category grows, will build and lead a team beneath them.

Importantly — this is not a traditional sales role. Rayna's marketing and sales run on a fully automated, AI-powered marketing and performance marketing engine. Anything that goes live on the Rayna platform is automatically promoted and sold through our marketing automation. This means the Category Head does not need to chase sales. Their focus is on sourcing, contracting, and securing the best events, festivals, and retreats, and getting them live on the platform. Our marketing engine does the selling.

  _____  

THE THREE CORE AREAS

1. Local Events Ticketing
Build a portfolio of event ticketing inventory by contracting directly with event organizers across the UAE — concerts, live shows, festivals, sporting events, exhibitions, and entertainment. Secure ticket allocations to go live on the Rayna platform. The benchmark for this area is Platinumlist — the goal is to build Rayna into a serious player in event ticketing over time. This will start modestly and grow month on month, year on year, as relationships with organizers deepen.

2. Music Festival Holiday Packages (Worldwide)
Travelers from UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India increasingly travel internationally to attend major music festivals — Tomorrowland, Coachella, Ultra, Sunburn, Burning Man, and many more. This role involves building relationships with festival organizers worldwide to secure event tickets and access, then collaborating with Rayna's destination heads in each city to build complete travel packages — festival tickets plus flights, hotels, transfers, and experiences.

3. Fitness & Wellness Retreats (Worldwide)
A fast-growing segment. Travelers are increasingly seeking fitness and wellness retreats in destinations like Bali, Phuket, Fukuoka, and other global wellness hubs. This role involves sourcing and partnering with leading retreats worldwide and, in collaboration with our destination teams, building complete retreat travel packages.

  _____  

HOW THE ROLE WORKS WITH OUR DESTINATION TEAMS

Rayna is activating 137 destination cities worldwide, each managed by a regional destination head. The Category Head does not build ground packages alone. When an event or festival is identified in a city — for example a music festival in Barcelona — this person secures the event tickets and access, then coordinates with the relevant destination head for that city to build the complete ground package. This collaboration model allows the role to operate globally without duplicating the destination teams' work.

  _____  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

*	Contract with event organizers across UAE for ticketing inventory
*	Build worldwide relationships with music festival organizers to secure tickets and access
*	Source and partner with fitness and wellness retreats globally
*	Collaborate with destination heads to build complete travel packages around events, festivals, and retreats
*	Get all inventory live on the Rayna platform, where the AI marketing engine promotes and sells it automatically
*	Grow each area month on month and build a team as the category scales
*	Negotiate commercial terms that protect Rayna's margins
*	Report category progress regularly to the Founder and CEO

emails.subject: Re: Block 3 — WS1 & WS3 Go-Live Timelines – Updated Deadlines
emails.body:
Dear Manoj Sir,

I hope you're doing well. I have discussed the timelines with Mr. Vaibhav and Mr. Anket, and wanted to share the updated go-live targets for Workstream 1 and Workstream 3.

 

1. Social Media Automation (WS3) — Target: 30 June 2026

We are currently testing the existing codebase. Week 1 is focused on incremental testing and stabilizing the system;

Week 2 will cover template buildout and integration. Go-live is on track for 30 June 2026.

 

2. Email Campaigns (WS1) — Target: 26–27 June 2026

   WhatsApp & SMS (WS1) — Target: 30 June 2026

The existing email infrastructure is ready. We are now layering in AI capabilities along with segments and journeys, and expect email to be fully live by end of next week.

WhatsApp and SMS require additional testing and will follow shortly after, with a target go-live of 30 June 2026.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions or would like to discuss further.

 

Best regards,

Malik Rupani




From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 01:59:45

To: Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, malik@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, nihad@raynatours.com, anket@raynatours.com, gaurav.verma@raynatours.com, alok@raynatours.com

Subject: Block 3 — Complete Scope | Three Workstreams + Affiliate Social Feature | Vaibhav + Malik

 

Hi Vaibhav and Malik,

I want to formally define the complete scope of Block 3 — our marketing distribution platform. This has grown beyond what was originally planned and I want both of you fully aligned on everything that needs to be built. Please read this fully.


BLOCK 3 — THREE WORKSTREAMS

Block 3 is no longer just email marketing. It is our complete AI-powered marketing distribution and automation platform covering three workstreams. Both of you own this together. I need a combined deadline for all three workstreams by end of this week.


WORKSTREAM 1 — EMAIL, WHATSAPP, SMS, PUSH NOTIFICATIONS

Owner: Vaibhav + Malik (ChatHead)

This is the core marketing platform already in progress. Complete end to end system covering:


	AI-planned email campaigns — planned, scheduled, previewed, approved, distributed automatically
	WhatsApp broadcasting through ChatHead — mass broadcast, personalized messaging, automated follow-ups
	SMS campaigns
	Push notifications — app-based once app is live
	Seed list testing — 10 members, 4 approvals required before any campaign goes live — AI user + Anket + any 2 others
	Co-branded campaigns — for corporate partners, reward programme partners, and affiliates
	Daily campaign forwarding to corporate and reward partners


I need your complete deadline for this workstream. By when is everything fully live and operational? Come back to me with a realistic date this week.


WORKSTREAM 2 — MADGICX INTEGRATION — GOOGLE ADS AND META ADS

Owner: Anket + Malik

AI-powered Google and Meta ads automation through Madgicx. Covers:


	Automated campaign creation from product feed — new destination goes live, ad campaign creates automatically
	Budget optimization — AI allocates spend across campaigns based on performance
	Audience targeting — separate campaigns for India, UAE, and Saudi
	Creative testing — AI tests which content performs best
	Full transparency — human sets rules, AI executes within those rules
	In-house build planning — Malik assesses building Rayna's own ad automation as an extension of Block 3 in parallel


Assessment and go-live date due June 15th from Anket and Malik jointly.


WORKSTREAM 3 — SOCIAL MEDIA AUTOMATION AGENT

Owner: Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad

This is a new and critical addition to Block 3. The Marketing Agent must connect to all Rayna social media platforms and post automatically — no human dependency.

Platforms to connect:


	Instagram — @raynatours_
	Facebook — Rayna Tours
	TikTok
	YouTube — Shorts and full videos
	LinkedIn
	Twitter / X


What the agent does automatically:


	Reads the product feed — every destination, every product, every offer
	Generates market-ready content — reels, static posts, stories, captions, YouTube shorts
	Posts across all platforms on an optimized schedule
	When a new destination goes live — automatically creates and posts destination content across all platforms within hours
	Adjusts posting frequency and timing based on engagement data


Rules we define once — AI executes forever:


	Brand tone and voice — "Your experience, our responsibility."
	Content types per platform — reels for Instagram and TikTok, long form for YouTube, professional for LinkedIn
	Posting frequency per platform
	Languages — English, Arabic, Hindi versions per market
	Offer codes and campaign links


Nihad — you are CC'd on this. The social media automation agent does not replace your role. Your role becomes defining the strategy, rules, and creative direction that the AI follows. You are the brain. The AI is the execution. Work with Vaibhav and Malik to define the exact rules and content guidelines the agent must follow.


ADDITIONAL FEATURE — AFFILIATE SOCIAL MEDIA AUTO-POSTING

Owner: Vaibhav (manages affiliate programme) + Gaurav (builds the feature)

This is one of the most powerful features we can add to our affiliate programme. Here is how it works:

Any affiliate who wants to — connects their social media handles to the Rayna affiliate platform. Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube — any platform they are active on. Once connected, the same AI agent that posts to Rayna's own accounts automatically creates and posts Rayna content to the affiliate's accounts as well.

What the affiliate gets:


	They connect their account once — AI does everything from that point
	AI creates content relevant to their audience and their niche
	AI posts automatically on an optimal schedule
	Their unique discount code and affiliate link is embedded in every post
	When their followers click and book — they earn commission automatically
	Zero effort required after the initial connection


Why this is a game changer:

The biggest reason affiliates sign up and never post is because creating content feels like work. With this feature — they connect once, AI posts for them forever, commission keeps coming in passively. This will dramatically increase affiliate activation rates and the volume of Rayna content reaching new audiences every day.

Gaurav — you are CC'd. This feature needs to be built into the Rayna affiliate platform. It uses the same AI content generation engine as Workstream 3 — the affiliate version personalizes content for each affiliate's audience and embeds their unique discount code and link automatically. Please confirm this is added to your development roadmap and share a build timeline.

Nihad — this feature will be your strongest recruitment pitch to influencers. "Connect your account once. We create the content. We post it. You earn commission automatically." Use this in your outreach immediately once the feature is live.


WHAT I NEED FROM YOU — THIS WEEK


	Vaibhav + Malik — Complete deadline for Workstream 1 — email, WhatsApp, SMS, push notifications. Fully live and operational by when?
	Anket + Malik — Madgicx assessment and go-live date — June 15th deadline stands
	Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad — Initial plan for Workstream 3 — social media automation agent. What platforms first? What is the build timeline? What rules and content guidelines does Nihad need to define?
	Gaurav — Confirm affiliate social auto-posting feature is added to development roadmap. By when can this be live?


Block 3 is the engine that connects everything — our products, our destinations, our offers — to our customers and affiliates across every channel simultaneously. Get this right and our September campaign launch will be unlike anything Rayna has done before.

Your experience, our responsibility.

Subject: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari

 

Hi Pari,

We need to recruit for a new and important role — Category Head, Events & Experiences. Please find the complete job description below and begin the search immediately.

  _____  

JOB DESCRIPTION — CATEGORY HEAD, EVENTS & EXPERIENCES

Reports to: Founder and CEO
Department: Events & Experiences (new category)
Location: Dubai, UAE

  _____  

ABOUT THE ROLE

Rayna Tours is launching a dedicated Events & Experiences category. We are looking for an entrepreneurial, well-connected individual to build this category from the ground up. This person will start by personally driving three areas and, as the category grows, will build and lead a team beneath them.

Importantly — this is not a traditional sales role. Rayna's marketing and sales run on a fully automated, AI-powered marketing and performance marketing engine. Anything that goes live on the Rayna platform is automatically promoted and sold through our marketing automation. This means the Category Head does not need to chase sales. Their focus is on sourcing, contracting, and securing the best events, festivals, and retreats, and getting them live on the platform. Our marketing engine does the selling.

  _____  

THE THREE CORE AREAS

1. Local Events Ticketing
Build a portfolio of event ticketing inventory by contracting directly with event organizers across the UAE — concerts, live shows, festivals, sporting events, exhibitions, and entertainment. Secure ticket allocations to go live on the Rayna platform. The benchmark for this area is Platinumlist — the goal is to build Rayna into a serious player in event ticketing over time. This will start modestly and grow month on month, year on year, as relationships with organizers deepen.

2. Music Festival Holiday Packages (Worldwide)
Travelers from UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India increasingly travel internationally to attend major music festivals — Tomorrowland, Coachella, Ultra, Sunburn, Burning Man, and many more. This role involves building relationships with festival organizers worldwide to secure event tickets and access, then collaborating with Rayna's destination heads in each city to build complete travel packages — festival tickets plus flights, hotels, transfers, and experiences.

3. Fitness & Wellness Retreats (Worldwide)
A fast-growing segment. Travelers are increasingly seeking fitness and wellness retreats in destinations like Bali, Phuket, Fukuoka, and other global wellness hubs. This role involves sourcing and partnering with leading retreats worldwide and, in collaboration with our destination teams, building complete retreat travel packages.

  _____  

HOW THE ROLE WORKS WITH OUR DESTINATION TEAMS

Rayna is activating 137 destination cities worldwide, each managed by a regional destination head. The Category Head does not build ground packages alone. When an event or festival is identified in a city — for example a music festival in Barcelona — this person secures the event tickets and access, then coordinates with the relevant destination head for that city to build the complete ground package. This collaboration model allows the role to operate globally without duplicating the destination teams' work.

  _____  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

*	Contract with event organizers across UAE for ticketing inventory
*	Build worldwide relationships with music festival organizers to secure tickets and access
*	Source and partner with fitness and wellness retreats globally
*	Collaborate with destination heads to build complete travel packages around events, festivals, and retreats
*	Get all inventory live on the Rayna platform, where the AI marketing engine promotes and sells it automatically
*	Grow each area month on month and build a team as the category scales
*	Negotiate commercial terms that protect Rayna's margins
*	Report category progress regularly to the Founder and CEO

  _____  

IDEAL CANDIDATE

*	5+ years experience in events, entertainment, ticketing, travel, or a related field
*	Existing relationships with event organizers, promoters, or venues — strongly preferred
*	Entrepreneurial mindset — comfortable building a category from scratch
*	Strong sourcing, contracting, and negotiation skills
*	Well-networked across the events and entertainment industry
*	Self-driven, able to deliver results independently before a team is built
*	Understanding of the UAE, Saudi, and Indian markets is an advantage
*	Willingness to grow into a senior category leadership position

  _____  

WHY THIS ROLE IS ATTRACTIVE

*	Build and own a brand new category within a major travel company
*	No pressure to chase sales — our AI marketing engine drives the selling. Focus purely on sourcing the best inventory.
*	Backed by Rayna's 137-city destination network, owned assets, technology platform, and automated marketing engine
*	Clear path to build and lead a team as the category scales
*	Direct reporting line to the Founder and CEO

  _____  

Pari — please post this role across all relevant channels and your recruitment network and begin shortlisting immediately. Prioritize candidates with existing event organizer relationships, as that will accelerate the category's growth significantly.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: AI Agents: Key considerations
emails.to:
Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com>, Alok Agrawal <alok@raynatours.com>, anwar@raynatours.com, 
	Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
emails.body:
Team,

Sharing on thoughts on what we're building.

1. Most AI agent projects fail. Gartner expects 40%+ cancelled by 2027. MIT
found 95% of enterprise GenAI pilots delivered no measurable P&L impact.
The cause isn't the models — it's poor economics, unclear outcomes, and
weak governance.

2. Not everything needs an agent. Deterministic workflows (read email →
extract → update → notify) are just automation. A true agent reasons,
plans, adapts, and handles exceptions. The advice: automate the predictable
80%, agent only the 20% that needs judgment. That 20% is where the ROI
actually lives.

3. Build a learning loop, not just agents. The real moat is encoding your
institutional knowledge into the system — private evaluations, private
learning on your own data, and a queryable knowledge base. The sovereignty
test: if you swapped out the underlying AI model tomorrow, would you keep
the expertise your system has accumulated? If yes, you own your loop. If
not, you're renting.

4. Tokenomics matter. A single agent uses ~4x the tokens of a chat turn. A
multi-agent system ~15x. Cost per token is falling fast, but cost per
outcome can still rise if agents are deployed carelessly.

5. Orchestration beats intelligence. Most AI systems fail at routing, state
management, approvals, escalation, and audit trails — not at the model
level. Think of agents as employees: you still need management structure
and accountability.

6. Measure outcomes, not agent count. Revenue generated, cost reduced,
cycle time improved — not "number of agents deployed."



Best,
Senthil

From: Manoj Tulsani

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; Vaibhav Gupta; nihad@raynatours.com; malik@raynatours.com; anket@raynatours.com; Deepak Hiranandani
Subject: You Are Now Head of Partnerships � UAE, Saudi Arabia & India | Azhar


Hi Azhar,

I am writing to formally expand your role and responsibility. Effective immediately, you are appointed as Head of Partnerships � UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India.

Everything we have discussed about corporate partnerships, roadshows, affiliate programme, app promotion, giveaway campaigns, and daily email forwarding � all of it applies across all three markets. Your job is to build, lead, and replicate this across UAE, Saudi, and India simultaneously.

Please read the complete brief sent to you separately covering the full partnership strategy. That is your playbook. Everything in it applies to all three markets.

________________________________

YOUR EXPANDED MANDATE

UAE � you are already active here. Continue and accelerate everything as briefed. UAE is your model market. Everything that works here gets replicated in Saudi and India.

Saudi Arabia � significant opportunity. Rich market, less competitive, strong corporate culture. The approach is identical to UAE. Language may need to be adapted to Arabic for some corporates but the strategy, the products, the affiliate model, the roadshow mechanics � all the same.

India � starting with Pune and North India clusters. Our Pune office already has presence. The corporate culture in Pune tech corridor and Mumbai financial sector is strong. Delhi, Chandigarh, and Ludhiana are high spending markets. Same approach � roadshows, affiliate programme, daily email forwarding, app promotion, giveaway campaigns.

________________________________

YOUR FIRST PRIORITY � RECRUIT TWO PEOPLE

You cannot manage three markets alone. I need you to recruit immediately:

  1.  One Partnership Manager for Saudi Arabia � based in Riyadh. Arabic speaking, strong corporate network in Saudi, understands the local corporate culture. This person replicates your UAE approach in Saudi.
  2.  One Partnership Manager for India � based in Pune. Strong corporate network in Maharashtra and North India. Understands Indian corporate culture � especially IT sector in Pune and financial sector in Mumbai. This person replicates your UAE approach in India.

Recruitment deadline: June 30th.
I need both people identified, interviewed, and offer letters issued by June 30th. They should be onboarded and operational by July 15th.

Work with Pari and Senthil on the recruitment process. Share the job descriptions with Pari by June 15th so the search begins immediately.

________________________________

TRAINING AND ONBOARDING

Once both people are onboarded, you are responsible for training them fully. Options:

  *   Bring them to Dubai for one week � shadow you at roadshows, see the complete operation, understand the products, meet the teams. This is the fastest way to train.
  *   Visit their markets with them for the first few roadshows � introduce them to key corporate contacts, demonstrate the approach, then hand over.
  *   Regular weekly calls � align on campaigns, offers, new products, and performance.

I will personally join a market visit to Saudi and India when the timing is right to meet key corporates and demonstrate Rayna's commitment to those markets.

________________________________

WHAT STAYS THE SAME ACROSS ALL THREE MARKETS

  *   Roadshow mechanics � spin the wheel, giveaway registration, app download, vouchers
  *   Affiliate programme � commission and non-commission two-track approach
  *   Daily email campaign forwarding to corporate contacts
  *   Channel acquisition objective � secure direct communication into every corporate
  *   App promotion � once live
  *   Giveaway campaign integration � once live
  *   Reporting structure � weekly report to Vaibhav

What adapts per market:

  *   Saudi � Arabic language for certain corporates and communications
  *   India � Hindi language for certain communications, local cultural nuances in approach
  *   Product emphasis � in India, international holiday packages and visas are the lead product. In Saudi, outbound holiday packages and UAE experiences are the lead product.

________________________________

IMMEDIATE ACTIONS � THIS WEEK

  1.  Today � Acknowledge this appointment and confirm you accept the expanded responsibility
  2.  June 15th � Job descriptions for Saudi and India Partnership Managers submitted to Pari
  3.  June 15th � All UAE popup stand materials updated with new brand line and product portfolio � coordinate with Asad
  4.  June 20th � July UAE roadshow calendar submitted � minimum 8 events
  5.  June 30th � Both new Partnership Managers identified and offer letters issued
  6.  July 15th � Both new managers onboarded and operational
  7.  July 31st � First roadshows completed in Saudi and India by new managers

________________________________

RESOURCES AND SUPPORT

You will have full support from the Rayna team across all three markets:

  *   Vaibhav � marketing campaigns, co-branded email content, giveaway and affiliate platform
  *   Malik � WhatsApp broadcasting, JetHeads platform, affiliate tracking
  *   Anket � performance marketing alignment
  *   Nihad � social media support for corporate activations
  *   Asad � updated stand designs and marketing materials
  *   Ranjan � India destination products and packages
  *   Rajkumar � Saudi and Dubai products

Azhar � this is a significant expansion of your role and I am giving it to you because I trust your ability to execute. The corporate partnership channel is one of Rayna's most powerful growth levers. You now own it across three of our most important markets. Make it count.

Your experience, our responsibility.

Subject: Corporate Partnership Strategy � Complete Updated Brief | Azhar


Hi Azhar,

I want to completely realign your team's approach to corporate partnerships and roadshows. Please read this fully � this is not just about doing roadshows. This is about building a permanent channel into every corporate in UAE so we can communicate with their employees directly, consistently, and at scale.

________________________________

THE BIGGER PICTURE � WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

Rayna is no longer just a Dubai activities company. We are a complete travel platform � international holiday packages, visas, cruises, attractions, transfers, and soon 116 destinations worldwide. We are adding 20-25 new cities to our holiday portfolio every month. We have an app launching very soon. We are launching a giveaway campaign platform. We are launching an affiliate programme.

Your team sits at the center of all of this for the UAE corporate market. Every corporate you engage is not just a roadshow opportunity � it is a channel into hundreds or thousands of employees who are not yet in our database. Your job is to open that channel and keep it open permanently.

________________________________

PART ONE � POPUP STANDS AND ROADSHOWS

Update all materials immediately:
Everything on your popup stands must be updated. We are no longer selling only Dubai activities. Your stands must reflect the complete Rayna portfolio:

  *   International holiday packages � Thailand, Bali, Singapore, Malaysia, Maldives, Georgia, London, Japan, Europe, Americas � and growing by 20-25 cities every month
  *   International visas � Schengen, USA, UK, and worldwide
  *   Dubai attractions and activities
  *   Water sports, desert safari, balloon, yacht, cruise, floating restaurant
  *   Cruises

Coordinate with Asad immediately to update all banner designs, counter graphics, and printed materials with the new brand line, new destination portfolio, and full product range. Do not go to a single roadshow with old materials.

The objective of every roadshow has two layers:

Layer 1 � Physical activation: Engage as many employees as possible during the event. Spin the wheel, giveaway registration, app download, vouchers. Every person who interacts with you must register on the Rayna platform or download the app before they leave.

Layer 2 � Channel acquisition (More important): A roadshow is a physical activity. It requires your team's time and presence. But if after the roadshow you have a direct communication channel into that corporate � the procurement team, the HR team, an affiliate employee � then every future offer, campaign, and update reaches their employees automatically without you needing to be physically present. This is the real value of every roadshow. Do not leave any corporate without securing a direct communication channel.

How to secure the channel:

  *   Get the procurement or HR manager's email and WhatsApp during the roadshow
  *   Register them as a Rayna affiliate or corporate partner before you leave
  *   From that point forward � every email campaign Rayna sends to its customers gets forwarded to that contact to distribute to their employees

________________________________

PART TWO � AFFILIATE PROGRAMME

We are launching a formal affiliate programme very soon. Every corporate employee can become a Rayna affiliate � they share our offers with their friends and family and earn commission on every booking made through their link.

However � some organizations, especially banks and government entities, do not allow their employees to receive personal commission. For these organizations, your approach must be different:

  *   Commission-friendly organizations � offer the full affiliate programme. Employee registers, gets unique link, earns commission on every booking through their link.
  *   Non-commission organizations � offer discount coupons only for their employees and customers. Any commission generated can be converted into team building activities � we sponsor a desert safari, yacht trip, or activity for their team. This way the organization benefits collectively rather than individual employees receiving cash.

You must know which type of organization you are dealing with before you approach them. Prepare separate proposal documents for each type � one for commission-friendly, one for non-commission. Have both ready before every roadshow.

________________________________

PART THREE � APP PROMOTION

Our app is launching very soon � UAE first. As soon as the app is live and properly tested, promoting app downloads becomes a core part of every roadshow and every corporate communication.

Stay in close contact with Gaurav and the product team. The moment the app is confirmed live � update all your roadshow materials, corporate emails, and WhatsApp communications to include app download as the primary call to action.

At roadshows � every employee who engages with you must download the app on the spot. Staff should assist them. The app download is the most valuable outcome of any roadshow interaction because once the app is on their phone, we have a permanent push notification channel to them.

Giveaway participation will also happen through the app. So app download and giveaway participation become one combined action at every roadshow.

________________________________

PART FOUR � GIVEAWAY CAMPAIGNS

Our giveaway platform is launching very soon. Start preparing your team and your corporate contacts now so you are ready to activate immediately when it goes live.

At every roadshow � promote the upcoming giveaway. Tell employees: "We are launching a massive giveaway very soon � free holiday packages, balloon rides, safari, yacht experiences � completely free to participate. Download our app and you will be the first to know when it launches."

Once giveaway is live � every roadshow becomes a giveaway activation event. Spin the wheel prizes connect to giveaway entries. App downloads connect to giveaway participation. Corporate HR forwards giveaway links to all employees. This creates a viral loop within every corporate.

________________________________

PART FIVE � DAILY EMAIL CAMPAIGN FORWARDING

This is one of the most important things I want you to implement immediately.

Our AI marketing engine sends daily email campaigns to our existing customer database. But corporate employees who are not yet in our database never see these campaigns. You are the bridge.

Here is the process I want you to build:

  1.  Every day � Anket's team sends you the latest Rayna email campaign
  2.  You or your team forwards this campaign to the procurement or HR contact at every registered corporate partner
  3.  That contact forwards it to all employees � or you request them to do so as part of the partnership agreement
  4.  For key corporate partners � we create a co-branded version of the email showing both the corporate logo and Rayna logo, with a special discount exclusive to their employees. This makes the corporate look good to their employees and makes our offer more compelling.
  5.  As employees click and register on Rayna � they enter our database directly. From that point we communicate with them directly.

This daily forwarding process must become an automatic practice. Not occasionally � every day. Coordinate with Anket and Malik to make sure you receive the right campaign content daily and that co-branded versions are prepared for key partners.

________________________________

PART SIX � STAYING ALIGNED WITH RAYNA'S PRODUCTS AND TECHNOLOGY

Your team must be the most informed team in Rayna when it comes to what we offer and what is coming. You cannot sell what you don't know about.

I expect the following from you:

  *   Weekly alignment call with Vaibhav � what new destinations went live this week, what campaigns are running, what offers are available, what technology is launching
  *   Immediate update when app launches � full briefing for your team on app features and how to demonstrate it at roadshows
  *   Immediate update when giveaway launches � full briefing on participation mechanics and how to integrate into roadshows
  *   Immediate update when affiliate programme launches � full briefing on commission structure, who qualifies, and the two-track approach for commission and non-commission organizations

You should never be in a situation where a corporate employee knows something about Rayna that you don't. Stay ahead.

________________________________

FREQUENCY AND TARGETS

  *   July 2026 � Restart at 8-10 roadshows per month. Summer focus � international holiday packages. Every roadshow secures a direct corporate communication channel.
  *   August 2026 � Scale to 12-15 per month
  *   September 2026 � 15-20 per month aligned with full campaign launch

________________________________

REPORTING � EVERY ROADSHOW

After every event, submit a report to Vaibhav covering:

  *   Company name and location
  *   Number of employees engaged
  *   Number of app downloads
  *   Number of registrations collected
  *   Number of giveaway entries
  *   Number of affiliate sign-ups
  *   Corporate contact secured � name, email, WhatsApp
  *   Type of partnership agreed � commission or non-commission
  *   Number of bookings converted within 30 days

________________________________

TWO IMMEDIATE ACTIONS:

  1.  By June 15th � Update all popup stand materials with new brand line, full product range, and destination portfolio. Coordinate with Asad.
  2.  By June 20th � Submit July roadshow calendar � minimum 8 events � and prepare two affiliate proposal documents: one for commission-friendly organizations and one for non-commission organizations.

Azhar � the corporate channel is one of Rayna's most powerful and underutilized assets. You have the relationships. We now have the products, the technology, and the campaigns to back you up. Let's make the most of it.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Rewards Programme Partnerships — Complete Brief + Tracking Sheet | Azhar
emails.body:
Dear Sir,
This is on track.
I have already started meeting with corporates and reward partners, and the team has also requested access to the Google Sheet, which is now approved and in place.
From the coming week onwards, you will start seeing regular daily and weekly updates on the partnership progress, meeting status, follow-ups, and next actions through the tracker.
We will keep this moving in a structured manner and ensure the sheet is updated consistently for clear visibility.
Thanks & Best Regards,

[cid:e355f5b6-806b-4eea-b46b-09324cb0f0b7]

This is on track. I have started meeting withe the corporate and my team has also requested for the google sheet access which is mow approved and in place. from coming week you will see daily and weekly update on partnerships progress.


________________________________
From: Manoj Tulsani
Sent: Sunday, June 7, 2026 5:17 PM
To: azhar@raynatours.com
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; Vaibhav Gupta; malik@raynatours.com; anket@raynatours.com
Subject: Rewards Programme Partnerships — Complete Brief + Tracking Sheet | Azhar


Hi Azhar,

This is your second brief — focused entirely on reward programme partnerships. Please read this carefully. This is one of the biggest untapped opportunities for Rayna in UAE, and I want you to own it fully alongside your roadshow and corporate activation work.

________________________________

WHY REWARD PROGRAMMES ARE CRITICAL FOR RAYNA

People spend reward points more freely than cash. When a customer uses ADCB TouchPoints or Emirates Skywards miles to book a desert safari or a Thailand holiday, they feel they are getting it for free. There is no psychological barrier — no hesitation, no price comparison. They just book. This means reward programme customers convert at significantly higher rates than regular customers.

On top of that — reward companies want their customers to burn their points. Unburned points are a liability on their books. When you approach them as a partner who helps their customers redeem points on meaningful experiences, they will welcome you. You are solving their problem, not just selling to them.

Our objective is simple — get Rayna's products in front of millions of UAE residents who are holding reward points they have not spent. Every reward company you connect with is a channel into thousands of pre-qualified, spending-ready customers.

________________________________

COMPLETE TARGET LIST — UAE REWARD PROGRAMMES

🔴 HIGH PRIORITY — Start immediately:

  1.  ADCB TouchPoints — Already in progress technically. You must manage the relationship side. Meet the ADCB partnerships team, confirm the commercial agreement, and ensure Rayna's products are promoted to their TouchPoints customers actively.
  2.  FAB Rewards — First Abu Dhabi Bank — Second largest bank in UAE. Travel redemption already built into their programme. Rayna is a natural partner. Approach their partnerships team immediately.
  3.  Emirates Skywards — Most recognized loyalty programme in the Middle East. Their members are frequent travelers — exactly our customer. Approach their partnerships and redemption team.
  4.  Etihad Guest — Etihad Airways loyalty programme. Abu Dhabi base — strong UAE resident overlap. Similar approach to Skywards.

🟡 MEDIUM PRIORITY — Target July-August:

  1.  Mashreq Salaam — Points on both credit and debit cards. Wide UAE customer base.
  2.  CBD Rewards — Commercial Bank of Dubai. Online redemption available — easy integration.
  3.  ADIB Exceed Rewards — Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank. Islamic banking segment — large UAE customer base. Instant redemption via app.
  4.  Emirates NBD SmartMiles — Large customer base, travel focused.
  5.  Shukran — Landmark Group — One of the largest retail rewards programmes in Dubai. Millions of members across Home Centre, Max, Splash, Centrepoint.
  6.  Blue Rewards — Al Futtaim Group — Travel and experiences already part of their programme. Rayna is a natural redemption partner.

🟢 GROWTH — Target September onwards:

  1.  Club Apparel — Apparel Group — 75+ brands, large member base
  2.  Aura Rewards — Lifestyle coalition programme
  3.  Beam Wallet — Coalition loyalty network
  4.  Air Arabia Airewards — Budget airline, large UAE resident base
  5.  du Points — Millions of UAE telecom subscribers
  6.  Etisalat (e&) Rewards — Largest telecom in UAE
  7.  DIB Points — Dubai Islamic Bank
  8.  RAK Bank Rewards

Your additional task — You know the UAE market better than anyone. If you are aware of any reward programmes not listed above, add them to the tracker sheet. We do not want to miss any channel.

________________________________

HOW TO APPROACH EVERY REWARD COMPANY

Your objective in every meeting is to agree on one or more of the following:

  1.  Direct API integration — Customer sees their points balance on Rayna's payment page and burns points directly at checkout. This is the best outcome. Tech integration handled by Anwar's team once commercial agreement is signed.
  2.  Voucher redemption partnership — Customer redeems points on the reward platform and receives a Rayna voucher code. They use the code on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780924308033000&sa=E>. Faster to implement while API is being built.
  3.  Co-branded email marketing access — Reward company sends co-branded Rayna emails to their members with special offers and coupon codes. You get access to their customer base without API integration. This is the minimum acceptable outcome from any meeting.
  4.  Gift card partnership — Rayna gift cards available as a redemption option on the reward platform. Customer uses points to buy a Rayna gift card and spends it on any service. This is simple to implement and works well for retail-focused reward programmes like Shukran and Blue Rewards.

If a company offers a technology connection you have not seen before — or if you identify a gap in how we can reach their customers — do not try to solve it yourself. Bring it to Senthil or me immediately. We will find the solution. Your job is to identify the opportunity and the gap. Our job is to build the technology to fill it.

________________________________

DAILY EMAIL CAMPAIGNS TO REWARD PARTNERS

Once any reward partnership is signed — that company becomes part of our daily email campaign distribution. Here is how it works:

  1.  Our AI marketing engine sends daily campaigns to Rayna customers
  2.  For each reward partner — a co-branded version of the same campaign is prepared showing both Rayna and the reward company branding, with a special offer and unique coupon code exclusive to their members
  3.  This co-branded email goes to the reward company's communication team for distribution to their members — or directly to members if they grant us access to their email list
  4.  Members who click and book enter our database directly. From that point we communicate with them directly.

Your job is to negotiate direct email list access wherever possible. If not possible — ensure the reward company commits to forwarding our co-branded campaigns to their members on a regular basis. Get this in writing as part of the partnership agreement.

________________________________

YOUR TRACKING SHEET

I have created a Google Sheet for you to track everything — reward programmes, roadshows, affiliates, and daily campaigns. Please open it, bookmark it, and update it after every meeting, roadshow, and campaign send.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI&source=gmail&ust=1780924308033000&sa=E>

The sheet has four sections:

  *   Rewards Programme Tracker — all target programmes, priority, contact details, meeting status, notes. Update after every contact or meeting.
  *   Corporate Roadshow Tracker — every roadshow across UAE, Saudi, and India. Registrations, app downloads, giveaway entries, affiliate signups, bookings converted.
  *   Affiliate Partner Tracker — every affiliate signed up, type, commission eligibility, bookings and revenue generated.
  *   Daily Email Campaign Tracker — every campaign sent to reward and corporate partners, response rates, registrations generated.

I will check this sheet regularly. This is how I will audit your progress without needing a meeting. Keep it updated — every row, every column, every week. If the sheet is not updated, I will assume the work is not happening.

________________________________

IMPORTANT — YOUR PLATE IS FULL

I know you now have a lot on your plate — managing UAE, recruiting for Saudi and India, roadshows, affiliates, and now rewards. I am not asking you to do all of this alone or immediately.

Sequence for rewards partnerships:

  *   June — July: Focus on the four High Priority programmes — ADCB, FAB, Skywards, Etihad. One meeting per week minimum with each.
  *   August: Medium priority programmes — Mashreq, CBD, ADIB, Shukran, Blue Rewards
  *   September onwards: Growth programmes — as your Saudi and India managers are onboarded and running independently, you will have more bandwidth for the full list

As soon as your Saudi and India managers are onboarded — brief them on the rewards strategy for their markets as well. Saudi has its own reward ecosystem we will brief you on separately.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Updates — Reservation flow, Follow-up Agent, Giveaway, Event Tech, Transfer Module
emails.body:
Hi Senthil,

A quick update on a few items for your awareness and follow-up.

1) Reservation Agent — end-to-end UX flow: Asad is already working on this
and will finish by Monday. It covers the full mobile and desktop customer
journey across all services — discovery, booking, cancellations, My
Bookings, and general customer questions. Please keep it on your follow-up
so it lands Monday.

2) Follow-up Agent (new): one central, company-wide agent that takes any
task from anyone and follows up until it is completed — supplier,
reservation, contracting, KYC, payment, document follow-ups, everything.
Nobody needs to remember or track follow-ups themselves; the task is handed
to this agent. Key design point: it must know when to stop chasing and
escalate to a human, and through which channel.

3) Giveaway (data-collection engine): a campaign offering free gifts,
vouchers and freebies to bring customers to our website — simple
participate-to-win with rules. Goal right now is to collect more customer
data, flowing straight into our database segmentation. Development sits
with Vaibhav; front-end design (to our approved UI/UX and brand guidelines)
sits with Asad.

4) Event Technology (new): Coca-Cola Arena and DTCM API to be fully tested
and live within this month. Follow-up: Adnan.

5) Transfer Module (new): end-to-end solution. Ahmed starts Monday.

Let us discuss anything that needs scoping.

emails.subject: Re: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari
emails.body:
Dear Pari

Can you set up an interview slot for Waleed next Tuesday.

Regards

________________________________
From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com>
Sent: Friday, 12 June 2026 1:01 AM
To: Pari <pari@raynatours.com>
Cc: Senthil Velan <senthil@raynatours.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: RE: New Role � Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari

Hi Pari,

Thank you for the shortlist. Going through all the CVs in detail, here is where I have landed.

Let us shortlist Waleed Elghoul. His background is exactly the profile we need � he has spent years on the ticketing-platform side (Platinumlist) working directly with the organizers of ticketed events across the UAE, Saudi Arabia and Bahrain. That is the core of what this role is about: knowing the event organizers personally and having strong relationships with them, so that whenever they plan an event � especially in Saudi Arabia � we can bring them onto our new ticketing platform, which goes live very soon.

To be clear on the profile we want more of: people who have worked with ticketing platforms and event-ticketing companies (Platinumlist, Virgin Tickets, BookMyShow, Coca-Cola Arena / arena box offices, and similar), whose job has been to sign up and onboard event organizers for ticketed events in the UAE and KSA. The relationships with organizers are what matter most � not a general sales background.

Please keep sourcing and sending me more CVs of this kind. We will finalise together as they come in.

Two things to confirm on Waleed specifically:
1. Whether he has any non-compete restriction from Platinumlist.
2. Whether the organizer relationships are his personally � i.e. he is the one they deal with � or were tied to the Platinumlist brand.

Thanks,
Manoj Tulsani
Founder, Rayna Group
https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781290166451000&sa=E

On Thu, Jun 11, 2026 06:18 PM, Pari <pari@raynatours.com<mailto:pari@raynatours.com>> wrote:

Dear Sir,



Please find attached the CVs I've shortlisted for the Category Head � Events & Experiences role. I've split them into two tiers based on how closely they match the brief.



Tier 1 � Recommend interviewing first:

� Amrrita Jhamm � 25+ years in events and travel, deep UAE roots, founder mindset, and Indian market background. (Emirates / Dnata / Arabian Adventures)

� Ross Byrne � Strong commercial/BD profile with festival, F1, and KSA market experience. (Formula 1, FIFA, EXPO 2020, ADNOC)

� Sophie Weeks � Built a sales and events function from scratch in Dubai, strong UAE partnerships track record.



Tier 2 � Worth considering if Tier 1 doesn't work out:

� Marija Sharif � 15 years in live events sponsorship and commercial strategy. Strong profile; probe whether her organiser relationships translate to inventory sourcing.

� Waleed Elghoul � Best ticketing specialist in the batch, GCC-wide experience (Platinumlist). Could also be a strong first hire under the Category Head.

� Elie Abou Samra � Best candidate for the wellness/retreats pillar specifically, with Red Bull (6 years, Bahrain & Lebanon), NEOM, Puma and AlUla Wellness Festival experience.



Let me know if you want me to go ahead and reach out to any of them.



Thanks & Regards,



[cid:image001.jpg@01DCF9BF.FB4C3EA0]



From: Manoj Tulsani manoj@raynatours.com<mailto:manoj@raynatours.com>
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 9:53 AM
To: Pari pari@raynatours.com<mailto:pari@raynatours.com>
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com<mailto:senthil@raynatours.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com<mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com>>
Subject: New Role � Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari



Hi Pari,

We need to recruit for a new and important role � Category Head, Events & Experiences. Please find the complete job description below and begin the search immediately.

________________________________

JOB DESCRIPTION � CATEGORY HEAD, EVENTS & EXPERIENCES

Reports to: Founder and CEO
Department: Events & Experiences (new category)
Location: Dubai, UAE

________________________________

ABOUT THE ROLE

Rayna Tours is launching a dedicated Events & Experiences category. We are looking for an entrepreneurial, well-connected individual to build this category from the ground up. This person will start by personally driving three areas and, as the category grows, will build and lead a team beneath them.

Importantly � this is not a traditional sales role. Rayna's marketing and sales run on a fully automated, AI-powered marketing and performance marketing engine. Anything that goes live on the Rayna platform is automatically promoted and sold through our marketing automation. This means the Category Head does not need to chase sales. Their focus is on sourcing, contracting, and securing the best events, festivals, and retreats, and getting them live on the platform. Our marketing engine does the selling.

________________________________

THE THREE CORE AREAS

1. Local Events Ticketing
Build a portfolio of event ticketing inventory by contracting directly with event organizers across the UAE � concerts, live shows, festivals, sporting events, exhibitions, and entertainment. Secure ticket allocations to go live on the Rayna platform. The benchmark for this area is Platinumlist � the goal is to build Rayna into a serious player in event ticketing over time. This will start modestly and grow month on month, year on year, as relationships with organizers deepen.

2. Music Festival Holiday Packages (Worldwide)
Travelers from UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India increasingly travel internationally to attend major music festivals � Tomorrowland, Coachella, Ultra, Sunburn, Burning Man, and many more. This role involves building relationships with festival organizers worldwide to secure event tickets and access, then collaborating with Rayna's destination heads in each city to build complete travel packages � festival tickets plus flights, hotels, transfers, and experiences.

3. Fitness & Wellness Retreats (Worldwide)
A fast-growing segment. Travelers are increasingly seeking fitness and wellness retreats in destinations like Bali, Phuket, Fukuoka, and other global wellness hubs. This role involves sourcing and partnering with leading retreats worldwide and, in collaboration with our destination teams, building complete retreat travel packages.

________________________________

HOW THE ROLE WORKS WITH OUR DESTINATION TEAMS

Rayna is activating 137 destination cities worldwide, each managed by a regional destination head. The Category Head does not build ground packages alone. When an event or festival is identified in a city � for example a music festival in Barcelona � this person secures the event tickets and access, then coordinates with the relevant destination head for that city to build the complete ground package. This collaboration model allows the role to operate globally without duplicating the destination teams' work.

________________________________

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  *   Contract with event organizers across UAE for ticketing inventory
  *   Build worldwide relationships with music festival organizers to secure tickets and access
  *   Source and partner with fitness and wellness retreats globally
  *   Collaborate with destination heads to build complete travel packages around events, festivals, and retreats
  *   Get all inventory live on the Rayna platform, where the AI marketing engine promotes and sells it automatically
  *   Grow each area month on month and build a team as the category scales
  *   Negotiate commercial terms that protect Rayna's margins
  *   Report category progress regularly to the Founder and CEO

________________________________

IDEAL CANDIDATE

  *   5+ years experience in events, entertainment, ticketing, travel, or a related field
  *   Existing relationships with event organizers, promoters, or venues � strongly preferred
  *   Entrepreneurial mindset � comfortable building a category from scratch
  *   Strong sourcing, contracting, and negotiation skills
  *   Well-networked across the events and entertainment industry
  *   Self-driven, able to deliver results independently before a team is built
  *   Understanding of the UAE, Saudi, and Indian markets is an advantage
  *   Willingness to grow into a senior category leadership position

________________________________

WHY THIS ROLE IS ATTRACTIVE

  *   Build and own a brand new category within a major travel company
  *   No pressure to chase sales � our AI marketing engine drives the selling. Focus purely on sourcing the best inventory.
  *   Backed by Rayna's 137-city destination network, owned assets, technology platform, and automated marketing engine
  *   Clear path to build and lead a team as the category scales
  *   Direct reporting line to the Founder and CEO

________________________________

Pari � please post this role across all relevant channels and your recruitment network and begin shortlisting immediately. Prioritize candidates with existing event organizer relationships, as that will accelerate the category's growth significantly.

Your experience, our responsibility.

I want to meet tomorrow to discuss two important AI agent initiatives that connect directly to our hotel integration work on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781126510802000&sa=E>:

Agenda � Two AI Agent Builds:

1. Hotel Reservation AI Agent
Once the hotel integration on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781126510802000&sa=E> is complete, we need an AI agent to handle hotel reservations end to end � the same model we are building for activities. Let us discuss the scope, what needs to be in place, and the build timeline.

2. Contract AI Agent � Supplier Master Automation
The second agent will read supplier contracts and automatically extract and populate the key data into our supplier master. This eliminates manual contract entry entirely. For complex contract structures, we can leverage Claude's function calling capability � this allows the AI to read unstructured contract documents, identify specific fields such as rates, terms, payment conditions, and validity dates, and write them directly into the supplier database with precision. This is a powerful fit for our contract onboarding workflow.

Meeting: Tomorrow, June 10th � 3:00 PM Dubai time.
Attendees: Senthil, Adnan, Manish, Rumi.

Please confirm.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Master Task Sheet — Senthil Owner, Vaibhav Co-Owner | Please Keep Updated
emails.body:
Hi Senthil and Vaibhav,

Every task and direction I have given across our recent discussions is now
consolidated into one master sheet. Going forward, this single sheet is
where all of it lives.

Sheet:
https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1JTHdqDmqqDPFupqKXmy8mLheAiSn-xb3WooU0y9aU1A/edit&source=gmail&ust=1781298574289000&sa=E

Ownership:
- Senthil — owner.
- Vaibhav — co-owner with Senthil.

Between you, please follow up with each and every person named against each
task, and keep this sheet updated. When a deadline moves, fill in the
Revised Deadline and add a short Remark explaining what is happening and
why. That way I stay updated on exactly what is moving on every front,
without needing day-to-day follow-ups — I will simply read this sheet.

A few points so we use it cleanly:
- The only columns to edit are Revised Deadline, Status, Remark, Updated
By, and Date. The task, owner, and original deadline stay fixed.
- No deadline should move without a remark. No remark means no extension.
- The named owners are who you follow up with — not a list for me.

If either of you needs to ask me anything, just email me or send me a
WhatsApp — whatever is comfortable.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: RE: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari
emails.body:
Hi Pari,

Thank you for the shortlist. Going through all the CVs in detail, here is
where I have landed.

Let us shortlist Waleed Elghoul. His background is exactly the profile we
need — he has spent years on the ticketing-platform side (Platinumlist)
working directly with the organizers of ticketed events across the UAE,
Saudi Arabia and Bahrain. That is the core of what this role is about:
knowing the event organizers personally and having strong relationships
with them, so that whenever they plan an event — especially in Saudi Arabia
— we can bring them onto our new ticketing platform, which goes live very
soon.

To be clear on the profile we want more of: people who have worked with
ticketing platforms and event-ticketing companies (Platinumlist, Virgin
Tickets, BookMyShow, Coca-Cola Arena / arena box offices, and similar),
whose job has been to sign up and onboard event organizers for ticketed
events in the UAE and KSA. The relationships with organizers are what
matter most — not a general sales background.

Please keep sourcing and sending me more CVs of this kind. We will finalise
together as they come in.

Two things to confirm on Waleed specifically:
1. Whether he has any non-compete restriction from Platinumlist.
2. Whether the organizer relationships are his personally — i.e. he is the
one they deal with — or were tied to the Platinumlist brand.

Thanks,
Manoj Tulsani
Founder, Rayna Group
https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781290166451000&sa=E
