emails.subject: RE: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

 

Please find attached the CVs I've shortlisted for the Category Head – Events & Experiences role. I've split them into two tiers based on how closely they match the brief.

 

Tier 1 – Recommend interviewing first:

• Amrrita Jhamm – 25+ years in events and travel, deep UAE roots, founder mindset, and Indian market background. (Emirates / Dnata / Arabian Adventures)

• Ross Byrne – Strong commercial/BD profile with festival, F1, and KSA market experience. (Formula 1, FIFA, EXPO 2020, ADNOC)

• Sophie Weeks – Built a sales and events function from scratch in Dubai, strong UAE partnerships track record.

 

Tier 2 – Worth considering if Tier 1 doesn't work out:

• Marija Sharif – 15 years in live events sponsorship and commercial strategy. Strong profile; probe whether her organiser relationships translate to inventory sourcing.

• Waleed Elghoul – Best ticketing specialist in the batch, GCC-wide experience (Platinumlist). Could also be a strong first hire under the Category Head.

• Elie Abou Samra – Best candidate for the wellness/retreats pillar specifically, with Red Bull (6 years, Bahrain & Lebanon), NEOM, Puma and AlUla Wellness Festival experience.

 

Let me know if you want me to go ahead and reach out to any of them.

 

Thanks & Regards,

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani manoj@raynatours.com <mailto:manoj@raynatours.com>  
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 9:53 AM
To: Pari pari@raynatours.com <mailto:pari@raynatours.com> 
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari

 

Hi Pari,

We need to recruit for a new and important role — Category Head, Events & Experiences. Please find the complete job description below and begin the search immediately.

  _____  

JOB DESCRIPTION — CATEGORY HEAD, EVENTS & EXPERIENCES

Reports to: Founder and CEO
Department: Events & Experiences (new category)
Location: Dubai, UAE

  _____  

ABOUT THE ROLE

Rayna Tours is launching a dedicated Events & Experiences category. We are looking for an entrepreneurial, well-connected individual to build this category from the ground up. This person will start by personally driving three areas and, as the category grows, will build and lead a team beneath them.

Importantly — this is not a traditional sales role. Rayna's marketing and sales run on a fully automated, AI-powered marketing and performance marketing engine. Anything that goes live on the Rayna platform is automatically promoted and sold through our marketing automation. This means the Category Head does not need to chase sales. Their focus is on sourcing, contracting, and securing the best events, festivals, and retreats, and getting them live on the platform. Our marketing engine does the selling.

  _____  

THE THREE CORE AREAS

1. Local Events Ticketing
Build a portfolio of event ticketing inventory by contracting directly with event organizers across the UAE — concerts, live shows, festivals, sporting events, exhibitions, and entertainment. Secure ticket allocations to go live on the Rayna platform. The benchmark for this area is Platinumlist — the goal is to build Rayna into a serious player in event ticketing over time. This will start modestly and grow month on month, year on year, as relationships with organizers deepen.

2. Music Festival Holiday Packages (Worldwide)
Travelers from UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India increasingly travel internationally to attend major music festivals — Tomorrowland, Coachella, Ultra, Sunburn, Burning Man, and many more. This role involves building relationships with festival organizers worldwide to secure event tickets and access, then collaborating with Rayna's destination heads in each city to build complete travel packages — festival tickets plus flights, hotels, transfers, and experiences.

3. Fitness & Wellness Retreats (Worldwide)
A fast-growing segment. Travelers are increasingly seeking fitness and wellness retreats in destinations like Bali, Phuket, Fukuoka, and other global wellness hubs. This role involves sourcing and partnering with leading retreats worldwide and, in collaboration with our destination teams, building complete retreat travel packages.

  _____  

HOW THE ROLE WORKS WITH OUR DESTINATION TEAMS

Rayna is activating 137 destination cities worldwide, each managed by a regional destination head. The Category Head does not build ground packages alone. When an event or festival is identified in a city — for example a music festival in Barcelona — this person secures the event tickets and access, then coordinates with the relevant destination head for that city to build the complete ground package. This collaboration model allows the role to operate globally without duplicating the destination teams' work.

  _____  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

*	Contract with event organizers across UAE for ticketing inventory
*	Build worldwide relationships with music festival organizers to secure tickets and access
*	Source and partner with fitness and wellness retreats globally
*	Collaborate with destination heads to build complete travel packages around events, festivals, and retreats
*	Get all inventory live on the Rayna platform, where the AI marketing engine promotes and sells it automatically
*	Grow each area month on month and build a team as the category scales
*	Negotiate commercial terms that protect Rayna's margins
*	Report category progress regularly to the Founder and CEO

  _____  

IDEAL CANDIDATE

*	5+ years experience in events, entertainment, ticketing, travel, or a related field
*	Existing relationships with event organizers, promoters, or venues — strongly preferred
*	Entrepreneurial mindset — comfortable building a category from scratch
*	Strong sourcing, contracting, and negotiation skills
*	Well-networked across the events and entertainment industry
*	Self-driven, able to deliver results independently before a team is built
*	Understanding of the UAE, Saudi, and Indian markets is an advantage
*	Willingness to grow into a senior category leadership position

  _____  

WHY THIS ROLE IS ATTRACTIVE

*	Build and own a brand new category within a major travel company
*	No pressure to chase sales — our AI marketing engine drives the selling. Focus purely on sourcing the best inventory.
*	Backed by Rayna's 137-city destination network, owned assets, technology platform, and automated marketing engine
*	Clear path to build and lead a team as the category scales
*	Direct reporting line to the Founder and CEO

  _____  

Pari — please post this role across all relevant channels and your recruitment network and begin shortlisting immediately. Prioritize candidates with existing event organizer relationships, as that will accelerate the category's growth significantly.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: MoM — Rayna AI & Agentic Infrastructure Discussion
emails.body:
Hi Manoj, Hi Senthil,

Sharing the minutes of our discussion on Rayna's AI / Agentic
Infrastructure.

---

CORE DECISION — AI ARCHITECTURE

Do not lock into a single LLM. Build a multi-LLM orchestration layer where
the underlying model is swappable. Use open-source / free models for bulk
and repetitive tasks (hotel contracting, vouchers, follow-ups). Use premium
models (Claude, GPT-4) only where intelligence is genuinely required.

LangChain and similar orchestration frameworks enable this — agents have
defined roles, escalation paths, and model routing based on task type.

Why this matters: Token costs explode at volume. Rayna is not doing 5
transactions a day — we are dealing with millions of records across
contracting, operations, and bookings. Locking into a single LLM and
tokenising everything will become unsustainable. The framework must allow
swapping models if a better or cheaper option appears tomorrow.

Key design principle: For repetitive tasks with no decision-making, use
workflow automation (scripts) — not LLM. LLM only where context-reading,
decision-making, or natural language generation is genuinely needed. Do not
feed raw data to the LLM for calculations — build business logic in the
database layer first, then pass the result.

---

AGENT ROSTER — SCOPED

1. Gatekeeper — Master supplier knowledge. Everything goes through it
before going live.
2. Operations Head — Hotels + Activities. Booking follow-ups,
confirmations, escalations.
3. Transport Manager — 400+ vehicles, insurance, reports (Mohit's domain).
4. HR Agent — Employee management, leave, attendance, internal processes.
5. Follow-up Agent — Automated follow-ups for partnerships, media, banks,
radio. Connects to email / app tools.
6. API Agent — Anwar building. API in / out — document to document. Target:
1–2 days per API integration.
7. Design / Catalog Agent — City pages, banners, sections, content mapping
when a new destination goes live.
8. Contracting Agent — Hotel contracting. Millions of records, low
intelligence required — open-source model.

Note: Manoj Sir to share the full list of 13–14 agents via email for
complete alignment.

---

DESIGN / CATALOG AGENT — DETAIL

Problem: When a new city goes live, multiple things break silently —
products aren't mapped to the city page, banners are missing or expired,
sections aren't configured, and the city isn't promoted on the website.
Currently 100% human-driven and frequently missed.

What the agent will do:
- Auto-map products to the city page when a city goes live
- Set up correct banners and sections from Rayna's content library
- Flag expired banners still running on the website
- Report broken frontend components to the design team
- Pull from Rayna's existing content library (4–5 TB of accumulated videos
and images — never formally segregated)

---

NEXT STEPS

1. Demo sessions — Langchain (Windows-based framework), AWS preferred
vendor, and Agno / freeware option. One demo each, one agent built per
framework, then evaluate and commit.
2. Parallel build — Coders to start on agents now, don't wait for the
framework decision. Use AI to generate code. Identified: Ankit, Hitesh,
Mohsin, Manish, Sumit.
3. Anwar + Sumit + Manish — building agents for their own code / APIs in
parallel.
4. Manoj Sir to share the full agent list (13–14 agents) via email.
5. Content library — formally segregate 4–5 TB of accumulated Rayna content
with city and product-level tagging.

---

Please revert with any corrections or additions.

Regards,
Vaibhav

emails.body:
Dear all

Summarizing the meeting held yesterday


  1.
Rumy to work with Adnan to automate the hotel operations via Agents (Rumy please provide timelines)
  2.
Manish to explore the new method of automating hotel contracting and come back with the results


Regards
Senthil

________________________________
From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com>
Sent: Wednesday, 10 June 2026 1:25 AM
To: senthil@raynatours.com <senthil@raynatours.com>
Cc: ww_anowar@yahoo.com <ww_anowar@yahoo.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>; adnan@raynab2b.com <adnan@raynab2b.com>
Subject: Meeting Tomorrow 3 PM � Hotel Reservation Agent + Contract AI Agent | Senthil Please Forward to Manish + Rumi


Hi Senthil,

Please forward this to Manish and Rumi and confirm their availability for tomorrow at 3 PM Dubai time.

I want to meet tomorrow to discuss two important AI agent initiatives that connect directly to our hotel integration work on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781126510802000&sa=E>:

Agenda � Two AI Agent Builds:

1. Hotel Reservation AI Agent
Once the hotel integration on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781126510802000&sa=E> is complete, we need an AI agent to handle hotel reservations end to end � the same model we are building for activities. Let us discuss the scope, what needs to be in place, and the build timeline.

2. Contract AI Agent � Supplier Master Automation
The second agent will read supplier contracts and automatically extract and populate the key data into our supplier master. This eliminates manual contract entry entirely. For complex contract structures, we can leverage Claude's function calling capability � this allows the AI to read unstructured contract documents, identify specific fields such as rates, terms, payment conditions, and validity dates, and write them directly into the supplier database with precision. This is a powerful fit for our contract onboarding workflow.

Meeting: Tomorrow, June 10th � 3:00 PM Dubai time.
Attendees: Senthil, Adnan, Manish, Rumi.

Please confirm.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Block 3 — Complete Scope | Three Workstreams + Affiliate Social Feature | Vaibhav + Malik

emails.body:
Hi Gaurav,

Thanks for the detailed plan — really appreciate the thorough breakdown. A
few points from my side:

---

TIMELINES

The July/August timeline is not acceptable. Manoj has been clear — end of
June is the only date we are working towards. We need to restructure the
plan accordingly and find a way to make this work within that window.

---

PENDING ITEMS & CONTEXT

Sharing the full list of open items we've discussed, so the team has
complete context:

1. All coupons against affiliate + sub-affiliate — Affiliate portal
2. Affiliate pages — need to sync with Affiliate portal + analytics around
this
3. Affiliate UTM links — BigQuery database integration with Affiliate portal
4. CC BIN — 1-2 tabs missing + CC BIN information & reporting in Affiliate
portal (Admin)
ClickUp:
https://www.google.com/url?q=https://app.clickup.com/t/86d243r6g&source=gmail&ust=1781253135384000&sa=E
5. PostForMe.dev configuration — Anket, please help Gaurav here on this
6. Product feed → Affiliate templates (Service > Country > City)
7. OpenAI — description/hashtags generation for posts in Affiliate portal

Reference ClickUp tasks:
-
https://www.google.com/url?q=https://app.clickup.com/t/86d2wmrnp&source=gmail&ust=1781253135384000&sa=E
-
https://www.google.com/url?q=https://app.clickup.com/t/86d1x0xv4&source=gmail&ust=1781253135384000&sa=E

---

CONTENT ENGINE & TEMPLATES

Once PostForMe.dev configuration is sorted, I will share the code for the
product feed to templates pipeline. This covers the full flow — Service >
Country > City — so templates are dynamically populated from the product
feed.

---

End of June is the target. Let's align on a call this week.

Regards,
Vaibhav

On Tue, Jun 09, 2026 09:46 AM, Gaurav Verma <gaurav.verma@raynatours.com>
wrote:

> Hi Manoj,
>
> Here's the grounded plan for the affiliate social auto-posting feature,
> after reviewing the actual codebase and infrastructure. The headline: *we
> are roughly half-built already* — account connection, token storage,
> scheduling, publishing, analytics and commission tracking exist and work.
> The remaining work is the intelligence layer (AI that writes brand-safe,
> personalised posts and publishes on autopilot) plus the production path and
> test coverage, which don't exist yet because the app has never been
> promoted past its dev environment.
> ------------------------------
>
> *The ask in one line*
> Ring-fence 4 developers at 100% + 1 QA + a full month of DevOps from 16
> Jun to 24 Aug, and clear three inputs this week (content-engine decision,
> PostForMe terms, data + brand-brief access). Do that, and the feature ships
> before September with buffer.
> ------------------------------
>
> *Where we stand*
> ✓ Already built (reuse) ● Must build (the feature)
> Connect IG / FB / YouTube / X / WhatsApp AI content engine (no AI code
> today)
> Token vault + refresh Audience profiling + niche matching
> Schedule, publish, drafts, webhooks Autonomous "posts-forever" scheduler
> Analytics pull (reach / clicks) Brand-compliance admin + kill-switch
> Commission / coupon / link tracking Product-feed sync + consent ledger
> Production path + test coverage
> ------------------------------
>
> *Three timelines*
> Plan Live Scope Verdict
> A — Aggressive ~25 Jul IG+FB, English, human approves each post Assistant,
> not autopilot
> *B — Medium ✓* *24 Aug* *IG+FB+YouTube, Arabic+English, full autopilot,
> real beta, security* *Recommended — full promise + buffer*
> C — Soft ~14 Sep B + TikTok + LinkedIn + big beta Lands on the wall, no
> slack
> ------------------------------
>
> *Recommended Plan B — three parallel tracks*
> Feature build, infra/environment promotion, and testing run concurrently
> from week one — that is what makes 24 Aug achievable.
> Track W1
> 6/16 W2
> 6/23 W3
> 6/30 W4
> 7/07 W5
> 7/14 W6
> 7/21 W7
> 7/28 W8
> 8/04 W9
> 8/11 W10
> 8/18
> *Feature build*
> Content engine (EN)
> Generate → publish
> Autopilot scheduler
> Attribution + earnings
> Admin / brand compliance
> Arabic + RTL
> *Infra / environment promotion*
> DB-secret + migration fix
> Rebuild test/pre-prod/prod pipelines
> Promote feature dev→test→pre-prod
> *Testing & quality*
> Test harness + mocks + CI gate
> Integration + E2E + AI-eval
> Security + load (pre-prod)
> Beta on test/pre-prod
> *Prod cutover + canary → GA*                   *GA*
>
> Feature Infra/Env Testing GA 24 Aug — ahead of the September wall
> ------------------------------
>
> *Why environments matter here*
> We run four environments in four separate AWS accounts — dev → test →
> pre-prod → prod, each with an approval gate. Today only dev works; the app
> has never been promoted to production, and the test/pre-prod/prod deploy
> pipelines need rebuilding for the current multi-container setup. On top of
> that, the riskiest code in the product — the social-posting service, the
> publish webhook, the OAuth flows — currently has zero automated tests. So
> this feature is what finally forces the production path and a real test
> suite to get built. That's why the plan resources a full DevOps month and a
> full QA from day one — it is prerequisite work, not polish.
> ------------------------------
>
> *Team & focus — the decision that determines September*
> Role Dedication Can be shared?
> Full-stack lead (scheduler/architecture) 100% ring-fenced No — never split
> AI/content engineer 100% No
> AI/prompt + Arabic 80–100% Light, Wk1–2 only
> Full-stack (dashboard/admin) 100% No
> QA / SDET 100% from Wk1 Greenfield test harness
> DevOps / infra ~1.0 FTE Wk1–4, 0.5 after Rebuild pipelines + prod cutover
>
> *Splitting the lead or AI engineer onto other fires doesn't make it 2×
> slower — context-switching makes it ~3× slower, because the engine and
> scheduler are deep and not parallelizable.*
> ------------------------------
>
> *What we need this week*
>
>    1. *Content engine:* hand over Vaibhav's engine by 18 Jun, or confirm
>    we build it fresh (biggest schedule swing).
>    2. *PostForMe:* confirm the contract permits auto-posting on
>    affiliates' behalf, and which platforms it covers.
>    3. *Access by 20 Jun:* product-feed, affiliate database, and Nihad's
>    brand-rules brief — engineers are blocked without these.
>    4. *Staffing:* approve ring-fencing the team above for 16 Jun – 24 Aug.
>
> ------------------------------
>
> *The one risk to watch*
> An AI-generated wrong price or claim posted in an affiliate's name —
> public and irreversible. Controlled by: block generation on stale data,
> keep human-approval ON until 500 live posts with zero errors, full audit
> log, and a one-click global kill-switch. Tracked weekly as *"live posts
> since last price/claim error."*
> ------------------------------
>
> Happy to walk through any of this. My recommendation is to lock Plan B and
> the staffing this week so we protect the September window.
>
> Best,
> Gaurav
>
> Rayna Tours · Affiliate Platform · Block 3 — Marketing Distribution
> Platform
>
> On Mon, Jun 08, 2026 12:29 AM, Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Vaibhav and Malik,
>>
>> I want to formally define the complete scope of Block 3 — our marketing
>> distribution platform. This has grown beyond what was originally planned
>> and I want both of you fully aligned on everything that needs to be built.
>> Please read this fully.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *BLOCK 3 — THREE WORKSTREAMS*
>>
>> Block 3 is no longer just email marketing. It is our complete AI-powered
>> marketing distribution and automation platform covering three workstreams.
>> Both of you own this together. I need a combined deadline for all three
>> workstreams by end of this week.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *WORKSTREAM 1 — EMAIL, WHATSAPP, SMS, PUSH NOTIFICATIONS*
>> *Owner: Vaibhav + Malik (ChatHead)*
>>
>> This is the core marketing platform already in progress. Complete end to
>> end system covering:
>>
>>    - AI-planned email campaigns — planned, scheduled, previewed,
>>    approved, distributed automatically
>>    - WhatsApp broadcasting through ChatHead — mass broadcast,
>>    personalized messaging, automated follow-ups
>>    - SMS campaigns
>>    - Push notifications — app-based once app is live
>>    - Seed list testing — 10 members, 4 approvals required before any
>>    campaign goes live — AI user + Anket + any 2 others
>>    - Co-branded campaigns — for corporate partners, reward programme
>>    partners, and affiliates
>>    - Daily campaign forwarding to corporate and reward partners
>>
>> I need your complete deadline for this workstream. By when is everything
>> fully live and operational? Come back to me with a realistic date this week.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *WORKSTREAM 2 — MADGICX INTEGRATION — GOOGLE ADS AND META ADS*
>> *Owner: Anket + Malik*
>>
>> AI-powered Google and Meta ads automation through Madgicx. Covers:
>>
>>    - Automated campaign creation from product feed — new destination
>>    goes live, ad campaign creates automatically
>>    - Budget optimization — AI allocates spend across campaigns based on
>>    performance
>>    - Audience targeting — separate campaigns for India, UAE, and Saudi
>>    - Creative testing — AI tests which content performs best
>>    - Full transparency — human sets rules, AI executes within those rules
>>    - In-house build planning — Malik assesses building Rayna's own ad
>>    automation as an extension of Block 3 in parallel
>>
>> Assessment and go-live date due June 15th from Anket and Malik jointly.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *WORKSTREAM 3 — SOCIAL MEDIA AUTOMATION AGENT*
>> *Owner: Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad*
>>
>> This is a new and critical addition to Block 3. The Marketing Agent must
>> connect to all Rayna social media platforms and post automatically — no
>> human dependency.
>>
>> *Platforms to connect:*
>>
>>    - Instagram — @raynatours_
>>    - Facebook — Rayna Tours
>>    - TikTok
>>    - YouTube — Shorts and full videos
>>    - LinkedIn
>>    - Twitter / X
>>
>> *What the agent does automatically:*
>>
>>    - Reads the product feed — every destination, every product, every
>>    offer
>>    - Generates market-ready content — reels, static posts, stories,
>>    captions, YouTube shorts
>>    - Posts across all platforms on an optimized schedule
>>    - When a new destination goes live — automatically creates and posts
>>    destination content across all platforms within hours
>>    - Adjusts posting frequency and timing based on engagement data
>>
>> *Rules we define once — AI executes forever:*
>>
>>    - Brand tone and voice — "Your experience, our responsibility."
>>    - Content types per platform — reels for Instagram and TikTok, long
>>    form for YouTube, professional for LinkedIn
>>    - Posting frequency per platform
>>    - Languages — English, Arabic, Hindi versions per market
>>    - Offer codes and campaign links
>>
>> Nihad — you are CC'd on this. The social media automation agent does not
>> replace your role. Your role becomes defining the strategy, rules, and
>> creative direction that the AI follows. You are the brain. The AI is the
>> execution. Work with Vaibhav and Malik to define the exact rules and
>> content guidelines the agent must follow.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *ADDITIONAL FEATURE — AFFILIATE SOCIAL MEDIA AUTO-POSTING*
>> *Owner: Vaibhav (manages affiliate programme) + Gaurav (builds the
>> feature)*
>>
>> This is one of the most powerful features we can add to our affiliate
>> programme. Here is how it works:
>>
>> Any affiliate who wants to — connects their social media handles to the
>> Rayna affiliate platform. Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube — any
>> platform they are active on. Once connected, the same AI agent that posts
>> to Rayna's own accounts automatically creates and posts Rayna content to
>> the affiliate's accounts as well.
>>
>> *What the affiliate gets:*
>>
>>    - They connect their account once — AI does everything from that point
>>    - AI creates content relevant to their audience and their niche
>>    - AI posts automatically on an optimal schedule
>>    - Their unique discount code and affiliate link is embedded in every
>>    post
>>    - When their followers click and book — they earn commission
>>    automatically
>>    - Zero effort required after the initial connection
>>
>> *Why this is a game changer:*
>> The biggest reason affiliates sign up and never post is because creating
>> content feels like work. With this feature — they connect once, AI posts
>> for them forever, commission keeps coming in passively. This will
>> dramatically increase affiliate activation rates and the volume of Rayna
>> content reaching new audiences every day.
>>
>> Gaurav — you are CC'd. This feature needs to be built into the Rayna
>> affiliate platform. It uses the same AI content generation engine as
>> Workstream 3 — the affiliate version personalizes content for each
>> affiliate's audience and embeds their unique discount code and link
>> automatically. Please confirm this is added to your development roadmap and
>> share a build timeline.
>>
>> Nihad — this feature will be your strongest recruitment pitch to
>> influencers. "Connect your account once. We create the content. We post it.
>> You earn commission automatically." Use this in your outreach immediately
>> once the feature is live.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *WHAT I NEED FROM YOU — THIS WEEK*
>>
>>    1. *Vaibhav + Malik* — Complete deadline for Workstream 1 — email,
>>    WhatsApp, SMS, push notifications. Fully live and operational by when?
>>    2. *Anket + Malik* — Madgicx assessment and go-live date — June 15th
>>    deadline stands
>>    3. *Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad* — Initial plan for Workstream 3 — social
>>    media automation agent. What platforms first? What is the build timeline?
>>    What rules and content guidelines does Nihad need to define?
>>    4. *Gaurav* — Confirm affiliate social auto-posting feature is added
>>    to development roadmap. By when can this be live?
>>
>> Block 3 is the engine that connects everything — our products, our
>> destinations, our offers — to our customers and affiliates across every
>> channel simultaneously. Get this right and our September campaign launch
>> will be unlike anything Rayna has done before.
>>
>> *Your experience, our responsibility.*
>>
>

Maharashtra + North India

 

Hi Manoj,

Thank you for the detailed brief on the India B2C activation strategy. I
have reviewed it fully and am aligned with the direction for Maharashtra and
North India.

Here is my plan for the next two weeks:

This week - I will connect with Ranjan and Rajkumar to align on the
activation plan, city priorities, and the product readiness status for each
Maharashtra and North India market. Vaibhav will be looped in for the
marketing piece.

By June 16th - I will send you a written activation plan covering: priority
cities, channel strategy, product readiness gaps, and the team structure for
execution.

Ranjan and Rajkumar - please block time this week for an alignment call. I
will share a calendar invite shortly. Come prepared with the current status
of supplier contracts and product live status for Maharashtra and North
India cities.

Regards,
Senthil

 

On Wed, Jun 03, 2026 08:08 PM, Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com
<mailto:manoj@raynatours.com> > wrote:

Hi Senthil,

I've been working through our India B2C strategy and want to share the
direction with you. Looping in Ranjan, Rajkumar, and Vaibhav as they'll be
central to execution.

You know the team and the ground better than anyone - I'm sharing the full
picture here so you can take this forward and shape the execution plan with
them.

  _____  

THE DIRECTION

India B2C is our biggest untapped opportunity right now. Our B2B network is
strong - that's solid. But Rayna as a direct consumer brand in India is
still underexposed. We are starting with two clusters where we have the
strongest presence, the right customer profile, and the highest online
booking behavior.

Our positioning is clear and powerful: we are not a marketplace, we are the
ground operator. We own what we sell. No local agent in any of these cities
can say that. That's our story and every campaign must carry it.

Brand line: "Your experience, our responsibility." - "Aapka safar, hamari
zimmedari."

  _____  

TARGET CITIES - TWO CLUSTERS

Cluster 1 - Maharashtra (Pune base, existing team):
Pune, Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad, Nagpur, Kolhapur

Cluster 2 - North India (High spending, corporate, strong online booking
culture):
Delhi, Chandigarh, Ludhiana, Amritsar, Jaipur, Agra, Meerut

Together these two clusters cover the highest value B2C travel market in
India. Maharashtra for volume and proximity to our Pune office. North India
for high spending customers who book online and travel frequently.

  _____  

TWO PHASE PLAN

Phase 1 - June to August (Soft launch, low cost)

We go quiet but smart. No expensive media yet. Build awareness, test
messaging, collect leads, and simultaneously expand our destination
portfolio.

Destinations to promote - everything currently live: Thailand, Singapore,
Bali, Malaysia, Dubai visas, international visas, Abu Dhabi, Saudi.

Channels - digital only:

*	Meta and Google - geo-targeted to both clusters - Maharashtra 6
cities + North India 7 cities
*	WhatsApp broadcasting to our existing customer database filtered by
these cities
*	PR - Maharashtra Times, ET Pune, Lokmat, Sakal, Hindustan Times
Delhi, Tribune Chandigarh, Dainik Bhaskar, Conde Nast India
*	Corporate partnerships - IT companies Pune tech corridor, financial
sector Mumbai, corporate sector Delhi and Chandigarh
*	Influencer program - hosted trips only, no cash spend. Shortlist
from both Maharashtra and North India creators to come through Vaibhav for
my approval before any outreach.

Two things must be in place before any spend: pricing competitive (Ranjan +
Rajkumar report June 8th) and website fixed (June 9th).

Phase 2 - September onwards (Full campaign, full spend)

TV, radio, newspaper, mall activations - both clusters simultaneously. But
by September we need a global portfolio behind us that no local agent can
match. That's what makes Rayna different. Anyone can sell Thailand and
Singapore. Nobody else can offer this:

*	Europe - London, Paris, Amsterdam, Barcelona, Switzerland, Rome,
Istanbul
*	Far East - Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong
*	Indian Ocean - Maldives, Mauritius, Seychelles
*	Americas - USA, Canada
*	Australia and New Zealand

September campaign: "From Thailand to London, from Bali to Switzerland -
Rayna Tours. Your experience, our responsibility."

Maharashtra channels: TV (Zee Marathi, Star Pravah, Colors Marathi, Star
Plus, Sony), Radio (Mirchi, Big FM, Red FM), Newspaper (Maharashtra Times,
Lokmat, Sakal, TOI Maharashtra), Mall activations (Phoenix Mumbai,
Palladium, Seawoods, Westend Pune)

North India channels: TV (Aaj Tak, News18 India, Star Plus, Zee News), Radio
(Mirchi Delhi, Red FM Delhi, Big FM), Newspaper (Hindustan Times, Times of
India Delhi, Tribune, Dainik Bhaskar, Punjabi Tribune), Mall activations
(Select Citywalk Delhi, DLF Malls, Elante Chandigarh, Ambience Mall Gurgaon)

  _____  

WHAT I NEED FROM YOU - TWO ACTIONS:

Action 1 - By June 20th:
Share execution ownership for each Phase 1 channel with me. Who owns what,
timelines, and how we're going to market across both clusters.

Action 2 - By June 30th:
Identify 5 people internally from Rayna to own the new destination groups.
Their job is supplier contracting, product management, and getting inventory
live by August 31st. Structure:

1.	Europe West - London, Paris, Amsterdam, Barcelona
2.	Europe East + Turkey - Switzerland, Rome, Istanbul, Prague
3.	Far East - Japan, South Korea, China, Hong Kong
4.	Indian Ocean + Africa - Maldives, Mauritius, Kenya, Seychelles
5.	Americas + Australia - USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand

These people start July 1st. August 31st - all destinations live. September
1st - we go loud across both clusters.

  _____  

Senthil, this is a big moment for Rayna in India. I know you and the team
will execute this well. Let's connect once you've had a chance to review
this with Ranjan, Rajkumar, and Vaibhav.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Re: Emergency Unpaid Leave Request
emails.body:
Our Wikidata listing has been deleted. In hindsight, after an experienced Wikidata contributor updated the item, we probably should not have continued making additional changes.
I have already raised a query with the administrator to understand the exact reason for the deletion. Based on their response, we will decide on the next course of action. Going forward, we will be more cautious and avoid making unnecessary changes after community contributors have reviewed or updated the listing.

For now, I will wait for their response and proceed accordingly.


From: aparna@raynatours.com

Sent: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 12:37:14

To: "senthil " <senthil@raynatours.com>

Cc: "vaibhav@raynatours.com " <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, "manish.y@raynatours.com " <manish.y@raynatours.com>, "manoj " <manoj@raynatours.com>

Subject: Re: Emergency Unpaid Leave Request

 

Hi Sir,

I am really sorry. I had only recently created the Wikidata account and asked the team to start updating it. I should have reviewed and verified the information.

The option for "Tour Operator" was not available in Wikidata, so the team selected "Travel Agency" as the closest available category. We have now updated the entity to "Business."

The "Emirati Travel Agency" and some other classifications were added by external Wikidata contributors, not by our team. I will review and update those as well today after 9 PM.



After we created and updated the Rayna Tours Wikidata entity, I noticed that several changes were subsequently made by other Wikidata contributors.





To avoid any confusion or incorrect updates in the future, I have prepared a document outlining the information and positioning we should use going forward. 



https://docs.google.com/document/d/1feQsNfMGhjNOO6YFR-Ky4_lnvs37dt8gWKv0GhKor_s/edit?tab=t.0

Could you please review the document and share your suggestions or corrections? Once I receive your confirmation, we will use it as the standard reference not only for updating Wikidata but also for maintaining consistency across all future entity-building activities.

We will also review and update our existing profiles and references to make sure they are aligned with the approved company positioning and brand information.

Looking forward to your feedback.



Thanks,

Aparna




From: senthil@raynatours.com

Sent: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 11:33:44

To: Aparna A <aparna@raynatours.com>, "manoj " <manoj@raynatours.com>

Cc: "vaibhav@raynatours.com" <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, "manish.y@raynatours.com" <manish.y@raynatours.com>

Subject: Re: Emergency Unpaid Leave Request

 
Aparna

 

While I appreciate the work done to meet deadlines, it is essential the work is done thoroughly.  With the amount of tools available to us via AI, I would have expected you do a validity check and address the gaps (or send the plan for making things better).....  I see neither in your response.  A simple check with Claude on our entry reveals the below gaps....  

 

Good, I can see the page clearly. Here's the analysis:

Critical Error


	instance of is set to "website" — this is wrong. It should be "travel agency" (Q2736768) or "tour operator". This is the most damaging issue as it misclassifies the entire entity.


Descriptions — Incomplete


	English: "Emirati travel agency" is too vague. Better: "Dubai-based travel agency and destination management company"
	Malayalam & Pashto: descriptions are blank — need to be filled in
	Default language row has no description at all


Missing Key Statements These properties are absent and should be added:


	
		
			
			Property
			
			
			Value
			
		
		
			
			country (P17)
			
			
			United Arab Emirates
			
		
		
			
			headquarters location (P159)
			
			
			Dubai
			
		
		
			
			official website (P856)
			
			
			raynatours.com
			
		
		
			
			founded (P571)
			
			
			founding year
			
		
		
			
			industry (P452)
			
			
			tourism / travel agency
			
		
		
			
			logo image (P154)
			
			
			upload to Wikimedia Commons first
			
		
		
			
			legal form (P1454)
			
			
			LLC (since "Rayna Tourism LLC" is an alias)
			
		
	


References


	The single reference on instance of needs to be fixed once the property is corrected
	All other statements added should include references (official website, press coverage, etc.)


Sitelinks

emails.subject: Rayna Tours AI Chatbot — Complete Research Findings & 6-Service Smart Workflows
emails.body:
Dear Manoj Sir,

I hope this email finds you well.

I have completed comprehensive research on leading AI Travel Chatbot
platforms (Layla.ai, Mindtrip, Trip.com) and developed a detailed
architecture for Rayna Tours' AI chatbot solution. This email includes
research findings, feature recommendations, and complete smart workflows
for all 6 services.
------------------------------
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

*Research Goal:* Design an AI chatbot that integrates all Rayna Tours
services into one conversational, searchable, bookable platform.

*Competitors Analyzed:*

   - *Layla.ai* — Visual itineraries, conversational tone, referral-based
   booking
   - *Mindtrip* — Agentic AI flight booking, real-time price comparison,
   direct checkout
   - *Trip.com* — Full OTA with AI layer, multi-currency, loyalty points

*Key Finding:* No competitor combines all-in-one service discovery
(Activities, Hotels, Visas, Cruises, Yachts, Holiday Packages) with AI trip
planning in one unified chat. *This is our competitive advantage.*
------------------------------
RAYNA CHATBOT CONCEPT

A unified AI chatbot where users can:

   1. Chat naturally for information and recommendations
   2. Search all Rayna services via map, text, or voice
   3. Discover products with interactive filters
   4. Book directly without leaving the chat
   5. Manage existing bookings through account integration
   6. Create custom holiday packages with AI assistance

------------------------------
MUST-HAVE FEATURES

   - ✅ Dual-entry system (Quick buttons + Natural chat)
   - ✅ Interactive map view for destination discovery
   - ✅ Progressive filtering (Service → Destination → Category → Products)
   - ✅ Direct checkout from product details (no tab switching)
   - ✅ Account integration for managing bookings
   - ✅ Information mode (travel tips, visa requirements, best times to
   visit)
   - ✅ Voice + text + button input support

*Nice-to-Have:*

   - Trip dashboard with post-booking reminders
   - Price lock feature (24-hour hold)
   - Multi-currency support
   - Real-time availability alerts
   - Customer reviews and ratings

------------------------------
6 SMART WORKFLOWS WORKFLOW 1: ACTIVITIES

*User Input:* "I want to book activities in Bali"

Step 1 - Destination Selection
├─ Show: Interactive world map
├─ User can: Click map OR type city OR speak city name
└─ Result: All activities available in that city

Step 2 - Category Filtering
├─ Show: [Adventure] [Cultural] [Beach] [Food] [Sports] [Other]
├─ User can: Click category OR describe preferences
└─ Result: Filtered activities with images, ratings, duration, price

Step 3 - Browse & Compare
├─ Show: List view + Map view toggle
├─ Each activity shows: Name, rating, duration, price, review count
└─ User can: Click for full details (photos, description, schedule)

Step 4 - Booking Decision
├─ Options: [Book Now] [Add to Wishlist] [Ask Questions] [Share]
├─ If "Book Now": Direct checkout in chat
└─ After booking: Confirmation + Add to trip itinerary

Information Available:
- "Tell me about this destination"
- "What's the best time to visit?"
- "Is it family-friendly?"
- "What should I pack?"

WORKFLOW 2: HOTELS

*User Input:* "Find a hotel in Bali for 5 nights"

Step 1 - Destination + Dates
├─ AI asks: City? Check-in? Check-out? Number of guests?
├─ User can: Click map OR type OR speak
└─ Result: All available hotels with live prices

Step 2 - Preferences Filter
├─ Show: [Budget] [3-Star] [4-Star] [5-Star] [Luxury]
├─ Additional: [Beachfront] [City Center] [Pool] [Spa] [Pet-friendly]
└─ Result: Filtered hotels sorted by rating/price/distance

Step 3 - Browse Hotels
├─ Show: Hotel cards with photos, rating, price/night, amenities
└─ User can: Click for full details (rooms, policies, reviews)

Step 4 - Room Selection & Booking
├─ Show: Available room types with prices
├─ If "Book Now": Proceed to checkout in chat
└─ After booking: Confirmation + check-in details

Information Available:
- "What's the best neighborhood?"
- "Is breakfast included?"
- "What's the cancellation policy?"
- "How far from the airport?"

WORKFLOW 3: VISAS

*User Input:* "I need a visa for Dubai"

Step 1 - Destination & Citizenship
├─ AI asks: Which country visa? Where are you from?
└─ Result: Visa options for that destination

Step 2 - Visa Type Selection
├─ Show: [Tourist] [Business] [Transit] [Residence]
└─ Result: Processing time, cost, requirements

Step 3 - Document Checklist
├─ AI shows: All required documents
├─ User can: Upload documents in chat
└─ AI validates: "All documents received" or "Missing X"

Step 4 - Application & Payment
├─ Show: Total cost breakdown (visa fee + service fee)
├─ If "Proceed": Payment in chat → Application submitted
└─ After booking: Reference number + Tracking link

Information Available:
- "How long does processing take?"
- "What's the visa fee?"
- "Do I need travel insurance?"
- "What if my visa is rejected?"

WORKFLOW 4: CRUISES

*User Input:* "Show me cruise packages for families"

Step 1 - Cruise Preferences
├─ AI asks: Departure city? Duration? Destination? Budget?
└─ Result: Available cruises matching criteria

Step 2 - Cruise Browsing
├─ Show: Cruise cards with ship, itinerary, price/person, rating
├─ View: List + Route map
└─ User can: Click for full details (cabins, entertainment, dining)

Step 3 - Cabin Selection
├─ Options: [Inside] [Oceanview] [Balcony] [Suite]
└─ User can: Compare cabin types & prices

Step 4 - Add-ons & Booking
├─ AI offers: Travel insurance, shore excursions, dining packages
├─ Final booking: Complete in chat with payment
└─ After booking: Cruise details + pre-boarding checklist

Information Available:
- "What's included in the price?"
- "Are there kids' activities?"
- "What's the dress code?"
- "Can I cancel or change dates?"

WORKFLOW 5: YACHTS

*User Input:* "I want to rent a private yacht for 3 days"

Step 1 - Yacht Requirements
├─ AI asks: Location? Duration? Number of guests? Budget?
└─ Result: Available yachts matching specs

Step 2 - Yacht Browsing
├─ Show: Photos, deck layout, cabins, amenities
├─ Each yacht shows: Size, capacity, price/day, crew included
└─ User can: Click for full details (specs, captain, reviews)

Step 3 - Customization
├─ AI offers: Captain, chef, water sports, jet ski
└─ Result: Custom yacht experience with pricing

Step 4 - Booking
├─ Show: Total cost breakdown (yacht + crew + add-ons)
├─ Payment: Complete checkout in chat
└─ After booking: Confirmation + captain contact details

Information Available:
- "Is a captain included?"
- "What water sports are available?"
- "What's the cancellation policy?"
- "Do I need any experience?"

WORKFLOW 6: HOLIDAY PACKAGES

*User Input:* "Plan a 7-day honeymoon to Bali"

*TWO OPTIONS PRESENTED:*

OPTION A — AI-GENERATED CUSTOM PACKAGE
─────────────────────────────────────────
Step 1 - Gather Preferences
├─ AI asks: Destination? Duration? Budget? Travel style?
├─ Also: Accommodation type? Activity preferences? Dietary needs?
└─ User provides: All inputs via voice/text/buttons

Step 2 - AI Builds Itinerary
├─ AI creates: Day-by-day itinerary using Rayna's inventory
├─ Shows: Hotels, activities, restaurants, transfers
└─ User can: Review full plan with photos and prices

Step 3 - Customization
├─ User can: Add/Remove activities, Swap hotels, Adjust dates
├─ AI shows: Updated price after each change
└─ Price breakdown: Flight + Hotel + Activities (separate)

Step 4 - Booking
├─ Show: Final itinerary + total cost
├─ Payment: Complete checkout in chat
└─ After booking: Trip dashboard + travel checklist + reminders

OPTION B — PRE-BUILT PACKAGES
─────────────────────────────────────────
Step 1 - Browse Packages
├─ Show: All Rayna holiday packages
├─ Filter: By destination, duration, budget, theme
└─ User can: Click for full details

Step 2 - Package Details
├─ Show: Day-by-day itinerary, hotels, activities, meals included
└─ User can: Ask questions about package

Step 3 - Booking
├─ Simple checkout: Confirm & pay
└─ Confirmation: Instant booking confirmation

Information Available (Both Options):
- "What's the best time to visit?"
- "Can I extend by 2 more days?"
- "What documents do I need?"
- "Can I travel solo?"
- "What's the weather like?"

------------------------------
INFORMATION / CHAT MODE (Available Across All Services)

Users can ask questions anytime without selecting a service:

   - "Tell me about Bali"
   - "Best budget destinations in Asia"
   - "What's the best time to visit Dubai?"
   - "How much should I budget for 5 days in Europe?"
   - "What are visa requirements for Schengen?"
   - "Recommend me a destination"

*AI Response:* Provides information + suggests relevant Rayna services
------------------------------
ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT (Post-Booking)

Users can ask:

   - "Show my bookings"
   - "Cancel my hotel booking"
   - "Modify my activity date"
   - "Download my booking confirmation"
   - "What's my trip itinerary?"

*AI shows:* Trip dashboard with all linked bookings + quick modify/cancel
options
------------------------------
RAYNA'S UNIQUE COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGES

   1. *All-in-one platform* — Activities, Hotels, Visas, Cruises, Yachts,
   Packages in ONE chat
   2. *Dual-path holiday planning* — Pre-built OR custom AI-generated
   packages
   3. *No tab-switching* — Complete booking journey inside chat
   4. *Account integration* — Manage all bookings from one place
   5. *Information + Booking combined* — Users never need to leave the chat

------------------------------
NEXT STEPS

   1. Finalize which features to build first (MVP)
   2. Design UI/UX for each workflow in Figma
   3. Integrate Rayna's existing API/database
   4. Set up payment gateway integration
   5. Deploy chatbot on web + mobile app

------------------------------
REQUEST FOR FEEDBACK

Sir, I would appreciate your thoughts on:

   1. Are these workflows aligned with Rayna's business model?
   2. Which services should we prioritize for the MVP?
   3. Do you see any gaps or additional features we should add?
   4. Should we include group travel or split billing features?

Looking forward to your guidance.

*Best regards,*
Asad

emails.subject: RE: Two Action Items — Competition API Access + 116 City Content Deadline | Deepak + Alok
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

As committed, here is the full status and plan for the content block,
worked backwards from the 31 August go-live.

One update on numbers first. The destination sheet has grown since the
brief. It now lists around *140 cities, of which 35 are marked high
priority*. We are building high priority first, then medium, then the rest.
All dates below follow that order.

*1. London — the proof city*

London activity content is complete. It has been built from multiple
sources, fully enriched by AI so the content is original and carries no
copyright risk, images mapped, and reviews regenerated. Cairo is also done
as a second test city. Together that is over 500 products built and in our
system.

Going live needs two steps, and only the first is finished. Step one,
getting the data, is done for London. Step two is review and push into the
new Admin Portal, then the supplier module, then the website. Anwar's team
is giving me the Admin Portal connection this week. Once it is in, the IT
team confirms the data is mapping correctly across a couple of products,
and from there we start pushing.

*London live target: third week of June*, assuming the portal connection
lands this week and the data check passes. This first publish is the
milestone that matters. Once London is proven through this flow, every
other city follows the same path.

*2. Product content — all cities*

The pipeline works. The honest variable is scraping speed at scale, and I
would rather give you a real date than a number I cannot hold.

Scraping is the slowest part of the chain. For each city the system finds
sources, I approve them, and only then does it pull data. Some sources
block automated access and have to be replaced. Many products carry several
options, each with its own price and inclusions, and the system opens each
one to capture the real detail. That depth is what makes our content better
than a marketplace, but it takes real time per city.

To speed this up we have added parallel scraping, with more than one
machine running at once into the same database.

Alongside this, we are bringing in direct APIs (detail in the next
section). Where an API covers a city, it cuts the scraping load sharply. So
the realistic timeline depends on two things settling this week: real
per-city scraping speed, and how much the APIs replace.

   - *35 high-priority cities:* content ready by end June.
   - *All cities:* once I have the scraping speed data and the API picture
   is clear, I will give you firm completion dates. I expect to have that
   clarity by 16 June.

*3. APIs — accelerating the content*

Three sources are moving in parallel:

   - *Headout:* Deepak is securing direct API access through Rayna's
   existing relationship. Documents and key expected around 16 June. I am
   coordinating with Deepak to move it as fast as possible. Headout gives both
   catalogue and live pricing.
   - *GlobalTix:* API documentation received and the team has requested the
   staging and production keys, expected shortly. Once active I will confirm
   which cities it covers and pull content from it instead of scraping where
   it helps.
   - *Other platforms (Klook and similar):* checking existing access
   through Deepak and Technoheaven before building anything new.

By around 16 June I will have a clear read on exactly what each API
provides and how much scraping it removes. That is what lets me commit firm
all-city dates.

*4. Blog and destination guides*

Two parts.

*The platform:* my developer, hired specifically for this two-month build,
is building the blog system. He is on the backend now, with the admin and
public site to follow. Platform expected ready in July, and I am pushing to
bring that forward.

*The content:* each guide needs a defined structure, image count, FAQs,
internal links, and the SEO and AEO signals that get it ranked and cited.
Sandstorm is finalising the content template and keyword mapping next week,
which sets the shape our articles follow. We will align to that. Review
capacity is being arranged through Aparna's team and a content editor.

Honest position: the platform timeline is clear. The article rollout will
be phased, high-priority cities first, and I will firm up that schedule as
the platform nears completion and Sandstorm's template is in hand.

*5. Existing live products — enrichment*

In parallel, I am taking up the enrichment of our existing live products so
their content is restructured to the same SEO, AEO and GEO standard. This
is the same pipeline, applied to content we already own rather than
building from scratch. It also strengthens every downstream system that
reads this content, including the reservation bot. I will fold this into
the same plan and timeline.

*6. Market Intelligence*

Separately, on the price comparison and Market Intelligence project:
Senthil has asked to see a working prototype, which Avinash is presenting
Friday morning. We will build from there. The competitor APIs above feed
directly into this.

*What could slow us down*

   - *Scraping speed* — the biggest variable. Parallel scraping added;
   measuring real per-city time this week.
   - *Admin Portal readiness* — our publish step depends on it. Connection
   due this week; we test immediately.
   - *API access timing* — Headout and others expected around mid-June;
   treated as an accelerator with Deepak and Technoheaven, not a blocker.
   - *Blog content structure* — depends on Sandstorm's template, expected
   next week.

*In short*

   - London content done; live by third week of June through the Admin
   Portal.
   - 35 high-priority cities content-ready by end June.
   - Firm all-city dates once scraping speed and API coverage are clear,
   which I expect by 16 June.
   - Blog platform expected in July; content phased, structured around
   Sandstorm.
   - Enrichment of existing live products running in parallel on the same
   pipeline.

I am treating 31 August as the anchor and working everything backwards from
it. I will come back with another update around 16 June covering the firm
all-city dates and the article rollout schedule.

Thanks,
Alok Agrawal

On Mon, Jun 08, 2026 10:21 PM, Alok Agrawal <alok@raynatours.com> wrote:

>
> 3. City count: I am working to the latest destination sheet, which is 140

>

>

> product content timeline for all cities, blog and destination guide

>

> Alok Agrawal
>

>>
>> Our September campaign launches September 1st. All 116 cities must be

emails.subject: RE: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

 

Further to the below, I kindly request to give me time until Friday to review and revert on any such pending employee complaints or settlement cases that remain unaddressed.

 

I am currently awaiting certain details from our India office, which are necessary to compile a complete and accurate status report for your review.

 

I will ensure a comprehensive update is shared with you by end of day Friday.

 

Thank you for your understanding.

 

Thanks & Regards,

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Friday, June 5, 2026 12:02 PM
To: Pari <pari@raynatours.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com
Subject: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered

 

Hi Pari and Vaibhav,

I want to address something that I feel very strongly about.

I have seen the email thread from Inder Thakral regarding his Full & Final settlement. This person has been following up for more than 8 months with no proper response from our side. He has now CC'd the Maharashtra Labour Commissioner, Ministry of External Affairs, and MOHRE in his emails. This situation has reached this point only because our team did not respond.

I want to make this absolutely clear — no employee email should ever go unanswered.

If we are holding someone's settlement because they did something wrong, that is our right. But we must communicate that in writing clearly and immediately. We must state exactly what the employee did wrong, what the consequences are, and what our position is. We have the proofs. We have the records. Use them. Put it in writing. Send it.

Silence is not a strategy. Silence makes us look guilty even when we are right. And when employees start CC'ing government authorities, it creates legal and reputational risk for Rayna that could have been completely avoided with one proper email response months ago.

Effective immediately — I expect the following:

1.	Every employee complaint or query must receive a written response within 48 hours — no exceptions
2.	If a settlement is being withheld for any reason, the employee must be informed in writing with the exact reason and supporting evidence
3.	No employee email — whether from current or ex-employees — should remain unanswered for more than 48 hours
4.	For this specific case — Inder Thakral — I want a full written response sent to him today explaining Rayna's position clearly. CC me and Senthil on that email.

Pari — please also review if there are any other pending employee complaints or settlement cases that have not been responded to. Share a status report with me by June 10th.

This is not negotiable. We are a company of 1,600 people. Our reputation with employees matters as much as our reputation with customers.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: RE: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy — Full Alignment + Direction
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

 

Thank you for this — the direction is crystal clear and I'm fully aligned. And Senthil, thanks for stepping in to clarify the model; you read the intent exactly right.

 

To confirm: yes, the model is creators posting to their own followers, not just recording videos for us. This is where the real value is. The more creators who post to their own audiences, the wider our distribution — each one putting the Rayna affiliate opportunity in front of their followers, most of whom are hearing about it for the first time. That is genuine distribution, not duplication, and it is exactly the brand buzz you described — affiliates, customers, even corporate accounts emerging from audiences we could never reach with paid media alone.

 

On the commission side — agreed, barter only to start. I'll keep all influencer deals on barter (experiences and trips from our owned assets) and I will take your approval before finalising the voucher value with any influencer, so the cost stays controlled while we learn what works.

 

On the tech — I'm taking full ownership of confirming the platform works end to end (registration, tracking, commission calculation and payouts) before anything goes out to influencers, exactly as you've directed. Nothing moves to outreach until I'm certain it's working flawlessly. The first step is a demo and status walkthrough with Vaibhav, which I've already lined up.

 

This also affects the timeline. The script was originally due on 13th June, but since the script has to be built around how the platform actually works — so I can explain it to the audience in the simplest way — I'd like to adjust that date until after I've seen the demo and understood the flow end to end. The sequence from there stays exactly as you laid out: I prepare the script, send it to you for approval, then produce the master video and post it to Rayna's own channels first, before the influencers replicate it to their own audiences. I'll come back to you with revised dates right after my session with Vaibhav.

 

I'll keep you posted as I clear each step. Excited to get this moving.

 

Best regards,

Nihad Ali

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Wednesday, June 10, 2026 11:28 AM
To: senthil@raynatours.com; nihad@raynatours.com
Cc: Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: Re: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy — Full Alignment + Direction

 

Hi Senthil and Nihad,

I want to clarify my earlier email because I think there was a misunderstanding — on my side as much as anyone's.

When I pushed back on the multi-creator idea, I was reading it as creators making videos purely for content testing — recording videos for us to use internally, without posting to their own audiences. In that reading, the distribution problem was unsolved and the cost was unjustified. That was my concern.

But if the model is that each creator posts to their own followers — that is a completely different proposition, and I am fully in.

Think about what we are actually building here. We are not just recruiting travel agents. We are creating a buzz at scale — paying real people (10% consumer discount + 5% affiliate commission) instead of paying Google and Meta. Every influencer who posts about the Rayna affiliate program is extending our brand to an audience that has never heard of us. Some of their followers become affiliates. Some become customers. Some are senior managers who become corporate accounts. You cannot predict where the buzz lands — but the more people who know about Rayna and know we have this program, the stronger our brand becomes. This compounds over time.

I am fully aligned. Here is the direction going forward:

ONE — Barter only to start.
All influencer deals are barter to begin with — experiences and trips from our owned assets. No cash commitments until we see the response and understand what works. Keep the cost controlled while we learn.

TWO — Tech must be fully tested first.
Nihad, the tech ownership is yours. Before any influencer posts a single video, you must personally verify that the affiliate program works completely end to end — registration, tracking, commission calculation, payouts, everything. You are responsible for confirming this is ready. Do not move to influencer outreach until you are certain the system works flawlessly. If someone sees a post, gets excited, and arrives at a broken experience, we waste all that reach and damage the brand.

THREE — Nihad goes first.
Once the tech is confirmed ready, Nihad creates the first explanatory video — this is the master video that shows influencers exactly how the program works, how to explain it, and what to say. Nihad posts this to Rayna's own followers first. This is both a test of the message and a training tool for the influencers who follow.

FOUR — Influencers replicate to their own audiences.
Once the master video is live and working, the influencers take the same message and post it in their own style to their own followers. That is where the reach multiplies. Each influencer speaks to their own network — their followers may not be travel customers today, but they are potential affiliates and potential customers tomorrow.

This is the right model. Move forward on this basis.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: RE: Two New Partnership Tracks — Radio + Trending Apps
emails.body:
Hi Sir, 

 

The meeting with ARN is confirmed for 16th June at 15:00 Hr.

 

Location: Rayna Office, Acico Business Park

Attendees: Sidharth Vohra (Head of City), Mini Mathur (Head of Marketing), Maryam Khan (Head of Commercial & Partnerships).

 

You may involve any designate from your side whom you would like to attend this meeting.

 

All the best.

 

Thanks,

Azhar

 

From: azhar@raynatours.com <azhar@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Wednesday, June 10, 2026 3:09 PM
To: 'Manoj Tulsani' <manoj@raynatours.com>
Cc: 'Vaibhav Gupta' <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: RE: Two New Partnership Tracks — Radio + Trending Apps

 

Hi Sir,

 

Spoke to the ARN team earlier today. They are currently discussing internally and will come down to our office for a meeting.

 

The meetings will include Vivek, Head of Business, Mini Mathur, Head of Marketing and Maryam Khan (050 755 9075), who looks after the commercial & partnerships side.

 

Once I receive the confirmed date and time, I will keep both you posted here.

 

Regards,

Azhar

 

From: azhar@raynatours.com <mailto:azhar@raynatours.com>  <azhar@raynatours.com <mailto:azhar@raynatours.com> > 
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 5:02 PM
To: 'Manoj Tulsani' <manoj@raynatours.com <mailto:manoj@raynatours.com> >
Cc: 'Vaibhav Gupta' <vaibhav@raynatours.com <mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com> >
Subject: RE: Two New Partnership Tracks — Radio + Trending Apps

 

Hi Sir,

 

I had a word with my contact at ARN, Maryam Khan, who looks after sales-side partnerships. For barter-related partnerships, this falls under their marketing team, and her colleague Mini Mathur handles this type of collaboration.

 

Maryam will speak to Mini and share her contact details with me. I will first take this forward from my side and, once there is clarity, I will officially introduce the project, campaign, and barter plan to Vaibhav for further alignment.

 

Under the ARN Group, they have stations such as Virgin Radio, City 1016, and Hit FM, which together cover a major share of the audience, approximately 75% of the total audience base. In parallel, I have also started discussions with 89.1 FM, Kadak FM, and Mirchi FM to connect with their respective marketing teams.

 

I will keep both of you posted once I receive the relevant contact details and further updates.

 

All the best, Vaibhav. Let me know if you would like me to arrange any specific data, market analysis, previous campaign, or any other support from my side.

 

Thanks,
Azhar

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com <mailto:manoj@raynatours.com> > 
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 4:34 PM
To: azhar@raynatours.com <mailto:azhar@raynatours.com> 
Cc: Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com <mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com> >
Subject: Re: Two New Partnership Tracks — Radio + Trending Apps

 

Hi Azhar,

Thank you for going through the brief in such detail and for the analysis — I appreciate the thought you put into it, and especially that you have direct contacts within the ARN network. That is genuinely useful.

Let me share my perspective on the website concern you raised. I understand the point, but I see this differently. A radio barter campaign is primarily a branding exercise, not a traffic-driving one. Listeners will hear the Rayna name, become familiar with the brand, and remember us — but it will not suddenly drive thousands of people to the website rushing to book. That is simply not how radio works. The value here is awareness and brand recall over time, not an immediate conversion surge.

On top of that, signing barter contracts with radio stations will itself take time. By the time campaigns are live, our team is more than capable of managing the website experience as it stands. So I do not see website readiness as a blocker to starting these conversations.

Please share your ARN contacts and all the details with Vaibhav. As the owner of this track, Vaibhav will take the decisions on how and when to move forward. Work closely with him.

Thanks again for the initiative — keep bringing this kind of thinking.

Your experience, our responsibility.

Deepak Hiranandani
________________________________

Subject: RE: Two Action Items � Competition API Access + 116 City Content Deadline | Deepak + Alok

Good analysis on the Headout API � that's the right direction. Two things on how we move forward:

ONE � Headout API via Deepak's relationship
Do not submit a formal partner application. Headout is a long-standing customer of Rayna � we have sold them Dubai products for years and have a strong relationship with them. emails.subject: Re: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required
emails.body:
Dear Manoj Sir,



I hope you're doing well. I'm writing to share a structured road-map for the Google & Meta Ads Automation project following our internal analysis.



I recently conducted an in-depth review alongside Mr. Vaibhav and Mr. Anket, covering the current ads posting workflows for both Google and Meta platforms.

Together, we assessed the existing processes, identified gaps, and finalised the feature set required for the automation system.



Based on this, here is the phase-wise development plan:



──────────────────────────────

Phase 1 – API Connector Sync (2–3 days)

Establishing and syncing the code connector with Google and Meta API endpoints.



Phase 2 – Back-end Development (7–8 days)

Building the core back-end: data input structures, processing logic, and feature implementation.



Phase 3 – Front-end Development (3–4 days)

Developing the UI: CRUD interfaces, feature controls, and environment variable management.



Phase 4 – AI Integration (3–4 days)

Integrating connector code to enable automated AI inputs and AI-driven ad optimization.



Phase 5 – Deployment & Testing (4–5 days)

End-to-end testing, environment deployment, and pre-launch validation.



→ Go Live

──────────────────────────────



Total estimated timeline: 19–24 working days.



I'm happy to walk you through the details or adjust any phase scope based on your inputs. Kindly share your thoughts at your earliest convenience so we can proceed accordingly.



Warm regards,

Malik



From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Fri, 05 Jun 2026 20:18:35

To: anket@raynatours.com, malik@raynatours.com

Cc: Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, senthil@raynatours.com

Subject: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required

 

Hi Anket and Malik,

I want to take our performance marketing to the next level — automated, AI-driven, and connected directly to our product inventory. Here is the plan and what I need from both of you.


THE DIRECTION

As we scale to 100+ cities and thousands of products, manually creating and managing ad campaigns is not possible. We need a system where a new product goes live on our website and an ad campaign is automatically created and starts running on Google and Meta — with zero manual work per product.

This will happen in two phases:

Phase 1 — Immediate: Use a third party tool called Madgicx to automate our Google and Meta campaigns right now. This gets us running properly and generating real performance data while we build our own solution.

Phase 2 — In-house build: Malik's team builds Rayna's own ad automation module as an extension of the marketing platform already being built. When a product goes live on the website, it triggers an automatic ad campaign on Google and Meta through our own system. Full ownership, full control, zero dependency on third parties.


ANKET — YOUR TASKS:


	Study Madgicx thoroughly — madgicx.com. Understand all its capabilities for Google and Meta automation, product feed integration, audience targeting, and budget management.
	Evaluate if Madgicx meets our requirements — automated campaign creation from product feed, separate targeting for India, UAE, and Saudi, full transparency on spend, hard budget limits the AI cannot exceed.
	Get a demo and pricing from Madgicx — understand the cost vs our current manual management cost.
	Come back to me with your assessment and a recommended go-live date for Madgicx. I want this information by June 12th.


MALIK — YOUR TASKS:


	Study the Madgicx product feed and API structure — understand exactly how it connects product data to Google and Meta ad platforms. This is the blueprint for what we will build in-house.
	Study Google Ads API and Meta Marketing API — specifically dynamic product advertising. When a product goes live in our database, how does that trigger an automatic ad campaign? What data does each platform need — image, description, price, URL, targeting parameters?
	Plan the in-house ad automation module — as an extension of the marketing platform you are already building. The vision: product goes live on raynatours.com → system automatically creates ad campaign on Google and Meta → campaign starts running with preset budget and targeting rules → performance data feeds back into the system for optimization.
	Come back to me with a feasibility assessment and estimated build timeline by June 15th.



WHAT I NEED FROM BOTH OF YOU TOGETHER:

A joint input by June 15th covering:


	Madgicx — is it the right tool? If not, suggest an alternative. What is the go-live date?
	In-house build — is it feasible as an extension of the current marketing platform? What is the realistic timeline?
	How do Phase 1 and Phase 2 connect — can Madgicx data inform the in-house build?


Vaibhav — please oversee this and make sure Anket and Malik are aligned. The marketing platform you are building and the ad automation must work together seamlessly.

This is a priority. Our September India and UAE campaign launch depends on having automated, scalable performance marketing in place.

Thanks,

Manoj

	Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>, ranjan@raynab2b.com, rajkumar@raynatours.com, 
	adnan@raynab2b.com, Pari <pari@raynatours.com>, Prateek <prateek@raynatours.com>, 
	manoj.gagwani@raynatours.com, sunil.p@raynab2b.com, asif@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi All,

One important communication guideline I want everyone to follow immediately.

*Do not CC me on internal communications between teams and departments.*

If you are coordinating with a colleague, following up on a task, sharing
an update within your team, or resolving something internally — handle it
directly between yourselves. I do not need to be copied.

The only time you should email me or CC me is when you need a decision, an
approval, or input from me specifically. If you are not asking me something
or waiting for my response, my name should not be on the email.

This applies to everyone across all departments. Internal coordination,
task updates, status replies, and team-to-team communications stay within
the relevant people. Keep my inbox for what actually requires my attention.

Please follow this immediately.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*
