Subject: Re: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil

 





Hello sir, 



one- Reply from Javed – I have already forwarded it to you.

two - Please find the attachment for your review.



Thanks



From: senthil@raynatours.com

Sent: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 13:35:36

To: "aparna@raynatours.com" <aparna@raynatours.com>

Cc: "manoj@raynatours.com" <manoj@raynatours.com>, "manish.y@raynatours.com" <manish.y@raynatours.com>, "vaibhav@raynatours.com" <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Re: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil

 
Hi Aparna,



Thank you for getting the options from Javed quickly. Here is the direction on both items.



ONE — Forbes India



Manoj's preference is a standalone feature, not a listicle. Of the four options received, the Brandconnect Dedicated Online Article (₹2,00,000 + GST) best aligns with this — exclusive, editorial-style, permanent online hosting, and a website backlink.



However, before we commit any cash, please go back to Javed with the following counter-proposal:



- We are interested in the Brandconnect Online Article format

- As a first step, we would like to offer Javed and his team a hosted Dubai experience with Rayna — complimentary tours, transfers, and a curated itinerary — in lieu of a portion of the fee

- Ask him if the fee can be reduced or waived in exchange for this experience, and what the revised terms would be



Do not agree to any payment until Manoj has reviewed the counter-offer response. Revert to me with Javed's response as soon as you hear back.



TWO — Senior Travel Writer JD



I have reviewed Aparna's JD draft. Please make the following three updates before posting:



1. Confirm location as India (Mumbai or Delhi preferred) — this changes the candidate profile and salary band

2. Revise the salary range to ₹1.5 lakh to ₹2 lakh per month (the current range of ₹1L–₹2.5L is too wide)

3. Add the following to the responsibilities section: "Oversee the Student Travel Writing Competition — review student submissions, select winners, and publish winning articles on  https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781077038903000&sa=E across partner schools in UAE, India, and Saudi Arabia"



Once updated, please share the revised JD with me for a quick review before posting. We need to move fast on this.



Regards,

Senthil

 

On Tue, Jun 09, 2026 09:12 AM, Aparna A <aparna@raynatours.com> wrote:


DEar Manojj sir and senthil Sir, I have already requested this information from him as well. Below is the response I received:

 

Dear Aparna,

Thank you for your response.

While the participating company list cannot be disclosed prior to publication, we can offer the following exclusive coverage options for Rayna Tours:

1. Forbes India Brandconnect Dedicated Online Article – ₹2,00,000 + 18% GST


	Exclusive feature focused entirely on Rayna Tours
	700–800 words editorial-style article
	Company and leadership profiling
	Website backlink
	Permanent online hosting


2. Forbes India E-Magazine Feature – ₹2,50,000 + 18% GST


	One full-page advertorial (600–650 words)
	One high-resolution image
	PDF copy of the magazine


3. Forbes India magazine Print + E-Magazine Feature – ₹5,00,000 + 18% GST


	One full-page advertorial (600–650 words)
	One high-resolution image
	PDF copy of the magazine
	Two complimentary hard copies
	Available across major bookstores and distribution channels in India and overseas


4. CEO Thoughts Magazine Cover Story Package – ₹1,50,000 + 5% GST


	6-page cover story feature
	Cover page placement
	Leadership profile
	Recognition certificate
	Website article and social media promotion
	Complimentary hard copies


Please let us know which option best aligns with your branding and marketing objectives, and we will be happy to share the next steps.

 

Regards,

 Javed | Corporate Team

 Fortune Summit Media

 +91 8920949627 





From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 23:51:41

To: senthil@raynatours.com

Cc: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil

 

Hi Senthil,

Welcome back. Two India items — wanted to share my thinking on both.


ONE — Forbes India

I looked at the Forbes India proposal and their sample articles. It is a listicle format — we would be featured alongside multiple other companies in one article. Looking at the samples, most of the other companies are relatively small and unknown. I am not comfortable with that positioning for Rayna. Being placed in that context could actually work against us in India rather than for us.

My preference would be to explore a standalone feature — founder story, company journey, AI transformation, global expansion. Something that gives Rayna its own space. If Forbes India can offer that kind of premium format at the right price, I think it is worth doing properly. If not, let's walk away and find a better fit.

Aparna has been asked to offer a hosted Dubai experience as part of the negotiation before any cash is discussed. Please take this forward with Javed and let me know what options are available. I will make the final call once we know what is on the table.

Contact: Javed Khan — javed@ceothoughts.in — +91 8920949627


TWO — Senior Travel Writer

Aparna has put together a solid JD. Profile is right — someone who creates original content and reviews our AI-generated content. The media network requirement she added is a smart touch.

Three things worth addressing before we post:


	Confirm location — India or UAE. Changes the candidate pool and salary entirely.
	Tighten the salary range once location is confirmed. ₹1 lakh to ₹2.5 lakh is too wide to attract the right person confidently.
	Add the Student Travel Writing Competition to the JD responsibilities — this person will oversee student submissions, select winners, and publish articles on  raynatours.com across our partner schools in UAE, India, and Saudi.


Once updated, please post immediately. We need this person on board as soon as possible given the AI content engine going live.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Emergency Unpaid Leave Request

Hi Sir,

I am really sorry. I had only recently created the Wikidata account and asked the team to start updating it. I should have reviewed and verified the information.

The option for "Tour Operator" was not available in Wikidata, so the team selected "Travel Agency" as the closest available category. We have now updated the entity to "Business."

The "Emirati Travel Agency" and some other classifications were added by external Wikidata contributors, not by our team. I will review and update those as well today after 9 PM.



After we created and updated the Rayna Tours Wikidata entity, I noticed that several changes were subsequently made by other Wikidata contributors.





To avoid any confusion or incorrect updates in the future, I have prepared a document outlining the information and positioning we should use going forward. 



https://docs.google.com/document/d/1feQsNfMGhjNOO6YFR-Ky4_lnvs37dt8gWKv0GhKor_s/edit?tab=t.0

Could you please review the document and share your suggestions or corrections? Once I receive your confirmation, we will use it as the standard reference not only for updating Wikidata but also for maintaining consistency across all future entity-building activities.

We will also review and update our existing profiles and references to make sure they are aligned with the approved company positioning and brand information.

Looking forward to your feedback.



Thanks,

Aparna

Aparna

 

While I appreciate the work done to meet deadlines, it is essential the work is done thoroughly.  With the amount of tools available to us via AI, I would have expected you do a validity check and address the gaps (or send the plan for making things better).....  I see neither in your response.  A simple check with Claude on our entry reveals the below gaps....  

 

Good, I can see the page clearly. Here's the analysis:

Critical Error


	instance of is set to "website" — this is wrong. It should be "travel agency" (Q2736768) or "tour operator". This is the most damaging issue as it misclassifies the entire entity.

	English: "Emirati travel agency" is too vague. Better: "Dubai-based travel agency and destination management company"

	Default language row has no description at all


Missing Key Statements These properties are absent and should be added:


	
		
			
			Property
			
			
			Value
			
		
		
			
			country (P17)
			
			
			United Arab Emirates
			
		
		
			
			headquarters location (P159)
			
			
			Dubai
			
		
		
			
			official website (P856)
			
			
			raynatours.com
			
		
		
			
			founded (P571)
			
			
			founding year
			
		
		
			
			industry (P452)
			
			
			tourism / travel agency
			
		
		
			
			logo image (P154)
			
			
			upload to Wikimedia Commons first
			
		
		
			
			legal form (P1454)
			
			
			LLC (since "Rayna Tourism LLC" is an alias)
			
		
	


References


	The single reference on instance of needs to be fixed once the property is corrected
	All other statements added should include references (official website, press coverage, etc.)


Sitelinks


	No Wikipedia article is linked. If a Wikipedia article about Rayna Tours exists in any language, it should be linked here. If not, creating one would significantly strengthen the entry's credibility.


Priority order: Fix instance of first — that's a factual error that affects how Wikidata categorizes the company.



>>
>>> Hi Vaibhav,
>>>
>>> Picked this up — root cause is confirmed at the code level, and it is

>>> against live production tokens: simpleHash(guestUserId) % 3 distributes a

>>> users who hashed to B or D
>>>
>>> So GTM is reporting the truth — the server simply wasn't producing B/D
>>> for most traffic. Re-tagging GTM would not have changed anything.
>>>
>>> The Fix
>>>
>>> Single change in the eligibility gate (getBookingInputFlow.ts): qualify
>>> a product on transfer alone, drop the time_slot requirement. This widens
>>> the eligible population so B and D start receiving real traffic and the
>>> split converges toward 33/33/33.
>>>
>>> Owner & Deadline
>>>
>>> - Fix owner: Ankit Patel
>>> - Code change: Ready (1-line gate change, documented in the ClickUp task)
>>> - Deploy target: Production — today, 09 Jun 2026 (EOD)
>>> - Pipeline: branch off development → PR development → main
>>> (sync-main-into-development flow) → prod deploy
>>> - Verification: same day, immediately post-deploy
>>>
>>> Verification Plan (post-deploy, today)
>>>
>>> 1. Open a transfer product's booking flow → confirm the API returns B/D
>>> variants for different users (not only A).
>>> 2. Check booking_flow GTM events / experiment dashboard → Flow B and D
>>> begin receiving meaningful traffic instead of <5% combined.
>>> 3. Confirm the split trends toward ~33/33/33 across eligible products
>>> over the next few hours.
>>>
>>> One Follow-up (does not block today's fix)
>>>
>>> Bucketing is currently not sticky — the unauthenticated token expires
>>> every 5 min and mints a new guestUserId on refresh, and the assignment
>>> cookie is keyed on that rotating id. So the same user can be re-bucketed
>>> across sessions (can appear in A, B and D), which inflates user counts.
>>> Today's change fixes the A-skew; I'll raise stickiness as a separate ticket
>>> (persist a stable UUID in the cookie and bucket on that) so the experiment
>>> counts are clean going forward.
>>>
>>> Recommendation: please continue to hold product decisions on this
>>> experiment until we've confirmed the 33/33/33 split post-deploy today, plus
>>> a short fresh data-collection window for B/D.
>>>
>>> I'll update the ClickUp task and reply here once it's live and verified
>>> on prod.
>>>
>>> Regards,
>>> Gaurav
>>>

>>> wrote:
>>>

>>>>
>>>> Regards,
>>>> Vaibhav
>>>>
>>>
>>

emails.subject: Re: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy
emails.body:
Hi Manoj

Your argument holds if these creators are only recording videos for us to use � in that case, creating content and reaching audiences are two different things and the distribution problem remains unsolved.

But the more natural reading of "creator-led videos" is that each creator posts to their own followers. That is distribution. A hundred creators each publishing to their own audiences is reach multiplied across a hundred different networks � which is actually the strongest version of what you yourself described as the right approach: influencers extending reach to new audiences. The proposal may be doing exactly what you asked for, just with more creators and lower individual reach per creator. Before rejecting it, we should ask Nihad to clarify the model.


Regards
Senthil


________________________________
From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com>
Sent: Tuesday, 9 June 2026 9:28 PM
To: nihad@raynatours.com <nihad@raynatours.com>
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com <senthil@raynatours.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: Re: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy


Hi Nihad,

I have read your follow-up on creating multiple creator-led videos, and I want to push back on it so we think this through properly.

Your idea is creative, but I am not able to see how it solves the actual problem we are trying to solve, which is distribution and reach. Let me explain my thinking and you tell me where I am wrong.

If we engage a hundred creators to each record the same explanatory message in their own face and style, what we end up with is a hundred versions of one explainer video. But for our affiliate program, we only need one strong explanatory video on our website and in our funnel. We cannot put a hundred versions of the same explainer in front of a customer. It has to be one clean piece. So the hundred variations do not help us there.

Now to produce those hundred videos, each creator will charge us, whether in cash or barter. So we would be paying a hundred times to duplicate one piece of content. And at the end of it, my real question is this � how has our reach to actual audiences increased?

Creating a video and distributing a video to an audience are two completely different things. If these creators are only recording videos for us to test, then nobody new has actually seen our message. We will have spent a hundred payments to get a hundred faces repeating the same script, and our message will still not have reached their followers. The distribution problem remains exactly where it was.

So help me understand � in your proposal, where exactly does distribution get solved? Because from what I can see, this generates content variations but does not generate reach.

Here is how I want you to think about it instead:

  *   If the goal is reach � then what matters is creators actually posting to their audiences, not just recording videos. Distribution is the point. A smaller number of genuine influencers who publish to their followers gives us real reach. Duplication does not.
  *   If the goal is creative testing � we do not need to pay a hundred creators for that. We have our own AI tools that can generate multiple video variations at almost no cost. We test cheaply in-house, identify the winner, and then put that one proven piece in front of real influencers for distribution.

For now, your focus stays on Step 1 � getting the affiliate program working a hundred percent and producing the one strong explanatory video. Let us not spread into paying many creators to duplicate content when we are not even running paid campaigns yet and the distribution question is unanswered.

Think about the distribution point and come back to me. I am open to being convinced if you can show me how reach actually gets solved.

Your experience, our responsibility.

Subject: Re: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil

Hi Aparna,

Thank you for getting the options from Javed quickly. Here is the direction on both items.

ONE — Forbes India

Manoj's preference is a standalone feature, not a listicle. Of the four options received, the Brandconnect Dedicated Online Article (₹2,00,000 + GST) best aligns with this — exclusive, editorial-style, permanent online hosting, and a website backlink.

However, before we commit any cash, please go back to Javed with the following counter-proposal:

- We are interested in the Brandconnect Online Article format
- As a first step, we would like to offer Javed and his team a hosted Dubai experience with Rayna — complimentary tours, transfers, and a curated itinerary — in lieu of a portion of the fee

Senthil

emails.subject: Re: RE: Please check if this is ok to go or u suggest some changes
emails.body:
Hi Manoj, Senthil,

QA agency update — here is the full picture, the numbers, and my
recommendation.

We received revised proposals from two shortlisted agencies today —
TestingXperts and BugRaptors. Both have now been fully briefed on our
product, stack, and requirements. Here is the side-by-side:

─────────────────────────────────────────
COMPETITIVE SUMMARY — REVISED PROPOSALS
─────────────────────────────────────────

TestingXperts (Dubai office — met us in person)
• Phase 1 cost: $14,000 (2 weeks)
• Team: 8 resources — 5 functional testers, 2 performance engineers, 1
security tester
• Device coverage: 6 physical devices + 18 via BrowserStack = 24 total
• Security testing: Included — OWASP mobile assessment, API security, PCI
DSS, UAE PDPL, GDPR
• 4-hour P1 escalation SLA: Committed in writing with a formal
minute-by-minute escalation process
• Performance: 5,000 concurrent users
• Phase 2 monthly retainer: Not yet quoted

BugRaptors (India — CMMI Level 5)
• Phase 1 cost: $6,572 (2 weeks)
• Team: 5 resources — 3 functional testers, 1 performance engineer, 1
security tester
• Device coverage: 23+ devices claimed
• Security testing: Included — OWASP, SQLi, XSS, PCI DSS, UAE PDPL
• 4-hour P1 escalation SLA: Not committed in writing (only "daily DSR"
mentioned)
• Performance: 1,000 concurrent users Phase 1 / 10,000 users Phase 2
• Phase 2 monthly retainer: Fully quoted — Functional $2,500/mo · Security
$3,300/mo · Automation $3,000/mo

─────────────────────────────────────────
BUDGET POSITION & NEGOTIATION
─────────────────────────────────────────

Phase 1 (Launch readiness — 2 weeks):
• Fair market rate for our scope: $8,000–$10,000
• We should not pay more than $11,500 for TestingXperts or more than $6,000
for BugRaptors
• Negotiation lever on TestingXperts: reduce functional team from 5 to 4
people → brings cost to ~$11,500
• Negotiation lever on BugRaptors: commit the 4-hour P1 SLA in writing as a
condition of signing

Phase 2 (Ongoing monthly QA partnership):
• Comfortable monthly budget: $5,000–$7,000/month
• Recommended starting team: 1 Functional QA + 1 Security QA =
~$5,800/month with BugRaptors
• Expand to automation after launch is stable — adds ~$3,000/month

─────────────────────────────────────────
MY RECOMMENDATION
─────────────────────────────────────────

I recommend TestingXperts at a negotiated $11,500 for the following reasons:

1. They are the only agency that committed the 4-hour P1 SLA in writing —
essential for a live payment platform.
2. They have a Dubai team and already met us in person — zero onboarding
friction, can start immediately.
3. They responded to every single revision request we made — a clear signal
of how they will behave during the engagement.
4. The $5,000 premium over BugRaptors is justified by the SLA commitment, 8
vs 5 resources, and Dubai presence.

If budget is the hard constraint and we need to stay under $8,000 for Phase
1, then BugRaptors at $6,572 is the right call — provided we get the 4-hour
SLA in writing before signing.

─────────────────────────────────────────
NEXT STEP — DECISION NEEDED TODAY
─────────────────────────────────────────

To meet the June 10 agency confirmation deadline, I need one of the
following from you today:

1. Green-light to negotiate TestingXperts down to $11,500 and sign — I will
close today.
2. Green-light to sign BugRaptors at $6,572 with SLA condition — I will
close today.
3. A monthly budget ceiling so I can negotiate accordingly.

Whichever agency we select, testing begins next week and we will have a
full testing timeline by Wednesday June 11 as requested.

Best regards,
Gaurav Verma
Rayna Tours
+971566995257

emails.subject: App Go-Live — Come and Meet Me | Gaurav + Anwar
emails.body:
Hi Gaurav and Anwar,

I want to meet both of you in person. Please come and see me.

I need to understand what is actually happening with the app because what I
am seeing does not add up.

The app is currently running. Payments are working. So the core
infrastructure is functional. And yet for the last month — every single
time I have asked — the answer has been "two days," "three days," "going
live this week." That has been the answer for four weeks straight. The app
is still not live.

Now I am being told it will take two and a half months.

I want you both to understand how this looks from my side. For a month, two
days became three days became two days again. Now suddenly it is two and a
half months. These two things cannot both be true. Either the app was never
two days away — in which case I was not being given accurate information —
or something has changed significantly that nobody has explained to me.

I need a straight, honest answer when we meet. Not an optimistic answer.
Not a comfortable answer. What is actually blocking the app from going
live? What exactly needs to be done? And what is a realistic date that you
are both willing to commit to and be held accountable for?

Come prepared with specifics.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

Hi Aparna and Manish,

I want to address something that has been flagged more than once and I need
it to stop.

The old company profile is no longer to be used. Ever. For anything.

Our new *Our Story* page on the website is our company profile. That is the
official, current representation of who Rayna is — our journey, our scale,
our owned assets, our values, and our brand positioning. Every piece of
outreach, every media pitch, every partnership proposal, every press kit
must be built from and aligned with what is written on that page.

Before you send anything to any media contact, journalist, publication, or
partner — read the Our Story page first. Make sure every word you use about
Rayna in your outreach reflects that page and nothing else. The old profile
has outdated numbers, outdated language, and does not represent who we are
today.

If I see the old company profile being used again in any outreach, it will
not be accepted and will need to be redone. This is not negotiable.

Please confirm you have both read the Our Story page in full and will use
it as the only reference going forward.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: RE: Two Action Items — Competition API Access + 116 City Content Deadline | Deepak + Alok
emails.body:
Hi Alok,

Good analysis on the Headout API — that's the right direction. Two things
on how we move forward:

*ONE — Headout API via Deepak's relationship*
Do not submit a formal partner application. Headout is a long-standing
customer of Rayna — we have sold them Dubai products for years and have a
strong relationship with them. Get the contact details from Deepak and
reach out to Headout directly using Deepak's reference. This will get you
the API access far faster than any formal application process.

Deepak — please share your Headout contact details with Alok immediately so
he can coordinate directly.

*TWO — Explore Bokun as an alternative route*
There is a platform called Bokun — a distribution and connectivity platform
that aggregates feeds from multiple operators and platforms including
Viator, GetYourGuide, and others. It may give us access to the pricing and
catalog data we need across multiple competitors from a single integration,
without needing individual API relationships with each one.

Please register on Bokun, explore what data feeds are available, and come
back to me with a clear answer: can Bokun give us the pricing and catalog
data we need from Headout, Viator, GetYourGuide, and Klook — or not? Check
this before we spend time building individual API integrations with each
platform.

Bokun website: bokun.io
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://bokun.io&source=gmail&ust=1781124296644000&sa=E>

Also — your content status report for all 116 cities is due tomorrow June
10th. Please make sure that is submitted on time.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Re: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required
emails.body:
Hi Anket,

This is excellent work. You did exactly what I want from this team —
instead of simply accepting Madgicx because I named it, you tested it
against our actual requirements, found the fatal flaw, and did the homework
to find alternatives that genuinely fit. Very well done.

Your finding on Madgicx is clear and I agree — if it only automates Meta
and cannot automate Google Ads, it does not meet our brief. We need both.
Madgicx is rejected. Good catch.

Now, before we commit to DeltaX or Smartly.io, I want us to be disciplined
about the sequence:

*1. Test the in-house and Claude route first.*
You mentioned you will be testing Meta and Google Ads automation directly
via Claude AI in the next 1-2 days. This is the most important step. If our
own system — built as an extension of our marketing platform, powered by
Claude — can handle automated campaign creation, geo-targeting, and budget
control, then we do not need to pay for a third-party platform at all. We
own it, we control it, and it connects directly to everything else we are
building. That is always our preferred path. Prioritize this test.

*2. Keep the third-party options ready as backup.*
DeltaX and Smartly.io are our fallback if the in-house route cannot deliver
in time for our September launch. Continue with your detailed comparison of
both — pricing, capabilities, and go-live dates — so we have a real
decision in hand.

*3. Bring everything together by end of week.*
I want one combined picture from you and Malik:

   - Results of the Claude AI automation test — what worked, what did not
   - DeltaX vs Smartly.io comparison with pricing and go-live dates
   - Malik's feasibility assessment and timeline for the in-house build

With all three in front of me, I can make the real decision — build
in-house, or buy a third-party platform as a bridge. The question I want
answered is simple: can we do this ourselves in time for September, or do
we need to rent a solution while we build our own?

Work closely with Malik on this. Strong analysis, Anket — keep it up.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Re: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy
emails.body:
Hi Nihad,

I have read your follow-up on creating multiple creator-led videos, and I
want to push back on it so we think this through properly.

Your idea is creative, but I am not able to see how it solves the actual
problem we are trying to solve, which is *distribution and reach.* Let me
explain my thinking and you tell me where I am wrong.

If we engage a hundred creators to each record the same explanatory message
in their own face and style, what we end up with is a hundred versions of
one explainer video. But for our affiliate program, we only need *one*
strong explanatory video on our website and in our funnel. We cannot put a
hundred versions of the same explainer in front of a customer. It has to be
one clean piece. So the hundred variations do not help us there.

Now to produce those hundred videos, each creator will charge us, whether
in cash or barter. So we would be paying a hundred times to duplicate one
piece of content. And at the end of it, my real question is this — *how has
our reach to actual audiences increased?*

Creating a video and distributing a video to an audience are two completely
different things. If these creators are only recording videos for us to
test, then nobody new has actually seen our message. We will have spent a
hundred payments to get a hundred faces repeating the same script, and our
message will still not have reached their followers. The distribution
problem remains exactly where it was.

So help me understand — in your proposal, where exactly does distribution
get solved? Because from what I can see, this generates content variations
but does not generate reach.

Here is how I want you to think about it instead:

   - *If the goal is reach* — then what matters is creators actually
   posting to their audiences, not just recording videos. Distribution is the
   point. A smaller number of genuine influencers who publish to their
   followers gives us real reach. Duplication does not.
   - *If the goal is creative testing* — we do not need to pay a hundred
   creators for that. We have our own AI tools that can generate multiple
   video variations at almost no cost. We test cheaply in-house, identify the
   winner, and then put that one proven piece in front of real influencers for
   distribution.

For now, your focus stays on Step 1 — getting the affiliate program working
a hundred percent and producing the one strong explanatory video. Let us
not spread into paying many creators to duplicate content when we are not
even running paid campaigns yet and the distribution question is unanswered.

Think about the distribution point and come back to me. I am open to being
convinced if you can show me how reach actually gets solved.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: RE: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi
emails.body:
Hello Sir,

 

We would like to update you that the JDs have been shared with Pari for the recruitment of Sales Manager positions in both Saudi Arabia and India.

 

One point I would like to bring to your attention is that the academic calendars in India and Saudi Arabia differ significantly.

 

In India, schools reopen in July after the summer break, making it an ideal time for the Sales Manager to begin school visits and business development activities. However, in Saudi Arabia, schools are entering their summer vacation period and are expected to reopen only towards the end of August.

 

Therefore, even if we successfully hire a resource for Saudi Arabia by the end of June, school visits and meetings would not be possible until the schools resume operations after the summer break.

 

Considering this, I would recommend onboarding the Saudi Arabia Sales Manager during the first week of August. This will allow sufficient time for training and induction, ensuring that the resource is fully prepared to start meeting schools and generating business as soon as the academic session resumes.

 

 

Regards,
Rishi Sethi

 

 

From: Rishi <rishi@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Monday, June 8, 2026 7:14 PM
To: 'Manoj Tulsani' <manoj@raynatours.com>
Cc: 'senthil@raynatours.com' <senthil@raynatours.com>; 'Vaibhav Gupta' <vaibhav@raynatours.com>; 'Deepak Hiranandani' <deepak@raynatours.com>; 'ranjan@raynab2b.com' <ranjan@raynab2b.com>; 'nihad@raynatours.com' <nihad@raynatours.com>; 'malik@raynatours.com' <malik@raynatours.com>; 'azhar@raynatours.com' <azhar@raynatours.com>
Subject: RE: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi

 

Dear Mr. Tulsani,

 

Thank you for entrusting me with the Edutainment vertical. I firmly believe this segment presents a significant growth opportunity, as student travel enables us to engage not only with students but also with parents and households, creating long-term brand awareness and loyalty.

 

Below are my observations and action points:

 

Expansion Strategy – Saudi Arabia & India

 

*	The Job Descriptions for the Saudi Arabia and India Sales Manager positions will be shared with Ms. Pari by tomorrow to facilitate recruitment at the earliest.
*	Saudi Arabia presents a strong opportunity due to the limited number of operators exclusively focused on student travel. Apart from Elite Enrichment Services, competition remains relatively low, although competitors such as ITL World have also identified the market potential.
*	In India, our initial focus will be Maharashtra and North India. While there are established players in the market, Rayna's global network, destination expertise, and operational presence across multiple countries provide us with a clear competitive advantage.
*	In addition to international educational programmes, we see substantial potential in domestic experiential travel for India, including hill station excursions, trekking programmes, wildlife safaris, leadership camps, and educational tours.
*	Beyond Saudi Arabia and India, Oman and Qatar should also be considered as strategic markets for future expansion due to their growing education sectors and increasing demand for student-focused experiences.

 

School Engagement & App Download Strategy

 

*	The App Download and School Engagement Programme is an excellent initiative that aligns perfectly with our objective of increasing brand visibility and building direct relationships with families.
*	Schools continuously seek sponsorship support for annual events, sports days, graduation ceremonies, award functions, and parent engagement activities. This initiative provides an opportunity to expand our presence beyond existing partner schools and establish relationships with a much broader education network.
*	By integrating app downloads with school campaigns, competitions, awards, and sponsorship activities, we create a sustainable ecosystem that benefits all stakeholders:

*	Schools receive enhanced engagement and sponsorship support.
*	Students gain access to rewarding experiences and recognition programmes.
*	Parents become directly connected to the Rayna ecosystem.
*	Rayna gains long-term brand visibility and a scalable customer acquisition channel.

 

*	The proposed affiliate model will allow us to effectively track participation, measure engagement, and strengthen relationships with schools while simultaneously expanding our parent database.

 

Vision & Commitment

 

*	Our objective is to establish Rayna as the preferred partner for educational travel and experiential learning across the UAE, Saudi Arabia, India, and future expansion markets.
*	Through meaningful school partnerships, we can create access to students, parents, and communities simultaneously, generating long-term opportunities across educational travel, family holidays, and experiential programmes.
*	We are fully aligned with the proposed strategy and committed to delivering the outlined milestones within the agreed timelines.

 

I appreciate your guidance and support and look forward to building this vertical into a significant contributor to Rayna's long-term growth.

 

Warm regards,

Rishi Sethi

 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com <mailto:manoj@raynatours.com> > 
Sent: Sunday, June 7, 2026 5:48 PM
To: azhar@raynatours.com <mailto:azhar@raynatours.com> ; rishi@raynatours.com <mailto:rishi@raynatours.com> 
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com <mailto:senthil@raynatours.com> ; Vaibhav Gupta vaibhav@raynatours.com <mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com> ; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com <mailto:deepak@raynatours.com> >; ranjan@raynab2b.com <mailto:ranjan@raynab2b.com> ; nihad@raynatours.com <mailto:nihad@raynatours.com> ; malik@raynatours.com <mailto:malik@raynatours.com> 
Subject: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi

 

Hi Azhar and Rishi,

This brief covers the Schools, Universities, and Edutainment segment. Azhar — you oversee this as part of your overall partnerships mandate. Rishi — you are heading the edutainment segment on the ground and this brief is primarily for you to execute. Both of you must read this fully and align.

We are expanding this segment from UAE to Saudi Arabia and India. Same playbook, dedicated people in each market, replicated under Rishi's leadership.

  _____  

WHAT EDUTAINMENT COVERS

*	Day trips — local school and university day excursions
*	Team building activities — desert safari, water sports, balloon, yacht, floating restaurant for school and university groups
*	Domestic overnight trips
*	Overseas educational trips — 116 destinations worldwide. Thailand, Singapore, Bali, London, Japan and more.

  _____  

RISHI — RECRUITMENT

1.	One Edutainment Manager for Saudi Arabia — based in Riyadh. Arabic speaking, strong school and university relationships.
2.	One Edutainment Manager for India — based in Pune. Strong school relationships in Maharashtra and North India.

Job descriptions to Pari by June 15th. Both hired by June 30th. Onboarded and trained by July 15th. First school visits in both markets by July 31st.

  _____  

HOW TO APPROACH SCHOOLS AND UNIVERSITIES

Decision maker is the Principal, Vice Principal for co-curricular activities, PTA chairperson, or student affairs department at universities.

At every visit:

1.	Show them who Rayna is — 15 years, 25 million guests, owned assets, 116 destinations worldwide
2.	Present the complete edutainment portfolio — day trips to full overseas educational tours
3.	Offer competitive group rates — work with Ranjan and destination heads for best group pricing
4.	Build annual relationships — schools renew every year. Become their permanent preferred operator.

As new destinations go live — immediately brief school contacts on new overseas trip options. Schools plan 6-12 months ahead. Get into their planning cycle early.

  _____  

APP DOWNLOAD — MANDATORY FOR ALL CAMPAIGNS

Every campaign, competition, award, and giveaway we run in schools must require the Rayna app download as the entry mechanism. No app download — no participation. This applies to students and parents equally.

This serves two purposes — we build our app user base from a completely new audience segment, and every participant enters our ecosystem where we can communicate with them permanently through push notifications and app-based campaigns.

Work with Malik to ensure the app registration flow connects directly to our giveaway and affiliate campaign platform. Students and parents who download the app must be able to see and participate in all live campaigns immediately.

  _____  

SCHOOL ENGAGEMENT PROGRAMMES — UNDER AFFILIATE PLATFORM

All school campaigns must be set up under our affiliate programme. Each school is registered as a Rayna affiliate. The school's dedicated affiliate link connects to our campaign platform where all active giveaways and competitions are visible. Teachers announce campaigns to students and parents. Students and parents download the app, register through the school's affiliate link, and participate. This way every participation is tracked back to the school, and schools earn recognition for driving engagement.

Programme 1 — Academic Achievement Award sponsored by Rayna
Rayna sponsors a monthly or termly "Best Student" or "Most Improved Student" award. Winning student and family receive a free Rayna experience — balloon, water sports, safari, or yacht. Announced publicly at assembly or parent teacher meeting.

To win — student must have the Rayna app downloaded and be registered. This drives app downloads among students and through them, their parents. Kids work harder knowing there is a prize. School gets better academic performance. We get permanent brand presence at almost zero cost — announced every term, every year.

Programme 2 — Parent Teacher Meeting Lucky Draw
Rayna sponsors a lucky draw at every PTM. Every attending parent who downloads the Rayna app and registers gets one entry. Winner announced at the end of the evening — free family balloon ride, yacht trip, or safari. Announced publicly in front of all parents.

Parents who skip PTMs now want to attend. School gets better parent engagement. Rayna gets in front of every parent in the school in one evening. Every attending parent is now in our app and database.

Programme 3 — End of Term Class Competition
Best class by attendance or grades wins a free Rayna group day trip. Every student in the class must have the Rayna app downloaded to qualify. Kids compete. Teachers motivated. Principal loves the results. 30-40 kids experience Rayna together and every parent hears about it.

Programme 4 — My Dream Holiday Competition
Drawing or essay competition — where does your family want to travel? Winners get a free family experience. Entry via the Rayna app only. Parent email and WhatsApp required. Every entry is a parent in our database.

  _____  

SCHOOL AWARD CEREMONIES — BRAND ACTIVATION

Every school holds an annual award ceremony — end of year, sports day, graduation. These are the moments when every student, parent, and teacher is in one room together. This is where Rayna must be present.

Approach every partner school to sponsor their annual award ceremony. What Rayna provides:

*	Branded screen sponsorship — a 15-20 second Rayna brand video plays on the main screen behind the stage during the ceremony. Every parent, student, and teacher sees it. This is premium brand visibility at a fraction of what a TV ad would cost.
*	Rayna popup stand at the venue — same configurations as our corporate roadshows. Staff present, app download QR codes, giveaway registration, family packages on display.
*	Sponsored giveaway prizes — Rayna announces free giveaway prizes at the ceremony. "Rayna Tours is giving one lucky family a free hot air balloon ride — scan this QR code to enter." Excitement fills the room. Every parent scans. Every parent downloads the app.
*	Co-branded event recognition — "This award ceremony is proudly sponsored by Rayna Tours — your experience, our responsibility." Announced by the principal. Printed on event materials. Posted on school social media.

The cost — a balloon ride, a safari, a water sports session. All owned by us. The return — brand presence in front of every parent, student, and teacher at every partner school, once a year minimum.

  _____  

REACHING PARENTS — DATABASE STRATEGY

Every school programme drives parent app downloads and database registrations:

*	Family Trip Card — every child takes home a Rayna card after every school trip. QR code for family discount. Parent downloads app to claim.
*	All competition entries — via app only. Parent WhatsApp and email required.
*	PTM lucky draw — app download required to enter.
*	Award ceremony QR codes — parent scans, downloads app, registers.
*	Achievement award announcements — sent to school WhatsApp groups with app download link.

Once a parent downloads the app and registers — they receive all our campaigns, holiday packages, visa offers, and giveaway campaigns automatically. One school relationship generates hundreds of parent contacts directly into our ecosystem.

  _____  

OVERSEAS EDUCATIONAL TRIPS

116 cities worldwide. Thailand, Singapore, Japan, London, Europe, Americas — all Rayna coordinated end to end. Flights, hotels, activities, transfers, visa assistance, local guide. No other edutainment operator in UAE can offer this range. As each destination goes live — build a school trip pipeline immediately.

  _____  

TARGET INSTITUTIONS

UAE: GEMS, Taaleem, Axiom, ADNOC schools, government schools, American University of Sharjah, University of Dubai, Khalifa University, Heriot-Watt Dubai, Middlesex University Dubai

Saudi: International schools Riyadh and Jeddah, SABIS, Saudi Aramco schools, King Abdulaziz University, King Saud University, KAUST

India: CBSE and ICSE schools in Pune, Mumbai, Delhi, Chandigarh. Engineering and MBA colleges Pune tech corridor. Delhi University colleges.

  _____  

TRACKING
Update the Google Sheet after every school visit, campaign, and ceremony activation.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI&source=gmail&ust=1780926315063000&sa=E> 

  _____  

DEADLINES

*	June 15th — Job descriptions for Saudi and India Edutainment Managers to Pari
*	June 30th — Both managers hired
*	July 15th — Both managers onboarded and trained
*	July 31st — First school visits in Saudi and India completed
*	August — All parent campaigns and school engagement programmes live in all three markets
*	September — Academic achievement awards, PTM lucky draws, and award ceremony sponsorships running in all partner schools

Rishi — schools give us access to students, parents, and the community simultaneously. Three revenue streams from one relationship — edutainment trips, parent holidays, community referrals. The engagement programmes above cost us almost nothing but create emotional connections no advertisement can replicate. Build this properly and it compounds every year.

Azhar — please ensure Rishi has everything he needs and that Saudi and India recruitment happens on schedule.

Your experience, our responsibility.

I had a word with my contact at ARN, Maryam Khan, who looks after sales-side partnerships. For barter-related partnerships, this falls under their marketing team, and her colleague Mini Mathur handles this type of collaboration.

 

Maryam will speak to Mini and share her contact details with me. I will first take this forward from my side and, once there is clarity, I will officially introduce the project, campaign, and barter plan to Vaibhav for further alignment.

All the best, Vaibhav. Let me know if you would like me to arrange any specific data, market analysis, previous campaign, or any other support from my side.

 

Thanks,
Azhar

Hi Azhar,

Thank you for going through the brief in such detail and for the analysis — I appreciate the thought you put into it, and especially that you have direct contacts within the ARN network. That is genuinely useful.

Let me share my perspective on the website concern you raised. I understand the point, but I see this differently. A radio barter campaign is primarily a branding exercise, not a traffic-driving one. Listeners will hear the Rayna name, become familiar with the brand, and remember us — but it will not suddenly drive thousands of people to the website rushing to book. That is simply not how radio works. The value here is awareness and brand recall over time, not an immediate conversion surge.

On top of that, signing barter contracts with radio stations will itself take time. By the time campaigns are live, our team is more than capable of managing the website experience as it stands. So I do not see website readiness as a blocker to starting these conversations.

Please share your ARN contacts and all the details with Vaibhav. As the owner of this track, Vaibhav will take the decisions on how and when to move forward. Work closely with him.

Thanks again for the initiative — keep bringing this kind of thinking.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

Thanks for the detailed direction — this is really helpful and I'm fully aligned on all three fronts.

On positioning, I'll make sure everything is anchored as "Join the Rayna Affiliate Program" with our trust signals front and centre, so the credibility is clear from the first impression.

On channels, understood — we'll keep it influencer-led on a barter basis for now, with no paid spend, and I'll bring any cash-required influencers to you and Senthil for approval before committing.

On rollout, the phased approach makes complete sense — prove it small before we scale. I'm starting on Step 1 immediately.

I'll keep you updated on progress as I work through the foundation.

Best Regards,
Nihad Ali



 

 

 

On Tue, Jun 09, 2026 04:16 PM, Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:

Hi Nihad,

Good thinking in this note. I agree with the core of your approach and I want to build on it with some important direction. Read this carefully.

  _____  

ON POSITIONING — AGREED, WITH ONE CRITICAL ANCHOR

I agree with marketing this as an AI-powered income opportunity. You are right that people are not searching for affiliate platforms, but millions are looking for ways to earn additional income.

However it must always be anchored as "Join the Rayna Affiliate Program." Rayna is the brand that makes this credible. This is the difference between us and a scam. A generic "AI income opportunity" looks like every get-rich-quick scheme online. But "Join the Rayna Affiliate Program — an AI-powered income opportunity" is completely different, because Rayna is an established, trusted name.

The affiliate landing page must carry every trust signal we have — our 25 million guests served, our Trustpilot reviews, our trust badges, our 15-year story, our owned assets. Anyone who searches Rayna on Google finds a wealth of genuine content. That depth of credibility is what removes any confusion between a scam and a real brand. Make sure the page is built to communicate this trust immediately and powerfully.

  _____  

ON CHANNELS — NO PAID ADVERTISING FOR NOW

We will not run paid Meta or performance marketing advertising for this at this stage. I want to be clear on that.

Our channel for this is influencer-led, on a barter basis:

*	Use Rayna's own Instagram and social accounts
*	Partner with known market influencers through barter deals — we provide experiences and trips from our own assets in exchange for them promoting "Join the Rayna Affiliate Program" to their audience
*	This solves the reach problem. Your own promotion only reaches Rayna's existing followers. Known influencers extend that reach to entirely new audiences.
*	If cash is required for a particular influencer, submit their profile to me and Senthil for review and approval before committing anything. No cash without approval.

  _____  

ON ROLLOUT — STRICT PHASED APPROACH

This is the most important part. We do not go to major influencers until the system is proven. Follow these steps in order:

Step 1 — Get the foundation 100% ready.
Your first responsibility is to personally verify that the affiliate program works completely — exactly as we discussed. Registration, UTM links, QR codes, discount codes, the content library, commission tracking, payouts. Everything must function end to end. Then create the explainer video — how to make money, how to register, how the system works, real earning potential. Nothing moves forward until this is done and verified.

Step 2 — Test small with micro influencers and our own followers.
Once the tech is ready, test with micro influencers and Rayna's own follower base — whatever you can do within your own capacity. The goal here is proof: confirm the technology works flawlessly, confirm people are coming on board, confirm those affiliates can actually operate the system and generate bookings. Even a few affiliates successfully using it gives us the confirmation we need that the tech has no problems.

Step 3 — Scale to major influencers (after ~3 months).
Only once Step 2 has proven the system works over the next three months do we go to major and mega influencers with the same campaigns. By then we will have confidence that the funnel converts, the tech holds, and affiliates succeed. That is when we scale reach aggressively.

The logic is simple — we do not put significant influencer reach or spend behind a system that is not yet proven. Prove it small, then scale it big.

  _____  

Nihad, your instinct on performance marketing and the income-opportunity angle is sharp. This phased approach protects the brand and ensures we only scale once we know everything works. Start with Step 1 immediately and keep me updated on the affiliate program readiness.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required
emails.body:
Hi Manoj Sir,

I've completed a thorough evaluation of Madgicx against the other three
platforms we are considering — Smartly.io, Scalable.Agency, and DeltaX —
specifically against our requirements: automated campaign creation from a
product feed, geo-specific targeting (India, UAE, Saudi), full spend
transparency, and hard budget limits the AI cannot exceed.

Here is my assessment:

---

TOOL EVALUATION SUMMARY

1. Madgicx
Madgicx is a strong Meta-focused AI platform built around autonomous budget
optimisation and audience intelligence. Its Autonomous Budget Optimizer
(ABO) lets you define a budget cap and performance target (ROAS/CPA), then
automatically reallocates daily spend across ad sets based on real-time
signals — which directly addresses our need for hard budget controls. The
AI Audience Studio builds and tests lookalike segments automatically,
reducing manual targeting work. It also has a Live Cross-Channel Reporting
Dashboard covering Facebook, Instagram, Google Ads, and Shopify.

However — and this is the critical gap for us — Madgicx is a Meta-only
automation platform as of 2026. It does NOT manage Google Ads campaigns,
bidding, or budget allocation. It provides Google reporting visibility, but
no Google Ads automation. Since our requirement includes both Meta and
Google Ads automation from a single product feed trigger, Madgicx alone
does not meet our full brief.

Key Features: Autonomous budget optimisation with hard spend caps | AI
Audience Studio for automated lookalike targeting | Creative Intelligence
suite identifying top-performing ad formats | Cross-channel reporting
dashboard (view only for Google)

---

2. Smartly.io
Smartly.io is an enterprise-grade platform that covers Meta, Google,
TikTok, Pinterest, Snapchat, and more from a single interface. It supports
true product feed-based dynamic ad creation — connect your catalogue and it
auto-generates personalised ads per SKU across channels. Its rule engine
allows geo-specific audience segmentation, meaning we can build India, UAE,
and Saudi campaigns with separate targeting rules, budgets, and creatives
from one workflow. Budget automation uses native platform bidding (Meta
Advantage+, Google Smart Bidding) with custom cross-channel allocation
rules layered on top.


Key Features: Full Meta + Google cross-channel automation from product feed
| Dynamic Creative Optimization (DCO) generating ad variants at scale |
Geo-specific multi-market campaign management | Automated budget allocation
rules across platforms and regions

---

3. Scalable.Agency
Scalable.Agency is a managed performance marketing service rather than a
self-serve SaaS platform. They run Meta and Google campaigns on your behalf
using their proprietary automation layer.  They offer geo-specific audience
segmentation, transparent spend reporting, and managed budget control.

Key Features: Fully managed Meta and Google campaign execution |
Geo-targeted audience segmentation for multiple markets | Automated
creative testing and iteration | Transparent spend reporting with budget
guardrails

---

4. DeltaX
DeltaX is an enterprise-grade cross-channel advertising platform
integrating with 100+ media providers including Google Ads and Facebook via
direct APIs. It offers dynamic creative optimisation, multi-channel
attribution, campaign automation, and rule-based budget management across
search, social, and display. It supports automated campaign creation tied
to product feeds, geo-specific targeting, and transparent spend reporting
with hard budget controls — covering all of our core requirements across
both Meta and Google.

Key Features: Cross-channel campaign management across Google, Meta, and
100+ platforms | Dynamic Creative Optimizer for personalized ads at scale |
Multi-channel attribution and reporting across all media | Rule-based
automation with budget controls and performance triggers

---

OVERALL RECOMMENDATION

Based on our evaluation against Rayna's core requirements—automated
campaign creation from a product feed, separate targeting for India, UAE,
and Saudi, full spend transparency, and hard budget limits across both Meta
and Google—I recommend moving forward with DeltaX or Smartly.io as the two
platforms that most comprehensively fulfil our brief.

DeltaX covers the full cross-channel requirement at a more accessible price
point, while Smartly.io brings best-in-class dynamic creative automation
and multi-market campaign management at scale. Together, they offer the
strongest foundation for Phase 1.

Madgicx, while strong on Meta, falls short due to the absence of Google Ads
automation.

Additionally — we will be testing Meta and Google Ads automation directly
via Claude AI in the next 1–2 days. This will help us validate automation
logic, campaign creation triggers, and budget control rules before
committing to any third-party platform.

I shared the BRD document with Malik. He is currently reviewing it. He will
provide a feasibility assessment and an estimated timeline for the in-house
build.


*Lastly, I will share a detailed comparison of Smarty.io and DeltaX with
pricing and go live date before end of this week.*
Thanks,
Anket

On Fri, Jun 5, 2026 at 6:48 PM Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:

> Hi Anket and Malik,
>
> I want to take our performance marketing to the next level — automated,
> AI-driven, and connected directly to our product inventory. Here is the
> plan and what I need from both of you.
> ------------------------------
>
> *THE DIRECTION*
>
> As we scale to 100+ cities and thousands of products, manually creating
> and managing ad campaigns is not possible. We need a system where a new
> product goes live on our website and an ad campaign is automatically
> created and starts running on Google and Meta — with zero manual work per
> product.
>
> This will happen in two phases:
>
> *Phase 1 — Immediate:* Use a third party tool called *Madgicx* to
> automate our Google and Meta campaigns right now. This gets us running
> properly and generating real performance data while we build our own
> solution.
>
> *Phase 2 — In-house build:* Malik's team builds Rayna's own ad automation
> module as an extension of the marketing platform already being built. When
> a product goes live on the website, it triggers an automatic ad campaign on
> Google and Meta through our own system. Full ownership, full control, zero
> dependency on third parties.
> ------------------------------
>
> *ANKET — YOUR TASKS:*
>
>    1. Study Madgicx thoroughly — madgicx.com
>    <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://madgicx.com&source=gmail&ust=1780756830737000&sa=E>.
>    Understand all its capabilities for Google and Meta automation, product
>    feed integration, audience targeting, and budget management.
>    2. Evaluate if Madgicx meets our requirements — automated campaign
>    creation from product feed, separate targeting for India, UAE, and Saudi,
>    full transparency on spend, hard budget limits the AI cannot exceed.
>    3. Get a demo and pricing from Madgicx — understand the cost vs our
>    current manual management cost.
>    4. Come back to me with your assessment and a recommended go-live date
>    for Madgicx. I want this information by *June 12th.*
>
> *MALIK — YOUR TASKS:*
>
>    1. Study the Madgicx product feed and API structure — understand
>    exactly how it connects product data to Google and Meta ad platforms. This
>    is the blueprint for what we will build in-house.
>    2. Study Google Ads API and Meta Marketing API — specifically dynamic
>    product advertising. When a product goes live in our database, how does
>    that trigger an automatic ad campaign? What data does each platform need —
>    image, description, price, URL, targeting parameters?
>    3. Plan the in-house ad automation module — as an extension of the
>    marketing platform you are already building. The vision: product goes live
>    on raynatours.com
>    <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780756830737000&sa=E>
>    → system automatically creates ad campaign on Google and Meta → campaign
>    starts running with preset budget and targeting rules → performance data
>    feeds back into the system for optimization.
>    4. Come back to me with a feasibility assessment and estimated build
>    timeline by *June 15th.*
>
> ------------------------------
>
> *WHAT I NEED FROM BOTH OF YOU TOGETHER:*
>
> A joint input by *June 15th* covering:
>
>    1. Madgicx — is it the right tool? If not, suggest an alternative.
>    What is the go-live date?
>    2. In-house build — is it feasible as an extension of the current
>    marketing platform? What is the realistic timeline?
>    3. How do Phase 1 and Phase 2 connect — can Madgicx data inform the
>    in-house build?
>
> Vaibhav — please oversee this and make sure Anket and Malik are aligned.
> The marketing platform you are building and the ad automation must work
> together seamlessly.
>
> This is a priority. Our September India and UAE campaign launch depends on
> having automated, scalable performance marketing in place.
>
> Thanks,
> Manoj
>

emails.subject: Re: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy
emails.body:
Sir,

One additional thought on the affiliate acquisition strategy.
Beyond creating a single UGC video and running performance marketing, I
believe we should create multiple creator-led videos around the same
concept.

Instead of promoting Rayna or specific travel products, creators would talk
about the opportunity itself and how people can generate additional income
through the AI-powered platform.

The objective is not influencer reach alone.
The objective is creative testing.
Every creator will communicate the same core message but in their own style
and personality.

This gives us:
• Multiple faces and personalities
• Different audience demographics
• Different hooks and storytelling styles
• Multiple creative variations for testing

Once these videos are published, we can track which videos generate the
highest engagement, click-through rates, lead generation, and affiliate
registrations.

The strongest performing videos can then be repurposed as performance
marketing assets.

This effectively turns influencer marketing into a creative generation
engine.

Rather than relying on a single spokesperson or a single campaign video, we
continuously build a library of proven ad creatives and scale the winners.

The result is a self improving acquisition system where influencer content
generates data and performance marketing identifies the best performing
assets for further scale.

Regards,
Nihad Ali

emails.subject: Rayna Tours — Omnichannel Win-Back Strategy: Segments to Create
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

Sharing the full omnichannel win-back strategy for Rayna Tours. The
complete plan is attached as a Word document — it covers all 14 journeys,
service × destination content branching, coupon rules, seasonal campaigns,
and the content production tracker.

Below is a summary of the *14 segments* we need to create in the platform
to power every journey.
------------------------------
PART 1 — Segments to Create

14 segments total — built once in the platform, power all 17 journeys
forever
Funnel Behavioral Segments (event-triggered)
# Segment Name Conditions
*S1* *Payment Abandoners* add_payment_info (last 30d) AND NO purchase
(Checkout page)
*S2* *Cart / Checkout Abandoners* add_to_cart OR begin_checkout (last 30d)
AND NO add_payment_info
*S3* *High Intent Browsers* check_availability_click OR view_item ≥3x (last
14d) AND NO add_to_cart
*S4* *Payment Failed* payment_failed (last 7d) AND NO subsequent purchase
|| Need to check with other Communication Module as well so that we don't
repeat
*S5* *Wishlist Savers* add_to_wishlist (last 30d) AND NO purchase
*S6* *Lead Not Converted* generate_lead OR lead (last 30d) AND NO
purchase Booking
Status–Based Segments
# Segment Name Booking Status / Conditions Notes
*S7* *Lapsed 90–180 Days* PAST_BOOKING + last booking 90–180d ago Recently
churned
*S8* *Lapsed 180d+* PAST_BOOKING + last booking >180d ago Deep churn
*S9* *High-Value Lapsed VIPs* Revenue > AED 2,000 + last booking >90d Highest
LTV
*S10a* *Cancelled Customers* CANCELLED Tried to book, cancelled
*S10b* *Enquiry Only* ENQUIRY_ONLY Raised hand, never booked
*S10c* *On Trip* ON_TRIP + Travel Date = today ±1d In-trip upsell window
*S10d* *Future Trip* FUTURE_TRIP + Travel Date in next 90d Pre-trip
journey Holiday
& Geography Targeting Segments
# Segment Name Conditions
*S11* *Indian Traveler Base* Is Indian = true OR Country = India
*S12* *UAE Residents* Country = UAE OR Geography = Local
*S13* *International* Geography = International
*S14* *Ghost Shoppers* GTM events ≥ 20 AND no purchase ever AND NOT
PAST_BOOKING
------------------------------

*Key rule to note:* 10% discount coupons apply to *Activities only*.
Holidays, Cruises, Yachts, and Visa — no discount. The hook for these is
expert consultation and urgency. Full coupon table (16 unique codes) is in
the attached document.

The attached Word document contains the full strategy including:

   - All 14 win-back journeys (J1–J14) with node-by-node detail
   - Seasonal campaigns based on country (Diwali, Christmas/NYE, UAE
   National Day)
   - Service × Destination content branching — per-service copy for all 5
   service types
   - Content production tracker — 56 email templates, 135 WhatsApp templates
   - Build sequence — Week 1 through Month 2 priority order

Happy to walk through this on a call whenever works for you.

Thanks,
*Vaibhav*
Rayna Tours

emails.subject: Reservation AI Platform — Your Independent Assessment Needed
emails.body:
Hi Anwar,

I have received the project delivery review for the Rayna AI Conversational
Commerce platform covering 15 April to 6 June. The document states the
application is built and operational, and that the remaining work —
completing the booking, visa, and payment flows — is blocked primarily by
inputs pending on the Rayna side: catalog data, product-line data, design
screens, and internal resources.

Before I form any conclusion or release any further payment, I want your
independent assessment as our CTO. The report was prepared by the build
side, and naturally it presents their perspective. I need your honest,
objective view on where we actually stand. Please answer the following
clearly:

*1. Is the account accurate?*
The report attributes almost every delay to inputs pending on the Rayna
side. In your honest assessment — is that a fair and complete picture, or
are there delays on the build side that the report does not reflect? I want
the truth, not a convenient version.

*2. Catalog data ownership.*
The 29 May review found 217 tours with no search enrichment, 143 with no
price, and 449 with no R-Points — out of 485 tours. This is data entry, not
engineering. Who on our side owns this? Why is it still incomplete after
nearly two months? And who will now own it with a firm completion date?

*3. Missing internal resources.*
The report says no product lead, no business-rules owner, and no UAT
booking agents have been assigned on our side — and that these are needed
before booking and payment flows can be completed and signed off. Why were
these not assigned earlier, and who will they be? This appears to be a
Rayna-side gap that we control.

*4. The three revenue flows.*
Booking, visa, and payment are the flows that actually generate revenue.
"Built" is not the same as "live and tested end to end with real data." For
each of these three flows, I want to know: what exactly remains, and the
realistic date each will be genuinely live and tested — not just
demonstrated.

*5. The data pipeline at scale.*
This is only 485 tours. We are expanding to 137 cities with thousands of
products. If filling data for 485 tours has been this difficult, how do we
scale this without it becoming a permanent bottleneck? I want your plan for
automating this — through the Global Tix API, supplier feeds, or otherwise
— rather than manual data entry.

*6. Payment recommendation.*
The report references milestone reviews and written acceptance needed to
release cycle payments. Based on the actual state of delivery — not the
demo — is the build side's work at a stage where the next payment is
justified? Give me your honest recommendation.

I want a written response from you, not a meeting. Take the time to assess
it properly and give me a clear, honest picture. You and Senthil co-own
this infrastructure, and I am relying on your judgment to tell me what is
really happening — including anything on our own side that we need to fix.

Please revert by Thursday.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Affiliate Acquisition Strategy
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

Following our discussion, I believe the fastest and most scalable way to
build the affiliate ecosystem is through performance marketing supported by
influencer awareness campaigns.

My recommendation is that we should not market this primarily as an
affiliate platform.

Instead, we should market it as an AI powered income opportunity.

The reason is simple. People are not actively looking for affiliate
platforms, but millions of people are actively looking for ways to generate
additional income.

Performance Marketing Strategy

I have previously created a UGC style video for one of our visa campaigns
which performed exceptionally well. The campaign continues to generate
leads even today through performance marketing.

I believe we should apply the same model to this platform.

Step 1

Create a high-converting UGC-style video explaining:

• How people can generate additional income
• How AI does the heavy lifting
• How users do not need content creation skills
• How the system automates distribution and lead generation
• Real earning potential and future opportunities

Step 2

Run performance marketing campaigns across Meta, TikTok, YouTube Shorts,
and Google Display.

The objective is not brand awareness.

The objective is affiliate acquisition.

Every lead generated enters the affiliate onboarding funnel.

This becomes our primary acquisition channel.

Influencer Awareness Campaign

As a secondary channel, we can activate influencer marketing.

Instead of asking influencers to promote destinations or products, we ask
them to introduce the income opportunity to their audience.

The creator message becomes:

“I recently came across an AI-powered platform that helps people generate
additional income through automated distribution. If you’re looking for a
side income opportunity, check it out.”

This feels natural and provides value to the influencer’s audience.

Since the objective is awareness and lead generation, we can focus
primarily on barter partnerships.

Expected Outcome

Performance marketing generates scalable affiliate leads.

Influencer marketing generates awareness and trust.

Both channels feed into the same affiliate acquisition funnel.

Rather than trying to recruit affiliates one by one, we build a system that
continuously attracts people interested in earning opportunities and
converts them into affiliates automatically.

I believe this approach will scale significantly faster than direct
outreach alone.

Regards,
Nihad Ali

On Sun, Jun 07, 2026 08:10 PM, Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com<mailto:manoj@raynatours.com>> wrote:

Hi Senthil and Deepak,

I am assigning both of you joint ownership of our tourism board outreach across all 137 cities we are activating. This is a strategic initiative that requires both seniority and deep destination knowledge — which is exactly why I want both of you on it together.

Senthil — you lead the senior-level conversations with tourism board directors and marketing heads. Your seniority and understanding of Rayna's business opens doors.

Deepak — you bring the destination and product depth. You know what we need from each tourism board, what content gaps we have, and what each destination requires to go live successfully. Your knowledge makes every conversation specific and credible.

Together you cover everything this initiative needs. Ranjan, Manoj Gagwani, Sunil Pawar, and Rajkumar are CC'd — as destination heads they will own city-specific follow-ups under your joint coordination.

________________________________

WHY THIS IS IMPORTANT

Tourism boards exist for one reason — to bring more visitors to their destination. When Rayna tells a tourism board that we are actively promoting their city to millions of customers across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia — they will want to support us. This is not a favour we are asking. This is a partnership that benefits them as much as it benefits us.

We have:

  *   1.8 million customers across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia
  *   Active marketing campaigns launching across three source markets
  *   137 cities being activated simultaneously
  *   AI-driven content engine producing destination content at scale
  *   B2C platform, app, email marketing, WhatsApp broadcasting, influencer network

Every tourism board we approach should see Rayna as one of the most valuable distribution partners they can have in their source markets. We are bringing their destination to millions of potential visitors. In return we need their support.

________________________________

WHAT TO ASK FROM EVERY TOURISM BOARD

  1.  Hosted FAM trips — Familiarization trips for Rayna's destination heads and product managers. A destination head who has visited Bangkok will sell Bangkok infinitely better than one who hasn't. Ask the tourism board to host and fund FAM trips for our team.
  2.  Marketing co-funding — Many tourism boards have co-op marketing funds for trade partners who promote their destination. Ask if they contribute towards our advertising spend — Meta ads, Google campaigns, email marketing, influencer programme for that destination.
  3.  Content — images, videos, destination guides — Every tourism board has a library of professional destination photography, videography, and written guides. Ask for full access to use across our website, app, social media, and marketing campaigns. This accelerates our Block 1 content engine significantly.
  4.  Articles and editorial content — Ask if they can write or co-write destination content for publication on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E>.
  5.  Press and media support — Ask if they can facilitate introductions to local journalists and travel publications who can cover Rayna's launch in their destination.
  6.  Trade shows and events — Ask to be included as a partner at any trade events they attend in India, UAE, or Saudi.
  7.  Preferred partner status — Ask to be formally recognized as a preferred or recommended tour operator for their destination.
  8.  White label technology partnership — This is our unique offer that no other operator can match. Some tourism boards need technology to promote their destination's holiday packages, activities, excursions, and events to travel agents and consumers. We can provide our white label technology platform — they brand it as their own, we power it behind the scenes. In return they give us preferred partner status, marketing funding, content access, and FAM trips. Bring this up with every tourism board where you sense a technology gap. Coordinate with Anwar when any tourism board expresses interest — he will handle the technical proposal.

________________________________

THE MARKETING PRESENTATION

Before any outreach begins, we need a professional Rayna partner presentation. Coordinate with Asad to design this — it must look premium. Deadline: June 20th.

Cover: who Rayna is, our source markets, our marketing reach, our September campaign, our destination commitment, our white label technology offer, and what we are asking in return.

________________________________

PRIORITY APPROACH

🔴 HIGH — Approach immediately:
Bangkok, Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Bali, Ho Chi Minh City, Tokyo, Seoul, Maldives, Mauritius, Dubai DTCM, Abu Dhabi DCT, Riyadh, Jeddah, Cairo, Muscat, London, Paris, Zurich, Amsterdam, Rome, Barcelona, Istanbul, New York, Los Angeles, Goa

🟡 MEDIUM — Approach July-August

🟢 GROWTH — Approach September onwards

________________________________

TOURISM BOARD TRACKER — ALL 137 CITIES

I have created a complete Google Sheet with all 137 cities pre-populated — tourism board names, official websites, trade partnership emails, priority levels, and notes on which boards have Dubai offices.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E>

Update after every interaction. I check this sheet every Monday.

________________________________

DEADLINES

________________________________
