emails.subject: Re: Booking Flow A/B/D Experiment — Critical Traffic Split Issue
emails.body:
Hi Vaibhav,

Picked this up — root cause is confirmed at the code level, and it is not a
GTM or RUM issue. A fix is ready and scheduled to hit prod today (09 Jun
2026).

What's actually happening

The A/B/D assignment itself is fine. I verified the bucketing maths against
live production tokens: simpleHash(guestUserId) % 3 distributes a clean
33/33/33, and every anonymous user does get a unique guestUserId. So the
split logic is not biased.

The skew comes from an eligibility gate that runs before the split. A
product only enters the A/B/D experiment if one of its options has both a
transfer and a time_slot dependency. Every product that doesn't have both
is hard-assigned to Flow A before the A/B/D split ever executes. Most
products don't have both → ~94% of traffic is forced into Flow A.

That exactly explains the numbers:
- Flow A = 2,182 → all non-eligible products + the ~1/3 of eligible users
who hash to A
- Flow B = 95 / Flow D = 116 → only eligible (transfer + time_slot) users
who hashed to B or D

So GTM is reporting the truth — the server simply wasn't producing B/D for
most traffic. Re-tagging GTM would not have changed anything.

The Fix

Single change in the eligibility gate (getBookingInputFlow.ts): qualify a
product on transfer alone, drop the time_slot requirement. This widens the
eligible population so B and D start receiving real traffic and the split
converges toward 33/33/33.

Owner & Deadline

- Fix owner: Ankit Patel
- Code change: Ready (1-line gate change, documented in the ClickUp task)
- Deploy target: Production — today, 09 Jun 2026 (EOD)
- Pipeline: branch off development → PR development → main
(sync-main-into-development flow) → prod deploy
- Verification: same day, immediately post-deploy

Verification Plan (post-deploy, today)

1. Open a transfer product's booking flow → confirm the API returns B/D
variants for different users (not only A).
2. Check booking_flow GTM events / experiment dashboard → Flow B and D
begin receiving meaningful traffic instead of <5% combined.
3. Confirm the split trends toward ~33/33/33 across eligible products over
the next few hours.

One Follow-up (does not block today's fix)

Bucketing is currently not sticky — the unauthenticated token expires every
5 min and mints a new guestUserId on refresh, and the assignment cookie is
keyed on that rotating id. So the same user can be re-bucketed across
sessions (can appear in A, B and D), which inflates user counts. Today's
change fixes the A-skew; I'll raise stickiness as a separate ticket
(persist a stable UUID in the cookie and bucket on that) so the experiment
counts are clean going forward.

Recommendation: please continue to hold product decisions on this
experiment until we've confirmed the 33/33/33 split post-deploy today, plus
a short fresh data-collection window for B/D.

I'll update the ClickUp task and reply here once it's live and verified on
prod.

Regards,
Gaurav

On Tue, Jun 09, 2026 11:43 AM, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com> wrote:

> ClickUp task created for tracking:
> https://app.clickup.com/t/9016502052/86d39gtfb
>
> Regards,
> Vaibhav
>

Subject: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil


Hi Senthil,

Welcome back. Two India items — wanted to share my thinking on both.

________________________________

ONE — Forbes India

I looked at the Forbes India proposal and their sample articles. It is a listicle format — we would be featured alongside multiple other companies in one article. Looking at the samples, most of the other companies are relatively small and unknown. I am not comfortable with that positioning for Rayna. Being placed in that context could actually work against us in India rather than for us.

My preference would be to explore a standalone feature — founder story, company journey, AI transformation, global expansion. Something that gives Rayna its own space. If Forbes India can offer that kind of premium format at the right price, I think it is worth doing properly. If not, let's walk away and find a better fit.

Aparna has been asked to offer a hosted Dubai experience as part of the negotiation before any cash is discussed. Please take this forward with Javed and let me know what options are available. I will make the final call once we know what is on the table.

Contact: Javed Khan — javed@ceothoughts.in<mailto:javed@ceothoughts.in> — +91 8920949627

________________________________

TWO — Senior Travel Writer

Aparna has put together a solid JD. Profile is right — someone who creates original content and reviews our AI-generated content. The media network requirement she added is a smart touch.

Three things worth addressing before we post:

  *   Confirm location — India or UAE. Changes the candidate pool and salary entirely.
  *   Tighten the salary range once location is confirmed. ₹1 lakh to ₹2.5 lakh is too wide to attract the right person confidently.
  *   Add the Student Travel Writing Competition to the JD responsibilities — this person will oversee student submissions, select winners, and publish articles on raynatours.com<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781029180296000&sa=E> across our partner schools in UAE, India, and Saudi.

Once updated, please post immediately. We need this person on board as soon as possible given the AI content engine going live.

Your experience, our responsibility.

Hi Senthil,

I've been working through our India B2C strategy and want to share the direction with you. Looping in Ranjan, Rajkumar, and Vaibhav as they'll be central to execution.

You know the team and the ground better than anyone � I'm sharing the full picture here so you can take this forward and shape the execution plan with them.

________________________________

THE DIRECTION

India B2C is our biggest untapped opportunity right now. Our B2B network is strong � that's solid. But Rayna as a direct consumer brand in India is still underexposed. We are starting with two clusters where we have the strongest presence, the right customer profile, and the highest online booking behavior.

Our positioning is clear and powerful: we are not a marketplace, we are the ground operator. We own what we sell. No local agent in any of these cities can say that. That's our story and every campaign must carry it.

Brand line: "Your experience, our responsibility." � "Aapka safar, hamari zimmedari."

________________________________

TARGET CITIES � TWO CLUSTERS

Cluster 1 � Maharashtra (Pune base, existing team):
Pune, Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad, Nagpur, Kolhapur

Cluster 2 � North India (High spending, corporate, strong online booking culture):
Delhi, Chandigarh, Ludhiana, Amritsar, Jaipur, Agra, Meerut

Together these two clusters cover the highest value B2C travel market in India. Maharashtra for volume and proximity to our Pune office. North India for high spending customers who book online and travel frequently.

________________________________

TWO PHASE PLAN

Phase 1 � June to August (Soft launch, low cost)

We go quiet but smart. No expensive media yet. Build awareness, test messaging, collect leads, and simultaneously expand our destination portfolio.

Destinations to promote � everything currently live: Thailand, Singapore, Bali, Malaysia, Dubai visas, international visas, Abu Dhabi, Saudi.

Channels � digital only:

  *   Meta and Google � geo-targeted to both clusters � Maharashtra 6 cities + North India 7 cities
  *   WhatsApp broadcasting to our existing customer database filtered by these cities
  *   PR � Maharashtra Times, ET Pune, Lokmat, Sakal, Hindustan Times Delhi, Tribune Chandigarh, Dainik Bhaskar, Conde Nast India
  *   Corporate partnerships � IT companies Pune tech corridor, financial sector Mumbai, corporate sector Delhi and Chandigarh
  *   Influencer program � hosted trips only, no cash spend. Shortlist from both Maharashtra and North India creators to come through Vaibhav for my approval before any outreach.

Two things must be in place before any spend: pricing competitive (Ranjan + Rajkumar report June 8th) and website fixed (June 9th).

Phase 2 � September onwards (Full campaign, full spend)

TV, radio, newspaper, mall activations � both clusters simultaneously. But by September we need a global portfolio behind us that no local agent can match. That's what makes Rayna different. Anyone can sell Thailand and Singapore. Nobody else can offer this:

  *   Europe � London, Paris, Amsterdam, Barcelona, Switzerland, Rome, Istanbul
  *   Far East � Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong
  *   Indian Ocean � Maldives, Mauritius, Seychelles
  *   Americas � USA, Canada
  *   Australia and New Zealand

September campaign: "From Thailand to London, from Bali to Switzerland � Rayna Tours. Your experience, our responsibility."

Maharashtra channels: TV (Zee Marathi, Star Pravah, Colors Marathi, Star Plus, Sony), Radio (Mirchi, Big FM, Red FM), Newspaper (Maharashtra Times, Lokmat, Sakal, TOI Maharashtra), Mall activations (Phoenix Mumbai, Palladium, Seawoods, Westend Pune)

North India channels: TV (Aaj Tak, News18 India, Star Plus, Zee News), Radio (Mirchi Delhi, Red FM Delhi, Big FM), Newspaper (Hindustan Times, Times of India Delhi, Tribune, Dainik Bhaskar, Punjabi Tribune), Mall activations (Select Citywalk Delhi, DLF Malls, Elante Chandigarh, Ambience Mall Gurgaon)

________________________________

WHAT I NEED FROM YOU � TWO ACTIONS:

Action 1 � By June 20th:
Share execution ownership for each Phase 1 channel with me. Who owns what, timelines, and how we're going to market across both clusters.

Action 2 � By June 30th:
Identify 5 people internally from Rayna to own the new destination groups. Their job is supplier contracting, product management, and getting inventory live by August 31st. Structure:

  1.  Europe West � London, Paris, Amsterdam, Barcelona
  2.  Europe East + Turkey � Switzerland, Rome, Istanbul, Prague
  3.  Far East � Japan, South Korea, China, Hong Kong
  4.  Indian Ocean + Africa � Maldives, Mauritius, Kenya, Seychelles
  5.  Americas + Australia � USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand

These people start July 1st. August 31st � all destinations live. September 1st � we go loud across both clusters.

________________________________

Senthil, this is a big moment for Rayna in India. I know you and the team will execute this well. Let's connect once you've had a chance to review this with Ranjan, Rajkumar, and Vaibhav.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Booking Flow A/B/D Experiment — Critical Traffic Split Issue
emails.body:
Hi Gaurav,

Wanting to flag a critical discrepancy in our Booking Flow A/B/D experiment
that needs urgent attention.

Current traffic split (2026-04-01 → 2026-06-07):
- Flow A: 2,182 users (2.61% conv) — LEADING
- Flow B: 95 users (2.11% conv) — Small N
- Flow D: 116 users (0.00% conv) — Small N

The experiment is supposed to have an equal split across all three flows,
but Flow A is receiving ~94% of total experiment traffic while Flow B and D
are getting less than 5% combined. This makes the results statistically
unreliable for Flow B and D, and the experiment is essentially inconclusive.

This issue was flagged earlier and needs to be resolved on priority.

Request:
1. Gaurav & Soumya — can you both sit together and investigate why the
traffic allocation is skewed? Likely a GTM/booking_flow event
misconfiguration or variant assignment logic issue at the Date Selection
step.
2. Gaurav — please confirm if this has already been picked up and whether a
fix is in progress.

Until this is corrected, we cannot make any product decisions based on this
experiment data.

Sharing the experiment screenshot for reference.
[image: image.png]
Regards,
Vaibhav

emails.subject: Re: RE: Please check if this is ok to go or u suggest some changes
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

Go/No-Go: NO GO for June 10 — but here is exactly where we stand and the
path forward.

Current testing status:
The app is substantially built across all five product lines, but is not
yet safe to take live customer payments. The engineering fixes are only 2–3
weeks of work. The date is set by three external queues we do not control —
payment certification (Network Int'l), QA agency onboarding, and app-store
review.

Blockers:
- Card details are currently collected in-app and routed via our server —
PCI exposure that must be moved to a hosted/tokenised flow before going live
- Visa travel-date selector is not functional
- No dropped-connection or double-charge protection
- No automated tests; store-readiness items (policy links, stubs) will
cause rejection
- QA agency not yet finalised — onboarding alone takes ~3 weeks

Realistic window: late July (best case) to mid-August (with normal slippage
on the external queues)

I have sent you a full Go/No-Go briefing with the complete critical path,
all five resource lanes, three timeline scenarios, and a risk register —
please refer to that email (subject: Mobile App — UAE Launch: Go/No-Go &
Recommended Plan) for the detailed picture.

Two answers this week will let us lock a firm date:
1. Is mobile in-app payment covered by our Network Int'l agreement?
(Finance/Ops)
2. Is our Google Play account an organisation or personal account?
(Release/Eng)

Best,
Gaurav

emails.subject: Mobile App — UAE Launch: Go/No-Go & Recommended Plan (action needed this week)
emails.body:
Mobile App — UAE Launch Readiness
Go/No-Go Update & Recommended Plan

Hi Manoj,

Thank you for the detailed plan and for setting a clear, quality-first bar.
Receipt confirmed — I'm leading the launch coordination and have completed
a full readiness review with Engineering, QA, Payments, Release and GTM.
Recommendation
Hold June 10. Target a late-July to mid-August window, and confirm a firm
date this week once two answers are in.

*The short version:*
•  The remaining *engineering is only ~2–3 weeks*.
•  The date is set by three *external queues we don't control* — payment
certification (Network Int'l), QA-agency onboarding, and app-store review.
•  June 10 isn't achievable, and even June 24 is too tight once those are
counted.
•  Getting UAE right first protects the India → Saudi → Global cascade,
which is gated on a stable, 4.0+ launch.

*What's still open before we can take live payments:*
Open item Why it matters
Card details collected in-app, routed via our server PCI exposure — must
move to provider's hosted/tokenised flow
Visa travel-date selector not wired Date is essential to a visa; the flow
is incomplete
No dropped-connection handling; payment confirmed via URL text Risk of
double charges / false confirmations
Non-tappable policy links; 'coming soon' stubs; no crash reporting Likely
store rejection; no production observability
No automated tests in the project All assurance is manual — raises the QA
burden

*The three timeline scenarios:*
Scenario Target date Conditions
Best case ~Late July Mobile already covered by merchant deal · reinforced
team · fast-tracked QA · no store rejection
Realistic ~Early–mid August One payment-UAT rework · one store rejection ·
agency legal ~1.5 weeks
Conservative ~Mid–late August New payment channel needed · two store
rejections · Play 14-day testing

*The five resource lanes (run in parallel — the longest one sets the date):*
Lane Lead time Confidence
Engineering (fixes listed above) 2–3 weeks High
*Payments — Network Int'l* (dominant pole) 3.5–9 weeks Medium — depends on
Q1
QA agency onboarding & testing ~3 weeks Medium-High
Store submission & review ~3 weeks Medium — depends on Q2
GTM & support (incl. Arabic agents) ~3 weeks High

*Two answers this week lock a firm date:*
1.  Is *mobile in-app payment* covered by our Network Int'l agreement?
(Finance/Ops
— needed in +3 days)
2.  Is our *Google Play account* an organisation or personal account?
(Release/Eng
— needed in +2 days)

*What I'm asking you to green-light this week:*
•  Adopt the *late-July to mid-August target window* (firm date follows the
Q1/Q2 answers)
•  Green-light the *QA fast-track* (pre-vetted vendor on LOI, or cloud
device farm) and *Arabic support hiring* — both starting now
•  Endorse *payment rework* as this week's top engineering priority
(Vaibhav)

*Long-lead items we can start today — no code dependency:*
•  *Email Network Int'l* — confirm mobile coverage + request sandbox & 3DS2
•  *QA* — issue LOI/NDA to a pre-vetted agency, or stand up a cloud device
farm
•  *Support* — designate a manager; begin Arabic-agent hiring/training
•  *Release* — verify the Google Play account type; open a testing track
early
•  *Legal* — update privacy policy for visa data + Arabic + PDPL consent
•  *Backend* — build booking-status + idempotency endpoints (double-charge
safety)

The *full briefing is attached* (*UAE-Launch-Briefing.pdf*) — it covers the
critical path, all five resource lanes, three timeline scenarios, a risk
register, and the source evidence with diagrams. The supporting deck (
*UAE-Launch-Readiness.pdf*) has the visual version. Happy to walk through
it live whenever suits you.
Why not just launch June 10 and patch later?
Over-the-air updates fix our JavaScript but *cannot* bypass payment
certification, store review, or the PDPL gate — the actual blockers.

Payment/money defects are the *#1 cause of week-one 1-star reviews* — which
trips the 4.0 expansion gate and stalls India/Saudi/Global.

Taking live cards through a non-compliant flow is a regulatory and
financial risk we shouldn't carry even briefly.

Best regards,

Gaurav Verma

Mobile App Launch Coordination
------------------------------
Attachments: UAE-Launch-Briefing.pdf  ·  UAE-Launch-Readiness.pdf  ·  cc:
Vaibhav, Senthil  ·  June 2026

>
> Once the agency is selected and testing timeline is confirmed, share the

>
>    - Access to app staging environment
>    - Test accounts with dummy payment credentials
>    - Full product documentation
>    - The QA testing brief already shared with you
>
> The app still launches UAE only first. India, Saudi, and global follow

>

emails.subject: New Role — Category Head, Events & Experiences | JD for Recruitment | Pari
emails.body:
Hi Pari,

We need to recruit for a new and important role — Category Head, Events &
Experiences. Please find the complete job description below and begin the
search immediately.
------------------------------

*JOB DESCRIPTION — CATEGORY HEAD, EVENTS & EXPERIENCES*

*Reports to:* Founder and CEO
*Department:* Events & Experiences (new category)
*Location:* Dubai, UAE
------------------------------

*ABOUT THE ROLE*

Rayna Tours is launching a dedicated Events & Experiences category. We are
looking for an entrepreneurial, well-connected individual to build this
category from the ground up. This person will start by personally driving
three areas and, as the category grows, will build and lead a team beneath
them.

Importantly — this is not a traditional sales role. Rayna's marketing and
sales run on a fully automated, AI-powered marketing and performance
marketing engine. Anything that goes live on the Rayna platform is
automatically promoted and sold through our marketing automation. This
means the Category Head does not need to chase sales. Their focus is on
sourcing, contracting, and securing the best events, festivals, and
retreats, and getting them live on the platform. Our marketing engine does
the selling.
------------------------------

*THE THREE CORE AREAS*

*1. Local Events Ticketing*
Build a portfolio of event ticketing inventory by contracting directly with
event organizers across the UAE — concerts, live shows, festivals, sporting
events, exhibitions, and entertainment. Secure ticket allocations to go
live on the Rayna platform. The benchmark for this area is Platinumlist —
the goal is to build Rayna into a serious player in event ticketing over
time. This will start modestly and grow month on month, year on year, as
relationships with organizers deepen.

*2. Music Festival Holiday Packages (Worldwide)*
Travelers from UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India increasingly travel
internationally to attend major music festivals — Tomorrowland, Coachella,
Ultra, Sunburn, Burning Man, and many more. This role involves building
relationships with festival organizers worldwide to secure event tickets
and access, then collaborating with Rayna's destination heads in each city
to build complete travel packages — festival tickets plus flights, hotels,
transfers, and experiences.

*3. Fitness & Wellness Retreats (Worldwide)*
A fast-growing segment. Travelers are increasingly seeking fitness and
wellness retreats in destinations like Bali, Phuket, Fukuoka, and other
global wellness hubs. This role involves sourcing and partnering with
leading retreats worldwide and, in collaboration with our destination
teams, building complete retreat travel packages.
------------------------------

*HOW THE ROLE WORKS WITH OUR DESTINATION TEAMS*

Rayna is activating 137 destination cities worldwide, each managed by a
regional destination head. The Category Head does not build ground packages
alone. When an event or festival is identified in a city — for example a
music festival in Barcelona — this person secures the event tickets and
access, then coordinates with the relevant destination head for that city
to build the complete ground package. This collaboration model allows the
role to operate globally without duplicating the destination teams' work.
------------------------------

*KEY RESPONSIBILITIES*

   - Contract with event organizers across UAE for ticketing inventory
   - Build worldwide relationships with music festival organizers to secure
   tickets and access
   - Source and partner with fitness and wellness retreats globally
   - Collaborate with destination heads to build complete travel packages
   around events, festivals, and retreats
   - Get all inventory live on the Rayna platform, where the AI marketing
   engine promotes and sells it automatically
   - Grow each area month on month and build a team as the category scales
   - Negotiate commercial terms that protect Rayna's margins
   - Report category progress regularly to the Founder and CEO

------------------------------

*IDEAL CANDIDATE*

   - 5+ years experience in events, entertainment, ticketing, travel, or a
   related field
   - Existing relationships with event organizers, promoters, or venues —
   strongly preferred
   - Entrepreneurial mindset — comfortable building a category from scratch
   - Strong sourcing, contracting, and negotiation skills
   - Well-networked across the events and entertainment industry
   - Self-driven, able to deliver results independently before a team is
   built
   - Understanding of the UAE, Saudi, and Indian markets is an advantage
   - Willingness to grow into a senior category leadership position

------------------------------

*WHY THIS ROLE IS ATTRACTIVE*

   - Build and own a brand new category within a major travel company
   - No pressure to chase sales — our AI marketing engine drives the
   selling. Focus purely on sourcing the best inventory.
   - Backed by Rayna's 137-city destination network, owned assets,
   technology platform, and automated marketing engine
   - Clear path to build and lead a team as the category scales
   - Direct reporting line to the Founder and CEO

------------------------------

Pari — please post this role across all relevant channels and your
recruitment network and begin shortlisting immediately. Prioritize
candidates with existing event organizer relationships, as that will
accelerate the category's growth significantly.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Re: Block 3 — Complete Scope | Three Workstreams + Affiliate Social Feature | Vaibhav + Malik
emails.cc:
vaibhav@raynatours.com, malik@raynatours.com, senthil@raynatours.com, 
	nihad@raynatours.com, anket@raynatours.com, alok@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

Here's the grounded plan for the affiliate social auto-posting feature,
after reviewing the actual codebase and infrastructure. The headline: *we
are roughly half-built already* — account connection, token storage,
scheduling, publishing, analytics and commission tracking exist and work.
The remaining work is the intelligence layer (AI that writes brand-safe,
personalised posts and publishes on autopilot) plus the production path and
test coverage, which don't exist yet because the app has never been
promoted past its dev environment.
------------------------------

*The ask in one line*
Ring-fence 4 developers at 100% + 1 QA + a full month of DevOps from 16 Jun
to 24 Aug, and clear three inputs this week (content-engine decision,
PostForMe terms, data + brand-brief access). Do that, and the feature ships
before September with buffer.
------------------------------

*Where we stand*
✓ Already built (reuse) ● Must build (the feature)
Connect IG / FB / YouTube / X / WhatsApp AI content engine (no AI code
today)
Token vault + refresh Audience profiling + niche matching
Schedule, publish, drafts, webhooks Autonomous "posts-forever" scheduler
Analytics pull (reach / clicks) Brand-compliance admin + kill-switch
Commission / coupon / link tracking Product-feed sync + consent ledger
Production path + test coverage
------------------------------

*Three timelines*
Plan Live Scope Verdict
A — Aggressive ~25 Jul IG+FB, English, human approves each post Assistant,
not autopilot
*B — Medium ✓* *24 Aug* *IG+FB+YouTube, Arabic+English, full autopilot,
real beta, security* *Recommended — full promise + buffer*
C — Soft ~14 Sep B + TikTok + LinkedIn + big beta Lands on the wall, no
slack
------------------------------

*Recommended Plan B — three parallel tracks*
Feature build, infra/environment promotion, and testing run concurrently
from week one — that is what makes 24 Aug achievable.
Track W1
6/16 W2
6/23 W3
6/30 W4
7/07 W5
7/14 W6
7/21 W7
7/28 W8
8/04 W9
8/11 W10
8/18
*Feature build*
Content engine (EN)
Generate → publish
Autopilot scheduler
Attribution + earnings
Admin / brand compliance
Arabic + RTL
*Infra / environment promotion*
DB-secret + migration fix
Rebuild test/pre-prod/prod pipelines
Promote feature dev→test→pre-prod
*Testing & quality*
Test harness + mocks + CI gate
Integration + E2E + AI-eval
Security + load (pre-prod)
Beta on test/pre-prod
*Prod cutover + canary → GA*                   *GA*

Feature Infra/Env Testing GA 24 Aug — ahead of the September wall
------------------------------

*Why environments matter here*
We run four environments in four separate AWS accounts — dev → test →
pre-prod → prod, each with an approval gate. Today only dev works; the app
has never been promoted to production, and the test/pre-prod/prod deploy
pipelines need rebuilding for the current multi-container setup. On top of
that, the riskiest code in the product — the social-posting service, the
publish webhook, the OAuth flows — currently has zero automated tests. So
this feature is what finally forces the production path and a real test
suite to get built. That's why the plan resources a full DevOps month and a
full QA from day one — it is prerequisite work, not polish.
------------------------------

*Team & focus — the decision that determines September*
Role Dedication Can be shared?
Full-stack lead (scheduler/architecture) 100% ring-fenced No — never split
AI/content engineer 100% No
AI/prompt + Arabic 80–100% Light, Wk1–2 only
Full-stack (dashboard/admin) 100% No
QA / SDET 100% from Wk1 Greenfield test harness
DevOps / infra ~1.0 FTE Wk1–4, 0.5 after Rebuild pipelines + prod cutover

*Splitting the lead or AI engineer onto other fires doesn't make it 2×
slower — context-switching makes it ~3× slower, because the engine and
scheduler are deep and not parallelizable.*
------------------------------

*What we need this week*

   1. *Content engine:* hand over Vaibhav's engine by 18 Jun, or confirm we
   build it fresh (biggest schedule swing).
   2. *PostForMe:* confirm the contract permits auto-posting on affiliates'
   behalf, and which platforms it covers.
   3. *Access by 20 Jun:* product-feed, affiliate database, and Nihad's
   brand-rules brief — engineers are blocked without these.
   4. *Staffing:* approve ring-fencing the team above for 16 Jun – 24 Aug.

------------------------------

*The one risk to watch*
An AI-generated wrong price or claim posted in an affiliate's name — public
and irreversible. Controlled by: block generation on stale data, keep
human-approval ON until 500 live posts with zero errors, full audit log,
and a one-click global kill-switch. Tracked weekly as *"live posts since
last price/claim error."*
------------------------------

Happy to walk through any of this. My recommendation is to lock Plan B and
the staffing this week so we protect the September window.

Best,
Gaurav

Rayna Tours · Affiliate Platform · Block 3 — Marketing Distribution Platform

On Mon, Jun 08, 2026 12:29 AM, Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:

> Hi Vaibhav and Malik,
>
> I want to formally define the complete scope of Block 3 — our marketing
> distribution platform. This has grown beyond what was originally planned
> and I want both of you fully aligned on everything that needs to be built.
> Please read this fully.
> ------------------------------
>
> *BLOCK 3 — THREE WORKSTREAMS*
>
> Block 3 is no longer just email marketing. It is our complete AI-powered
> marketing distribution and automation platform covering three workstreams.
> Both of you own this together. I need a combined deadline for all three
> workstreams by end of this week.
> ------------------------------
>
> *WORKSTREAM 1 — EMAIL, WHATSAPP, SMS, PUSH NOTIFICATIONS*
> *Owner: Vaibhav + Malik (ChatHead)*
>
> This is the core marketing platform already in progress. Complete end to
> end system covering:
>
>    - AI-planned email campaigns — planned, scheduled, previewed,
>    approved, distributed automatically
>    - WhatsApp broadcasting through ChatHead — mass broadcast,
>    personalized messaging, automated follow-ups
>    - SMS campaigns
>    - Push notifications — app-based once app is live
>    - Seed list testing — 10 members, 4 approvals required before any
>    campaign goes live — AI user + Anket + any 2 others
>    - Co-branded campaigns — for corporate partners, reward programme
>    partners, and affiliates
>    - Daily campaign forwarding to corporate and reward partners
>
> I need your complete deadline for this workstream. By when is everything
> fully live and operational? Come back to me with a realistic date this week.
> ------------------------------
>
> *WORKSTREAM 2 — MADGICX INTEGRATION — GOOGLE ADS AND META ADS*
> *Owner: Anket + Malik*
>
> AI-powered Google and Meta ads automation through Madgicx. Covers:
>
>    - Automated campaign creation from product feed — new destination goes
>    live, ad campaign creates automatically
>    - Budget optimization — AI allocates spend across campaigns based on
>    performance
>    - Audience targeting — separate campaigns for India, UAE, and Saudi
>    - Creative testing — AI tests which content performs best
>    - Full transparency — human sets rules, AI executes within those rules
>    - In-house build planning — Malik assesses building Rayna's own ad
>    automation as an extension of Block 3 in parallel
>
> Assessment and go-live date due June 15th from Anket and Malik jointly.
> ------------------------------
>
> *WORKSTREAM 3 — SOCIAL MEDIA AUTOMATION AGENT*
> *Owner: Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad*
>
> This is a new and critical addition to Block 3. The Marketing Agent must
> connect to all Rayna social media platforms and post automatically — no
> human dependency.
>
> *Platforms to connect:*
>
>    - Instagram — @raynatours_
>    - Facebook — Rayna Tours
>    - TikTok
>    - YouTube — Shorts and full videos
>    - LinkedIn
>    - Twitter / X
>
> *What the agent does automatically:*
>
>    - Reads the product feed — every destination, every product, every
>    offer
>    - Generates market-ready content — reels, static posts, stories,
>    captions, YouTube shorts
>    - Posts across all platforms on an optimized schedule
>    - When a new destination goes live — automatically creates and posts
>    destination content across all platforms within hours
>    - Adjusts posting frequency and timing based on engagement data
>
> *Rules we define once — AI executes forever:*
>
>    - Brand tone and voice — "Your experience, our responsibility."
>    - Content types per platform — reels for Instagram and TikTok, long
>    form for YouTube, professional for LinkedIn
>    - Posting frequency per platform
>    - Languages — English, Arabic, Hindi versions per market
>    - Offer codes and campaign links
>
> Nihad — you are CC'd on this. The social media automation agent does not
> replace your role. Your role becomes defining the strategy, rules, and
> creative direction that the AI follows. You are the brain. The AI is the
> execution. Work with Vaibhav and Malik to define the exact rules and
> content guidelines the agent must follow.
> ------------------------------
>
> *ADDITIONAL FEATURE — AFFILIATE SOCIAL MEDIA AUTO-POSTING*
> *Owner: Vaibhav (manages affiliate programme) + Gaurav (builds the
> feature)*
>
> This is one of the most powerful features we can add to our affiliate
> programme. Here is how it works:
>
> Any affiliate who wants to — connects their social media handles to the
> Rayna affiliate platform. Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube — any
> platform they are active on. Once connected, the same AI agent that posts
> to Rayna's own accounts automatically creates and posts Rayna content to
> the affiliate's accounts as well.
>
> *What the affiliate gets:*
>
>    - They connect their account once — AI does everything from that point
>    - AI creates content relevant to their audience and their niche
>    - AI posts automatically on an optimal schedule
>    - Their unique discount code and affiliate link is embedded in every
>    post
>    - When their followers click and book — they earn commission
>    automatically
>    - Zero effort required after the initial connection
>
> *Why this is a game changer:*
> The biggest reason affiliates sign up and never post is because creating
> content feels like work. With this feature — they connect once, AI posts
> for them forever, commission keeps coming in passively. This will
> dramatically increase affiliate activation rates and the volume of Rayna
> content reaching new audiences every day.
>
> Gaurav — you are CC'd. This feature needs to be built into the Rayna
> affiliate platform. It uses the same AI content generation engine as
> Workstream 3 — the affiliate version personalizes content for each
> affiliate's audience and embeds their unique discount code and link
> automatically. Please confirm this is added to your development roadmap and
> share a build timeline.
>
> Nihad — this feature will be your strongest recruitment pitch to
> influencers. "Connect your account once. We create the content. We post it.
> You earn commission automatically." Use this in your outreach immediately
> once the feature is live.
> ------------------------------
>
> *WHAT I NEED FROM YOU — THIS WEEK*
>
>    1. *Vaibhav + Malik* — Complete deadline for Workstream 1 — email,
>    WhatsApp, SMS, push notifications. Fully live and operational by when?
>    2. *Anket + Malik* — Madgicx assessment and go-live date — June 15th
>    deadline stands
>    3. *Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad* — Initial plan for Workstream 3 — social
>    media automation agent. What platforms first? What is the build timeline?
>    What rules and content guidelines does Nihad need to define?
>    4. *Gaurav* — Confirm affiliate social auto-posting feature is added
>    to development roadmap. By when can this be live?
>
> Block 3 is the engine that connects everything — our products, our
> destinations, our offers — to our customers and affiliates across every
> channel simultaneously. Get this right and our September campaign launch
> will be unlike anything Rayna has done before.
>
> *Your experience, our responsibility.*
>

emails.subject: Re: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil
emails.body:
DEar Manojj sir and senthil Sir, I have already requested this information from him as well. Below is the response I received:

 

Dear Aparna,

Thank you for your response.

While the participating company list cannot be disclosed prior to publication, we can offer the following exclusive coverage options for Rayna Tours:

1. Forbes India Brandconnect Dedicated Online Article – ₹2,00,000 + 18% GST


	Exclusive feature focused entirely on Rayna Tours
	700–800 words editorial-style article
	Company and leadership profiling
	Website backlink
	Permanent online hosting


2. Forbes India E-Magazine Feature – ₹2,50,000 + 18% GST


	One full-page advertorial (600–650 words)
	One high-resolution image
	PDF copy of the magazine


3. Forbes India magazine Print + E-Magazine Feature – ₹5,00,000 + 18% GST


	One full-page advertorial (600–650 words)
	One high-resolution image
	PDF copy of the magazine
	Two complimentary hard copies
	Available across major bookstores and distribution channels in India and overseas


4. CEO Thoughts Magazine Cover Story Package – ₹1,50,000 + 5% GST


	6-page cover story feature
	Cover page placement
	Leadership profile
	Recognition certificate
	Website article and social media promotion
	Complimentary hard copies


Please let us know which option best aligns with your branding and marketing objectives, and we will be happy to share the next steps.

 

Regards,




 Javed | Corporate Team

 Fortune Summit Media

 +91 8920949627 






From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 23:51:41

To: senthil@raynatours.com

Cc: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil

 

Hi Senthil,

Welcome back. Two India items — wanted to share my thinking on both.


ONE — Forbes India

I looked at the Forbes India proposal and their sample articles. It is a listicle format — we would be featured alongside multiple other companies in one article. Looking at the samples, most of the other companies are relatively small and unknown. I am not comfortable with that positioning for Rayna. Being placed in that context could actually work against us in India rather than for us.

My preference would be to explore a standalone feature — founder story, company journey, AI transformation, global expansion. Something that gives Rayna its own space. If Forbes India can offer that kind of premium format at the right price, I think it is worth doing properly. If not, let's walk away and find a better fit.

Aparna has been asked to offer a hosted Dubai experience as part of the negotiation before any cash is discussed. Please take this forward with Javed and let me know what options are available. I will make the final call once we know what is on the table.

Contact: Javed Khan — javed@ceothoughts.in — +91 8920949627


TWO — Senior Travel Writer

Aparna has put together a solid JD. Profile is right — someone who creates original content and reviews our AI-generated content. The media network requirement she added is a smart touch.

Three things worth addressing before we post:


	Confirm location — India or UAE. Changes the candidate pool and salary entirely.
	Tighten the salary range once location is confirmed. ₹1 lakh to ₹2.5 lakh is too wide to attract the right person confidently.
	Add the Student Travel Writing Competition to the JD responsibilities — this person will oversee student submissions, select winners, and publish articles on raynatours.com across our partner schools in UAE, India, and Saudi.


Once updated, please post immediately. We need this person on board as soon as possible given the AI content engine going live.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: RE: Two New Partnership Tracks — Radio + Trending Apps | Vaibhav Owns
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

 

Thank you for looping me in on the radio and app partnership initiative. I've gone through the full brief and put together a quick analysis (attached).

 

A couple of things I wanted to flag before we activate:

 

On the radio side: I do have direct contacts within the ARN network (Virgin Radio, 100.3, Mirchi, City 1016). Happy to connect Vaibhav with the right people as soon as we're ready to move.

 

But my honest concern is our website readiness. Right now, only activities are fully bookable on raynatours.com. Holidays, cruise, Hotels, and visas are still enquiry-based. If we run a large campaign and drive thousands of listeners to the site, we'll build brand awareness, but we won't convert them across most of our verticals. That's a missed opportunity given the barter inventory we'd be spending.

 

My recommendation in the attached report:

*	We can pilot immediately with activity prizes only (Desert Safari, Balloon Ride — these are fully bookable)
*	Get a committed timeline from tech to bring holidays and cruise online
*	Then scale the campaign with full confidence

 

Happy to discuss when you have a moment.

 

Thank you.

 

Azhar

 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Monday, June 8, 2026 10:50 PM
To: Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; azhar@raynatours.com; rishi@raynatours.com; malik@raynatours.com; anket@raynatours.com; alok@raynatours.com; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>; nihad@raynatours.com
Subject: Two New Partnership Tracks — Radio + Trending Apps | Vaibhav Owns

 

Hi Vaibhav,

I want to open two new partnership tracks that I believe can significantly build Rayna's brand across UAE, India, and Saudi at very low cost. You are the owner of both. Please read this fully.

  _____  

ONE — RADIO PARTNERSHIPS

I want you to start approaching radio stations across all three of our target markets — UAE, India, and Saudi Arabia. The objective is simple — get Rayna's name, products, and destinations in front of millions of radio listeners through exciting on-air campaigns and listener giveaways.

Approach — Barter first, no paid campaigns to start.
Do not commit any cash at this stage. Our offer to every radio station is barter — we provide free experiences, vouchers, and holiday packages as prizes for their listeners. They get exciting content and prizes for their audience. We get brand visibility and reach.

What we offer radio stations:

*	Small prizes — Activity vouchers — desert safari, water sports, balloon ride, city tour, attraction tickets. Redeemable on raynatours.com <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443484000&sa=E>  via QR code.
*	Mid prizes — Cruise packages, short getaways, weekend experiences.
*	Large prizes — Complete holiday packages — Thailand, Bali, Maldives, Dubai — for 2 persons.

Voucher process — your responsibility:
Please design the complete end-to-end voucher system. How vouchers are generated, how QR codes are created, how listeners redeem them on raynatours.com <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443484000&sa=E> , how we track redemptions, and how we capture listener details into our database. Every person who redeems a voucher must enter our database.

Campaign ideas to propose to radio stations:

*	"Win a Dubai Desert Safari" — daily winner for one week
*	"Win a family holiday to Thailand" — weekly grand prize campaign
*	"Rayna's Summer Escape" — themed summer travel campaign with multiple prize tiers
*	"Guess the destination and win" — interactive on-air quiz with activity vouchers as prizes
*	Morning show travel tips sponsored by Rayna — brand mention every day

Target radio stations — start with:

*	UAE — Virgin Radio Dubai, Dubai 92, City 1016, Hum FM, Radio 4
*	India — Radio Mirchi, Red FM, Big FM — across Mumbai, Pune, Delhi, Chandigarh
*	Saudi — MBC FM, Rotana FM, SBC Radio

Approach all of them. Once you have explored options — bring me a complete menu of what campaigns are available and your recommendation on which to activate first.

  _____  

TWO — TRENDING APP PARTNERSHIPS — THIS IS VERY IMPORTANT

This is one of the most powerful brand and lead generation strategies I have seen for our business. Please understand this fully.

The model:

We approach trending subscription-based apps — Spotify, Anghami, Careem, Zomato, Noon, CRED, and others — with a simple and mutually beneficial proposition:

"Announce to your subscribers — anyone who subscribes or renews their subscription this month is automatically entered to win a free holiday to Thailand, Vietnam, Dubai, or Maldives — courtesy of Rayna Tours."

Why the app benefits:
The app gets a powerful reason to drive new subscriptions and prevent existing subscriber churn. "Subscribe now and win a holiday" is a far more compelling message than a standard renewal reminder. The app gets more revenue. We get their audience.

The complete loop — this is key:

1.	App announces the campaign to their full subscriber base — email, push notification, in-app banner
2.	User subscribes or renews — motivated by the prize. They enter the campaign automatically.
3.	To enter, users provide their name, email, and optionally their dream destination — this data comes to Rayna
4.	One winner is announced jointly by the app and Rayna — big moment, social media, PR coverage for both
5.	Every participant who did not win receives an email from Rayna: "You didn't win this time — but you're still a winner. Here is a 10% discount on any Rayna booking. Use it to book your dream destination today."
6.	Winner costs Rayna one holiday package. The 10% coupon converts a percentage of the thousands of runners-up into actual bookings.

Why this is a lead generation machine:
Every person who enters this campaign did so because they wanted to win a trip to Thailand or Vietnam or Dubai. These are not random people. These are intent customers — people with a desire to travel to a specific destination. When we send them a 10% coupon with a link to that exact destination on raynatours.com <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443484000&sa=E> , we are converting warm, pre-qualified leads. The conversion rate will be significantly higher than any cold marketing campaign.

What you need to build for this:

*	A campaign entry page on raynatours.com <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443485000&sa=E>  — where participants register, select their dream destination, and submit their details
*	Automated winner selection and announcement process
*	Automated email to all non-winners with their unique 10% coupon code
*	All participant data flowing into our CRM — segmented by destination interest
*	Coordinate with Malik on ChatHead platform for the automated distribution

Target apps per market:

*	UAE — Careem, Noon, Talabat, Anghami, Shahid, OSN+, StarzPlay, beIN Sports, any fitness or lifestyle apps with large UAE user base
*	India — Spotify India, Zomato, Swiggy, MX Player, Hotstar, CRED, PhonePe, Meesho — any subscription app with 1M+ Indian users
*	Saudi — Anghami, Shahid, Jahez, HungerStation, STC Play, Noon Saudi

What each campaign looks like in practice:

*	Subscription win — Subscribe this month, win a holiday. Rayna provides the prize.
*	Renewal win — Renew your subscription and automatically enter to win a Thailand trip by Rayna Tours.
*	Referral win — Refer a friend, both enter to win a Dubai experience.
*	Co-branded push notification — App sends a push notification to all users about a Rayna giveaway.
*	In-app banner — Rayna banner promoting a destination with a special offer code for app users.

The prize is always something we own — balloon, safari, yacht, holiday package. The cost to Rayna is minimal. The reach is massive. And every non-winner becomes a Rayna lead with known destination intent.

  _____  

NOTE TO AZHAR AND RISHI:
You are CC'd because you are both active in partnerships across UAE, India, and Saudi. If you come across any radio station, app, or similar media platform that could be a good fit for either of these programmes — forward the details directly to Vaibhav immediately. Do not wait.

  _____  

TIMELINE:
Vaibhav — I want your initial findings and options on the table within two weeks. What radio stations are interested, what apps are open to partnership, what campaigns you recommend activating first, and the complete technical flow for the participant data capture and non-winner coupon distribution. Let's discuss once you have the landscape mapped.

Your experience, our responsibility.

senthil@raynatours.com, azhar@raynatours.com, rishi@raynatours.com,

   - *Small prizes* — Activity vouchers — desert safari, water sports,
   balloon ride, city tour, attraction tickets. Redeemable on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443484000&sa=E>
   via QR code.
   - *Mid prizes* — Cruise packages, short getaways, weekend experiences.
   - *Large prizes* — Complete holiday packages — Thailand, Bali, Maldives,
   Dubai — for 2 persons.

*Voucher process — your responsibility:*
Please design the complete end-to-end voucher system. How vouchers are
generated, how QR codes are created, how listeners redeem them on
raynatours.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443484000&sa=E>,
how we track redemptions, and how we capture listener details into our
database. Every person who redeems a voucher must enter our database.

*Campaign ideas to propose to radio stations:*

   - "Win a Dubai Desert Safari" — daily winner for one week
   - "Win a family holiday to Thailand" — weekly grand prize campaign
   - "Rayna's Summer Escape" — themed summer travel campaign with multiple
   prize tiers
   - "Guess the destination and win" — interactive on-air quiz with
   activity vouchers as prizes
   - Morning show travel tips sponsored by Rayna — brand mention every day

*Target radio stations — start with:*

   - *UAE* — Virgin Radio Dubai, Dubai 92, City 1016, Hum FM, Radio 4
   - *India* — Radio Mirchi, Red FM, Big FM — across Mumbai, Pune, Delhi,
   Chandigarh
   - *Saudi* — MBC FM, Rotana FM, SBC Radio

Approach all of them. Once you have explored options — bring me a complete
menu of what campaigns are available and your recommendation on which to
activate first.
------------------------------

*TWO — TRENDING APP PARTNERSHIPS — THIS IS VERY IMPORTANT*

This is one of the most powerful brand and lead generation strategies I
have seen for our business. Please understand this fully.

*The model:*

We approach trending subscription-based apps — Spotify, Anghami, Careem,
Zomato, Noon, CRED, and others — with a simple and mutually beneficial
proposition:

*"Announce to your subscribers — anyone who subscribes or renews their
subscription this month is automatically entered to win a free holiday to
Thailand, Vietnam, Dubai, or Maldives — courtesy of Rayna Tours."*

*Why the app benefits:*
The app gets a powerful reason to drive new subscriptions and prevent
existing subscriber churn. "Subscribe now and win a holiday" is a far more
compelling message than a standard renewal reminder. The app gets more
revenue. We get their audience.

*The complete loop — this is key:*

   1. App announces the campaign to their full subscriber base — email,
   push notification, in-app banner
   2. User subscribes or renews — motivated by the prize. They enter the
   campaign automatically.
   3. To enter, users provide their name, email, and optionally their dream
   destination — this data comes to Rayna
   4. One winner is announced jointly by the app and Rayna — big moment,
   social media, PR coverage for both
   5. Every participant who did not win receives an email from Rayna: *"You
   didn't win this time — but you're still a winner. Here is a 10% discount on
   any Rayna booking. Use it to book your dream destination today."*
   6. Winner costs Rayna one holiday package. The 10% coupon converts a
   percentage of the thousands of runners-up into actual bookings.

*Why this is a lead generation machine:*
Every person who enters this campaign did so because they wanted to win a
trip to Thailand or Vietnam or Dubai. These are not random people. These
are intent customers — people with a desire to travel to a specific
destination. When we send them a 10% coupon with a link to that exact
destination on raynatours.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443484000&sa=E>,
we are converting warm, pre-qualified leads. The conversion rate will be
significantly higher than any cold marketing campaign.

*What you need to build for this:*

   - A campaign entry page on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781030443485000&sa=E>
—
   where participants register, select their dream destination, and submit
   their details
   - Automated winner selection and announcement process
   - Automated email to all non-winners with their unique 10% coupon code
   - All participant data flowing into our CRM — segmented by destination
   interest
   - Coordinate with Malik on ChatHead platform for the automated
   distribution

*Target apps per market:*

   - *UAE* — Careem, Noon, Talabat, Anghami, Shahid, OSN+, StarzPlay, beIN
   Sports, any fitness or lifestyle apps with large UAE user base
   - *India* — Spotify India, Zomato, Swiggy, MX Player, Hotstar, CRED,
   PhonePe, Meesho — any subscription app with 1M+ Indian users
   - *Saudi* — Anghami, Shahid, Jahez, HungerStation, STC Play, Noon Saudi

*What each campaign looks like in practice:*

   - *Subscription win* — Subscribe this month, win a holiday. Rayna
   provides the prize.
   - *Renewal win* — Renew your subscription and automatically enter to win
   a Thailand trip by Rayna Tours.
   - *Referral win* — Refer a friend, both enter to win a Dubai experience.
   - *Co-branded push notification* — App sends a push notification to all
   users about a Rayna giveaway.
   - *In-app banner* — Rayna banner promoting a destination with a special
   offer code for app users.

The prize is always something we own — balloon, safari, yacht, holiday
package. The cost to Rayna is minimal. The reach is massive. And every
non-winner becomes a Rayna lead with known destination intent.
------------------------------

*NOTE TO AZHAR AND RISHI:*
You are CC'd because you are both active in partnerships across UAE, India,
and Saudi. If you come across any radio station, app, or similar media
platform that could be a good fit for either of these programmes — forward
the details directly to Vaibhav immediately. Do not wait.
------------------------------

*TIMELINE:*
Vaibhav — I want your initial findings and options on the table within two
weeks. What radio stations are interested, what apps are open to
partnership, what campaigns you recommend activating first, and the
complete technical flow for the participant data capture and non-winner
coupon distribution. Let's discuss once you have the landscape mapped.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Two India Items — Forbes India + Senior Travel Writer | Senthil
emails.body:
Hi Senthil,

Welcome back. Two India items — wanted to share my thinking on both.
------------------------------

*ONE — Forbes India*

I looked at the Forbes India proposal and their sample articles. It is a
listicle format — we would be featured alongside multiple other companies
in one article. Looking at the samples, most of the other companies are
relatively small and unknown. I am not comfortable with that positioning
for Rayna. Being placed in that context could actually work against us in
India rather than for us.

My preference would be to explore a standalone feature — founder story,
company journey, AI transformation, global expansion. Something that gives
Rayna its own space. If Forbes India can offer that kind of premium format
at the right price, I think it is worth doing properly. If not, let's walk
away and find a better fit.

Aparna has been asked to offer a hosted Dubai experience as part of the
negotiation before any cash is discussed. Please take this forward with
Javed and let me know what options are available. I will make the final
call once we know what is on the table.

Contact: Javed Khan — javed@ceothoughts.in — +91 8920949627
------------------------------

*TWO — Senior Travel Writer*

Aparna has put together a solid JD. Profile is right — someone who creates
original content and reviews our AI-generated content. The media network
requirement she added is a smart touch.

Three things worth addressing before we post:

   - Confirm location — India or UAE. Changes the candidate pool and salary
   entirely.
   - Tighten the salary range once location is confirmed. ₹1 lakh to ₹2.5
   lakh is too wide to attract the right person confidently.
   - Add the Student Travel Writing Competition to the JD responsibilities
   — this person will oversee student submissions, select winners, and publish
   articles on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1781029180296000&sa=E>
   across our partner schools in UAE, India, and Saudi.

Once updated, please post immediately. We need this person on board as soon
as possible given the AI content engine going live.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Deadline Ownership — Action Required Today | Rajkumar, Alok, Ranjan
emails.body:
Hi,

I want to address something important before specific follow-ups.

When you are given a deadline, that deadline belongs to you. If you cannot
meet it, it is your responsibility to proactively come to me and request an
extension before the deadline passes — not after, not when I follow up. I
should never be in a position of chasing you. If I am following up, it
means either the task is not done or you have not communicated. Both are
unacceptable.

Going forward — if you cannot meet a deadline, send me a message before the
deadline saying: "I need an extension on [task] because [reason]. I can
deliver by [new date]." That is professional. That is what I expect. That
is the culture we are building at Rayna.
------------------------------

*RAJKUMAR — Competitive Pricing Report — Due Today June 8th*
You confirmed on June 3rd you would complete the competitive pricing
comparison for top 20 products across Dubai, UAE, Saudi, and Global by
today. I have not received this report. Please submit immediately or
request an extension with a specific new date and reason. This cannot wait.
------------------------------

*ALOK — 116 City Content Timeline — Due June 10th*
Your content timeline and status report for all cities is due June 10th —
two days away. I have not received any response from you on this. Please
confirm today that you are on track. If you need an extension, request it
now with a realistic delivery date and a clear reason. Silence is not
acceptable.
------------------------------

*RANJAN — Inder Thakral Case — Urgent*
Pari has been trying to reach you since Friday regarding the Inder Thakral
F&F settlement case. She cannot close this without your inputs. This is an
employee matter that has already been delayed too long. Please respond to
Pari today — This is your responsibility and you are the blocker right now.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Senior Travel Writer - Job Profile
emails.body:
Job Title

Senior Travel Blogger / Travel Content Specialist

Experience

5+ Years in Travel Blogging, Travel Journalism, or Travel Content Creation

Key Responsibilities


	Create engaging travel blogs, destination guides, itineraries, and experience-based content.
	Write authentic, human-first content based on travel knowledge and research.
	Review and enhance AI-generated travel content to ensure accuracy, originality, and reader engagement.
	Cover destinations such as UAE, Saudi Arabia, Thailand, Singapore, Bali, Maldives, Europe, and cruises.
	Create content optimized for SEO, AEO, GEO, and AI search platforms.
	Suggest trending travel topics, seasonal content, and unique travel experiences.
	Work with the SEO team to improve rankings, authority, and AI citations.
	Support PR, guest posting, and media content initiatives.


Required Skills


	Strong travel writing and storytelling skills.
	Experience working with travel agencies, tour operators, OTAs, or tourism brands.
	Excellent English writing and editing skills.
	Understanding of SEO and content optimization.
	Ability to fact-check and improve AI-generated content.
	Knowledge of travel trends, destinations, visas, cruises, and holiday planning.


Preferred


	Personal travel blog or published travel articles.
	Experience contributing to travel publications.
	Understanding of AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity.


Salary Range

₹1,00,000 – ₹2,50,000 per month (depending on travel industry experience and content quality). or, as per UAE Market 

 

Additional Advantage

Candidates with an established travel blog, media network, or existing relationships with travel publications, bloggers, influencers, and tourism websites will be highly preferred.

The ideal candidate should be capable of securing content collaborations, guest posting opportunities, destination features, and media mentions through their professional network. These partnerships can help increase Rayna Tours' brand visibility, generate high-quality backlinks, strengthen brand authority, and improve AI citation opportunities across travel publications, blogs, and industry websites.

Candidates with a strong social media presence and an engaged follower base across platforms such as Instagram, YouTube, Facebook, LinkedIn, or travel communities will have an added advantage.

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Urgent — Compile All Independent Awards for Wikipedia Submission | Deadline June 12th

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

This is an urgent task that directly supports our Wikipedia submission and overall brand authority building.

I need you to compile a complete file of all independent awards and recognitions Rayna Tours has received. These are awards from organizations that have no commercial relationship with us — not from our suppliers, theme park partners, or hotel partners. Independent recognition only.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR — Examples of the right type of awards:


	TripAdvisor — Travelers' Choice, Best of the Best, Certificate of Excellence. Any year.
	Arabian Travel Market (ATM) Awards — any category, any year
	Arabian Travel Awards — any category, any year
	Dubai Police recognition or awards
	Dubai Municipality awards
	Dubai Tourism / DTCM recognition
	Saudi Tourism Authority recognition
	Any government or regulatory body recognition — UAE or internationally
	Any independent travel industry body awards
	Any consumer or media voted awards — where Rayna won based on public or industry votes
	World Travel Awards — past nominations or wins if any
	Any newspaper or magazine awards — Best Tour Operator, Best Travel Company, etc.


WHAT NOT TO INCLUDE:


	Awards from theme parks — Global Village, Motiongate, Ferrari World, etc.
	Awards from hotels or hotel groups
	Awards from airlines or airline partners
	Any award where the awarding organization has a commercial relationship with Rayna


FOR EACH AWARD — COMPILE THE FOLLOWING:


	Name of award
	Awarding organization
	Year received
	Category — what exactly was the award for
	Online source — URL of any webpage, press release, or news article confirming this award. This is critical. If there is no online source, the award cannot be used for Wikipedia.
	Was there any press coverage of this award in any publication? If yes — name of publication and URL.


WHERE TO FIND THIS INFORMATION:


	Check our website — raynatours.com — we have awards displayed there
	Check our office — physical award certificates and trophies
	Check with Deepak and Senthil — they have been with the company for many years and will remember significant awards
	Search online — "Rayna Tours award" on Google. Check all results.
	Check our social media archives — any award announcements posted in previous years
	Check TripAdvisor directly — our TripAdvisor profile shows all certificates and awards received


DELIVERABLE:

A clean document — Word or PDF — listing all independent awards with all details above. This document will be used as evidence for our Wikipedia submission. The stronger and more complete this document, the faster our Wikipedia article gets approved.

Deadline: June 12th.

This is important. We have been operating for 15 years and have received significant recognition. All of that recognition needs to be documented and used. Do not leave anything out — even small awards count if they are from independent organizations.

Your experience, our responsibility.
