Subject: World Travel Awards 2026 — Enter Four Categories Immediately | Action Required

Hi Aparna and Manish,

The World Travel Awards 2026 Call for Entries is now open. This is one of the most recognized awards programmes in the global travel industry — known as the Oscars of travel. Winners include Emirates, Atlantis, Qatar Airways, and Etihad. Being nominated or winning gives Rayna significant brand credibility, press coverage, and importantly — independent third party recognition that supports our Wikipedia notability case and PR strategy.

I want Rayna to enter this year. Here is exactly what to do.


STEP 1 — SUBMIT ENTRIES IMMEDIATELY

Go to worldtravelawards.com/enter and submit Rayna Tours for the following four categories only. Do not enter any other categories — we are concentrating our votes on these four to maximize our chances.


	Dubai's Leading Tour Operator — Our primary target. We are the largest ground operator in Dubai with owned desert camps, hot air balloons, marine operations, floating restaurants, and 500+ vehicles. 25 million+ guests served. No competitor in Dubai matches our owned ground operations.
	Dubai's Leading Transfer Company — 500+ vehicles operating daily across Dubai. We dominate this space.
	UAE's Leading Tour Operator — Natural extension of our Dubai position across the full UAE.
	Middle East's Leading Online Travel Company — Connects our AI transformation, B2C platform, and global expansion to 116 cities.


Submit all four entries today. Deadline is approaching — do not delay.


STEP 2 — PLAN THE VOTING CAMPAIGN

World Travel Awards is a voting-based award — the organization with the most votes wins. This is where Rayna has a massive advantage. We have:


	1.8 million customer database
	Hundreds of active B2B travel agents across UAE, India, and Saudi
	Corporate partners — banks, companies, universities
	100+ hotel desk contacts across UAE
	Our own team of 1,600 people


Once we receive our nomination confirmation, plan the following voting campaign:

Customer voting push — email + WhatsApp + push notification:

Send a dedicated campaign to our full UAE database asking customers to vote for Rayna in all four categories. Keep it simple — one click link to the voting page. Message: "You've experienced Rayna. Now vote for us. Help us become Dubai's Leading Tour Operator." Send twice — once when voting opens, once one week before voting closes.

B2B agent voting push:

Send a dedicated email to all active B2B agents on our portal asking them to vote. Travel agents and industry professionals carry extra weight in WTA voting. Personal appeal from the founder level will increase response rate.

Corporate partner push:

Ask Azhar to mobilize all corporate partners — ask HR departments to share the voting link with their employees as a quick way to support their travel partner.

Team voting:

All 1,600 Rayna staff must vote. Vaibhav to coordinate internally.

Social media campaign:

Nihad's team to run a social media voting campaign across UAE, India, and Saudi — Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn. "Vote for Rayna — Dubai's Leading Tour Operator." Include the voting link in bio and all posts for the duration of the voting period.


STEP 3 — USE FOR PR AND WIKIPEDIA

Whether we win or are nominated — both are valuable:


	Nomination announcement — send a press release to Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, and Economic Times immediately when nominations are confirmed
	Win announcement — major press release to all publications, social media campaign, email to full database
	Wikipedia — WTA nomination and win count as significant independent industry recognition. Add to Wikipedia evidence file immediately.



DEADLINES:


	Submit all four entries — Today or tomorrow at the latest
	Confirm entry submissions to me — June 9th
	Voting campaign plan ready — June 15th


This is a real opportunity to win four awards that will significantly boost Rayna's brand authority across UAE, India, and Saudi. Do not miss the entry window.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: RE: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi
emails.body:
Dear Mr. Tulsani,

 

Thank you for entrusting me with the Edutainment vertical. I firmly believe this segment presents a significant growth opportunity, as student travel enables us to engage not only with students but also with parents and households, creating long-term brand awareness and loyalty.

 

Below are my observations and action points:

 

Expansion Strategy – Saudi Arabia & India

 

*	The Job Descriptions for the Saudi Arabia and India Sales Manager positions will be shared with Ms. Pari by tomorrow to facilitate recruitment at the earliest.
*	Saudi Arabia presents a strong opportunity due to the limited number of operators exclusively focused on student travel. Apart from Elite Enrichment Services, competition remains relatively low, although competitors such as ITL World have also identified the market potential.
*	In India, our initial focus will be Maharashtra and North India. While there are established players in the market, Rayna's global network, destination expertise, and operational presence across multiple countries provide us with a clear competitive advantage.
*	In addition to international educational programmes, we see substantial potential in domestic experiential travel for India, including hill station excursions, trekking programmes, wildlife safaris, leadership camps, and educational tours.
*	Beyond Saudi Arabia and India, Oman and Qatar should also be considered as strategic markets for future expansion due to their growing education sectors and increasing demand for student-focused experiences.

 

School Engagement & App Download Strategy

 

*	The App Download and School Engagement Programme is an excellent initiative that aligns perfectly with our objective of increasing brand visibility and building direct relationships with families.
*	Schools continuously seek sponsorship support for annual events, sports days, graduation ceremonies, award functions, and parent engagement activities. This initiative provides an opportunity to expand our presence beyond existing partner schools and establish relationships with a much broader education network.
*	By integrating app downloads with school campaigns, competitions, awards, and sponsorship activities, we create a sustainable ecosystem that benefits all stakeholders:

*	Schools receive enhanced engagement and sponsorship support.
*	Students gain access to rewarding experiences and recognition programmes.
*	Parents become directly connected to the Rayna ecosystem.
*	Rayna gains long-term brand visibility and a scalable customer acquisition channel.

 

*	The proposed affiliate model will allow us to effectively track participation, measure engagement, and strengthen relationships with schools while simultaneously expanding our parent database.

 

Vision & Commitment

 

*	Our objective is to establish Rayna as the preferred partner for educational travel and experiential learning across the UAE, Saudi Arabia, India, and future expansion markets.
*	Through meaningful school partnerships, we can create access to students, parents, and communities simultaneously, generating long-term opportunities across educational travel, family holidays, and experiential programmes.
*	We are fully aligned with the proposed strategy and committed to delivering the outlined milestones within the agreed timelines.

 

I appreciate your guidance and support and look forward to building this vertical into a significant contributor to Rayna's long-term growth.

 

Warm regards,

Rishi Sethi

 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Sunday, June 7, 2026 5:48 PM
To: azhar@raynatours.com <mailto:azhar@raynatours.com> ; rishi@raynatours.com <mailto:rishi@raynatours.com> 
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com <mailto:senthil@raynatours.com> ; Vaibhav Gupta vaibhav@raynatours.com <mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com> ; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>; ranjan@raynab2b.com; nihad@raynatours.com; malik@raynatours.com
Subject: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi

 

Hi Azhar and Rishi,

This brief covers the Schools, Universities, and Edutainment segment. Azhar — you oversee this as part of your overall partnerships mandate. Rishi — you are heading the edutainment segment on the ground and this brief is primarily for you to execute. Both of you must read this fully and align.

We are expanding this segment from UAE to Saudi Arabia and India. Same playbook, dedicated people in each market, replicated under Rishi's leadership.

  _____  

WHAT EDUTAINMENT COVERS

*	Day trips — local school and university day excursions
*	Team building activities — desert safari, water sports, balloon, yacht, floating restaurant for school and university groups
*	Domestic overnight trips
*	Overseas educational trips — 116 destinations worldwide. Thailand, Singapore, Bali, London, Japan and more.

  _____  

RISHI — RECRUITMENT

1.	One Edutainment Manager for Saudi Arabia — based in Riyadh. Arabic speaking, strong school and university relationships.
2.	One Edutainment Manager for India — based in Pune. Strong school relationships in Maharashtra and North India.

Job descriptions to Pari by June 15th. Both hired by June 30th. Onboarded and trained by July 15th. First school visits in both markets by July 31st.

  _____  

HOW TO APPROACH SCHOOLS AND UNIVERSITIES

Decision maker is the Principal, Vice Principal for co-curricular activities, PTA chairperson, or student affairs department at universities.

At every visit:

1.	Show them who Rayna is — 15 years, 25 million guests, owned assets, 116 destinations worldwide
2.	Present the complete edutainment portfolio — day trips to full overseas educational tours
3.	Offer competitive group rates — work with Ranjan and destination heads for best group pricing
4.	Build annual relationships — schools renew every year. Become their permanent preferred operator.

As new destinations go live — immediately brief school contacts on new overseas trip options. Schools plan 6-12 months ahead. Get into their planning cycle early.

  _____  

APP DOWNLOAD — MANDATORY FOR ALL CAMPAIGNS

Every campaign, competition, award, and giveaway we run in schools must require the Rayna app download as the entry mechanism. No app download — no participation. This applies to students and parents equally.

This serves two purposes — we build our app user base from a completely new audience segment, and every participant enters our ecosystem where we can communicate with them permanently through push notifications and app-based campaigns.

Work with Malik to ensure the app registration flow connects directly to our giveaway and affiliate campaign platform. Students and parents who download the app must be able to see and participate in all live campaigns immediately.

  _____  

SCHOOL ENGAGEMENT PROGRAMMES — UNDER AFFILIATE PLATFORM

All school campaigns must be set up under our affiliate programme. Each school is registered as a Rayna affiliate. The school's dedicated affiliate link connects to our campaign platform where all active giveaways and competitions are visible. Teachers announce campaigns to students and parents. Students and parents download the app, register through the school's affiliate link, and participate. This way every participation is tracked back to the school, and schools earn recognition for driving engagement.

Programme 1 — Academic Achievement Award sponsored by Rayna
Rayna sponsors a monthly or termly "Best Student" or "Most Improved Student" award. Winning student and family receive a free Rayna experience — balloon, water sports, safari, or yacht. Announced publicly at assembly or parent teacher meeting.

To win — student must have the Rayna app downloaded and be registered. This drives app downloads among students and through them, their parents. Kids work harder knowing there is a prize. School gets better academic performance. We get permanent brand presence at almost zero cost — announced every term, every year.

Programme 2 — Parent Teacher Meeting Lucky Draw
Rayna sponsors a lucky draw at every PTM. Every attending parent who downloads the Rayna app and registers gets one entry. Winner announced at the end of the evening — free family balloon ride, yacht trip, or safari. Announced publicly in front of all parents.

Parents who skip PTMs now want to attend. School gets better parent engagement. Rayna gets in front of every parent in the school in one evening. Every attending parent is now in our app and database.

Programme 3 — End of Term Class Competition
Best class by attendance or grades wins a free Rayna group day trip. Every student in the class must have the Rayna app downloaded to qualify. Kids compete. Teachers motivated. Principal loves the results. 30-40 kids experience Rayna together and every parent hears about it.

Programme 4 — My Dream Holiday Competition
Drawing or essay competition — where does your family want to travel? Winners get a free family experience. Entry via the Rayna app only. Parent email and WhatsApp required. Every entry is a parent in our database.

  _____  

SCHOOL AWARD CEREMONIES — BRAND ACTIVATION

Every school holds an annual award ceremony — end of year, sports day, graduation. These are the moments when every student, parent, and teacher is in one room together. This is where Rayna must be present.

Approach every partner school to sponsor their annual award ceremony. What Rayna provides:

*	Branded screen sponsorship — a 15-20 second Rayna brand video plays on the main screen behind the stage during the ceremony. Every parent, student, and teacher sees it. This is premium brand visibility at a fraction of what a TV ad would cost.
*	Rayna popup stand at the venue — same configurations as our corporate roadshows. Staff present, app download QR codes, giveaway registration, family packages on display.
*	Sponsored giveaway prizes — Rayna announces free giveaway prizes at the ceremony. "Rayna Tours is giving one lucky family a free hot air balloon ride — scan this QR code to enter." Excitement fills the room. Every parent scans. Every parent downloads the app.
*	Co-branded event recognition — "This award ceremony is proudly sponsored by Rayna Tours — your experience, our responsibility." Announced by the principal. Printed on event materials. Posted on school social media.

The cost — a balloon ride, a safari, a water sports session. All owned by us. The return — brand presence in front of every parent, student, and teacher at every partner school, once a year minimum.

  _____  

REACHING PARENTS — DATABASE STRATEGY

Every school programme drives parent app downloads and database registrations:

*	Family Trip Card — every child takes home a Rayna card after every school trip. QR code for family discount. Parent downloads app to claim.
*	All competition entries — via app only. Parent WhatsApp and email required.
*	PTM lucky draw — app download required to enter.
*	Award ceremony QR codes — parent scans, downloads app, registers.
*	Achievement award announcements — sent to school WhatsApp groups with app download link.

Once a parent downloads the app and registers — they receive all our campaigns, holiday packages, visa offers, and giveaway campaigns automatically. One school relationship generates hundreds of parent contacts directly into our ecosystem.

  _____  

OVERSEAS EDUCATIONAL TRIPS

116 cities worldwide. Thailand, Singapore, Japan, London, Europe, Americas — all Rayna coordinated end to end. Flights, hotels, activities, transfers, visa assistance, local guide. No other edutainment operator in UAE can offer this range. As each destination goes live — build a school trip pipeline immediately.

  _____  

TARGET INSTITUTIONS

UAE: GEMS, Taaleem, Axiom, ADNOC schools, government schools, American University of Sharjah, University of Dubai, Khalifa University, Heriot-Watt Dubai, Middlesex University Dubai

Saudi: International schools Riyadh and Jeddah, SABIS, Saudi Aramco schools, King Abdulaziz University, King Saud University, KAUST

India: CBSE and ICSE schools in Pune, Mumbai, Delhi, Chandigarh. Engineering and MBA colleges Pune tech corridor. Delhi University colleges.

  _____  

TRACKING
Update the Google Sheet after every school visit, campaign, and ceremony activation.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI&source=gmail&ust=1780926315063000&sa=E> 

  _____  

DEADLINES

*	June 15th — Job descriptions for Saudi and India Edutainment Managers to Pari
*	June 30th — Both managers hired
*	July 15th — Both managers onboarded and trained
*	July 31st — First school visits in Saudi and India completed
*	August — All parent campaigns and school engagement programmes live in all three markets
*	September — Academic achievement awards, PTM lucky draws, and award ceremony sponsorships running in all partner schools

Rishi — schools give us access to students, parents, and the community simultaneously. Three revenue streams from one relationship — edutainment trips, parent holidays, community referrals. The engagement programmes above cost us almost nothing but create emotional connections no advertisement can replicate. Build this properly and it compounds every year.

Azhar — please ensure Rishi has everything he needs and that Saudi and India recruitment happens on schedule.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: RE: Urgent — Competitive Price Review | Action Required This Week
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

 

Thank you for highlighting this critical pricing concern.

 

We have already completed a comprehensive benchmarking exercise covering approximately 175 key products across our major operating destinations, including Phuket, Krabi, Pattaya, Bangkok, Koh Samui, Singapore, Bali, Vietnam, the Philippines, Langkawi, and Kuala Lumpur. Based on the findings, we have updated the costing wherever immediate price corrections were required. The competitive pricing sheet prepared by the team is attached for your review. 



As per Indian Market - https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1suW1t0i9aHSocRTWr2k3pGJAg_LrVYLoP-B102fu4cI/edit?gid=559330770#gid=559330770

 

For products where our pricing remains higher than that of competitors, we are actively engaging with suppliers, attraction partners, and local operators to renegotiate rates and improve our buying costs. Our objective is to ensure that our attraction-only ticket pricing remains competitive and aligned with market expectations, particularly for products where the customer experience is delivered directly by the attraction.

 

In parallel, we have also initiated a separate benchmarking exercise for the UAE and Saudi Arabia markets. This analysis is currently underway and is being conducted in a structured manner, taking into consideration the pricing variations across India, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and other global source markets.

 

As per UAE & Saudi Market - https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ClFanqQKEZSp7B1izrQxoJuZWatafZk_sXE6oWBrYY0/edit?gid=1929408478#gid=1929408478

 

We will continue to update the benchmarking sheet as discussions with suppliers progress and revised rates are secured. Our focus remains on strengthening our market competitiveness while maintaining sustainable margins across all destinations.

 

Thank you for your guidance and support.

 

Ranjan Singh | Country Head – India

Office: +91 20 6683 8800| Mobile: +91 9315253294

 <mailto:ranjan@raynatours.com> ranjan@raynatours.com  |  <http://www.raynatours.com/> www.raynatours.com 

DMC | UAE | Singapore | Thailand |Cruises

India Office | Mitcon Lane Balewadi | Maharashtra | Pune- 411045

Offices | UAE | Singapore | Thailand | Oman | UK | Switzerland 

 

From: Manoj Tulsani [mailto:manoj@raynatours.com] 
Sent: Wednesday, June 3, 2026 12:11 PM
To: ranjan@raynab2b.com; rajkumar@raynatours.com
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: Urgent — Competitive Price Review | Action Required This Week

 

Hi Ranjan and Rajkumar,

I have reviewed our pricing across key destinations and we have a serious problem. Our prices are higher than competition on several products — especially attraction tickets. This must be fixed immediately before we begin any marketing push into India, UAE, and Saudi.

The Problem:
For attraction-only tickets, the experience is delivered by the attraction — not by Rayna. The customer has no reason to pay more for the same ticket from us. We are losing bookings purely on price. This is not acceptable.

IMMEDIATE ACTION — Manual Price Review (Complete by June 8th):

Both of you must submit a competitive price comparison report for your top 20 products by June 8th. Format is simple:

1.	Product name
2.	Our current selling price (adult and child)
3.	Top 3 competitor prices for the same product online — MakeMyTrip, Viator, GetYourGuide, or whoever is most relevant for that destination
4.	Are our inclusions the same, better, or different? Note any differences.
5.	Recommended action — reduce markup, match competition, or justify premium with inclusions

Ranjan — destinations to cover: Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, and India as a destination (products promoted to UAE, Saudi, and global travelers coming to India).

Rajkumar — destinations to cover: Dubai (all attractions, holidays, tours, transfers) and International Visas across all markets — India, UAE, Saudi, and Global.

Important — how to check competition per market:
Each destination must be checked across all four source markets separately — India, UAE, Saudi, and Global. A product may be competitively priced for a UAE customer but expensive for an Indian customer because local OTAs in India are selling the same product cheaper. Check competition from each market's perspective.

Pricing Rules — Effective Immediately:

1.	Attraction tickets only — our price must always be at or below the lowest competitor. Target 1% below the cheapest. No exceptions. Reduce markup accordingly.
2.	Tours with shared transfers — competitive. We have a cost advantage with our own fleet. Use it.
3.	Tours with inclusions — match competition minimum. If our inclusions are better, communicate them clearly on the product page. Do not assume the customer will notice.
4.	Holiday packages — competitive pricing with clear inclusion advantage. This is where we can justify our expertise.
5.	Visas (Rajkumar) — check competition from all three core markets. Visa pricing must be competitive across India, UAE, and Saudi independently.

Important:
Do not wait for the report to start fixing obvious pricing gaps. If you already know a product is overpriced compared to competition — fix the markup today. The report is to ensure nothing is missed.

We are also building an AI-powered dynamic pricing tool that will automate competitor price monitoring and markup adjustment going forward. But that takes time to build. The manual fix must happen now.

Report due: June 8th. No extensions.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: RE: Tourism Board Outreach — All 137 Cities | Senthil + Deepak Co-Own
emails.cc:
senthil@raynatours.com, vaibhav@raynatours.com, ranjan@raynab2b.com, 
	manoj.gagwani@raynatours.com, rajkumar@raynatours.com, alok@raynatours.com,

emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

Thank you for assigning this responsibility to Senthil and me.

I acknowledge the task and will co-own the Tourism Board outreach across
all 137 cities. Senthil and I will connect to align on the approach and
division of responsibilities, and will start preparations as per the
specified timelines. Kindly provide access to the sheet for review.

Best regards,
Deepak

On Sun, Jun 07, 2026 08:10 PM, Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:

> Hi Senthil and Deepak,
>
> I am assigning both of you joint ownership of our tourism board outreach
> across all 137 cities we are activating. This is a strategic initiative
> that requires both seniority and deep destination knowledge — which is
> exactly why I want both of you on it together.
>
> Senthil — you lead the senior-level conversations with tourism board
> directors and marketing heads. Your seniority and understanding of Rayna's
> business opens doors.
>
> Deepak — you bring the destination and product depth. You know what we
> need from each tourism board, what content gaps we have, and what each

>
> Together you cover everything this initiative needs. Ranjan, Manoj
> Gagwani, Sunil Pawar, and Rajkumar are CC'd — as destination heads they

> ------------------------------
>
> *WHY THIS IS IMPORTANT*
>
> Tourism boards exist for one reason — to bring more visitors to their
> destination. When Rayna tells a tourism board that we are actively
> promoting their city to millions of customers across India, UAE, and Saudi
> Arabia — they will want to support us. This is not a favour we are asking.

>

> York, Los Angeles, Goa

>

>
> *TOURISM BOARD TRACKER — ALL 137 CITIES*
>

>

> <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E>*

>
> *DEADLINES*

> + Deepak June 30th

> + Deepak July 31st

>

>

emails.subject: RE: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

 

Further to the below, with regards to Point #4, I have been trying to reach Ranjan (HOD) since Friday to obtain his inputs and final updates on this case, in order to send a detailed response to the staff.

 

However, he has informed me that he is currently out of office and will only be available from Friday onwards.

 

I will revert to you with a detailed email to the staff once I have received the necessary inputs from him.

 

Thank you for your understanding.

 

Thanks & Regards,

 



 

From: Pari <pari@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Friday, June 5, 2026 12:55 PM
To: 'Manoj Tulsani' <manoj@raynatours.com>
Cc: 'senthil@raynatours.com' <senthil@raynatours.com>; 'Vaibhav Gupta' <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Subject: RE: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered

 

Dear Sir,

 

Good Afternoon. 

 

With regard to Inder Thakral's case, I would like to provide some background from the HR side.

 

After his exit, the HR team in India initiated the exit formalities and followed up with his dept (B2B India / Ranjan) for the required clearance and NOC to process his Full & Final Settlement. However, the required NOC from HOD was not received, and the matter was subsequently escalated.

 

Upon receiving Inder's follow-up emails, I again checked with the concerned Ranjan, who advised that the settlement should be kept on hold and that he would handle the matter directly from his side.

As per the process, HR cannot proceed with a Full & Final Settlement without the final clearance and NOC from the concerned HOD. Since the required approval was not provided, the settlement could not be processed from our end. (Please refer to attached mail)

 

I would also like to clarify that HR does not have the authority to direct HODs to issue clearances or approve settlements. We follow up, facilitate the process, and can only proceed once the necessary approvals are received.

 

While HR has been responding to employee complaints and queries on a regular basis, in this particular case no further response was sent as the matter had been taken over by the HOD for direct handling.

Going forward, we will ensure that all such employee emails receive a formal response from HR, even if the matter is pending with another department, so that employees remain informed of the status of their case.

 

We will also review all pending employee complaints and settlement cases and share a status report by 10 June, as requested.

 

Thanks & Regards,

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com <mailto:manoj@raynatours.com> > 
Sent: Friday, June 5, 2026 12:02 PM
To: Pari <pari@raynatours.com <mailto:pari@raynatours.com> >; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com <mailto:vaibhav@raynatours.com> >
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com <mailto:senthil@raynatours.com> 
Subject: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered

 

Hi Pari and Vaibhav,

I want to address something that I feel very strongly about.

I have seen the email thread from Inder Thakral regarding his Full & Final settlement. This person has been following up for more than 8 months with no proper response from our side. He has now CC'd the Maharashtra Labour Commissioner, Ministry of External Affairs, and MOHRE in his emails. This situation has reached this point only because our team did not respond.

I want to make this absolutely clear — no employee email should ever go unanswered.

If we are holding someone's settlement because they did something wrong, that is our right. But we must communicate that in writing clearly and immediately. We must state exactly what the employee did wrong, what the consequences are, and what our position is. We have the proofs. We have the records. Use them. Put it in writing. Send it.

Silence is not a strategy. Silence makes us look guilty even when we are right. And when employees start CC'ing government authorities, it creates legal and reputational risk for Rayna that could have been completely avoided with one proper email response months ago.

Effective immediately — I expect the following:

1.	Every employee complaint or query must receive a written response within 48 hours — no exceptions
2.	If a settlement is being withheld for any reason, the employee must be informed in writing with the exact reason and supporting evidence
3.	No employee email — whether from current or ex-employees — should remain unanswered for more than 48 hours
4.	For this specific case — Inder Thakral — I want a full written response sent to him today explaining Rayna's position clearly. CC me and Senthil on that email.

Pari — please also review if there are any other pending employee complaints or settlement cases that have not been responded to. Share a status report with me by June 10th.

This is not negotiable. We are a company of 1,600 people. Our reputation with employees matters as much as our reputation with customers.

Thanks,
Manoj

Ranjan Singh | Country Head – India

Offices | UAE | Singapore | Thailand | Oman | UK | Switzerland 

 

 

From: Manoj Tulsani [mailto:manoj@raynatours.com] 
Sent: Sunday, June 7, 2026 9:40 PM
To: senthil@raynatours.com; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>
Cc: Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>; ranjan@raynab2b.com; manoj.gagwani@raynatours.com; sunil.p@raynab2b.com; rajkumar@raynatours.com; alok@raynatours.com
Subject: Tourism Board Outreach — All 137 Cities | Senthil + Deepak Co-Own

 

Hi Senthil and Deepak,

I am assigning both of you joint ownership of our tourism board outreach across all 137 cities we are activating. This is a strategic initiative that requires both seniority and deep destination knowledge — which is exactly why I want both of you on it together.

Senthil — you lead the senior-level conversations with tourism board directors and marketing heads. Your seniority and understanding of Rayna's business opens doors.

Deepak — you bring the destination and product depth. You know what we need from each tourism board, what content gaps we have, and what each destination requires to go live successfully. Your knowledge makes every conversation specific and credible.

Together you cover everything this initiative needs. Ranjan, Manoj Gagwani, Sunil Pawar, and Rajkumar are CC'd — as destination heads they will own city-specific follow-ups under your joint coordination.

  _____  

WHY THIS IS IMPORTANT

Tourism boards exist for one reason — to bring more visitors to their destination. When Rayna tells a tourism board that we are actively promoting their city to millions of customers across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia — they will want to support us. This is not a favour we are asking. This is a partnership that benefits them as much as it benefits us.

We have:

*	1.8 million customers across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia
*	Active marketing campaigns launching across three source markets
*	137 cities being activated simultaneously
*	AI-driven content engine producing destination content at scale
*	B2C platform, app, email marketing, WhatsApp broadcasting, influencer network

Every tourism board we approach should see Rayna as one of the most valuable distribution partners they can have in their source markets. We are bringing their destination to millions of potential visitors. In return we need their support.

  _____  

WHAT TO ASK FROM EVERY TOURISM BOARD

1.	Hosted FAM trips — Familiarization trips for Rayna's destination heads and product managers. A destination head who has visited Bangkok will sell Bangkok infinitely better than one who hasn't. Ask the tourism board to host and fund FAM trips for our team.
2.	Marketing co-funding — Many tourism boards have co-op marketing funds for trade partners who promote their destination. Ask if they contribute towards our advertising spend — Meta ads, Google campaigns, email marketing, influencer programme for that destination.
3.	Content — images, videos, destination guides — Every tourism board has a library of professional destination photography, videography, and written guides. Ask for full access to use across our website, app, social media, and marketing campaigns. This accelerates our Block 1 content engine significantly.
4.	Articles and editorial content — Ask if they can write or co-write destination content for publication on raynatours.com <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E> .
5.	Press and media support — Ask if they can facilitate introductions to local journalists and travel publications who can cover Rayna's launch in their destination.
6.	Trade shows and events — Ask to be included as a partner at any trade events they attend in India, UAE, or Saudi.
7.	Preferred partner status — Ask to be formally recognized as a preferred or recommended tour operator for their destination.
8.	White label technology partnership — This is our unique offer that no other operator can match. Some tourism boards need technology to promote their destination's holiday packages, activities, excursions, and events to travel agents and consumers. We can provide our white label technology platform — they brand it as their own, we power it behind the scenes. In return they give us preferred partner status, marketing funding, content access, and FAM trips. Bring this up with every tourism board where you sense a technology gap. Coordinate with Anwar when any tourism board expresses interest — he will handle the technical proposal.

  _____  

THE MARKETING PRESENTATION

Before any outreach begins, we need a professional Rayna partner presentation. Coordinate with Asad to design this — it must look premium. Deadline: June 20th.

Cover: who Rayna is, our source markets, our marketing reach, our September campaign, our destination commitment, our white label technology offer, and what we are asking in return.

  _____  

PRIORITY APPROACH

🔴 HIGH — Approach immediately:
Bangkok, Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Bali, Ho Chi Minh City, Tokyo, Seoul, Maldives, Mauritius, Dubai DTCM, Abu Dhabi DCT, Riyadh, Jeddah, Cairo, Muscat, London, Paris, Zurich, Amsterdam, Rome, Barcelona, Istanbul, New York, Los Angeles, Goa

🟡 MEDIUM — Approach July-August

🟢 GROWTH — Approach September onwards

  _____  

TOURISM BOARD TRACKER — ALL 137 CITIES

I have created a complete Google Sheet with all 137 cities pre-populated — tourism board names, official websites, trade partnership emails, priority levels, and notes on which boards have Dubai offices.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0 <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E> 

Update after every interaction. I check this sheet every Monday.

  _____  

DEADLINES


Task

Owner

Deadline


Partner presentation designed and ready

Senthil + Deepak + Asad

June 20th


First outreach wave — top 25 High Priority cities

Senthil + Deepak

June 25th


All destination heads briefed and doing outreach for their cities

Senthil + Deepak

June 30th


First FAM trip scheduled

Deepak + Ranjan

July 31st


Content received from first 15 tourism boards

All destination heads

July 31st


White label technology conversations initiated with 5 tourism boards

Senthil + Deepak

July 31st


All 137 cities contacted

All destination heads

August 31st

  _____  

Tourism boards have content, funding, media relationships, and technology needs that align perfectly with what Rayna can offer. Our white label technology is something very few operators can bring to the table — use it as our strongest negotiating point. Start immediately upon return Monday.

Your experience, our responsibility.

	nihad@raynatours.com, anket@raynatours.com, alok@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Manoj,

Thanks for the comprehensive brief. Sharing my workstream-wise status and
action points below.

---

WORKSTREAM 1 — CAMPAIGN DISTRIBUTION

Email campaigns — AI-planned campaign creation is live. The pending item
from our side is template management: specifically, for every template, we
need to define (a) what the dynamic fields are, and (b) how each field is
fetched — whether directly from customer data (e.g. customer name, booking
history) or from the product feed via the AI. I'll document this mapping
and share with Malik for alignment.

WhatsApp, SMS, Push Notifications — Work in progress. Will share timelines
shortly.

Seed List — Already in place. We are working on adding the AI user to the
seed list today. Expecting the AI approver to be fully set up within the
next 2 days.

Co-branded & Daily Campaigns — The trigger logic (corporate / reward
programme / affiliate) needs to be architected. Will align with Anowar and
Gaurav's team to define how these triggers get pulled from the database.
@Malik - Please see if we already have these data points.

---

WORKSTREAM 2 — AD AUTOMATION

Malik and Anket are evaluating the available MCP servers and API connectors
— Meta Ads MCP (official, live) and Google Ads API — to map what features
are actually accessible vs. what needs custom build. Once we have that
assessment, we'll share the gap analysis and what needs to be built
in-house.

On campaign logic @Anket - prepare the product-wise rules covering:
- What to optimize for (CAC vs CPS per product category)
- Audience segmentation logic (India / UAE / Saudi)
- Budget allocation rules
- Creative testing parameters

This will cover Visa, Cruises, Holidays, Attractions, and Transfers as
separate campaign structures.

---

WORKSTREAM 3 — SOCIAL MEDIA AGENT

Malik — The base code for the social posting agent is largely done, with a
few bugs to fix. I'll share the code with you today. The content generation
engine is already built as part of our promotions tool. WhatsApp templates
and social templates follow the same structure — I'll share both codebases
together.

Gaurav — Affiliate social auto-posting confirmed on roadmap (per your
reply). Templates are the missing piece — I'll walk you through the
structure. We can also reuse the WhatsApp template code as a base if that's
easier.

---

NEXT STEPS (My side)

1. Template dynamic field mapping doc — sharing with Malik
2. Co-branded campaign trigger spec — aligning with Anwar/Gaurav
3. Block 3 code handoff to Malik — today
4. Ad automation MCP/API assessment — Malik + Anket to complete (ETA: 2-3
days)
5. Campaign logic document (product-wise CAC/CPS rules) — I'll prepare and
share

Will keep this thread updated as things move.

Regards,
Vaibhav

>>

>>
>> *WORKSTREAM 2 — MADGICX INTEGRATION — GOOGLE ADS AND META ADS*

>>

>>

>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *WORKSTREAM 3 — SOCIAL MEDIA AUTOMATION AGENT*

>>

>>    - Content types per platform — reels for Instagram and TikTok, long
>>    form for YouTube, professional for LinkedIn
>>    - Posting frequency per platform
>>    - Languages — English, Arabic, Hindi versions per market
>>    - Offer codes and campaign links
>>
>> Nihad — you are CC'd on this. The social media automation agent does not
>> replace your role. Your role becomes defining the strategy, rules, and
>> creative direction that the AI follows. You are the brain. The AI is the
>> execution. Work with Vaibhav and Malik to define the exact rules and
>> content guidelines the agent must follow.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *ADDITIONAL FEATURE — AFFILIATE SOCIAL MEDIA AUTO-POSTING*
>> *Owner: Vaibhav (manages affiliate programme) + Gaurav (builds the
>> feature)*
>>
>> This is one of the most powerful features we can add to our affiliate
>> programme. Here is how it works:
>>
>> Any affiliate who wants to — connects their social media handles to the
>> Rayna affiliate platform. Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube — any
>> platform they are active on. Once connected, the same AI agent that posts
>> to Rayna's own accounts automatically creates and posts Rayna content to
>> the affiliate's accounts as well.
>>
>> *What the affiliate gets:*
>>
>>    - They connect their account once — AI does everything from that point
>>    - AI creates content relevant to their audience and their niche
>>    - AI posts automatically on an optimal schedule
>>    - Their unique discount code and affiliate link is embedded in every
>>    post
>>    - When their followers click and book — they earn commission
>>    automatically
>>    - Zero effort required after the initial connection
>>
>> *Why this is a game changer:*
>> The biggest reason affiliates sign up and never post is because creating
>> content feels like work. With this feature — they connect once, AI posts
>> for them forever, commission keeps coming in passively. This will
>> dramatically increase affiliate activation rates and the volume of Rayna
>> content reaching new audiences every day.
>>
>> Gaurav — you are CC'd. This feature needs to be built into the Rayna
>> affiliate platform. It uses the same AI content generation engine as
>> Workstream 3 — the affiliate version personalizes content for each
>> affiliate's audience and embeds their unique discount code and link
>> automatically. Please confirm this is added to your development roadmap and
>> share a build timeline.
>>
>> Nihad — this feature will be your strongest recruitment pitch to
>> influencers. "Connect your account once. We create the content. We post it.
>> You earn commission automatically." Use this in your outreach immediately
>> once the feature is live.
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *WHAT I NEED FROM YOU — THIS WEEK*
>>
>>    1. *Vaibhav + Malik* — Complete deadline for Workstream 1 — email,
>>    WhatsApp, SMS, push notifications. Fully live and operational by when?
>>    2. *Anket + Malik* — Madgicx assessment and go-live date — June 15th
>>    deadline stands
>>    3. *Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad* — Initial plan for Workstream 3 — social
>>    media automation agent. What platforms first? What is the build timeline?
>>    What rules and content guidelines does Nihad need to define?
>>    4. *Gaurav* — Confirm affiliate social auto-posting feature is added
>>    to development roadmap. By when can this be live?
>>
>> Block 3 is the engine that connects everything — our products, our
>> destinations, our offers — to our customers and affiliates across every
>> channel simultaneously. Get this right and our September campaign launch
>> will be unlike anything Rayna has done before.
>>
>> *Your experience, our responsibility.*
>>
>

emails.subject: RE: You Are Now Head of Partnerships — UAE, Saudi Arabia & India | Azhar
emails.body:
Dear Mr. Manoj,

 

Thank you for your trust and confidence in expanding my role as Head of Partnerships — UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India.

 

I acknowledge and accept this expanded responsibility with immediate effect.

 

I understand the importance of this mandate and the scale of opportunity across all three markets. As discussed, earlier UAE will continue to remain our model market, while Saudi Arabia and India will be developed with the same structure, discipline, and execution approach adapted wherever required to suit the local language, culture, product focus, and corporate environment.

 

I am fully aligned with the partnership playbook covering corporate partnerships, roadshows, affiliate programme, app promotion, giveaway campaigns, daily email forwarding, and channel acquisition. I will ensure the same is implemented across UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India with proper ownership, tracking, and weekly reporting.

 

As immediate next steps, I will proceed with the following:

*	Coordinate with Pari and Senthil to initiate recruitment for the Saudi and India Partnership Managers.
*	Submit both JDs to Pari by 15 June.
*	Work with Asad to update the UAE popup stand materials with the new brand line and product portfolio.
*	Prepare and submit the July UAE roadshow calendar by 20 June, targeting a minimum of 8 events.
*	Ensure both Partnership Managers are identified and offer letters are issued by 30 June.
*	Plan their onboarding, training, and market execution so they are operational by 15 July.
*	Target first roadshows in Saudi and India by 31 July.

 

Once the team is onboarded, I will personally ensure they are trained on the UAE model, product knowledge, roadshow mechanics, affiliate structure, reporting format, and corporate engagement approach. Wherever required, I will also work with the concerned teams to support market visits, partner introductions, and initial execution until the model is properly stabilized in each market.

 

I also understand the importance of using the full Rayna support ecosystem to make this a coordinated and scalable channel across all three countries. This is a significant responsibility, and I truly appreciate the trust you have placed in me. I will make sure this is executed with seriousness, speed, and clear accountability.

 

The corporate partnership channel has strong potential to become one of Rayna’s most powerful growth engines, and I will work towards building it into a structured, scalable, and measurable business vertical across UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India.

 

Thanks & Best Regards,

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Sunday, June 7, 2026 4:57 PM
To: azhar@raynatours.com
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>; nihad@raynatours.com; malik@raynatours.com; anket@raynatours.com; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>
Subject: You Are Now Head of Partnerships — UAE, Saudi Arabia & India | Azhar

 

Hi Azhar,

I am writing to formally expand your role and responsibility. Effective immediately, you are appointed as Head of Partnerships — UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India.

Everything we have discussed about corporate partnerships, roadshows, affiliate programme, app promotion, giveaway campaigns, and daily email forwarding — all of it applies across all three markets. Your job is to build, lead, and replicate this across UAE, Saudi, and India simultaneously.

Please read the complete brief sent to you separately covering the full partnership strategy. That is your playbook. Everything in it applies to all three markets.

  _____  

YOUR EXPANDED MANDATE

UAE — you are already active here. Continue and accelerate everything as briefed. UAE is your model market. Everything that works here gets replicated in Saudi and India.

Saudi Arabia — significant opportunity. Rich market, less competitive, strong corporate culture. The approach is identical to UAE. Language may need to be adapted to Arabic for some corporates but the strategy, the products, the affiliate model, the roadshow mechanics — all the same.

India — starting with Pune and North India clusters. Our Pune office already has presence. The corporate culture in Pune tech corridor and Mumbai financial sector is strong. Delhi, Chandigarh, and Ludhiana are high spending markets. Same approach — roadshows, affiliate programme, daily email forwarding, app promotion, giveaway campaigns.

  _____  

YOUR FIRST PRIORITY — RECRUIT TWO PEOPLE

You cannot manage three markets alone. I need you to recruit immediately:

1.	One Partnership Manager for Saudi Arabia — based in Riyadh. Arabic speaking, strong corporate network in Saudi, understands the local corporate culture. This person replicates your UAE approach in Saudi.
2.	One Partnership Manager for India — based in Pune. Strong corporate network in Maharashtra and North India. Understands Indian corporate culture — especially IT sector in Pune and financial sector in Mumbai. This person replicates your UAE approach in India.

Recruitment deadline: June 30th.
I need both people identified, interviewed, and offer letters issued by June 30th. They should be onboarded and operational by July 15th.

Work with Pari and Senthil on the recruitment process. Share the job descriptions with Pari by June 15th so the search begins immediately.

  _____  

TRAINING AND ONBOARDING

Once both people are onboarded, you are responsible for training them fully. Options:

*	Bring them to Dubai for one week — shadow you at roadshows, see the complete operation, understand the products, meet the teams. This is the fastest way to train.
*	Visit their markets with them for the first few roadshows — introduce them to key corporate contacts, demonstrate the approach, then hand over.
*	Regular weekly calls — align on campaigns, offers, new products, and performance.

I will personally join a market visit to Saudi and India when the timing is right to meet key corporates and demonstrate Rayna's commitment to those markets.

  _____  

WHAT STAYS THE SAME ACROSS ALL THREE MARKETS

*	Roadshow mechanics — spin the wheel, giveaway registration, app download, vouchers
*	Affiliate programme — commission and non-commission two-track approach
*	Daily email campaign forwarding to corporate contacts
*	Channel acquisition objective — secure direct communication into every corporate
*	App promotion — once live
*	Giveaway campaign integration — once live
*	Reporting structure — weekly report to Vaibhav

What adapts per market:

*	Saudi — Arabic language for certain corporates and communications
*	India — Hindi language for certain communications, local cultural nuances in approach
*	Product emphasis — in India, international holiday packages and visas are the lead product. In Saudi, outbound holiday packages and UAE experiences are the lead product.

  _____  

IMMEDIATE ACTIONS — THIS WEEK

1.	Today — Acknowledge this appointment and confirm you accept the expanded responsibility
2.	June 15th — Job descriptions for Saudi and India Partnership Managers submitted to Pari
3.	June 15th — All UAE popup stand materials updated with new brand line and product portfolio — coordinate with Asad
4.	June 20th — July UAE roadshow calendar submitted — minimum 8 events
5.	June 30th — Both new Partnership Managers identified and offer letters issued
6.	July 15th — Both new managers onboarded and operational
7.	July 31st — First roadshows completed in Saudi and India by new managers

  _____  

RESOURCES AND SUPPORT

You will have full support from the Rayna team across all three markets:

*	Vaibhav — marketing campaigns, co-branded email content, giveaway and affiliate platform
*	Malik — WhatsApp broadcasting, JetHeads platform, affiliate tracking
*	Anket — performance marketing alignment
*	Nihad — social media support for corporate activations
*	Asad — updated stand designs and marketing materials
*	Ranjan — India destination products and packages
*	Rajkumar — Saudi and Dubai products

Azhar — this is a significant expansion of your role and I am giving it to you because I trust your ability to execute. The corporate partnership channel is one of Rayna's most powerful growth levers. You now own it across three of our most important markets. Make it count.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: RE: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi
emails.body:
Dear Mr. Manoj,

Trust you are doing well.

 

I have gone through all four emails, fully aligned with Rishi on the overall direction and execution plan.

 

From my side, I will ensure Rishi has the required support to execute this properly across UAE, Saudi Arabia, and India. I will also make sure the Saudi and India hiring moves as per the agreed timelines.

 

For the school engagement side, I will personally position the Writing Competition and Rayna Inspire at the principal level as flagship Rayna brand programmes, rather than just regular school activities. Before we commit any scholarship value to schools, I will first align and lock the budget with Mr. Senthil.

 

Rishi will be responsible for the complete ground execution across UAE, Saudi, and India, including school visits, all four engagement programmes, the Writing Competition, and Rayna Inspire. Nihad’s team will be involved in every Rayna Inspire event, and the tracking sheet will be updated after every activity to keep the progress clear and measurable.

 

Action Plan

*	Rishi will share recruitment JD with Pari by 15 June for the Saudi and India Edutainment Managers.
*	Saudi manager to be based in Riyadh, Arabic-speaking, with strong school and university relationships.
*	India manager to be based in Pune, with strong school networks across Maharashtra and North India.
*	Both managers to be hired by 30 June, trained by 15 July, and ready for first school visits by 31 July.
*	All four school engagement programmes will run through the affiliate platform.
*	Rayna app download will remain mandatory for every entry point, with no exceptions.
*	Malik to be briefed this week to align the app registration and campaign flow.
*	Phase 1 of the Writing Competition to be launched in 2–3 UAE pilot schools in July.
*	Monthly visibility to be created by publishing the winner along with 3–5 supporting articles per school.
*	Scholarship commitment will only be shared with schools once the budget is confirmed with Mr. Senthil.
*	First Rayna Inspire experience to be offered to the Phase 1 writing competition winner in July or August, with full school approval and parental consent in place.

 

Timeline

*	15 June: JDs to Pari
*	30 June: Saudi and India managers hired
*	15 July: Managers onboarded and trained
*	31 July: First school visits completed in Saudi and India
*	August: All programmes live across UAE, Saudi, and India; Phase 1 competition and first Rayna Inspire experience launched
*	September: Awards, PTMs, and ceremonies running; competition to expand across Dubai schools
*	Q4 2026: UAE-wide expansion and Rayna-hosted annual winner event
*	2027: International expansion across UAE, Saudi, and India, with scholarship positioning and media opportunities across Gulf News, Arab News, and Economic Times

 

Target Educational Groups

*	UAE – Onboarded: GEMS, Aldar, BEAMS, LEAMS, Amity Group, DPS, HIS, Bhavans Group, Middlesex, Heriot-Watt, BITS, MAHE Dubai, Zayed University, AUS Sharjah & Dubai
UAE – Working on: Taaleem, Axiom, ADNOC, government schools, Khalifa University
*	Saudi Arabia – Onboarded: BISR, Misk, Al Faris International, SABIS, Yara, City
Saudi Arabia – Working on: Aramco schools, KAU, KSU, KAUST
*	India: CBSE and ICSE schools across Pune, Mumbai, Delhi, and Chandigarh, along with Pune tech corridor colleges and Delhi University.

 

Overall, the structure is clear and the opportunity is strong. We will ensure this is executed with proper ownership, timelines, tracking, and brand positioning so Rayna can build long-term relationships with schools, universities, students, and parents across all three markets.

TRACKING
Update the Google Sheet after every school visit, campaign, and ceremony activation.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI&source=gmail&ust=1780926315063000&sa=E> 

Thanks & Best Regards,

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Sunday, June 7, 2026 5:48 PM
To: azhar@raynatours.com; rishi@raynatours.com
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>; Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>; ranjan@raynab2b.com; nihad@raynatours.com; malik@raynatours.com
Subject: Schools, Universities & Edutainment — Complete Brief | Azhar + Rishi

 

Hi Azhar and Rishi,

This brief covers the Schools, Universities, and Edutainment segment. Azhar — you oversee this as part of your overall partnerships mandate. Rishi — you are heading the edutainment segment on the ground and this brief is primarily for you to execute. Both of you must read this fully and align.

We are expanding this segment from UAE to Saudi Arabia and India. Same playbook, dedicated people in each market, replicated under Rishi's leadership.

  _____  

WHAT EDUTAINMENT COVERS

*	Day trips — local school and university day excursions
*	Team building activities — desert safari, water sports, balloon, yacht, floating restaurant for school and university groups
*	Domestic overnight trips
*	Overseas educational trips — 116 destinations worldwide. Thailand, Singapore, Bali, London, Japan and more.

  _____  

RISHI — RECRUITMENT

1.	One Edutainment Manager for Saudi Arabia — based in Riyadh. Arabic speaking, strong school and university relationships.
2.	One Edutainment Manager for India — based in Pune. Strong school relationships in Maharashtra and North India.

Job descriptions to Pari by June 15th. Both hired by June 30th. Onboarded and trained by July 15th. First school visits in both markets by July 31st.

  _____  

HOW TO APPROACH SCHOOLS AND UNIVERSITIES

Decision maker is the Principal, Vice Principal for co-curricular activities, PTA chairperson, or student affairs department at universities.

At every visit:

1.	Show them who Rayna is — 15 years, 25 million guests, owned assets, 116 destinations worldwide
2.	Present the complete edutainment portfolio — day trips to full overseas educational tours
3.	Offer competitive group rates — work with Ranjan and destination heads for best group pricing
4.	Build annual relationships — schools renew every year. Become their permanent preferred operator.

As new destinations go live — immediately brief school contacts on new overseas trip options. Schools plan 6-12 months ahead. Get into their planning cycle early.

  _____  

APP DOWNLOAD — MANDATORY FOR ALL CAMPAIGNS

Every campaign, competition, award, and giveaway we run in schools must require the Rayna app download as the entry mechanism. No app download — no participation. This applies to students and parents equally.

This serves two purposes — we build our app user base from a completely new audience segment, and every participant enters our ecosystem where we can communicate with them permanently through push notifications and app-based campaigns.

Work with Malik to ensure the app registration flow connects directly to our giveaway and affiliate campaign platform. Students and parents who download the app must be able to see and participate in all live campaigns immediately.

  _____  

SCHOOL ENGAGEMENT PROGRAMMES — UNDER AFFILIATE PLATFORM

All school campaigns must be set up under our affiliate programme. Each school is registered as a Rayna affiliate. The school's dedicated affiliate link connects to our campaign platform where all active giveaways and competitions are visible. Teachers announce campaigns to students and parents. Students and parents download the app, register through the school's affiliate link, and participate. This way every participation is tracked back to the school, and schools earn recognition for driving engagement.

Programme 1 — Academic Achievement Award sponsored by Rayna
Rayna sponsors a monthly or termly "Best Student" or "Most Improved Student" award. Winning student and family receive a free Rayna experience — balloon, water sports, safari, or yacht. Announced publicly at assembly or parent teacher meeting.

To win — student must have the Rayna app downloaded and be registered. This drives app downloads among students and through them, their parents. Kids work harder knowing there is a prize. School gets better academic performance. We get permanent brand presence at almost zero cost — announced every term, every year.

Programme 2 — Parent Teacher Meeting Lucky Draw
Rayna sponsors a lucky draw at every PTM. Every attending parent who downloads the Rayna app and registers gets one entry. Winner announced at the end of the evening — free family balloon ride, yacht trip, or safari. Announced publicly in front of all parents.

Parents who skip PTMs now want to attend. School gets better parent engagement. Rayna gets in front of every parent in the school in one evening. Every attending parent is now in our app and database.

Programme 3 — End of Term Class Competition
Best class by attendance or grades wins a free Rayna group day trip. Every student in the class must have the Rayna app downloaded to qualify. Kids compete. Teachers motivated. Principal loves the results. 30-40 kids experience Rayna together and every parent hears about it.

Programme 4 — My Dream Holiday Competition
Drawing or essay competition — where does your family want to travel? Winners get a free family experience. Entry via the Rayna app only. Parent email and WhatsApp required. Every entry is a parent in our database.

  _____  

SCHOOL AWARD CEREMONIES — BRAND ACTIVATION

Every school holds an annual award ceremony — end of year, sports day, graduation. These are the moments when every student, parent, and teacher is in one room together. This is where Rayna must be present.

Approach every partner school to sponsor their annual award ceremony. What Rayna provides:

*	Branded screen sponsorship — a 15-20 second Rayna brand video plays on the main screen behind the stage during the ceremony. Every parent, student, and teacher sees it. This is premium brand visibility at a fraction of what a TV ad would cost.
*	Rayna popup stand at the venue — same configurations as our corporate roadshows. Staff present, app download QR codes, giveaway registration, family packages on display.
*	Sponsored giveaway prizes — Rayna announces free giveaway prizes at the ceremony. "Rayna Tours is giving one lucky family a free hot air balloon ride — scan this QR code to enter." Excitement fills the room. Every parent scans. Every parent downloads the app.
*	Co-branded event recognition — "This award ceremony is proudly sponsored by Rayna Tours — your experience, our responsibility." Announced by the principal. Printed on event materials. Posted on school social media.

The cost — a balloon ride, a safari, a water sports session. All owned by us. The return — brand presence in front of every parent, student, and teacher at every partner school, once a year minimum.

  _____  

REACHING PARENTS — DATABASE STRATEGY

Every school programme drives parent app downloads and database registrations:

*	Family Trip Card — every child takes home a Rayna card after every school trip. QR code for family discount. Parent downloads app to claim.
*	All competition entries — via app only. Parent WhatsApp and email required.
*	PTM lucky draw — app download required to enter.
*	Award ceremony QR codes — parent scans, downloads app, registers.
*	Achievement award announcements — sent to school WhatsApp groups with app download link.

Once a parent downloads the app and registers — they receive all our campaigns, holiday packages, visa offers, and giveaway campaigns automatically. One school relationship generates hundreds of parent contacts directly into our ecosystem.

  _____  

OVERSEAS EDUCATIONAL TRIPS

116 cities worldwide. Thailand, Singapore, Japan, London, Europe, Americas — all Rayna coordinated end to end. Flights, hotels, activities, transfers, visa assistance, local guide. No other edutainment operator in UAE can offer this range. As each destination goes live — build a school trip pipeline immediately.

  _____  

TARGET INSTITUTIONS

UAE: GEMS, Taaleem, Axiom, ADNOC schools, government schools, American University of Sharjah, University of Dubai, Khalifa University, Heriot-Watt Dubai, Middlesex University Dubai

Saudi: International schools Riyadh and Jeddah, SABIS, Saudi Aramco schools, King Abdulaziz University, King Saud University, KAUST

India: CBSE and ICSE schools in Pune, Mumbai, Delhi, Chandigarh. Engineering and MBA colleges Pune tech corridor. Delhi University colleges.

  _____  

TRACKING
Update the Google Sheet after every school visit, campaign, and ceremony activation.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mjl0OiNEpb4S1Gm-yUCg4H3cbg_MeNA2745KT_lY5bI&source=gmail&ust=1780926315063000&sa=E> 

  _____  

DEADLINES

*	June 15th — Job descriptions for Saudi and India Edutainment Managers to Pari
*	June 30th — Both managers hired
*	July 15th — Both managers onboarded and trained
*	July 31st — First school visits in Saudi and India completed
*	August — All parent campaigns and school engagement programmes live in all three markets
*	September — Academic achievement awards, PTM lucky draws, and award ceremony sponsorships running in all partner schools

Rishi — schools give us access to students, parents, and the community simultaneously. Three revenue streams from one relationship — edutainment trips, parent holidays, community referrals. The engagement programmes above cost us almost nothing but create emotional connections no advertisement can replicate. Build this properly and it compounds every year.

Azhar — please ensure Rishi has everything he needs and that Saudi and India recruitment happens on schedule.

Your experience, our responsibility.

To: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>

Subject: World Travel Awards 2026 — Enter Four Categories Immediately | Action Required

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

The World Travel Awards 2026 Call for Entries is now open. This is one of the most recognized awards programmes in the global travel industry — known as the Oscars of travel. Winners include Emirates, Atlantis, Qatar Airways, and Etihad. Being nominated or winning gives Rayna significant brand credibility, press coverage, and importantly — independent third party recognition that supports our Wikipedia notability case and PR strategy.

I want Rayna to enter this year. Here is exactly what to do.


STEP 1 — SUBMIT ENTRIES IMMEDIATELY

Go to worldtravelawards.com/enter and submit Rayna Tours for the following four categories only. Do not enter any other categories — we are concentrating our votes on these four to maximize our chances.


	Dubai's Leading Tour Operator — Our primary target. We are the largest ground operator in Dubai with owned desert camps, hot air balloons, marine operations, floating restaurants, and 500+ vehicles. 25 million+ guests served. No competitor in Dubai matches our owned ground operations.
	Dubai's Leading Transfer Company — 500+ vehicles operating daily across Dubai. We dominate this space.
	UAE's Leading Tour Operator — Natural extension of our Dubai position across the full UAE.
	Middle East's Leading Online Travel Company — Connects our AI transformation, B2C platform, and global expansion to 116 cities.


Submit all four entries today. Deadline is approaching — do not delay.


STEP 2 — PLAN THE VOTING CAMPAIGN

World Travel Awards is a voting-based award — the organization with the most votes wins. This is where Rayna has a massive advantage. We have:


	1.8 million customer database
	Hundreds of active B2B travel agents across UAE, India, and Saudi
	Corporate partners — banks, companies, universities
	100+ hotel desk contacts across UAE
	Our own team of 1,600 people


Once we receive our nomination confirmation, plan the following voting campaign:

Customer voting push — email + WhatsApp + push notification:

Send a dedicated campaign to our full UAE database asking customers to vote for Rayna in all four categories. Keep it simple — one click link to the voting page. Message: "You've experienced Rayna. Now vote for us. Help us become Dubai's Leading Tour Operator." Send twice — once when voting opens, once one week before voting closes.

B2B agent voting push:

Send a dedicated email to all active B2B agents on our portal asking them to vote. Travel agents and industry professionals carry extra weight in WTA voting. Personal appeal from the founder level will increase response rate.

Corporate partner push:

Ask Azhar to mobilize all corporate partners — ask HR departments to share the voting link with their employees as a quick way to support their travel partner.

Team voting:

All 1,600 Rayna staff must vote. Vaibhav to coordinate internally.

Social media campaign:

Nihad's team to run a social media voting campaign across UAE, India, and Saudi — Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn. "Vote for Rayna — Dubai's Leading Tour Operator." Include the voting link in bio and all posts for the duration of the voting period.


STEP 3 — USE FOR PR AND WIKIPEDIA

Whether we win or are nominated — both are valuable:


	Nomination announcement — send a press release to Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, and Economic Times immediately when nominations are confirmed
	Win announcement — major press release to all publications, social media campaign, email to full database
	Wikipedia — WTA nomination and win count as significant independent industry recognition. Add to Wikipedia evidence file immediately.



DEADLINES:


	Submit all four entries — Today or tomorrow at the latest
	Confirm entry submissions to me — June 9th
	Voting campaign plan ready — June 15th


This is a real opportunity to win four awards that will significantly boost Rayna's brand authority across UAE, India, and Saudi. Do not miss the entry window.

Your experience, our responsibility.
