emails.subject: Re: Pending SEO Issues, Growth Recommendations & Support Requirements
emails.body:
Hello Sir,

Thank you for the detailed response and for taking the time to review all the recommendations.

Regarding the technical SEO items, we have already created and shared the initial list with Manish. We will coordinate further, consolidate all open points, and share a finalized version with Sandstorm so they can prioritize accordingly.

Thank you for the update on the homepage initiative. A dedicated homepage with the company story, trust signals, owned assets, destination coverage, and review badges will definitely strengthen both SEO and brand authority.

Regarding hreflang, my concern is that hreflang is primarily designed to help Google choose between multiple versions of the same page. For example:


	UAE version → URL A
	India version → URL B
	Saudi version → URL C


In our current setup, all markets use the same URL. Since there are no separate URLs for Google to choose from, Google may simply ignore the hreflang annotations. I do not believe it will negatively impact rankings, but it may add implementation complexity without delivering the full intended benefit. That said, I am happy to discuss this further with Sandstorm and align with their recommendation.

Regarding the review process, my recommendation was not for page-by-page content approval of products after they go live. My concern is mainly around new developments and technical implementations. Many times we only discover issues after launch because we are not informed when new code, templates, or functionality are deployed. If the team can simply notify us before or immediately after implementation, we can test accordingly and identify potential SEO issues early. In any case, we will continue our regular testing and monitoring process.

Thank you as well for approving the centralized SEO budget initiative. This will help us maintain consistency in SEO activities without operational delays.

We will continue working on the Wikipedia assessment, Wikidata, entity standardization, PR outreach, and media partnerships. I will keep you updated regularly on the progress of the India publications and Wikipedia-related activities. I will also share the senior travel writer job description and salary expectations with Senthil Sir by EOD.

Thank you again for your support, guidance, and quick action on these initiatives.

Best Regards,

Aparna Sisodia




From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Sat, 06 Jun 2026 23:08:31

To: aparna@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, manish.y@raynatours.com

Subject: Re: Pending SEO Issues, Growth Recommendations & Support Requirements

 

Hi Aparna,

Thank you for this detailed report. This is exactly the kind of proactive communication I expect. I have reviewed everything carefully and here is where things stand.

ONE — Technical SEO Issues

We have already engaged Sandstorm Digital Worldwide for a full 12-week SEO, GEO, and AEO programme. Their scope covers the majority of technical issues you have flagged — XML sitemap, OG URL mismatches, hidden H1 tags, FAQ schema, organizational schema, staging subdomain indexation, Core Web Vitals, image alt text, and more.

Senthil will coordinate a briefing between you, Manish, and Sandstorm as soon as he is back this week. Your role will be to work alongside Sandstorm — not duplicate their work. Please prepare a list of all open tickets you have raised so Sandstorm can prioritize accordingly.

TWO — Homepage

You are correct that the homepage being a replica of the activities page is a problem. I have addressed this today. Asad has been briefed to design a dedicated homepage for raynatours.com with the complete company story, trust signals, owned assets, destination portfolio, and review badges. Deadline for mockup is June 20th. Once approved, Anwar's team will implement the separation of homepage and activities page. Organizational schema can then be implemented properly.

THREE — Country-Specific URL Folders

I understand your recommendation and the logic is correct. However the timing is wrong. We are currently rebuilding the entire website with Sandstorm, launching an AI content engine that will produce hundreds of new pages, and going live across 116 cities simultaneously. Adding country-specific URL folders on top of all this creates enormous complexity and will slow everything down significantly.



Instead — please work with Sandstorm to implement hreflang tags on our existing pages. This signals to Google which market each page is most relevant for — UAE, India, or Saudi — and gives us 60-70% of the benefit of country folders with almost zero development work. This is already part of Sandstorm's scope. Country-specific URL folders will be revisited in 2027 once the website is stable and content engine is proven.

FOUR — Pre-Launch Content Review Process

You are right that pages should not go live with poor quality content. However page-by-page human SEO approval is not practical at our scale — we are about to go live with hundreds of pages across 116 cities. This would take months and defeat the purpose of our AI content engine.



Instead — Sandstorm will provide us with a fixed AEO content template by Week 4 of their engagement. Every page produced by our AI content engine will follow this template automatically. The template ensures every page is SEO, AEO, and GEO ready from the moment it is created. No page-by-page human review needed because the quality is built into the template itself.



Your role is to work with Sandstorm to define and validate this template — that is where your expertise adds the most value. Once the template is approved, the AI engine handles the rest at scale.

FIVE — Budget Centralization

Agreed. SEO budget should not be dependent on individual sales heads. Senthil will set up a centralized SEO budget under your team. No more interruptions to link building or tool subscriptions because a sales head paused spending.

SIX — Your Responsibilities Going Forward

The following tasks remain with you and Manish — these are NOT covered by Sandstorm:


	Wikipedia article — research notability, compile existing press coverage, draft article, submit once 3+ major articles are published. Deadline for notability assessment — June 10th.
	Wikidata entity creation — deadline June 9th.
	Brand entity standardization — Google Business, LinkedIn, Facebook, Trustpilot, TripAdvisor, all directories. Deadline June 10th.
	PR outreach — Gulf News, Arabian Business, Economic Times, Forbes India, local print in Pune and Delhi. Brief already sent to you.
	Work with Sandstorm on AEO content template definition — this is your most important near-term task alongside Sandstorm.


SEVEN — Senior Travel Writer

Your recommendation to hire a senior travel writer with SEO and AEO experience is approved in principle. Please share a job description and salary expectation with Senthil for review. We need someone who understands travel, writes for humans and AI engines simultaneously, and can review and improve AI-generated content before it goes live.

Good work on this report. Keep this standard of communication going forward.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: World Travel Awards 2026 — Enter Four Categories Immediately | Action Required
emails.body:
I will complete it today. Manish and Deepak Sir, shall I share your name as the contact person for invoices and other communication details?



From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Sun, 07 Jun 2026 01:28:30

To: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>

Subject: World Travel Awards 2026 — Enter Four Categories Immediately | Action Required

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

The World Travel Awards 2026 Call for Entries is now open. This is one of the most recognized awards programmes in the global travel industry — known as the Oscars of travel. Winners include Emirates, Atlantis, Qatar Airways, and Etihad. Being nominated or winning gives Rayna significant brand credibility, press coverage, and importantly — independent third party recognition that supports our Wikipedia notability case and PR strategy.

I want Rayna to enter this year. Here is exactly what to do.


STEP 1 — SUBMIT ENTRIES IMMEDIATELY

Go to worldtravelawards.com/enter and submit Rayna Tours for the following four categories only. Do not enter any other categories — we are concentrating our votes on these four to maximize our chances.


	Dubai's Leading Tour Operator — Our primary target. We are the largest ground operator in Dubai with owned desert camps, hot air balloons, marine operations, floating restaurants, and 500+ vehicles. 25 million+ guests served. No competitor in Dubai matches our owned ground operations.
	Dubai's Leading Transfer Company — 500+ vehicles operating daily across Dubai. We dominate this space.
	UAE's Leading Tour Operator — Natural extension of our Dubai position across the full UAE.
	Middle East's Leading Online Travel Company — Connects our AI transformation, B2C platform, and global expansion to 116 cities.


Submit all four entries today. Deadline is approaching — do not delay.


STEP 2 — PLAN THE VOTING CAMPAIGN

World Travel Awards is a voting-based award — the organization with the most votes wins. This is where Rayna has a massive advantage. We have:


	1.8 million customer database
	Hundreds of active B2B travel agents across UAE, India, and Saudi
	Corporate partners — banks, companies, universities
	100+ hotel desk contacts across UAE
	Our own team of 1,600 people


Once we receive our nomination confirmation, plan the following voting campaign:

Customer voting push — email + WhatsApp + push notification:

Send a dedicated campaign to our full UAE database asking customers to vote for Rayna in all four categories. Keep it simple — one click link to the voting page. Message: "You've experienced Rayna. Now vote for us. Help us become Dubai's Leading Tour Operator." Send twice — once when voting opens, once one week before voting closes.

B2B agent voting push:

Send a dedicated email to all active B2B agents on our portal asking them to vote. Travel agents and industry professionals carry extra weight in WTA voting. Personal appeal from the founder level will increase response rate.

Corporate partner push:

Ask Azhar to mobilize all corporate partners — ask HR departments to share the voting link with their employees as a quick way to support their travel partner.

Team voting:

All 1,600 Rayna staff must vote. Vaibhav to coordinate internally.

Social media campaign:

Nihad's team to run a social media voting campaign across UAE, India, and Saudi — Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn. "Vote for Rayna — Dubai's Leading Tour Operator." Include the voting link in bio and all posts for the duration of the voting period.


STEP 3 — USE FOR PR AND WIKIPEDIA

Whether we win or are nominated — both are valuable:


	Nomination announcement — send a press release to Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, and Economic Times immediately when nominations are confirmed
	Win announcement — major press release to all publications, social media campaign, email to full database
	Wikipedia — WTA nomination and win count as significant independent industry recognition. Add to Wikipedia evidence file immediately.



DEADLINES:


	Submit all four entries — Today or tomorrow at the latest
	Confirm entry submissions to me — June 9th
	Voting campaign plan ready — June 15th


This is a real opportunity to win four awards that will significantly boost Rayna's brand authority across UAE, India, and Saudi. Do not miss the entry window.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Urgent — Compile All Independent Awards for Wikipedia Submission | Deadline June 12th
emails.body:
Noted, Manoj Sir. We have started working on this and are compiling all awards, supporting sources, and references. I will share the initial sheet by tonight around 11:00 PM.



From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Sun, 07 Jun 2026 01:35:15

To: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Urgent — Compile All Independent Awards for Wikipedia Submission | Deadline June 12th

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

This is an urgent task that directly supports our Wikipedia submission and overall brand authority building.

I need you to compile a complete file of all independent awards and recognitions Rayna Tours has received. These are awards from organizations that have no commercial relationship with us — not from our suppliers, theme park partners, or hotel partners. Independent recognition only.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR — Examples of the right type of awards:


	TripAdvisor — Travelers' Choice, Best of the Best, Certificate of Excellence. Any year.
	Arabian Travel Market (ATM) Awards — any category, any year
	Arabian Travel Awards — any category, any year
	Dubai Police recognition or awards
	Dubai Municipality awards
	Dubai Tourism / DTCM recognition
	Saudi Tourism Authority recognition
	Any government or regulatory body recognition — UAE or internationally
	Any independent travel industry body awards
	Any consumer or media voted awards — where Rayna won based on public or industry votes
	World Travel Awards — past nominations or wins if any
	Any newspaper or magazine awards — Best Tour Operator, Best Travel Company, etc.


WHAT NOT TO INCLUDE:


	Awards from theme parks — Global Village, Motiongate, Ferrari World, etc.
	Awards from hotels or hotel groups
	Awards from airlines or airline partners
	Any award where the awarding organization has a commercial relationship with Rayna


FOR EACH AWARD — COMPILE THE FOLLOWING:


	Name of award
	Awarding organization
	Year received
	Category — what exactly was the award for
	Online source — URL of any webpage, press release, or news article confirming this award. This is critical. If there is no online source, the award cannot be used for Wikipedia.
	Was there any press coverage of this award in any publication? If yes — name of publication and URL.


WHERE TO FIND THIS INFORMATION:


	Check our website — raynatours.com — we have awards displayed there
	Check our office — physical award certificates and trophies
	Check with Deepak and Senthil — they have been with the company for many years and will remember significant awards
	Search online — "Rayna Tours award" on Google. Check all results.
	Check our social media archives — any award announcements posted in previous years
	Check TripAdvisor directly — our TripAdvisor profile shows all certificates and awards received


DELIVERABLE:

A clean document — Word or PDF — listing all independent awards with all details above. This document will be used as evidence for our Wikipedia submission. The stronger and more complete this document, the faster our Wikipedia article gets approved.

Deadline: June 12th.

This is important. We have been operating for 15 years and have received significant recognition. All of that recognition needs to be documented and used. Do not leave anything out — even small awards count if they are from independent organizations.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Re: Media Partnerships — India, UAE & Saudi Arabia | Complete Brief | Aparna + Manish
emails.body:
Thank you, Sir. I have reviewed the plan and will start working on the India publications and outreach immediately. Also, I currently do not have edit access to the Google Sheet—could you please grant access so I can update my section?



From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Sun, 07 Jun 2026 20:54:07

To: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, alok@raynatours.com

Subject: Media Partnerships — India, UAE & Saudi Arabia | Complete Brief | Aparna + Manish

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

I am assigning both of you joint ownership of Rayna's media partnerships across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia. You have the same responsibility — same model, same approach, same targets — but different markets. You will work together, support each other, and be measured together.

Aparna — you own India.

Manish — you own UAE and Saudi Arabia.

Please read this fully together.


THE OBJECTIVE

Get Rayna featured in as many publications as possible across all three markets — print, online, regional language, English, travel focused, and business focused. Every article must include a unique Rayna coupon code exclusive to that publication's readers. Every reader who uses that code to book is tracked back to whoever secured that partnership. That is how your individual performance is measured — articles published and coupon code registrations generated.


THE COMMERCIAL MODEL

Primary — Barter:

We offer free experiences and packages in exchange for articles and coverage. No cash. Barter options you can offer any media partner:


	Free hosted experience in Dubai — hot air balloon, desert safari, yacht, floating restaurant, water sports
	Free holiday package — Thailand, Bali, Maldives, Singapore, or any destination live on our platform
	Combination — Dubai experience plus destination package for major publications
	Annual barter agreement — regular monthly or quarterly articles in exchange for regular experience credits


Always start with barter. Most travel editors, journalists, and bloggers will accept this — they get a free experience, we get an article. Everyone wins.

Secondary — Cash:

If a publication does not accept barter — cash is an option. However every cash deal must be approved by Senthil before you commit. Send Senthil the proposal — publication name, reach, article deliverables, and cash amount requested. He approves or declines within 48 hours. Do not commit any cash payment without his written approval.


WHAT WE EXPECT IN RETURN FROM EVERY MEDIA PARTNER


	Minimum one article or feature — minimum 500 words — published on their main platform
	Article must include Rayna's unique coupon code exclusive to their readers
	Article must include a link to raynatours.com
	Social media post on their official accounts sharing the article
	For print publications — online version of the article as well
	For Arabic publications — article must be in Arabic. Do not accept English-only for Arabic publications.
	You review and approve every article before publication — no article goes live without your review. Ensure it reflects our positioning correctly — destination expert and ground operator, not a marketplace.



THREE STORY ANGLES — USE THESE FOR ALL PITCHES


	The Founder Story — From one travel desk in Dubai in 2006 to 1,600 employees, 25 million guests, 116 cities worldwide. Indian founder, built in Dubai, now going global. Best for Economic Times, Forbes India, Gulf News, Arabian Business.
	The AI Transformation Story — How Rayna is using AI to expand from 17 cities to 116 cities in 90 days. Best for tech and business publications across all three markets.
	The Destination Expert Story — Why booking with a ground operator gives travelers a better experience than booking with a marketplace. Best for travel publications and bloggers in all markets.


Manish — for Saudi publications, add a fourth angle: Rayna's Saudi expansion — our Riyadh office, growing presence in Saudi, and commitment to Vision 2030 tourism. Saudi media responds very well to local investment stories.


YOUR TARGET PUBLICATIONS

APARNA — INDIA:

National English — Economic Times Travel, Times of India Travel, Hindustan Times Travel, Business Standard, Forbes India (already in progress — close immediately), Conde Nast Traveller India, Travel + Leisure India, Outlook Traveller

Maharashtra Regional — Maharashtra Times, Lokmat, Sakal, Loksatta, Pune Mirror, Mid-Day Mumbai

North India — Tribune Chandigarh, Dainik Bhaskar, Punjabi Tribune Amritsar, Amar Ujala, Hindustan Times Delhi

Online Bloggers — Top 20 India-based travel bloggers with domain rating above 30 and monthly traffic above 50,000. Use Ahrefs to identify and evaluate.

MANISH — UAE AND SAUDI:

UAE English — Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, Gulf Business, Time Out Dubai, Trade Arabia, Hindustan Times Gulf

UAE Arabic — Al Khaleej, Al Bayan, Emarat Al Youm, Al Ittihad

Saudi English — Arab News, Saudi Gazette, Arabian Business Saudi edition

Saudi Arabic — Okaz, Al Riyadh, Al Watan, Sabq

Online Bloggers UAE and Saudi — Top 20 travel bloggers in both markets — Arabic and English. Use Ahrefs to evaluate. Arabic travel bloggers in Saudi especially have large, engaged audiences and are very receptive to barter deals.


HOW TO APPROACH EACH PUBLICATION


	Find the travel editor or lifestyle editor directly — not the general inbox. Use LinkedIn, the publication's masthead, or Lusha to find direct contact.
	Send a personalized pitch — reference a recent article they published, explain why Rayna is a story their readers will care about, and propose the barter clearly.
	Pitch in the publication's language — English for English publications, Arabic for Arabic publications, Hindi for Hindi publications. Never send a generic English pitch to a regional language publication.
	Follow up once after 5 days if no response. If still no response — move to next contact or next publication.
	Once agreed — send them the Rayna story angles, About Us page link, and brand positioning document. Make it easy for them to write a strong article.
	Review every article before publication. If positioning is wrong — correct it before it goes live.
	Once published — provide the unique coupon code for inclusion in the article and their social media posts.
	Track everything in the Google Sheet immediately after every interaction.



YOUR TRACKING SHEET

I have created a shared Google Sheet for both of you. Aparna updates the India section. Manish updates the UAE and Saudi section. Both update the weekly performance report every Friday.

Sheet link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1hzyKEo9_0cOZz740iscoBoJeqQl8Yq6By8lbkdcGgRg

I check this sheet every Monday. If it is not updated — I assume the work is not happening.


YOUR DEADLINES


	
		
			Task
			Owner
			Deadline
		
		
			Complete contact list of all target publications and editors built
			Both
			June 12th
		
		
			Forbes India proposal closed
			Aparna
			June 10th — already in progress
		
		
			First outreach wave sent — top 10 publications
			Both
			June 15th
		
		
			First barter deal agreed
			Both
			June 20th
		
		
			First article published
			Both
			July 15th
		
		
			5 articles published each
			Both
			August 1st
		
		
			10 articles published each
			Both
			August 31st
		
		
			Weekly report updated in sheet
			Both
			Every Friday
		
	


The media coverage you build now directly supports our September campaign launch, our Wikipedia submission, and our long term brand authority across all three markets. Every article is evidence that Rayna is a serious, credible company. Every coupon code registration is a new customer in our database. Take this seriously and move fast.

Your experience, our responsibility.

emails.subject: Block 3 — Complete Scope | Three Workstreams + Affiliate Social Feature | Vaibhav + Malik
emails.body:
Hi Vaibhav and Malik,

I want to formally define the complete scope of Block 3 — our marketing
distribution platform. This has grown beyond what was originally planned
and I want both of you fully aligned on everything that needs to be built.
Please read this fully.
------------------------------

*BLOCK 3 — THREE WORKSTREAMS*

Block 3 is no longer just email marketing. It is our complete AI-powered
marketing distribution and automation platform covering three workstreams.
Both of you own this together. I need a combined deadline for all three
workstreams by end of this week.
------------------------------

*WORKSTREAM 1 — EMAIL, WHATSAPP, SMS, PUSH NOTIFICATIONS*
*Owner: Vaibhav + Malik (ChatHead)*

This is the core marketing platform already in progress. Complete end to
end system covering:

   - AI-planned email campaigns — planned, scheduled, previewed, approved,
   distributed automatically
   - WhatsApp broadcasting through ChatHead — mass broadcast, personalized
   messaging, automated follow-ups
   - SMS campaigns
   - Push notifications — app-based once app is live
   - Seed list testing — 10 members, 4 approvals required before any
   campaign goes live — AI user + Anket + any 2 others
   - Co-branded campaigns — for corporate partners, reward programme
   partners, and affiliates
   - Daily campaign forwarding to corporate and reward partners

I need your complete deadline for this workstream. By when is everything
fully live and operational? Come back to me with a realistic date this week.
------------------------------

*WORKSTREAM 2 — MADGICX INTEGRATION — GOOGLE ADS AND META ADS*
*Owner: Anket + Malik*

AI-powered Google and Meta ads automation through Madgicx. Covers:

   - Automated campaign creation from product feed — new destination goes
   live, ad campaign creates automatically
   - Budget optimization — AI allocates spend across campaigns based on
   performance
   - Audience targeting — separate campaigns for India, UAE, and Saudi
   - Creative testing — AI tests which content performs best
   - Full transparency — human sets rules, AI executes within those rules
   - In-house build planning — Malik assesses building Rayna's own ad
   automation as an extension of Block 3 in parallel

Assessment and go-live date due June 15th from Anket and Malik jointly.
------------------------------

*WORKSTREAM 3 — SOCIAL MEDIA AUTOMATION AGENT*
*Owner: Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad*

This is a new and critical addition to Block 3. The Marketing Agent must
connect to all Rayna social media platforms and post automatically — no
human dependency.

*Platforms to connect:*

   - Instagram — @raynatours_
   - Facebook — Rayna Tours
   - TikTok
   - YouTube — Shorts and full videos
   - LinkedIn
   - Twitter / X

*What the agent does automatically:*

   - Reads the product feed — every destination, every product, every offer
   - Generates market-ready content — reels, static posts, stories,
   captions, YouTube shorts
   - Posts across all platforms on an optimized schedule
   - When a new destination goes live — automatically creates and posts
   destination content across all platforms within hours
   - Adjusts posting frequency and timing based on engagement data

*Rules we define once — AI executes forever:*

   - Brand tone and voice — "Your experience, our responsibility."
   - Content types per platform — reels for Instagram and TikTok, long form
   for YouTube, professional for LinkedIn
   - Posting frequency per platform
   - Languages — English, Arabic, Hindi versions per market
   - Offer codes and campaign links

Nihad — you are CC'd on this. The social media automation agent does not
replace your role. Your role becomes defining the strategy, rules, and
creative direction that the AI follows. You are the brain. The AI is the
execution. Work with Vaibhav and Malik to define the exact rules and
content guidelines the agent must follow.
------------------------------

*ADDITIONAL FEATURE — AFFILIATE SOCIAL MEDIA AUTO-POSTING*
*Owner: Vaibhav (manages affiliate programme) + Gaurav (builds the feature)*

This is one of the most powerful features we can add to our affiliate
programme. Here is how it works:

Any affiliate who wants to — connects their social media handles to the
Rayna affiliate platform. Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube — any
platform they are active on. Once connected, the same AI agent that posts
to Rayna's own accounts automatically creates and posts Rayna content to
the affiliate's accounts as well.

*What the affiliate gets:*

   - They connect their account once — AI does everything from that point
   - AI creates content relevant to their audience and their niche
   - AI posts automatically on an optimal schedule
   - Their unique discount code and affiliate link is embedded in every post
   - When their followers click and book — they earn commission
   automatically
   - Zero effort required after the initial connection

*Why this is a game changer:*
The biggest reason affiliates sign up and never post is because creating
content feels like work. With this feature — they connect once, AI posts
for them forever, commission keeps coming in passively. This will
dramatically increase affiliate activation rates and the volume of Rayna
content reaching new audiences every day.

Gaurav — you are CC'd. This feature needs to be built into the Rayna
affiliate platform. It uses the same AI content generation engine as
Workstream 3 — the affiliate version personalizes content for each
affiliate's audience and embeds their unique discount code and link
automatically. Please confirm this is added to your development roadmap and
share a build timeline.

Nihad — this feature will be your strongest recruitment pitch to
influencers. "Connect your account once. We create the content. We post it.
You earn commission automatically." Use this in your outreach immediately
once the feature is live.
------------------------------

*WHAT I NEED FROM YOU — THIS WEEK*

   1. *Vaibhav + Malik* — Complete deadline for Workstream 1 — email,
   WhatsApp, SMS, push notifications. Fully live and operational by when?
   2. *Anket + Malik* — Madgicx assessment and go-live date — June 15th
   deadline stands
   3. *Vaibhav + Malik + Nihad* — Initial plan for Workstream 3 — social
   media automation agent. What platforms first? What is the build timeline?
   What rules and content guidelines does Nihad need to define?
   4. *Gaurav* — Confirm affiliate social auto-posting feature is added to
   development roadmap. By when can this be live?

Block 3 is the engine that connects everything — our products, our
destinations, our offers — to our customers and affiliates across every
channel simultaneously. Get this right and our September campaign launch
will be unlike anything Rayna has done before.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Re: Influencer & Affiliate Programme — Complete Brief + Meeting Tomorrow | Nihad
emails.cc:
Senthil Velan <senthil@raynatours.com>, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, alok@raynatours.com, 
	Gaurav.verma@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Sir,

Mail Noted, thank you.

I have received the brief and will go through it in detail. Happy to
discuss everything with you tomorrow.

Regards,
Nihad Ali



On Sun, 7 Jun 2026, 6:51 pm Manoj Tulsani, <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:

> Hi Nihad,
>
> I am assigning you full ownership of Rayna's influencer and affiliate
> programme across UAE, India, and Saudi Arabia. This is one of the most
> important growth channels we are building and I need you to move fast,
> execute well, and report weekly.
>
> Please read this fully. I also want you to come and speak to me tomorrow
> to discuss this in person.
> ------------------------------
>
> *THE BIG PICTURE — WHAT WE ARE BUILDING*
>
> We are building a self-sustaining affiliate army — not just influencers,
> but anyone with an audience. Travel bloggers, community admins, WhatsApp
> group owners, Instagram creators, YouTube channels, TikTok creators, micro
> influencers, nano influencers. Anyone who has followers and wants to make
> money promoting travel can be a Rayna affiliate.
>
> The model is simple:
>
>    - Register on Rayna affiliate platform
>    - Get a unique UTM link and QR code immediately upon registration
>    - Get a unique discount code for their audience
>    - Access Rayna's ready-to-post content library — reels, static posts,
>    story formats, WhatsApp-ready content — all pre-made, plug and play
>    - Schedule and post directly from the library
>    - Their audience clicks, books using their code, and they earn
>    commission automatically
>
> No filming required. No editing required. No waiting. Content is already
> made. They just post it.
> ------------------------------
>
> *TASK 1 — CREATE THE AFFILIATE EXPLAINER VIDEO*
> *Deadline: June 20th*
>
> This is your first and most urgent task. I need you to create a complete
> explainer video that shows influencers and potential affiliates exactly how
> the Rayna affiliate programme works and how they can make money from it.
>
> The video must cover:
>
>    1. What is the Rayna affiliate programme and who can join — anyone
>    with any size audience
>    2. How to register on the Rayna affiliate platform — step by step
>    3. What you get immediately upon registration — unique UTM link, QR
>    code, discount code
>    4. How to access the ready-to-post content library — browse, select,
>    customize if needed
>    5. How to schedule and post content directly — plug and play
>    6. How your audience uses your discount code to book
>    7. How commission is tracked and paid — real time dashboard
>    8. Real earning examples — if 10 of your followers book a Thailand
>    package, here is how much you earn
>
> Format options — choose what you are most confident with:
>
>    - *Option A — You present on camera* — if you are confident on camera,
>    do a clean, professional walkthrough. Show the platform on screen. Talk
>    through each step. Keep it under 3 minutes.
>    - *Option B — AI avatar* — use HeyGen to create an AI presenter who
>    delivers the script. Coordinate with Adnan who is already working with
>    HeyGen for training videos.
>
> Once the video is produced and approved by me — publish it on:
>
>    - Instagram Reels — with a strong caption and call to action to
>    register
>    - YouTube — full version for anyone who wants to watch in detail
>    - TikTok
>    - The link must be included in every email campaign you send to
>    influencers going forward
>
> ------------------------------
>
> *TASK 2 — BUILD THE READY-TO-POST CONTENT LIBRARY*
> *Deadline: June 20th — first batch. Ongoing forever after.*
>
> Work with Malik and Vaibhav to build a library of ready-to-post affiliate
> content. Every affiliate who registers must find content already waiting
> for them — not a blank page.
>
> For every destination and product — create:
>
>    - 3 short form video reels — destination highlight, activity showcase,
>    package offer
>    - 3 static posts — clean design, product image, price, coupon code
>    placeholder
>    - 2 story formats — swipe up design, countdown offer
>    - 1 WhatsApp-ready image — simple, designed to be forwarded
>
> Start with our highest demand products:
>
>    - Dubai — Desert Safari, Balloon Ride, Yacht, Water Sports, Floating
>    Restaurant
>    - Thailand — Bangkok, Phuket, Pattaya packages
>    - Bali packages
>    - Singapore packages
>    - Malaysia packages
>    - Maldives packages
>    - Georgia packages
>
> As new destinations go live — add content for them immediately. The
> library must always be current. An affiliate should never log in and find
> outdated content.
>
> Every piece of content must have a placeholder for the affiliate's unique
> discount code — so it is personalized when they post it.
> ------------------------------
>
> *TASK 3 — EMAIL CAMPAIGN TO INFLUENCERS*
> *Deadline: June 23rd — first campaign sent*
>
> Two separate email campaigns to run simultaneously and continuously:
>
> *Campaign 1 — Invite to Join Rayna Affiliate Programme*
> Target: All influencers and content creators across UAE, India, and Saudi
> who are not yet registered. Travel creators, lifestyle creators, food
> creators, family creators, anyone with an engaged audience above 1,000
> followers.
>
> Message: "Turn your audience into income. Join Rayna's affiliate programme
> — promote travel experiences your followers will love and earn commission
> on every booking. Register in 2 minutes. Content already made for you.
> Start earning today."
>
> Include the explainer video link prominently. Include the registration
> link. Include real earning examples.
>
> *Campaign 2 — Weekly Offers to All Registered Affiliates*
> Target: Everyone already registered on the affiliate platform.
>
> Every week — send them the latest offers, new destinations that went live,
> new content added to the library, and their performance stats from the
> previous week. Keep them engaged, keep them posting.
>
> Message format: "This week's top offers for your audience. New content
> ready to post. Your earnings last week: [X]. Post this week and earn more."
>
> Build your influencer contact list by:
>
>    - Searching Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube for travel creators in UAE,
>    India, and Saudi
>    - Using Lusha — we have it connected — to find contact emails for
>    larger creators
>    - Reaching out via DM on Instagram for smaller creators
>    - Posting about the affiliate programme on Rayna's own social media
>    channels
>
> ------------------------------
>
> *TASK 4 — HOSTED EXPERIENCES FOR TOP TIER INFLUENCERS*
> *Ongoing — selective and strategic*
>
> For our top tier influencers — 1 million+ followers — hosted experiences
> are reserved for brand storytelling, not product promotion. Their job is to
> tell the Rayna story — the ground operator, the owned assets, the founder
> journey, what makes Rayna different.
>
> Maximum 2-3 hosted experiences per market per quarter. Every hosted
> experience must be fully documented — video, photography, behind the scenes
> content. Coordinate with me and Senthil before committing to any hosted
> experience.
> ------------------------------
>
> *MULTILINGUAL APPROACH — ALL THREE MARKETS*
>
>    - *UAE* — Hindi creators for Indian expat audience, Arabic creators
>    for Emirati and Arab audience, Urdu creators for Pakistani audience,
>    English creators for Western expat audience
>    - *India* — Hindi creators primarily, regional language creators for
>    Maharashtra (Marathi) and Punjab
>    - *Saudi* — Arabic creators primarily, English for international expat
>    audience in Saudi
>
> Content library must have language-appropriate versions for each market.
> Coordinate with Alok's content team to ensure Arabic and Hindi versions are
> available.
> ------------------------------
>
> *YOUR TRACKING SHEET*
>
> I have created a Google Sheet for you to track everything — video
> production, email campaigns, affiliate registrations, content library
> status, and weekly performance.
>
> *Sheet link:
> https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PsluCD1LOb5TOtuwuulJKwJBDeznxr4j4oD1EicjUHU
> <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PsluCD1LOb5TOtuwuulJKwJBDeznxr4j4oD1EicjUHU&source=gmail&ust=1780929645959000&sa=E>*
>
> Update this sheet every week without fail. Every Friday — fill in the
> weekly performance report section. I will check this sheet every Monday. If
> it is not updated, I will assume the work is not happening.
> ------------------------------
>
> *YOUR DEADLINES*
> Task Deadline
> Affiliate explainer video script written June 13th
> Video produced and submitted to me for approval June 17th
> Video approved and published on all platforms June 20th
> First batch of content library ready — top 7 destinations June 20th
> First invite email campaign sent to influencers — UAE June 23rd
> First invite email campaign sent to influencers — India June 25th
> First invite email campaign sent to influencers — Saudi June 25th
> First weekly offer campaign sent to registered affiliates June 30th
> 100 affiliates registered across all three markets July 15th
> 500 affiliates registered across all three markets August 15th
> Full affiliate army active for September campaign launch September 1st
> ------------------------------
>
> *COME AND SEE ME TOMORROW*
>
> I want to discuss this with you in person tomorrow. Come prepared with:
>
>    1. Your initial thinking on the explainer video — format, script
>    outline, how you plan to produce it
>    2. Your plan for building the influencer contact list across all three
>    markets
>    3. Any questions or resources you need from the team
>
> Nihad — this programme has the potential to give Rayna reach that no paid
> media budget can match. Thousands of people promoting Rayna to their
> audiences every day — automatically, at scale, in every language, across
> every market. Build it right and it becomes one of Rayna's most powerful
> growth engines. I am counting on you.
>
> *Your experience, our responsibility.*
>

emails.subject: Tourism Board Outreach — All 137 Cities | Senthil + Deepak Co-Own
emails.cc:
Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, ranjan@raynab2b.com, 
	manoj.gagwani@raynatours.com, sunil.p@raynab2b.com, rajkumar@raynatours.com, 
	alok@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Senthil and Deepak,

I am assigning both of you joint ownership of our tourism board outreach
across all 137 cities we are activating. This is a strategic initiative
that requires both seniority and deep destination knowledge — which is
exactly why I want both of you on it together.

Senthil — you lead the senior-level conversations with tourism board
directors and marketing heads. Your seniority and understanding of Rayna's
business opens doors.

Deepak — you bring the destination and product depth. You know what we need
from each tourism board, what content gaps we have, and what each
destination requires to go live successfully. Your knowledge makes every
conversation specific and credible.

Together you cover everything this initiative needs. Ranjan, Manoj Gagwani,
Sunil Pawar, and Rajkumar are CC'd — as destination heads they will own
city-specific follow-ups under your joint coordination.
------------------------------

*WHY THIS IS IMPORTANT*

Tourism boards exist for one reason — to bring more visitors to their
destination. When Rayna tells a tourism board that we are actively
promoting their city to millions of customers across India, UAE, and Saudi
Arabia — they will want to support us. This is not a favour we are asking.
This is a partnership that benefits them as much as it benefits us.

We have:

   - 1.8 million customers across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia
   - Active marketing campaigns launching across three source markets
   - 137 cities being activated simultaneously
   - AI-driven content engine producing destination content at scale
   - B2C platform, app, email marketing, WhatsApp broadcasting, influencer
   network

Every tourism board we approach should see Rayna as one of the most
valuable distribution partners they can have in their source markets. We
are bringing their destination to millions of potential visitors. In return
we need their support.
------------------------------

*WHAT TO ASK FROM EVERY TOURISM BOARD*

   1. *Hosted FAM trips* — Familiarization trips for Rayna's destination
   heads and product managers. A destination head who has visited Bangkok will
   sell Bangkok infinitely better than one who hasn't. Ask the tourism board
   to host and fund FAM trips for our team.
   2. *Marketing co-funding* — Many tourism boards have co-op marketing
   funds for trade partners who promote their destination. Ask if they
   contribute towards our advertising spend — Meta ads, Google campaigns,
   email marketing, influencer programme for that destination.
   3. *Content — images, videos, destination guides* — Every tourism board
   has a library of professional destination photography, videography, and
   written guides. Ask for full access to use across our website, app, social
   media, and marketing campaigns. This accelerates our Block 1 content engine
   significantly.
   4. *Articles and editorial content* — Ask if they can write or co-write
   destination content for publication on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E>
   .
   5. *Press and media support* — Ask if they can facilitate introductions
   to local journalists and travel publications who can cover Rayna's launch
   in their destination.
   6. *Trade shows and events* — Ask to be included as a partner at any
   trade events they attend in India, UAE, or Saudi.
   7. *Preferred partner status* — Ask to be formally recognized as a
   preferred or recommended tour operator for their destination.
   8. *White label technology partnership* — This is our unique offer that
   no other operator can match. Some tourism boards need technology to promote
   their destination's holiday packages, activities, excursions, and events to
   travel agents and consumers. We can provide our white label technology
   platform — they brand it as their own, we power it behind the scenes. In
   return they give us preferred partner status, marketing funding, content
   access, and FAM trips. Bring this up with every tourism board where you
   sense a technology gap. Coordinate with Anwar when any tourism board
   expresses interest — he will handle the technical proposal.

------------------------------

*THE MARKETING PRESENTATION*

Before any outreach begins, we need a professional Rayna partner
presentation. Coordinate with Asad to design this — it must look premium.
Deadline: *June 20th.*

Cover: who Rayna is, our source markets, our marketing reach, our September
campaign, our destination commitment, our white label technology offer, and
what we are asking in return.
------------------------------

*PRIORITY APPROACH*

*🔴 HIGH — Approach immediately:*
Bangkok, Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Bali, Ho Chi Minh City, Tokyo, Seoul,
Maldives, Mauritius, Dubai DTCM, Abu Dhabi DCT, Riyadh, Jeddah, Cairo,
Muscat, London, Paris, Zurich, Amsterdam, Rome, Barcelona, Istanbul, New
York, Los Angeles, Goa

*🟡 MEDIUM — Approach July-August*

*🟢 GROWTH — Approach September onwards*
------------------------------

*TOURISM BOARD TRACKER — ALL 137 CITIES*

I have created a complete Google Sheet with all 137 cities pre-populated —
tourism board names, official websites, trade partnership emails, priority
levels, and notes on which boards have Dubai offices.

*Sheet link:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0
<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FHBXwsQlpznwYIpakgg9I1SJrnPZsLdtSAGZ7b7dWc0&source=gmail&ust=1780934739308000&sa=E>*

Update after every interaction. I check this sheet every Monday.
------------------------------

*DEADLINES*
Task Owner Deadline
Partner presentation designed and ready Senthil + Deepak + Asad June 20th
First outreach wave — top 25 High Priority cities Senthil + Deepak June 25th
All destination heads briefed and doing outreach for their cities Senthil +
Deepak June 30th
First FAM trip scheduled Deepak + Ranjan July 31st
Content received from first 15 tourism boards All destination heads July
31st
White label technology conversations initiated with 5 tourism boards Senthil
+ Deepak July 31st
All 137 cities contacted All destination heads August 31st
------------------------------

Tourism boards have content, funding, media relationships, and technology
needs that align perfectly with what Rayna can offer. Our white label
technology is something very few operators can bring to the table — use it
as our strongest negotiating point. Start immediately upon return Monday.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Media Partnerships — India, UAE & Saudi Arabia | Complete Brief | Aparna + Manish
emails.body:
Hi Aparna and Manish,

I am assigning both of you joint ownership of Rayna's media partnerships
across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia. You have the same responsibility —
same model, same approach, same targets — but different markets. You will
work together, support each other, and be measured together.

*Aparna — you own India.*
*Manish — you own UAE and Saudi Arabia.*

Please read this fully together.
------------------------------

*THE OBJECTIVE*

Get Rayna featured in as many publications as possible across all three
markets — print, online, regional language, English, travel focused, and
business focused. Every article must include a unique Rayna coupon code
exclusive to that publication's readers. Every reader who uses that code to
book is tracked back to whoever secured that partnership. That is how your
individual performance is measured — articles published and coupon code
registrations generated.
------------------------------

*THE COMMERCIAL MODEL*

*Primary — Barter:*
We offer free experiences and packages in exchange for articles and
coverage. No cash. Barter options you can offer any media partner:

   - Free hosted experience in Dubai — hot air balloon, desert safari,
   yacht, floating restaurant, water sports
   - Free holiday package — Thailand, Bali, Maldives, Singapore, or any
   destination live on our platform
   - Combination — Dubai experience plus destination package for major
   publications
   - Annual barter agreement — regular monthly or quarterly articles in
   exchange for regular experience credits

Always start with barter. Most travel editors, journalists, and bloggers
will accept this — they get a free experience, we get an article. Everyone
wins.

*Secondary — Cash:*
If a publication does not accept barter — cash is an option. However every
cash deal must be approved by Senthil before you commit. Send Senthil the
proposal — publication name, reach, article deliverables, and cash amount
requested. He approves or declines within 48 hours. Do not commit any cash
payment without his written approval.
------------------------------

*WHAT WE EXPECT IN RETURN FROM EVERY MEDIA PARTNER*

   - Minimum one article or feature — minimum 500 words — published on
   their main platform
   - Article must include Rayna's unique coupon code exclusive to their
   readers
   - Article must include a link to raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780931619602000&sa=E>
   - Social media post on their official accounts sharing the article
   - For print publications — online version of the article as well
   - For Arabic publications — article must be in Arabic. Do not accept
   English-only for Arabic publications.
   - You review and approve every article before publication — no article
   goes live without your review. Ensure it reflects our positioning correctly
   — destination expert and ground operator, not a marketplace.

------------------------------

*THREE STORY ANGLES — USE THESE FOR ALL PITCHES*

   1. *The Founder Story* — From one travel desk in Dubai in 2006 to 1,600
   employees, 25 million guests, 116 cities worldwide. Indian founder, built
   in Dubai, now going global. Best for Economic Times, Forbes India, Gulf
   News, Arabian Business.
   2. *The AI Transformation Story* — How Rayna is using AI to expand from
   17 cities to 116 cities in 90 days. Best for tech and business publications
   across all three markets.
   3. *The Destination Expert Story* — Why booking with a ground operator
   gives travelers a better experience than booking with a marketplace. Best
   for travel publications and bloggers in all markets.

Manish — for Saudi publications, add a fourth angle: *Rayna's Saudi
expansion* — our Riyadh office, growing presence in Saudi, and commitment
to Vision 2030 tourism. Saudi media responds very well to local investment
stories.
------------------------------

*YOUR TARGET PUBLICATIONS*

*APARNA — INDIA:*

National English — Economic Times Travel, Times of India Travel, Hindustan
Times Travel, Business Standard, Forbes India (already in progress — close
immediately), Conde Nast Traveller India, Travel + Leisure India, Outlook
Traveller

Maharashtra Regional — Maharashtra Times, Lokmat, Sakal, Loksatta, Pune
Mirror, Mid-Day Mumbai

North India — Tribune Chandigarh, Dainik Bhaskar, Punjabi Tribune Amritsar,
Amar Ujala, Hindustan Times Delhi

Online Bloggers — Top 20 India-based travel bloggers with domain rating
above 30 and monthly traffic above 50,000. Use Ahrefs to identify and
evaluate.

*MANISH — UAE AND SAUDI:*

UAE English — Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, Gulf Business,
Time Out Dubai, Trade Arabia, Hindustan Times Gulf

UAE Arabic — Al Khaleej, Al Bayan, Emarat Al Youm, Al Ittihad

Saudi English — Arab News, Saudi Gazette, Arabian Business Saudi edition

Saudi Arabic — Okaz, Al Riyadh, Al Watan, Sabq

Online Bloggers UAE and Saudi — Top 20 travel bloggers in both markets —
Arabic and English. Use Ahrefs to evaluate. Arabic travel bloggers in Saudi
especially have large, engaged audiences and are very receptive to barter
deals.
------------------------------

*HOW TO APPROACH EACH PUBLICATION*

   1. Find the travel editor or lifestyle editor directly — not the general
   inbox. Use LinkedIn, the publication's masthead, or Lusha to find direct
   contact.
   2. Send a personalized pitch — reference a recent article they
   published, explain why Rayna is a story their readers will care about, and
   propose the barter clearly.
   3. Pitch in the publication's language — English for English
   publications, Arabic for Arabic publications, Hindi for Hindi publications.
   Never send a generic English pitch to a regional language publication.
   4. Follow up once after 5 days if no response. If still no response —
   move to next contact or next publication.
   5. Once agreed — send them the Rayna story angles, About Us page link,
   and brand positioning document. Make it easy for them to write a strong
   article.
   6. Review every article before publication. If positioning is wrong —
   correct it before it goes live.
   7. Once published — provide the unique coupon code for inclusion in the
   article and their social media posts.
   8. Track everything in the Google Sheet immediately after every
   interaction.

------------------------------

*YOUR TRACKING SHEET*

I have created a shared Google Sheet for both of you. Aparna updates the
India section. Manish updates the UAE and Saudi section. Both update the
weekly performance report every Friday.

*Sheet link:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1hzyKEo9_0cOZz740iscoBoJeqQl8Yq6By8lbkdcGgRg
<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1hzyKEo9_0cOZz740iscoBoJeqQl8Yq6By8lbkdcGgRg&source=gmail&ust=1780931619602000&sa=E>*

I check this sheet every Monday. If it is not updated — I assume the work
is not happening.
------------------------------

*YOUR DEADLINES*
Task Owner Deadline
Complete contact list of all target publications and editors built Both June
12th
Forbes India proposal closed Aparna June 10th — already in progress
First outreach wave sent — top 10 publications Both June 15th
First barter deal agreed Both June 20th
First article published Both July 15th
5 articles published each Both August 1st
10 articles published each Both August 31st
Weekly report updated in sheet Both Every Friday

The media coverage you build now directly supports our September campaign
launch, our Wikipedia submission, and our long term brand authority across
all three markets. Every article is evidence that Rayna is a serious,
credible company. Every coupon code registration is a new customer in our
database. Take this seriously and move fast.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Influencer & Affiliate Programme — Complete Brief + Meeting Tomorrow | Nihad
emails.body:
Hi Nihad,

I am assigning you full ownership of Rayna's influencer and affiliate
programme across UAE, India, and Saudi Arabia. This is one of the most
important growth channels we are building and I need you to move fast,
execute well, and report weekly.

Please read this fully. I also want you to come and speak to me tomorrow to
discuss this in person.
------------------------------

*THE BIG PICTURE — WHAT WE ARE BUILDING*

We are building a self-sustaining affiliate army — not just influencers,
but anyone with an audience. Travel bloggers, community admins, WhatsApp
group owners, Instagram creators, YouTube channels, TikTok creators, micro
influencers, nano influencers. Anyone who has followers and wants to make
money promoting travel can be a Rayna affiliate.

The model is simple:

   - Register on Rayna affiliate platform
   - Get a unique UTM link and QR code immediately upon registration
   - Get a unique discount code for their audience
   - Access Rayna's ready-to-post content library — reels, static posts,
   story formats, WhatsApp-ready content — all pre-made, plug and play
   - Schedule and post directly from the library
   - Their audience clicks, books using their code, and they earn
   commission automatically

No filming required. No editing required. No waiting. Content is already
made. They just post it.
------------------------------

*TASK 1 — CREATE THE AFFILIATE EXPLAINER VIDEO*
*Deadline: June 20th*

This is your first and most urgent task. I need you to create a complete
explainer video that shows influencers and potential affiliates exactly how
the Rayna affiliate programme works and how they can make money from it.

The video must cover:

   1. What is the Rayna affiliate programme and who can join — anyone with
   any size audience
   2. How to register on the Rayna affiliate platform — step by step
   3. What you get immediately upon registration — unique UTM link, QR
   code, discount code
   4. How to access the ready-to-post content library — browse, select,
   customize if needed
   5. How to schedule and post content directly — plug and play
   6. How your audience uses your discount code to book
   7. How commission is tracked and paid — real time dashboard
   8. Real earning examples — if 10 of your followers book a Thailand
   package, here is how much you earn

Format options — choose what you are most confident with:

   - *Option A — You present on camera* — if you are confident on camera,
   do a clean, professional walkthrough. Show the platform on screen. Talk
   through each step. Keep it under 3 minutes.
   - *Option B — AI avatar* — use HeyGen to create an AI presenter who
   delivers the script. Coordinate with Adnan who is already working with
   HeyGen for training videos.

Once the video is produced and approved by me — publish it on:

   - Instagram Reels — with a strong caption and call to action to register
   - YouTube — full version for anyone who wants to watch in detail
   - TikTok
   - The link must be included in every email campaign you send to
   influencers going forward

------------------------------

*TASK 2 — BUILD THE READY-TO-POST CONTENT LIBRARY*
*Deadline: June 20th — first batch. Ongoing forever after.*

Work with Malik and Vaibhav to build a library of ready-to-post affiliate
content. Every affiliate who registers must find content already waiting
for them — not a blank page.

For every destination and product — create:

   - 3 short form video reels — destination highlight, activity showcase,
   package offer
   - 3 static posts — clean design, product image, price, coupon code
   placeholder
   - 2 story formats — swipe up design, countdown offer
   - 1 WhatsApp-ready image — simple, designed to be forwarded

Start with our highest demand products:

   - Dubai — Desert Safari, Balloon Ride, Yacht, Water Sports, Floating
   Restaurant
   - Thailand — Bangkok, Phuket, Pattaya packages
   - Bali packages
   - Singapore packages
   - Malaysia packages
   - Maldives packages
   - Georgia packages

As new destinations go live — add content for them immediately. The library
must always be current. An affiliate should never log in and find outdated
content.

Every piece of content must have a placeholder for the affiliate's unique
discount code — so it is personalized when they post it.
------------------------------

*TASK 3 — EMAIL CAMPAIGN TO INFLUENCERS*
*Deadline: June 23rd — first campaign sent*

Two separate email campaigns to run simultaneously and continuously:

*Campaign 1 — Invite to Join Rayna Affiliate Programme*
Target: All influencers and content creators across UAE, India, and Saudi
who are not yet registered. Travel creators, lifestyle creators, food
creators, family creators, anyone with an engaged audience above 1,000
followers.

Message: "Turn your audience into income. Join Rayna's affiliate programme
— promote travel experiences your followers will love and earn commission
on every booking. Register in 2 minutes. Content already made for you.
Start earning today."

Include the explainer video link prominently. Include the registration
link. Include real earning examples.

*Campaign 2 — Weekly Offers to All Registered Affiliates*
Target: Everyone already registered on the affiliate platform.

Every week — send them the latest offers, new destinations that went live,
new content added to the library, and their performance stats from the
previous week. Keep them engaged, keep them posting.

Message format: "This week's top offers for your audience. New content
ready to post. Your earnings last week: [X]. Post this week and earn more."

Build your influencer contact list by:

   - Searching Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube for travel creators in UAE,
   India, and Saudi
   - Using Lusha — we have it connected — to find contact emails for larger
   creators
   - Reaching out via DM on Instagram for smaller creators
   - Posting about the affiliate programme on Rayna's own social media
   channels

------------------------------

*TASK 4 — HOSTED EXPERIENCES FOR TOP TIER INFLUENCERS*
*Ongoing — selective and strategic*

For our top tier influencers — 1 million+ followers — hosted experiences
are reserved for brand storytelling, not product promotion. Their job is to
tell the Rayna story — the ground operator, the owned assets, the founder
journey, what makes Rayna different.

Maximum 2-3 hosted experiences per market per quarter. Every hosted
experience must be fully documented — video, photography, behind the scenes
content. Coordinate with me and Senthil before committing to any hosted
experience.
------------------------------

*MULTILINGUAL APPROACH — ALL THREE MARKETS*

   - *UAE* — Hindi creators for Indian expat audience, Arabic creators for
   Emirati and Arab audience, Urdu creators for Pakistani audience, English
   creators for Western expat audience
   - *India* — Hindi creators primarily, regional language creators for
   Maharashtra (Marathi) and Punjab
   - *Saudi* — Arabic creators primarily, English for international expat
   audience in Saudi

Content library must have language-appropriate versions for each market.
Coordinate with Alok's content team to ensure Arabic and Hindi versions are
available.
------------------------------

*YOUR TRACKING SHEET*

I have created a Google Sheet for you to track everything — video
production, email campaigns, affiliate registrations, content library
status, and weekly performance.

*Sheet link:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PsluCD1LOb5TOtuwuulJKwJBDeznxr4j4oD1EicjUHU
<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PsluCD1LOb5TOtuwuulJKwJBDeznxr4j4oD1EicjUHU&source=gmail&ust=1780929645959000&sa=E>*

Update this sheet every week without fail. Every Friday — fill in the
weekly performance report section. I will check this sheet every Monday. If
it is not updated, I will assume the work is not happening.
------------------------------

*YOUR DEADLINES*
Task Deadline
Affiliate explainer video script written June 13th
Video produced and submitted to me for approval June 17th
Video approved and published on all platforms June 20th
First batch of content library ready — top 7 destinations June 20th
First invite email campaign sent to influencers — UAE June 23rd
First invite email campaign sent to influencers — India June 25th
First invite email campaign sent to influencers — Saudi June 25th
First weekly offer campaign sent to registered affiliates June 30th
100 affiliates registered across all three markets July 15th
500 affiliates registered across all three markets August 15th
Full affiliate army active for September campaign launch September 1st
------------------------------

*COME AND SEE ME TOMORROW*

I want to discuss this with you in person tomorrow. Come prepared with:

   1. Your initial thinking on the explainer video — format, script
   outline, how you plan to produce it
   2. Your plan for building the influencer contact list across all three
   markets
   3. Any questions or resources you need from the team

Nihad — this programme has the potential to give Rayna reach that no paid
media budget can match. Thousands of people promoting Rayna to their
audiences every day — automatically, at scale, in every language, across
every market. Build it right and it becomes one of Rayna's most powerful
growth engines. I am counting on you.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Addition 3 — Rayna Inspire Programme | Azhar + Rishi
emails.cc:
senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, alok@raynatours.com, 
	aparna@raynatours.com, nihad@raynatours.com, malik@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Azhar and Rishi,

One final addition to the edutainment programme. This one is close to my
heart.
------------------------------

*RAYNA INSPIRE — EXPERIENCE LUXURY, ACHIEVE MORE*

Most children from middle income families have never sat in a Rolls Royce.
Never stepped onto a yacht. Never seen their city from a hot air balloon.
They have seen these things on screens — but never felt them. And if you
have never felt what is possible, it is very hard to build the ambition to
achieve it.

Rayna Inspire is a programme where we give selected students — top
performers, most improved, essay competition winners — a curated luxury
experience day. Not as a reward only. As an inspiration.

*The Experience:*

   - Rolls Royce or premium luxury vehicle transfer to the venue — their
   first time in a car like this
   - Hot air balloon ride over Dubai — seeing their city from above for the
   first time
   - Yacht experience on Dubai Marina — stepping onto a private yacht for
   the first time
   - Fine dining or floating restaurant experience
   - Professional photography throughout — every moment captured and given
   to the student and their family as a keepsake

*The Message — delivered personally during the experience:*

A Rayna representative sits with the students during the experience and
speaks to them directly. The message is simple and honest:

*"Everything you are experiencing today — this balloon, this yacht, this
car — this is what the world can look like when you work hard and stay
disciplined. Rayna started from one single travel desk in a hotel lobby in
Dubai. One desk. Today we serve 25 million guests and operate across 116
cities. We built this through discipline and consistency — nothing else. If
we could build what we built, you can build whatever you dream of. Do good.
Stay disciplined. The world will open up for you."*

That message — coming from a real company that actually built something
from nothing, delivered while a child is floating above Dubai in a hot air
balloon — will stay with them for the rest of their life. No advertisement,
no assembly speech, no poster can do what that moment does.

*Why this works:*

   - Children who experience luxury understand what they are working
   towards. Ambition needs a destination. We give them one.
   - Students will photograph and share everything — organic social media
   content that no agency can produce
   - Parents see it and feel deeply grateful — these families become Rayna
   customers and advocates forever
   - School principals see genuine community investment — not advertising.
   They will promote Rayna permanently.
   - Media will cover this — "Travel company gives top students their first
   luxury experience" is a story Gulf News, Khaleej Times, and Economic Times
   India will publish. This directly supports our PR and Wikipedia strategy.
   - Every student who doesn't win will work harder next time to win. Every
   parent will want their child in a school that has this partnership.

*What this costs us:*
A balloon ride. A yacht hour. A luxury vehicle transfer. All assets we own
and operate. The operational cost is minimal. The return — lifetime brand
loyalty, press coverage, parent database, and genuine community impact — is
immeasurable.

*Important — Always with teacher and principal approval.*
Every Rayna Inspire experience is formally sanctioned by the school. Age
appropriate. Fully supervised. Parents informed and consenting. This is a
school partnership programme — not a surprise. Work with the principal on
every detail before any experience is organized.

*Nihad — note for you:*
Every Rayna Inspire experience must be documented on video and photography.
Short emotional reels for Instagram and TikTok — the moment a child sees
Dubai from a balloon for the first time. The moment they step onto a yacht.
The moment they receive their professional photographs. These are the most
powerful brand content pieces we can produce. Coordinate with Rishi to have
a content creator present at every Rayna Inspire event.

*Start:*
Run the first Rayna Inspire experience as part of the Phase 1 pilot schools
in July-August. Use it as the grand prize for the first Student Travel
Writing Competition winner. Document everything. Build the story. Then
expand.

Rishi — this programme is yours to own and build. Do it with care, with
genuine intention, and with the belief that what we are doing for these
children matters. Because it does.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Addition 2 — Student Travel Writing Competition | Scholarship + Multi-School Roadmap
emails.cc:
senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, alok@raynatours.com, 
	aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com, malik@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Azhar and Rishi,

Further additions to the Student Travel Writing Competition I outlined in
my previous email. Please read alongside that email.
------------------------------

*ADDITION 1 — ANNUAL SCHOLARSHIP AWARD*

Students who win the monthly competition throughout the year accumulate
points. The student with the most wins — or the strongest body of writing —
across the full academic year is announced as the *Rayna Annual Travel
Writing Scholar.*

The prize for the Annual Scholar:

   - *50% or 100% of school fees for the following year — sponsored by
   Rayna.* This is decided based on the quality of work and the impact of
   the programme at that school.
   - Announced at the school's annual award ceremony — the biggest event of
   the school year, in front of every student, parent, and teacher.
   - Rayna branded screen behind the stage plays during the announcement.
   Rayna representative presents the scholarship personally if possible.

Think about what this does. A child wins a scholarship because of their
writing about travel. Their parents' biggest annual expense — school fees —
is covered by Rayna. That family will be Rayna customers for life. Every
parent in that school will know about it. Every parent will want their
child to participate next year. The school will promote Rayna permanently
because we are funding their students' education.

This is not just marketing. This is genuine community investment that
creates lifelong brand loyalty at almost zero cost compared to what we get
in return.

Coordinate with Senthil and finance team to confirm the scholarship budget
per school per year before making this offer to any school.
------------------------------

*ADDITION 2 — PUBLISH MULTIPLE ARTICLES, NOT JUST THE WINNER*

The monthly winner gets the prize and the primary feature on raynatours.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780927349079000&sa=E>.
But there will always be other strong articles that deserve to be
published. Do not limit publication to one article per month.

Process for Alok and Aparna's content team:

   1. Review all monthly submissions
   2. Select the winner — published as the featured article with full
   promotion
   3. Select 3-5 additional strong articles — published on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780927349079000&sa=E>
   in the Student Travel Writers section with the student's name and school
   4. All published students are notified and encouraged to share their
   article with family and friends
   5. All articles SEO and AEO optimized before going live

This means instead of 1 article per month per school, we potentially get
4-6 published articles per school per month. Across 20-30 partner schools
across three markets — that is hundreds of fresh human-written articles
every month going onto raynatours.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780927349080000&sa=E>
permanently. This is a content engine that no agency can replicate and no
competitor will think to build.
------------------------------

*ADDITION 3 — COMPETITION GROWTH ROADMAP*

This competition starts small and grows into something significant. Here is
the roadmap:

*Phase 1 — Single school (July-August 2026)*
Launch in 2-3 pilot schools in UAE. Test the format, refine the topic list,
build the submission and publication process. Learn what works.

*Phase 2 — Multi-school within one city (September 2026)*
Expand to all partner schools in Dubai. Now students from different schools
compete against each other. Adds competition and excitement. Schools
promote it because they want their students to win against other schools.

*Phase 3 — Multi-city within UAE (Q4 2026)*
Expand across UAE — Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, RAK. UAE-wide competition.
Winner announced at a Rayna-hosted annual event. Rayna becomes known as the
company that runs the UAE's biggest student travel writing competition.

*Phase 4 — Multi-country (2027)*
India and Saudi schools join. Now it is an international competition — UAE
vs India vs Saudi students writing about travel. Cross-cultural
perspectives on travel. Rayna publishes the best from each country. Annual
international winner announced. Scholarship awarded. This is where the
competition becomes a significant PR story — Gulf News, Economic Times,
Arab News will cover it.

At Phase 4 — we have thousands of students writing about travel
destinations, tourist needs, and the future of travel across three
countries every month. All published on raynatours.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780927349080000&sa=E>.
All indexed by Google. All cited by AI search engines. All driving traffic.
All at almost zero cost.
------------------------------

Rishi — start Phase 1 immediately with your first 2-3 partner schools in
UAE. The goal is to have the pilot running by August so we can expand to
Phase 2 in September alongside our full campaign launch.

Azhar — this competition is a flagship Rayna brand programme. Treat it as
such when presenting to school principals. This is not a small activity.
This is a company that is investing in the education and development of
their students while building a meaningful connection with the school
community.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Addition to Edutainment Brief — Student Travel Writing Competition | Azhar + Rishi
emails.cc:
senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, alok@raynatours.com, 
	aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com
emails.body:
Hi Azhar and Rishi,

One important addition to the edutainment brief I just sent you. This is
one of the most powerful content and brand building ideas we have discussed.
------------------------------

*STUDENT TRAVEL WRITING COMPETITION — PERMANENT PROGRAMME*

We are launching a permanent student travel writing competition across all
partner schools in UAE, Saudi, and India. This runs every month — new
topics, new winners, new content.

*How it works:*

   1. Rayna publishes a list of trending travel topics every month — drawn
   from our destination data, search trends, new product launches, and
   industry news
   2. Students choose any topic from the list and write — essay, article,
   or opinion piece. Minimum 300 words.
   3. All entries submitted through the Rayna app — app download mandatory
   to participate
   4. Rayna's content team reviews all submissions
   5. Winners announced monthly — one winner per topic category
   6. Winning articles published on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780927056327000&sa=E>
   with the student's full name and school — they become published authors
   7. Prize — free Rayna experience for the student and their family.
   Balloon ride, water sports, desert safari, or a voucher for a holiday
   package.

*Example topics we can give students to write about:*

   - What would make Dubai the perfect holiday destination for my family
   - My dream holiday and why I want to go there
   - How AI will transform the travel industry in the next 10 years
   - What tourists really want from a beach holiday
   - The most underrated travel destination in the world and why
   - Why my country is the best destination for international tourists
   - What a perfect family holiday looks like in 2030
   - New travel products or experiences I wish existed — and why
   - How sustainable travel can change the world

Topics will be updated every month based on what is trending in travel,
what destinations we are launching, and what our AI content engine
identifies as high-demand search topics. Alok and Aparna will supply the
topic list to you monthly.

*Why this is powerful:*

   - We get hundreds and thousands of fresh, original, human-written
   articles — continuously, permanently, at almost zero cost
   - Human-written content is valued more by Google and AI search engines
   than AI-generated content
   - Students from India write about Indian travel preferences — directly
   relevant to our India source market. Students from Saudi write about Saudi
   traveler needs. Students from UAE write about what UAE residents want from
   travel. This is exactly the content our SEO and AEO strategy needs.
   - Published articles with student names — students share with family and
