emails.subject: Two Action Items — Competition API Access + 116 City Content Deadline | Deepak + Alok
emails.body:
Hi Deepak,

Two action items — one for you, one for Alok who is CC'd.
------------------------------

*DEEPAK — API Access from Competition*

We are building an AI-powered dynamic pricing engine that monitors
competitor prices in real time and adjusts our markups accordingly.
Currently we are using scraping tools but scraping is slow, sometimes
inaccurate, and misses options hidden behind availability checks. The right
solution is direct API access.

Please reach out to the following four companies and request access to
their affiliate or distribution API. You know these people well — please
use your existing relationships to get this done quickly.

   1. Headout
   2. Viator
   3. GetYourGuide
   4. Klook

Please forward the following draft to each of them:
------------------------------

*--- DRAFT EMAIL TO FORWARD ---*

Subject: API Access Request — Rayna Tours

Hi [Name],

Hope you are doing well. As you know, we are expanding our destination
portfolio significantly and are building a more robust technology
infrastructure to manage real-time pricing and availability across all our
markets.

We would like to explore API access to your platform so we can:

   - Access your product catalogue and availability data in real time
   - Integrate your inventory into our booking platform for our customers
   across India, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and globally
   - Maintain accurate and up-to-date pricing across our platform

Could you please share details of your affiliate or distribution API
programme — documentation, access requirements, and commercial terms?

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Deepak
Rayna Tours

*--- END OF DRAFT ---*
------------------------------

Please share all responses with me and Alok as they come in. Follow up
deadline: *June 10th.*
------------------------------

*ALOK — Complete Content Block for 116 Cities*

Alok — you are heading the content block for our destination expansion. I
need complete clarity on timelines from you.

Here is everything I need from you and your team:

   1. *Product content for all 116 cities* — tours, activities, transfers,
   attractions. Complete descriptions, images mapped, supplier linked, live on
   website.
   2. *Blog articles for all 116 cities* — destination guides, things to
   do, travel tips, FAQs. SEO and AEO optimized as per Sandstorm's content
   template once received.
   3. *Global Tix API* — we already have API access to Global Tix.
   Coordinate with Anwar immediately to understand what product content Global
   Tix can provide across our 116 cities. Use this to accelerate content
   wherever possible instead of building from scratch. I need to know which
   cities Global Tix covers and what content is available.
   4. *All other content sources* — whatever other APIs, tools, or sources
   you are using to build content at scale — confirm these are all connected
   and working.

I need you to come back to me with a clear, realistic deadline for:

   - By when will the first city — London — be fully complete and live?
   - By when will all 116 cities have product content live on the website?
   - By when will all 116 cities have blog articles and destination guides
   live?
   - What is blocking you right now — people, technology, supplier data, or
   something else?

Our September campaign launches September 1st. All 116 cities must be live
by August 31st. Work backwards from that date and give me your plan.

I need your timeline and status report by *June 10th.* No extensions on
this.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Destination Heads & Product Managers — Expectations, Approach & Deadlines
emails.cc:
senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>, 
	Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>, adnan@raynab2b.com
emails.body:
Hi Team,

As we expand Rayna Tours from our existing destinations to 116 cities
worldwide, I want to set clear expectations for every destination head and
product manager joining this journey. Please read this carefully. This is
not just a task list — it is the philosophy behind how we built Rayna into
the number one activity provider in Dubai, and how we will replicate that
in every city we enter.
------------------------------

*WHO THIS EMAIL IS FOR*
Every destination head and product manager — existing and new. Whether you
are managing Dubai, Singapore, London, Japan, or Maldives — these
expectations apply to you.
------------------------------

*PART ONE — WHAT YOU NEED TO DELIVER FOR EVERY DESTINATION*

*1. Hotels*
Initially, hotels will be sourced through Expedia. This is acceptable as a
starting point to get destinations live quickly. However your medium term
goal is to move towards direct hotel contracting. When you buy directly
from the hotel rather than through Expedia, our margins increase
significantly. As you build relationships in your destination, identify
hotels where direct contracting is possible and begin those conversations.
Upload all direct contracts into our contracting module as they are signed.
The more direct contracts you build, the more profitable your destination
becomes for Rayna.

*2. Activities — Three Ways to Connect With Suppliers*
This is where the real game is played. Understanding these three methods
deeply is non-negotiable. Adnan has recorded a complete training video
explaining how our tour module and all supplier connection methods work.
Watch this video fully before you begin any supplier outreach — link will
be shared by Adnan shortly.

*Method One — Supplier Module*
The simplest connection. We give the supplier access to our extranet. They
enter their own products directly. If they don't, our AI block enters the
products on their behalf. When a booking is made on Rayna, it goes directly
to the supplier. Either the supplier confirms manually and the customer
gets a voucher, or the system auto-confirms and the customer gets
confirmation immediately. The supplier has their own login to see all
bookings, transactions, and history. This is the starting point for most
new suppliers.

*Method Two — Own Barcode Technology*
This is where Rayna has a significant competitive advantage. Let me explain
with an example from Dubai. Dubai Dolphinarium used to confirm bookings via
email. Every agent in the market would email them asking for a specific
time slot and wait. There was no live booking system. We identified this as
a gap. We approached Dolphinarium and offered them our barcode system —
live time slot availability, instant confirmation, barcodes that scan at
the gate. They agreed. We became the only operator in Dubai selling
Dolphinarium with live availability. Business increased. We got better
prices because of volume. We controlled the B2B market for that product.

This is your job in every destination. Find the suppliers who have no live
booking system. Convince them to work with Rayna's barcode technology. Our
system gives the supplier full control — they can manage inventory, set
stop sales, control by date and time slot, limit numbers. For us, it means
live bookings, better margins, and market control.

Some suppliers will resist because they think it increases their admin.
Your job is to convince them that increased business justifies it. Most
suppliers, when they see the numbers, agree. We did this with almost every
major attraction in Dubai. You can do the same in your destination.

*Barcode Priority Order — This is Important:*
Not all barcode situations are the same. Here is the priority order I
expect every destination head to follow:

   1. *Priority 1 — Rayna's own barcodes.* Always your first approach with
   every supplier. The reason is simple — when we hold the barcodes, we have
   full control over no-shows and cancellations. Any barcode that goes
   unredeemed due to a no-show is effectively additional margin for Rayna.
   This is a real financial benefit that most people in this industry
   overlook. Always try to get the supplier onto our own barcode system first.
   2. *Priority 2 — Supplier's own barcodes, but only with barcode status
   reporting.* If a supplier insists on using their own barcode format,
   that is acceptable — but only if they can provide us with barcode status.
   We need to know which barcodes have been used and which have not. This can
   come through API or through regular supplier communication. Without status
   visibility, we lose the no-show and cancellation benefit. Always push for
   status reporting when dealing with supplier barcodes.
   3. *Priority 3 — Supplier module direct booking.* Use this as a starting
   point when a supplier is not yet ready for barcodes. But always work
   towards upgrading to Priority 1 or Priority 2 as the relationship develops.
   In the supplier module model, we have zero control over no-shows and
   cancellations.

*Method Three — PDF and QR Upload*
Some suppliers have their own barcode format and do not want to use ours.
No problem. We buy their PDFs or barcodes directly, upload them into our
system, and distribute to customers under our platform. The supplier gets
the business. The customer gets a seamless experience. We get the margin.
Your job is to maintain a strong enough relationship with the supplier that
they keep you informed about inventory status, availability, and any
changes.

*3. API Connections*
For large suppliers — major theme parks, world class attractions — who have
their own technology, we connect through API. This gives us real time
availability, time slot management, and live inventory. For products where
the customer needs to know availability instantly — like Burj Khalifa or
Museum of the Future — API is the right connection. Our tech team handles
the API build. Your job is to identify which suppliers in your destination
have API capability and bring that information to Adnan's team.

*4. Concierge and B2B Technology*
In every city there are concierges — hotel desks, travel agents, local
operators — who want to sell experiences to their customers but cannot deal
directly with big attractions because those attractions only work with
companies, not individuals. This is a gap. Rayna has technology that allows
any concierge to sell at retail price using the customer's credit card and
receive their commission automatically. We used this model successfully
with Ferrari World in Dubai for years. As you build your destination,
identify where this gap exists. Which attractions in your city cannot deal
directly with concierges? Bring that information to me and we will deploy
our technology to fill that gap. That gap becomes our business.
------------------------------

*PART TWO — THE MINDSET I EXPECT FROM EVERY DESTINATION HEAD*

Contracting and getting products live is the minimum expectation. That is
table stakes. What separates a good destination head from a great one is
the ability to find gaps in the market and solve them.

We did not become the number one activity provider in Dubai by simply
contracting suppliers and listing products. We became number one because we
identified problems in the market that nobody else was solving and we
solved them using our technology. Every gap we identified became a
competitive advantage — live bookings where only email existed, barcodes
where only paper tickets existed, concierge technology where no solution
existed.

Your job in your destination is the same. Go to the market. Understand how
people are trading. Talk to suppliers. Talk to agents. Ask: what is the
problem here? What is inefficient? What is manual that should be automated?
What is offline that should be online?

When you find a problem — bring it to me. If our existing technology solves
it, we deploy it. If it doesn't, we build something that does. Once we
solve a problem that nobody else in your market has solved, we win that
market.

This is how Rayna thinks. This is how I expect you to think.
------------------------------

*PART THREE — WHAT IS CENTRALIZED AND WHAT IS YOUR RESPONSIBILITY*

*Centralized — handled by Rayna HQ:*

   - All marketing — SEO, AEO, performance marketing, social media, email,
   WhatsApp
   - AI driven content and distribution
   - Competition analysis
   - Customer support
   - Reservation AI agent
   - Technology platform — website, app, supplier module, booking engine

*Your responsibility — destination level:*

   - Supplier contracting — hotels and activities
   - Pricing and margin management
   - Inventory management
   - Supplier relationship management
   - Market gap identification
   - Technology adoption at supplier level
   - Direct hotel contracting progression over time

You focus on the destination. We handle everything else.
------------------------------

*TIMELINES*

All destinations must be fully contracted and live on raynatours.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780832414261000&sa=E>
by *August 31st 2026.* Our September marketing campaign launches September
1st across India, UAE, and Saudi Arabia. Every destination must be ready.
This is a hard deadline — not a target.
------------------------------

*NEXT STEPS*

   1. *Adnan* will share a complete training video covering the full tour
   module and all supplier connection methods. Watch it fully before beginning
   any supplier outreach.
   2. *Deepak* will share destination assignments via the destination
   ownership tracker shortly.
   3. Once assigned — begin supplier outreach immediately. Do not wait.
   4. Weekly update to your reporting head — what has been contracted, what
   is live, what problems have been identified.

I am available to anyone who needs guidance on supplier strategy or gap
identification. Do not hesitate to reach out directly.

We built the best operation in Dubai from scratch. Now we do it in 116
cities. The opportunity is enormous. I expect each of you to approach your
destination with the same hunger and problem-solving mindset that built
this company.

*Your experience, our responsibility.*

emails.subject: Pending SEO Issues, Growth Recommendations & Support Requirements
emails.body:
Dear Manish,

As discussed yesterday, I am sharing the list of pending issues, recommendations, and requirements.

Note: Ranking fluctuations may continue until the below technical, content, and SEO improvements are implemented. Delays in implementing these recommendations may result in continued ranking volatility and missed growth opportunities.

Key Areas Requiring Attention


	XML Sitemap improvements (missing URLs, incorrect URLs, outdated URLs).
	Redirect management process for discontinued pages.
	Hidden H1 tags on search and city pages.
	Incorrect OG URLs on Packages and Activities city pages.
	Dynamic schema implementation on Visa pages.
	FAQ schema missing on detail pages.
	Organizational schema implementation (currently cannot be implemented as the homepage is a replica of the Activities page).
	Staging and development subdomain indexation control.
	Core Web Vitals and page speed improvements.
	Mobile-first and responsive design enhancements.
	Dynamic image alt text implementation.
	Missing content on country and category pages.
	Homepage content is a replica of the Activities page.
	Read More and review sections require better visibility. (When the new design was initially shared, I agreed with the approach. However, based on our recent analysis, we recommend moving the Read More content back to a more visible position on the page. For activity pages, the Highlights section can remain at the top, but we recommend adding a short introductory paragraph with a "Read More" option before the Highlights section. This will improve content visibility for both users and search engines while maintaining the current design structure.)


The issues have already been reported through tickets; however, some of them have remained unresolved for a considerable period.

Travel Guide & Topical Authority Development

((Alok and Team, along with the SEO team, are working on this. By this Monday, the SEO team will share all requirements, topic suggestions, content formats, and template structures with Alok for better understanding and implementation. We have already shared the editor requirements and one sample article format.).


	Build destination-based topic clusters.
	Strengthen internal linking between Travel Guides, Activities, Packages, Cruises, and Visa pages.
	Implement Article, FAQ, Video, How-To, and Breadcrumb schema.
	Add author attribution and expertise signals.
	Create question-based travel content targeting AI Overviews and Featured Snippets.
	Improve semantic coverage and entity-based content strategy.


SEO & Content Optimization Activities


	Ongoing title and meta optimization.
	Better keyword placement in headings and content.
	Internal linking improvements.
	Featured Snippet and AI Overview optimization.
	Content refresh program.
	Review management and brand authority building.
	Entity SEO and brand mention expansion.
	Monitoring AI search visibility and competitor performance.


We need an experienced travel writer who has previously worked in the travel and tourism industry and understands how to create SEO, AEO (Answer Engine Optimization), and AI-friendly content. This will help us produce more authoritative, user-focused, and search-optimized content that aligns with current search trends and AI-driven search experiences.

Recommendations

Content Team

The content across the website is currently quite basic and does not fully align with modern SEO, AEO, and GEO requirements. To improve content quality, authority, and competitiveness, I recommend:


	Collaborating with experienced travel bloggers and industry contributors.
	Hiring an additional senior travel writer with travel industry experience.
	Expanding destination, attraction, visa, cruise, and activity content.
	Creating more experience-driven and authoritative travel content.
	Strengthening editorial review and content quality processes.


There are several keywords assigned by the Sales Team, such as "Singapore Packages from Dubai" and "Singapore Packages from India." If we can introduce destination/country-specific folders (for example, raynatours.com/in/), it would greatly help our SEO strategy.

This would allow us to target users from different markets with dedicated pages, such as targeting Dubai users on the default page and India users through country-specific pages. Currently, it is very challenging to optimize a single URL for three or four different location-based keywords and search intents simultaneously.

Review & Content Management

Many pages currently have missing, outdated, or irrelevant reviews, as well as spelling and content errors. Since manually reviewing all pages is very time-consuming, we should either hire a dedicated resource/team for review and content quality management or provide the SEO team with the necessary backend access to manage reviews and content updates efficiently. This will help improve user trust, content quality, and SEO performance.

Change Management & Product Release Process

All website changes should be communicated to the SEO team before they go live. Currently, we often become aware of changes only after deployment, and by the time issues are identified and reported, rankings may already be affected.

Before releasing any new page, content update, design change, or product update, it should be reviewed by the SEO and Content teams. In many cases, pages are published with AI-generated content and then updated gradually over time. However, these pages can get indexed and start ranking before proper content optimization and human review are completed.

Since AI-generated content alone is often insufficient without expert review, a pre-launch review process involving the Product, Content, SEO, and IT teams should be established. This will help ensure quality, reduce SEO risks, and prevent ranking fluctuations caused by avoidable issues.



Note: it would be even better if the entire process could be automated and AI-driven, reducing manual effort, improving efficiency, and ensuring consistency across all pages.

Budget & Tool Management

A dedicated monthly SEO budget should be allocated for homepage promotions, link building, PR activities, and SEO tools. Currently, backlink budgets are managed separately by individual sales departments, which creates inconsistency in execution. If any Sales Head pauses guest post spending for a month, link-building activities stop immediately, directly impacting SEO growth and rankings.

Similarly, tool subscriptions should be managed under a centralized SEO/core team budget rather than depending on individual departments.

SEO requires continuous investment and cannot be managed in cycles where activities are stopped for a few months and then restarted. For example, when guest post activities were paused, backlink acquisition stopped for nearly two months, affecting SEO momentum. A centralized budget and approval process through the core management team would ensure consistency, faster approvals, and uninterrupted SEO execution across all departments.

Best Regards,

Aparna Sisodia

SEO

Rayna Tours

emails.subject: Action Required — Training Video + Barcode Strategy for Destination Heads
emails.body:
Hi Adnan,

Two things from me today.

*ONE — Record a Complete Training Video on the Tour Module*
I want you to record a comprehensive video explaining how our complete
activity operations and tour module works. This video will be shared with
all destination heads and product managers so they are fully briefed on day
one — without you needing to repeat this explanation every time someone new
joins.

Please create this using AI avatar technology — *HeyGen (heygen.com
<https://www.google.com/url?q=http://heygen.com&source=gmail&ust=1780831576327000&sa=E>)*
is the recommended tool. Here is how:

   1. Go to heygen.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://heygen.com&source=gmail&ust=1780831576327000&sa=E>
   and create an account
   2. Record a short video of yourself — minimum 2 minutes, speaking
   clearly to camera in good lighting. HeyGen creates your AI avatar clone
   from this.
   3. Write a complete script covering everything below
   4. Enter the script into HeyGen — your AI clone delivers the full
   explanation
   5. Review, finalize, and share with me for approval

*Script must cover:*

   1. Introduction — what Rayna's activity operation model is and why it is
   different from other operators
   2. Supplier module — complete walkthrough, booking flow, confirmation
   types, supplier login
   3. Barcode strategy — priority order explained below in detail
   4. PDF and QR upload — when and how to use
   5. API connections — when needed, how to identify API-ready suppliers,
   what to communicate to the tech team
   6. Concierge technology — the Ferrari World example, how to identify the
   gap in a new destination
   7. Finding market gaps — the mindset, what to look for, how to escalate
   to Manoj

*Deadline for the video: June 20th.*
After my approval it goes into Rayna's internal training library. Every new
destination head watches this on day one.
------------------------------

*TWO — Barcode Priority Strategy for All Destination Heads*
Please make sure the following priority order is clearly explained in the
training video. This is critical for every destination head to understand.

*Priority 1 — Rayna's Own Barcodes (Always first approach)*
When approaching any new supplier, always try to get them on our own Rayna
barcode system first. This is our strongest position. Here is why: when a
customer does not show up or cancels, that barcode is in our hands. We
control it. That unredeemed barcode represents real additional margin — it
is effectively a no-show benefit that very few people in this industry talk
about openly. On top of that, our own barcode system gives us live
inventory control, time slot management, and stop sale capability. Every
destination head must make this their first conversation with every
supplier.

*Priority 2 — Supplier's Own Barcodes (Acceptable with one condition)*
If a supplier insists on using their own barcode format, that is acceptable
— but only on one condition. The supplier must be able to provide barcode
status — which barcodes have been used and which have not. This status can
come through API integration or through regular supplier communication.
Without barcode status visibility, we lose the no-show and cancellation
benefit entirely. When negotiating with any supplier on their own barcodes,
always push for status reporting. If they agree — proceed. If they cannot
provide status — go back to Priority 1 and push harder for our own barcodes.

*Priority 3 — Supplier Module Direct Booking (Last resort)*
The standard supplier module where bookings go directly to the supplier is
perfectly functional for getting products live quickly. However in this
model we have zero control over no-shows and cancellations. The supplier
owns the booking. Use this as a starting point when a supplier is not ready
for barcodes — but always work towards upgrading to Priority 1 or Priority
2 over time as the relationship develops.

*Summary for destination heads:*
Always ask yourself — can I get this supplier on our barcodes? If yes, do
it. If not, can I get their barcodes with status reporting? If yes, do it.
Only fall back to the supplier module when neither barcode option is
possible right now. And even then — keep working on upgrading the
relationship.
------------------------------

Please confirm receipt and share the completed training video with me by
June 20th.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Re: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required
emails.body:
Sure



From: vaibhav@raynatours.com

Sent: Sat, 06 Jun 2026 01:25:59

To: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com>

Cc: anket@raynatours.com, malik@raynatours.com, senthil@raynatours.com

Subject: Re: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required

 
Anket/Malik - let’s connect on this on Monday. We did a demo with madgicx and also scalable agency. 

 

Anket - given the work we have done so far and previous analysis we have done here, let’s summarise our findings

 


Vaibhav Gupta
+971 55 873 9290 





 

On Fri, 5 Jun 2026 at 8:18 PM Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> wrote:



Hi Anket and Malik,

I want to take our performance marketing to the next level — automated, AI-driven, and connected directly to our product inventory. Here is the plan and what I need from both of you.


THE DIRECTION

As we scale to 100+ cities and thousands of products, manually creating and managing ad campaigns is not possible. We need a system where a new product goes live on our website and an ad campaign is automatically created and starts running on Google and Meta — with zero manual work per product.

This will happen in two phases:

Phase 1 — Immediate: Use a third party tool called Madgicx to automate our Google and Meta campaigns right now. This gets us running properly and generating real performance data while we build our own solution.

Phase 2 — In-house build: Malik's team builds Rayna's own ad automation module as an extension of the marketing platform already being built. When a product goes live on the website, it triggers an automatic ad campaign on Google and Meta through our own system. Full ownership, full control, zero dependency on third parties.


ANKET — YOUR TASKS:


	Study Madgicx thoroughly — madgicx.com. Understand all its capabilities for Google and Meta automation, product feed integration, audience targeting, and budget management.
	Evaluate if Madgicx meets our requirements — automated campaign creation from product feed, separate targeting for India, UAE, and Saudi, full transparency on spend, hard budget limits the AI cannot exceed.
	Get a demo and pricing from Madgicx — understand the cost vs our current manual management cost.
	Come back to me with your assessment and a recommended go-live date for Madgicx. I want this information by June 12th.


MALIK — YOUR TASKS:

The vision: product goes live on raynatours.com → system automatically creates ad campaign on Google and Meta → campaign starts running with preset budget and targeting rules → performance data feeds back into the system for optimization.

This is a priority. Our September India and UAE campaign launch depends on having automated, scalable performance marketing in place.

Thanks,

Manoj

emails.subject: Performance Marketing Automation — Madgicx + In-House Build | Action Required
emails.body:
Hi Anket and Malik,

I want to take our performance marketing to the next level — automated,
AI-driven, and connected directly to our product inventory. Here is the
plan and what I need from both of you.
------------------------------

*THE DIRECTION*

As we scale to 100+ cities and thousands of products, manually creating and
managing ad campaigns is not possible. We need a system where a new product
goes live on our website and an ad campaign is automatically created and
starts running on Google and Meta — with zero manual work per product.

This will happen in two phases:

*Phase 1 — Immediate:* Use a third party tool called *Madgicx* to automate
our Google and Meta campaigns right now. This gets us running properly and
generating real performance data while we build our own solution.

*Phase 2 — In-house build:* Malik's team builds Rayna's own ad automation
module as an extension of the marketing platform already being built. When
a product goes live on the website, it triggers an automatic ad campaign on
Google and Meta through our own system. Full ownership, full control, zero
dependency on third parties.
------------------------------

*ANKET — YOUR TASKS:*

   1. Study Madgicx thoroughly — madgicx.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=https://madgicx.com&source=gmail&ust=1780756830737000&sa=E>.
   Understand all its capabilities for Google and Meta automation, product
   feed integration, audience targeting, and budget management.
   2. Evaluate if Madgicx meets our requirements — automated campaign
   creation from product feed, separate targeting for India, UAE, and Saudi,
   full transparency on spend, hard budget limits the AI cannot exceed.
   3. Get a demo and pricing from Madgicx — understand the cost vs our
   current manual management cost.
   4. Come back to me with your assessment and a recommended go-live date
   for Madgicx. I want this information by *June 12th.*

*MALIK — YOUR TASKS:*

   1. Study the Madgicx product feed and API structure — understand exactly
   how it connects product data to Google and Meta ad platforms. This is the
   blueprint for what we will build in-house.
   2. Study Google Ads API and Meta Marketing API — specifically dynamic
   product advertising. When a product goes live in our database, how does
   that trigger an automatic ad campaign? What data does each platform need —
   image, description, price, URL, targeting parameters?
   3. Plan the in-house ad automation module — as an extension of the
   marketing platform you are already building. The vision: product goes live
   on raynatours.com
   <https://www.google.com/url?q=http://raynatours.com&source=gmail&ust=1780756830737000&sa=E>
   → system automatically creates ad campaign on Google and Meta → campaign
   starts running with preset budget and targeting rules → performance data
   feeds back into the system for optimization.
   4. Come back to me with a feasibility assessment and estimated build
   timeline by *June 15th.*

------------------------------

*WHAT I NEED FROM BOTH OF YOU TOGETHER:*

A joint input by *June 15th* covering:

   1. Madgicx — is it the right tool? If not, suggest an alternative. What
   is the go-live date?
   2. In-house build — is it feasible as an extension of the current
   marketing platform? What is the realistic timeline?
   3. How do Phase 1 and Phase 2 connect — can Madgicx data inform the
   in-house build?

Vaibhav — please oversee this and make sure Anket and Malik are aligned.
The marketing platform you are building and the ad automation must work
together seamlessly.

This is a priority. Our September India and UAE campaign launch depends on
having automated, scalable performance marketing in place.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Re: Please check if this is ok to go or u suggest some changes
emails.body:
Hi,

Confirming receipt of both emails — the QA onboarding brief and the phased
launch plan. Here is where things stand and an honest read on what is
achievable.

QA agency search — progress
I have called the marketing agency and 5 QA agencies. 1 has submitted a
proposal; 2 more will submit by June 8. All have relevant travel/ecommerce
experience, and I will have my top-two recommendations to you by June 8 as
planned.

The hard reality on timeline
Every agency I have spoken to needs a minimum of 2 weeks for onboarding and
knowledge transfer, plus roughly a month for a first full regression cycle
— even with AI tools (Testim/Mabl/Katalon). None can begin meaningful
testing by June 9 or validate the app before June 10. This is not about
cost or quality; it is the structural reality of bringing an external team
up to speed on our product, staging, and test accounts. The 48-hour
assumption is not achievable with any serious agency.

What this means: external QA cannot be the gate for June 10. But it fits
perfectly as the gate for market expansion.

What IS achievable for June 10
The UAE launch is achievable — gated on internal QA. Our internal team is
running the full booking, payment, push, offline, and support flows, and we
fix the bugs they surface before launch. UAE being a controlled first
market is exactly why the phased plan works as the safety net.

The real blocker — app store release timing
Flagging this today as time-sensitive. Even after approval, Google Play
(new-app review + staged rollout) can take more than 48 hours, and Apple
has its own review window. To be genuinely live on June 10, builds need to
be submitted for review by June 6–7 at the latest. If submission slips past
that, the public availability date moves regardless of how ready we are.

Recommendation — decouple the two tracks
1. Launch June 10 to UAE gated on internal QA + bug fixes (GO, conditional
on app store submission this week).
2. Onboard the external agency now as our permanent QA partner over the
next 2–4 weeks. They become the formal quality gate before India (June 25)
— exactly when they will be ready, and aligned with your "only expand if
UAE is stable at 4.0+" rule.

This gives us both: we hit June 10, and we do not compromise the
external-QA standard — we apply it at the gate where it adds real value
(expansion), not at a deadline no agency can hit.

Go/no-go for June 10: GO — conditional on (a) app store builds submitted by
June 7, and (b) internal QA clearing critical/high bugs by June 9.



Thanks,
Gaurav

emails.subject: RE: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered
emails.body:
Dear Sir,

 

Good Afternoon. 

 

With regard to Inder Thakral's case, I would like to provide some background from the HR side.

 

After his exit, the HR team in India initiated the exit formalities and followed up with his dept (B2B India / Ranjan) for the required clearance and NOC to process his Full & Final Settlement. However, the required NOC from HOD was not received, and the matter was subsequently escalated.

 

Upon receiving Inder's follow-up emails, I again checked with the concerned Ranjan, who advised that the settlement should be kept on hold and that he would handle the matter directly from his side.

As per the process, HR cannot proceed with a Full & Final Settlement without the final clearance and NOC from the concerned HOD. Since the required approval was not provided, the settlement could not be processed from our end. (Please refer to attached mail)

 

I would also like to clarify that HR does not have the authority to direct HODs to issue clearances or approve settlements. We follow up, facilitate the process, and can only proceed once the necessary approvals are received.

 

While HR has been responding to employee complaints and queries on a regular basis, in this particular case no further response was sent as the matter had been taken over by the HOD for direct handling.

Going forward, we will ensure that all such employee emails receive a formal response from HR, even if the matter is pending with another department, so that employees remain informed of the status of their case.

 

We will also review all pending employee complaints and settlement cases and share a status report by 10 June, as requested.

 

Thanks & Regards,

 



 

From: Manoj Tulsani <manoj@raynatours.com> 
Sent: Friday, June 5, 2026 12:02 PM
To: Pari <pari@raynatours.com>; Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>
Cc: senthil@raynatours.com
Subject: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered

 

Hi Pari and Vaibhav,

I want to address something that I feel very strongly about.

I have seen the email thread from Inder Thakral regarding his Full & Final settlement. This person has been following up for more than 8 months with no proper response from our side. He has now CC'd the Maharashtra Labour Commissioner, Ministry of External Affairs, and MOHRE in his emails. This situation has reached this point only because our team did not respond.

I want to make this absolutely clear — no employee email should ever go unanswered.

If we are holding someone's settlement because they did something wrong, that is our right. But we must communicate that in writing clearly and immediately. We must state exactly what the employee did wrong, what the consequences are, and what our position is. We have the proofs. We have the records. Use them. Put it in writing. Send it.

Silence is not a strategy. Silence makes us look guilty even when we are right. And when employees start CC'ing government authorities, it creates legal and reputational risk for Rayna that could have been completely avoided with one proper email response months ago.

Effective immediately — I expect the following:

1.	Every employee complaint or query must receive a written response within 48 hours — no exceptions
2.	If a settlement is being withheld for any reason, the employee must be informed in writing with the exact reason and supporting evidence
3.	No employee email — whether from current or ex-employees — should remain unanswered for more than 48 hours
4.	For this specific case — Inder Thakral — I want a full written response sent to him today explaining Rayna's position clearly. CC me and Senthil on that email.

Pari — please also review if there are any other pending employee complaints or settlement cases that have not been responded to. Share a status report with me by June 10th.

This is not negotiable. We are a company of 1,600 people. Our reputation with employees matters as much as our reputation with customers.

Thanks,
Manoj

Hi Pari 

 

We will not do for him , let him struggle as he left us when I needed him most .

 

 

Thanks

 

Ranjan Singh | Country Head – India

emails.subject: Employee Communication Policy — No Email Should Go Unanswered
emails.body:
Hi Pari and Vaibhav,

I want to address something that I feel very strongly about.

I have seen the email thread from Inder Thakral regarding his Full & Final
settlement. This person has been following up for more than 8 months with
no proper response from our side. He has now CC'd the Maharashtra Labour
Commissioner, Ministry of External Affairs, and MOHRE in his emails. This
situation has reached this point only because our team did not respond.

I want to make this absolutely clear — *no employee email should ever go
unanswered.*

If we are holding someone's settlement because they did something wrong,
that is our right. But we must communicate that in writing clearly and
immediately. We must state exactly what the employee did wrong, what the
consequences are, and what our position is. We have the proofs. We have the
records. Use them. Put it in writing. Send it.

Silence is not a strategy. Silence makes us look guilty even when we are
right. And when employees start CC'ing government authorities, it creates
legal and reputational risk for Rayna that could have been completely
avoided with one proper email response months ago.

*Effective immediately — I expect the following:*

   1. Every employee complaint or query must receive a written response
   within 48 hours — no exceptions
   2. If a settlement is being withheld for any reason, the employee must
   be informed in writing with the exact reason and supporting evidence
   3. No employee email — whether from current or ex-employees — should
   remain unanswered for more than 48 hours
   4. For this specific case — Inder Thakral — I want a full written
   response sent to him today explaining Rayna's position clearly. CC me and
   Senthil on that email.

Pari — please also review if there are any other pending employee
complaints or settlement cases that have not been responded to. Share a
status report with me by June 10th.

This is not negotiable. We are a company of 1,600 people. Our reputation
with employees matters as much as our reputation with customers.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Re: Addition to PR Brief — Local Print Media Pune & North India | Phase 1
emails.body:
Noted sir 



From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Fri, 05 Jun 2026 13:13:11

To: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Addition to PR Brief — Local Print Media Pune & North India | Phase 1

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

One addition to the PR brief I just sent.

While working on national and international publications, please do not ignore local print media in our Phase 1 India activation cities — Pune, Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad, Nagpur, Kolhapur, Delhi, Chandigarh, Ludhiana, and Amritsar.

Local print scope to add to your plan:

Maharashtra:

Maharashtra Times, Lokmat, Sakal, Pune Mirror, Loksatta, Mid-Day Mumbai

North India:

Hindustan Times Delhi, Tribune Chandigarh, Dainik Bhaskar, Punjabi Tribune Amritsar, Amar Ujala

What to do locally:


	Paid advertorial features — travel supplement pages in Sunday editions. These are paid but editorial in format — much more effective than display ads for brand building.
	Press releases to local travel editors announcing Rayna's India activation — new destinations, summer packages, local office in Pune.
	Local journalist relationships — identify the travel editor at each publication and build a direct relationship. Local journalists are much easier to access than national ones and their readership is exactly our Phase 1 target audience.


This is part of Phase 1 India activation — June to August. Low cost, high local impact. Complements the national publications you are targeting simultaneously.

Please incorporate this into your plan and include local print in your journalist contact list due June 10th.

Thanks,

Manoj

emails.subject: PR Outreach Brief — UAE, India & Global Media | Wikipedia Evidence Building
emails.body:
Hi Aparna and Manish,

Following your update on Wikipedia — you are right that we need more
independent press coverage before Wikipedia will approve a Rayna Tours
article. This makes our PR push urgent for two reasons: brand building
across India, UAE, and Saudi, and building the evidence base Wikipedia
requires to approve our article.

I want you to treat this as a high priority workstream starting today. Here
is the complete brief.
------------------------------

*STEP ZERO — READ THIS BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE*
Before you contact a single journalist or write a single press release,
both of you must read our complete About Us page thoroughly:
https://www.raynatours.com/about-us
<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.raynatours.com/about-us&source=gmail&ust=1780730690260000&sa=E>

This page has the complete founder story, the company journey, our
philosophy, our owned assets, and everything that makes Rayna different.
Every press release, every pitch, every story angle must come from this.
Journalists and editors need to feel that they understand who we are and
why our story matters. If you haven't read this deeply, you cannot pitch us
correctly.
------------------------------

*WHY THIS IS URGENT*

Wikipedia and Wikidata are among the most heavily weighted sources for AI
engines — ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews all use Wikipedia to
verify entity authority. Without a Wikipedia article and sufficient
independent press coverage, Rayna's AI citability score will remain low
regardless of what Sandstorm does on the technical side. Press coverage and
Wikipedia are the brand authority layer that makes everything else work.
------------------------------

*TARGET PUBLICATIONS — PRIORITY ORDER*

*UAE / Middle East (Highest priority — these directly support Wikipedia
notability):*

   1. Gulf News — largest English daily in UAE. Target: founder story
   feature, Rayna's expansion to 116 destinations, AI transformation story
   2. Khaleej Times — second largest UAE English daily. Target: travel
   industry expert opinion piece
   3. Arabian Business — business audience, B2B credibility. Target:
   Rayna's growth story, ground operator vs marketplace positioning
   4. Gulf Business — Target: AI transformation at Rayna, founder profile
   5. Trade Arabia — travel trade publication. Target: destination
   expansion, 116 cities announcement
   6. Time Out Dubai — lifestyle angle. Target: top experiences in Dubai,
   Rayna as the operator behind them
   7. Arab News — Saudi edition. Target: Rayna's Saudi expansion, Vision
   2030 travel opportunity

*India (Brand building + Wikipedia evidence):*

   1. Economic Times Travel — Target: Indian founder built from one travel
   desk to 1,600 people story
   2. Times of India Travel — Target: Best destinations for Indian
   travelers, Rayna as the expert
   3. Hindustan Times Travel — Target: Summer travel packages from India,
   Rayna guide
   4. Business Standard — Target: Rayna's India B2C launch, AI
   transformation story
   5. Conde Nast Traveller India — Target: Luxury travel experiences,
   Rayna's owned assets
   6. Travel + Leisure India — Target: Destination expert profile, top
   experiences in Dubai
   7. Outlook Traveller — Target: Rayna's ground operations story

*Global / Digital (High domain authority — critical for Wikipedia and AEO):*

   1. Forbes India — already in progress. Close this immediately.
   2. Skift — leading global travel industry publication. DR 80+. Target:
   AI transformation in travel, Rayna as case study
   3. PhocusWire — travel technology publication. Target: Rayna's AI
   reservation agent and content engine
   4. Travel Weekly — trade publication. Target: Rayna's global expansion,
   116 destinations

------------------------------

*THREE STORY ANGLES — USE THESE FOR ALL OUTREACH*

*Angle 1 — The Founder Story (Best for India media and Forbes)*
"From one travel desk at Flora Grand Hotel Dubai in 2006 to 1,600
employees, 9 global offices, and 25 million guests served — how Manoj
Tulsani built one of the UAE's largest ground operators from scratch
without external funding."

*Angle 2 — The AI Transformation Story (Best for business and tech media)*
"How Rayna Tours is using AI to expand from 17 cities to 116 cities in 90
days — replacing human content teams with AI content engines, building an
AI reservation agent, and automating marketing distribution across 1.8
million customers."

*Angle 3 — The Ground Operator vs Marketplace Story (Best for travel trade
media)*
"Why the future of travel belongs to ground operators, not marketplaces —
how Rayna Tours is positioning owned operations, direct supplier
relationships, and local expertise against global OTAs like Viator and
GetYourGuide."
------------------------------

*IMPORTANT — FORMAT FOR ALL PRESS FEATURES*
We will not be doing live one-on-one journalist interviews at this stage.
All features must use one of the following formats only:

   1. *Written Q&A* — journalist sends questions, we provide written
   answers. Answers reviewed and approved by me before submission. This is the
   preferred format.
   2. *Ghost-written opinion piece* — you draft a thought leadership
   article in my voice using the About Us story and the angles above. I
   review, approve, and it gets published under my name. Works very well for
   Arabian Business, Gulf Business, and Economic Times.
   3. *Company story feature* — journalist writes about Rayna as a company,
   draws from About Us and press materials. Minimal direct interaction
   required. Most common format for business features.

Do not commit to any live interview format without checking with me first.
------------------------------

*WHAT YOU NEED TO DO — STEP BY STEP*

*Step 1 — Compile existing press coverage (By June 7th)*
Search and compile every article, mention, or feature Rayna Tours has
received in any publication — UAE, India, or global. Include URL,
publication name, date, and domain rating if known. This is the evidence
file we need for Wikipedia notability assessment.

*Step 2 — Build journalist contact list (By June 10th)*
For each target publication above, find the name and email of the travel
editor or business editor. Build a contact spreadsheet — publication,
journalist name, email, beat, last article they wrote. Do not send generic
press releases to info@ addresses. Find the right journalist for each story
angle.

*Step 3 — Write three press release templates (By June 12th)*
One press release for each of the three story angles above. These are
templates — they get customized for each publication. Keep them tight — 400
words maximum, quote from me in each one, clear news hook, company
background at the bottom. All content must be based on the About Us page
and approved by me before any outreach.

*Step 4 — Begin outreach (From June 13th)*
Start with UAE publications first — Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian
Business. Then India. Then global. Personalize every pitch — reference a
recent article the journalist wrote, explain why this story is relevant to
their readers. Never send a mass blind pitch.

*Step 5 — Wikipedia draft (Once 3+ new articles are published)*
Once we have at least 3 new significant independent articles published —
preferably including Gulf News or Arabian Business — begin drafting the
Wikipedia article. The article must be written in a neutral encyclopedic
tone, not marketing language. It must cite the independent press coverage
as sources. Do not submit to Wikipedia until Manish and I have reviewed and
approved the draft.
------------------------------

*DEADLINES:*

   - Read About Us page fully — Today
   - Existing press coverage compiled — June 7th
   - Journalist contact list built — June 10th
   - Three press release templates written and submitted to me for approval
   — June 12th
   - UAE outreach begins — June 13th
   - India outreach begins — June 15th
   - First article published target — July 15th
   - Wikipedia draft ready — August 1st
   - Wikipedia article submitted — August 15th

This is one of the most important long-term brand building exercises we are
doing. Press coverage compounds — one good Gulf News article gets picked up
by other publications, improves our AI citability, supports our Wikipedia
submission, and builds trust with customers in India and Saudi who don't
know us yet.

Please confirm receipt and share the existing press coverage file by June
7th.

Thanks,
Manoj

emails.subject: Re: Urgent — Wikipedia + Wikidata + Brand Entity | Start Today
emails.body:
Dear Sir,



The Wikidata entity can be completed by Monday.

Regarding Wikipedia, I reviewed the requirements and found that it requires multiple independent and reliable sources with significant coverage over time. Press releases and company-owned content are generally not considered sufficient.

Over the last 10 years, I have tried 3–4 times to create or get a Wikipedia page approved, but each attempt was rejected due to insufficient independent coverage. I have also tried to obtain mentions in reputable publications; however, Wikipedia mainly accepts references from established sources such as Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, Economic Times, Times of India, and similar publications.

Large brands like MakeMyTrip often receive this type of coverage organically. For brands like ours, obtaining placements in such publications typically requires a considerable PR investment, which has not been approved so far.

I will discuss this further with Manish & Vaibhav and share a detailed plan covering the current gaps, available opportunities, and the steps required to improve Rayna Tours' eligibility for Wikipedia while also strengthening our overall brand entity presence.




From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Fri, 05 Jun 2026 00:48:31

To: aparna@raynatours.com, manish.y@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Urgent — Wikipedia + Wikidata + Brand Entity | Start Today

 

Hi Aparna and Manish,

This is urgent and must start today. Do not wait for Sandstorm to begin this.

WHY THIS IS CRITICAL:

Wikipedia and Wikidata are among the most heavily weighted sources for AI search engines — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and Copilot all use Wikipedia to verify entity authority. Right now Rayna Tours has no Wikipedia article and no Wikidata entity. This means AI engines cannot fully verify who Rayna is, which directly hurts our AI citability score. Sandstorm confirmed our AI citability is currently 22/100 — Wikipedia is one of the fastest ways to improve this. However Wikipedia approval by volunteer editors can take weeks or months. We cannot afford to wait.

YOUR TASKS — START TODAY:

Task 1 — Create Wikidata Entity for Rayna Tours (This Week)

Wikidata is easier and faster than Wikipedia — it can be done immediately without editorial approval. Go to wikidata.org, create an account, and create a new entity for Rayna Tours with the following properties:


	Instance of: company
	Official name: Rayna Tours
	Founded: 2006
	Headquarters: Dubai, UAE
	Industry: Tourism, Destination Management
	Website: raynatours.com
	Number of employees: 1,600+
	Social media links: LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X
	Review profiles: Trustpilot, TripAdvisor, Google


This alone will immediately improve how AI engines recognise and cite Rayna.

Task 2 — Assess Wikipedia Notability and Begin Draft (This Week)

Wikipedia requires "notability" — meaning Rayna must be covered by significant independent reliable sources before an article can be created. Research and compile the following:


	All press coverage of Rayna Tours in Gulf News, Khaleej Times, Arabian Business, Economic Times, Times of India, Mirror Review, Trade Arabia, and any other reputable publications
	Any awards or industry recognition Rayna has received
	Any significant milestones — founding story, major partnerships, expansion announcements


Once you have compiled this, assess whether we have enough independent sources to meet Wikipedia's notability criteria. If yes — begin drafting the Wikipedia article immediately. If not — identify the gaps and tell me what additional PR coverage we need to qualify.

Task 3 — Standardise Brand Entity Across All Platforms (This Week)

AI engines verify entity consistency by checking if Rayna's name, address, phone number, and description are consistent across all platforms. Please audit and standardise the following immediately:


	Google Business Profile — name, address, phone, description, category
	LinkedIn company page — description, founded year, industry, website
	Facebook — description, contact details, website
	Trustpilot profile — description, website link
	TripAdvisor profile — description, contact details
	Any travel directories — Lonely Planet, Yelp, Yellow Pages UAE, etc.


Everything must say the same thing. Company name: Rayna Tours. Description must reflect our positioning: destination expert and ground operator, not a marketplace. Brand line: "Your experience, our responsibility."

DEADLINE:

Wikidata entity — June 7th.

Wikipedia notability assessment — June 10th.

Brand entity audit — June 10th.

Do not wait for Sandstorm on any of this. This runs in parallel with their engagement and is entirely within your capability to execute now.

Update me with progress by June 10th.

Thanks,

Manoj

emails.subject: India B2C Activation — Direction + Action Required | Maharashtra + North India
emails.body:
Hi Senthil,

I've been working through our India B2C strategy and want to share the
direction with you. Looping in Ranjan, Rajkumar, and Vaibhav as they'll be
central to execution.

You know the team and the ground better than anyone — I'm sharing the full
picture here so you can take this forward and shape the execution plan with
them.
------------------------------

*THE DIRECTION*

India B2C is our biggest untapped opportunity right now. Our B2B network is
strong — that's solid. But Rayna as a direct consumer brand in India is
still underexposed. We are starting with two clusters where we have the
strongest presence, the right customer profile, and the highest online
booking behavior.

Our positioning is clear and powerful: *we are not a marketplace, we are
the ground operator.* We own what we sell. No local agent in any of these
cities can say that. That's our story and every campaign must carry it.

Brand line: *"Your experience, our responsibility." — "Aapka safar, hamari
zimmedari."*
------------------------------

*TARGET CITIES — TWO CLUSTERS*

*Cluster 1 — Maharashtra (Pune base, existing team):*
Pune, Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad, Nagpur, Kolhapur

*Cluster 2 — North India (High spending, corporate, strong online booking
culture):*
Delhi, Chandigarh, Ludhiana, Amritsar, Jaipur, Agra, Meerut

Together these two clusters cover the highest value B2C travel market in
India. Maharashtra for volume and proximity to our Pune office. North India
for high spending customers who book online and travel frequently.
------------------------------

*TWO PHASE PLAN*

*Phase 1 — June to August (Soft launch, low cost)*

We go quiet but smart. No expensive media yet. Build awareness, test
messaging, collect leads, and simultaneously expand our destination
portfolio.

Destinations to promote — everything currently live: Thailand, Singapore,
Bali, Malaysia, Dubai visas, international visas, Abu Dhabi, Saudi.

Channels — digital only:

   - Meta and Google — geo-targeted to both clusters — Maharashtra 6 cities
   + North India 7 cities
   - WhatsApp broadcasting to our existing customer database filtered by
   these cities
   - PR — Maharashtra Times, ET Pune, Lokmat, Sakal, Hindustan Times Delhi,
   Tribune Chandigarh, Dainik Bhaskar, Conde Nast India
   - Corporate partnerships — IT companies Pune tech corridor, financial
   sector Mumbai, corporate sector Delhi and Chandigarh
   - Influencer program — hosted trips only, no cash spend. Shortlist from
   both Maharashtra and North India creators to come through Vaibhav for my
   approval before any outreach.

Two things must be in place before any spend: pricing competitive (Ranjan +
Rajkumar report June 8th) and website fixed (June 9th).

*Phase 2 — September onwards (Full campaign, full spend)*

TV, radio, newspaper, mall activations — both clusters simultaneously. But
by September we need a global portfolio behind us that no local agent can
match. That's what makes Rayna different. Anyone can sell Thailand and
Singapore. Nobody else can offer this:

   - Europe — London, Paris, Amsterdam, Barcelona, Switzerland, Rome,
   Istanbul
   - Far East — Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong
   - Indian Ocean — Maldives, Mauritius, Seychelles
   - Americas — USA, Canada
   - Australia and New Zealand

September campaign: *"From Thailand to London, from Bali to Switzerland —
Rayna Tours. Your experience, our responsibility."*

*Maharashtra channels:* TV (Zee Marathi, Star Pravah, Colors Marathi, Star
Plus, Sony), Radio (Mirchi, Big FM, Red FM), Newspaper (Maharashtra Times,
Lokmat, Sakal, TOI Maharashtra), Mall activations (Phoenix Mumbai,
Palladium, Seawoods, Westend Pune)

*North India channels:* TV (Aaj Tak, News18 India, Star Plus, Zee News),
Radio (Mirchi Delhi, Red FM Delhi, Big FM), Newspaper (Hindustan Times,
Times of India Delhi, Tribune, Dainik Bhaskar, Punjabi Tribune), Mall
activations (Select Citywalk Delhi, DLF Malls, Elante Chandigarh, Ambience
Mall Gurgaon)
------------------------------

*WHAT I NEED FROM YOU — TWO ACTIONS:*

*Action 1 — By June 20th:*
Share execution ownership for each Phase 1 channel with me. Who owns what,
timelines, and how we're going to market across both clusters.

*Action 2 — By June 30th:*
Identify 5 people internally from Rayna to own the new destination groups.
Their job is supplier contracting, product management, and getting
inventory live by August 31st. Structure:

   1. Europe West — London, Paris, Amsterdam, Barcelona
   2. Europe East + Turkey — Switzerland, Rome, Istanbul, Prague
   3. Far East — Japan, South Korea, China, Hong Kong
   4. Indian Ocean + Africa — Maldives, Mauritius, Kenya, Seychelles
   5. Americas + Australia — USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand

These people start July 1st. August 31st — all destinations live. September
1st — we go loud across both clusters.
------------------------------

Senthil, this is a big moment for Rayna in India. I know you and the team
will execute this well. Let's connect once you've had a chance to review
this with Ranjan, Rajkumar, and Vaibhav.

Thanks,
Manoj

   - Structure multiple agents into a coordinated workflow.
   - Connect AI systems to live business data sources.
   - Reduce repetitive reporting and operational work.
   - Build reusable workflows for audits, QA, and planning.
   - Avoid common implementation mistakes.

This workshop explains how to move beyond isolated prompts and start
building operational AI systems that support real marketing work. Here’s
what we’ll cover:

   - *Multi-agent orchestration: *How specialized agents can handle
   different stages of work together.
   - *Working with live business data: *Connecting workflows to Search

   - *Shared data structures: *Using a single source of truth to keep

   automatically.
   - *Common setup pitfalls: *Mistakes teams make early and how to avoid

emails.subject: Re: Urgent — Competitive Price Review | Action Required This Week
emails.body:
Dear Sir,



Noted. I will start correcting obvious pricing gaps immediately and will complete the market-wise competitor comparison for the top 20 products across India, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Global by June 8th.

I will also clearly highlight inclusion differences, recommended pricing action, and ensure attraction-only tickets are brought at or below competitor pricing without delay.

Thanks & Regards,

Rajkummar Gaikwad

Business Head – Rayna Tours

📞 Office: +91 20 6683 8800 (Ext - 273) | Mobile/WhatsApp: +91 90962 96070



India Head Office

Rayna Group Pvt. Ltd.

Sr. No. 33/2, Ashtavinayak Rd, MITCON Lane,

Balewadi, Pune – 411045, Maharashtra, India



Other Offices: Dubai (UAE) | Abu Dhabi (UAE) | RAK (UAE) | Singapore | Malaysia | Thailand | UK | Switzerland





From: manoj@raynatours.com

Sent: Wed, 03 Jun 2026 12:11:49

To: ranjan@raynab2b.com, rajkumar@raynatours.com

Cc: senthil@raynatours.com, Deepak Hiranandani <deepak@raynatours.com>, Vaibhav Gupta <vaibhav@raynatours.com>

Subject: Urgent — Competitive Price Review | Action Required This Week

 

Hi Ranjan and Rajkumar,

I have reviewed our pricing across key destinations and we have a serious problem. Our prices are higher than competition on several products — especially attraction tickets. This must be fixed immediately before we begin any marketing push into India, UAE, and Saudi.

The Problem:

For attraction-only tickets, the experience is delivered by the attraction — not by Rayna. The customer has no reason to pay more for the same ticket from us. We are losing bookings purely on price. This is not acceptable.

IMMEDIATE ACTION — Manual Price Review (Complete by June 8th):

Both of you must submit a competitive price comparison report for your top 20 products by June 8th. Format is simple:


	Product name
	Our current selling price (adult and child)
	Top 3 competitor prices for the same product online — MakeMyTrip, Viator, GetYourGuide, or whoever is most relevant for that destination
	Are our inclusions the same, better, or different? Note any differences.
	Recommended action — reduce markup, match competition, or justify premium with inclusions


Ranjan — destinations to cover: Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, and India as a destination (products promoted to UAE, Saudi, and global travelers coming to India).

Rajkumar — destinations to cover: Dubai (all attractions, holidays, tours, transfers) and International Visas across all markets — India, UAE, Saudi, and Global.

Important — how to check competition per market:

Each destination must be checked across all four source markets separately — India, UAE, Saudi, and Global. A product may be competitively priced for a UAE customer but expensive for an Indian customer because local OTAs in India are selling the same product cheaper. Check competition from each market's perspective.

Pricing Rules — Effective Immediately:


	Attraction tickets only — our price must always be at or below the lowest competitor. Target 1% below the cheapest. No exceptions. Reduce markup accordingly.
	Tours with shared transfers — competitive. We have a cost advantage with our own fleet. Use it.
	Tours with inclusions — match competition minimum. If our inclusions are better, communicate them clearly on the product page. Do not assume the customer will notice.
	Holiday packages — competitive pricing with clear inclusion advantage. This is where we can justify our expertise.
	Visas (Rajkumar) — check competition from all three core markets. Visa pricing must be competitive across India, UAE, and Saudi independently.


Important:

Do not wait for the report to start fixing obvious pricing gaps. If you already know a product is overpriced compared to competition — fix the markup today. The report is to ensure nothing is missed.

We are also building an AI-powered dynamic pricing tool that will automate competitor price monitoring and markup adjustment going forward. But that takes time to build. The manual fix must happen now.

Report due: June 8th. No extensions.

Thanks,

Manoj
